boardgame: Botanical Bliss: A Floral Battle of Wits
description:
Bloom brighter than the rest and win in this battle of wits! Place your tokens on the game board to create beautiful patterns. Dominate the board with elegance and skill by filling the spaces wisely. You will need a little bit of strategy and a sprinkle of luck. Will you flourish or wilt?

—description from the publisher

boardgame: No Angels Came: The Battle of Dai Do, May-April 1968
description:
Following the Tet Offensive, the forces of the South Vietnamese Communist’s NLF (National Liberation Front) were devastated. However, the war’s momentum had clearly shifted in favor of the Communists, and the military leaders in Hanoi strongly believed that maintaining the battlefield pressure against the US and ARVN forces was needed now more than ever.

One of the first areas to feel the effects of the continued Communist offensive effort was in I Corps, just south of the Demilitarized Zone (DMZ) that separated North and South Vietnam. The PAVN’s 320th Infantry Division, that had been guarding the coast north of the DMZ from a threatened US invasion (Operation Cisco/Durango City) moved south and successfully infiltrated positions guarded by the ARVN’s 2nd  Infantry Division. Occupying fortified and concealed bunker complexes constructed earlier, the PAVN units were ideally placed to launch attacks along the crucial Bo Dieu River that could cut-off a significant portion of I Corps from other units protecting Hue and in the northern section of the Central Highlands. To restore the integrity of I Corps and eliminate the Communist threat, the US 3rd Marine Division, along with elements of the ARVN 2nd Infantry Division engaged in what was another costly and bitter battle; one that sowed more doubts back in the USA that the war in Vietnam was not just unwinnable, but one that could very well be lost by the US military. Perhaps you can do as well or better?

—description from the designer

This gameplay expansion for Syncanite Foundation allows you to master the CRIMSON PROTOCOL – the unofficial rules of power!

Pulling these hidden strings allows you to influence the game on a whole new level. Systems get deeper, allowing you to build long term advantages, what introduces new strategies and thus ways to trigger the crisis of your choice you aspire to win by.

Features of Crimson Protocol:
Area Majority Mechanic that gives 2 new resources:

     Favours (from each of the three nations on the map)
     Prestige (that can be lost by playing nasty operation cards)


Use favors to trigger up to three additional, unique effects of your role card or claim a doctrine of your choice and use prestige to level it to unlock another two unique, very strong game changing effects.

But be careful: Having negative prestige might activate maluses on your victory conditions what debuffs your momentum when trying to win or even punishes you right away if you hit -3 prestige!

On the other side, you can build networks through your buying choices to access new ways to play the game:

     The establishment network to steer politics as you wish and manipulate the global courts
     The criminal network to commit more precise criminal operations
     The mercenary network to initiate piracy and sabotage operstions aka use lethal force even without the crisis "War"


All these networks can also enable access to buffs for your victory conditions, what – together with the debuff system – makes victory conditions asymmetric for all players – a lot of opportunities and challenges lie ahead!

All of this gets spiced up by another two, new system:

     Corporations you can invest in to access their technology
     The Syncanite Foundation Reserve System that ensures stability on the syncanite market to avoid greater chaos


Building a cooperation requires resources. Players can share this expensive investments to become joint shareholders. But by investing capital into foundation certificates, you can increase your amount of shares in a company to unlock even stronger technology. These certificates however need to be paid back to ensure the stability of the syncanite market as the Reserve system must ensure that the market is always within the legal trading range that all players change together by vocal vote.

This system can be used to push the crisis of your choice as using tech allows you to increase a crisis of your choice. The shares in companies also add to the area majority counting + create capital revenue so owning them builds long term advantages. And who knows ... maybe destabilizing the syncanite reserve system is exactly what you need to plunge the continent into the chaos you aspire? Or will you use it to avoid Tyranny from occurring that might end you all?

Because crisis get counted till 30 now unlocking multipe crisis events each time a crisis hits 3, 6 or 9 after initial activation. Whoever pushed the crisis over the next edge gets to decide what global effect will trigger … but not with Tyranny as it is the Ascendant – the Emperor – the tyrant himself who will decide what game mechanic will be eliminated before he gets rid of you winning the game all by himself when Tyranny hits 30.

So ... playing with fire has never been more dangerous. But also ... never been that rewarding!
Which hiding string of power will you pull to take over the Syncanite Foundation?

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Shallow Sea: Fish Duo Ecosystem Card
description:
FISH DUO: 3 VPs for every 2 Completed Fish of the SAME color.

This Ecosystem Card was included with Promo Pack A during the Dice Tower 2026 crowdfunding campaign.

boardgame: Star Wars: Shatterpoint – Padawan Ahsoka Tano Promo
description:
This is a Secondary Unit for the Galactic Republic faction. Mechanically, this promo figure is identical to the version later released in the "Lead by Example" Squad Pack; it served as an early access release for local hobby stores.

Padawan Ahsoka Tano is characterized by extreme mobility, primarily through her Jump abilities, and offers strong synergies with Jedi Primary Units. As a defensive specialist, she excels at repositioning and countering enemy strikes, making her a slippery and difficult target to pin down on the battlefield.

boardgame: Runes of Ede Expandable Card Game
description: In this simple, two-player game, players pick one or two factions to play: Mystics (purple), Intellects (blue), Marvels (yellow), or Savages (red), composed of chibi characters. Then they construct two draw decks: a Stockpile consisting of Rune cards, and an Archive of Units and Events. Players start with a hand of seven Archive cards and take turns drawing one rune, which are used to put Units or Events into play. Players then battle each other, losing runes each time they're damaged, the goal being to inflict nine damage or deplete the opponent's Stockpile.

boardgame: Boss Fighters QR: Mysteriöses Ei Promo
description:
Mysterious Egg

Place this item with the other items. Scan the Mysterious Egg as an action to hatch it. What might emerge this time? You may use this item an unlimited number of times.

Party Flipper is a fast-paced party game of skill, luck and a bit of chaos.

Place a cup filled with a drink in the center of the table on a platform. Players take turns flipping their cards onto the platform, aiming to touch the previous player’s card in mid-air or on landing.

If a player fails to make contact, they receive a penalty, each other player gives them one card. If cards fall off the platform, the player who made the throw collects all fallen cards.

The goal is simple: be the first to get rid of all your cards. The losing player drinks whatever is in the cup, from soft drinks for family play to more extreme options for party settings such as a raw egg.

Additional penalties apply for rule violations, such as throwing out of turn, missing the platform entirely, failing to flip the card, or touching the cards or platform during the game.

In Dumb Animals (低等动物), players enter a chaotic animal kingdom where deception, memory, and psychological play determine victory. Each card represents an animal within a quirky food-chain hierarchy, but not everything is as it seems—players can play cards face-down and bluff about their strength, daring others to challenge them.


     On your turn, either play cards or challenge the previous player
     Cards can be played face-up (triggering effects) or face-down while declaring a stronger animal
     Other players may challenge the declared card
     If a challenge succeeds, the challenger gains the cards; otherwise, the challenged player gains them
     Each animal card has unique effects that influence gameplay
     A special Carrot Coin can be used once per game to cancel or initiate a challenge
     The game ends when the draw pile is empty, and players score based on collected cards

In Tabula Smaragdina (翠玉录), players explore a world inspired by ancient philosophy and alchemy, seeking the fundamental truth behind existence. Through symbolic transformations and logical chains, players reconstruct meaning from chaos.

Players take turns activating cards on their personal board to gain or transform resources. Colored cubes represent stages of alchemical transformation. Complete objectives on the central board to gain coins and meet victory conditions. Characters provide unique abilities inspired by historical philosophers. Includes competitive, solo, and cooperative modes.

9 Cards: Season 3 (九张牌：第三季) is the third edition of 9 Cards (九张牌), an initiative that gives designers the opportunity to create a full gaming experience using only nine cards.

The 9 games are:


     S3E1 情绪骰子 (Emotional Dice)
     S3E2 喵喵开箱 (Meme Unboxing)
     S3E3 舞池假面 (Dancing with Masks)
     S3E4 尼罗之心 (Heart of Nile)
     S3E5 春秋赛马 (Horse Racing)
     S3E6 关原之战 (Battle of Sekihara)
     S3E7 炼金考核 (Alchemist's Trial), out of scope for BGG
     S3E8 翼装飞行 (Wingsuit Flying)
     S3E9 纷争三国 (Three Kingdoms to One)


Small accessories may be needed, but not included in the box.
publishers: 九张牌玩具公司(9 Cards Toy Company)

description:
「家庭教師ヒットマンREBORN! THE 100 DILEMMAS -ザハンドレッドジレンマ-」is a psychological party board game based on the classic anime.

The game features 100 "extreme dilemma" scenarios with no absolute right or wrong answers (e.g., conflicts between loyalty and family interests). Players must make their own tough choices while predicting what others will choose.

Core Gameplay: Score points by correctly guessing your friends' decisions, sparking hilarious and heated debates.
Anime Immersion: Scenarios incorporate Vongola Famiglia and Mafia elements, allowing fans to role-play and argue from the characters' perspectives.
Easy to Play: With simple rules and high interactivity, it is a perfect icebreaker for party gatherings.

boardgame: 銀魂将軍だーれだ (Gintama Who’s the Shogun?)
description:
Gintama Who’s the Shogun? is a party game themed around a funny face contest, where players respond to prompts using character face expressions.

Match funny faces to secret prompts using Gintama character cards, but one player doesn't know the prompt! Spot the clueless Player (Shogun) among 108 cards of pure comedic chaos, featuring the series' most iconic and insane expressions.

Crown of Critters is a competitive fantasy board game for 2-6 players. Choose your Hero and assemble teams of 5 Critters to embark on Quests and earn the favor of King Shellford. Each Critter is worth a certain amount of points and has powerful abilities that can be used to increase your chances at success. Match your Critter Classes to the Classes on the current Quest card to avoid the Quest Class Penalty, which causes Critters to lose points. Play a variety of Action cards and use your unique Hero ability to hinder opponents and aid your team of Critters. At the end of each Quest, the players calculate the total point value of the Critters on their Team and move their Hero Token along the main board. The player with the most points after all Quests are complete wins the game.

—description from the publisher

Cards of Honor – Eastern front is a card game for one to two players (best played with 2) that in very simplified way presents battles on the Eastern front between Germans and Soviets during Wold War II. Units in game are presented by cards, and players use them as they clash each-other.
Objective of the game is to score more points than your opponent, in two battles (with a possibility of third), by destroying enemy units.

Each battle consist of turns in which top card from the Army deck is drawn and pitted against a top card drawn from the enemy's Army deck. Their fight is resolved, and depending on that resolve, the next units in line will fight either with a surviving unit or a new one.
Players can always see which units will be next to fight, as Army decks stays face up throughout the duration of the battle.

After battle is finished, each player will summarize his score from the destroyed units he collected. Hollow stars are worth half a point, one full star is worth 1 point.

Depending on used generals, add a bonus points if you managed to achieve a Tactical, Strategic or a Morale victory, as written on his card. You only check for victory conditions from the card of a general you have used.
For a Tactical victory, you need to have more cards with a hollow star than your opponent. If both of you have same amount of those cards, neither achieved a Tactical victory. For a Tactical victory you gain +3 points to your score.
For a Strategic victory, you need to have more cards with a star than your opponent. If both of you have same amount of those cards, neither achieved a Strategic victory. For a Strategic victory you gain +4 points to your score.
For a Morale victory, you need to have more cards with two stars than your opponent. If both of you have same amount of those cards, neither achieved a Morale victory. For a Morale victory you gain +5 points to your score.
Player with most points wins that Battle round.

—description from the designer

Can you drop it right on the target ?!

This is the new updated edition of Nice Egg, featuring Bacon, Lettuce, and Tomatoes!
Join in the fried-egg party and set your sites on getting a 'Nice Egg'!

Players take turns cracking the egg and dropping the yolk.  If you land on the target you can collect a bacon, lettuce or tomato.  Become the star of the party by collecting all 3!

—description from the publisher

It's a jumping frog tournament!

Ping Pond Frog! is a fun frog hopping game!
Bounce the frog and make it jump off the lily pad to the other side of the pond! 
Take turns aiming for the target on the other side of the pond and collect them.  
The center targets may help either of you achieve your goals. 
Fun antics await you in the pond!

—description from the publisher

It's a mysterious box full of candy. What candy will you get?

Candy Tap is a mysterious candy box.  Tap it and some candy will fall out.

Players take turns in tapping on the candy box to drop the candy out and collect them.  
Will you drop them out little by little or go all in?

The Inner Box is designed by Masakazu Takizawa (Koguma Koubou).

—description from the publisher

Pack Pack! Back Back!

Feed your robots with energy cubes in Pack-Back Robot! A 2 player energy collection game.

The game is played in 2 phases.  In phase one, you simultaneously race to feed your robots. 
Then  in phase two, once you've gathered enough use the cubes to charge up the energy.  You'll need a keen eye to collect the colors that match your energy meter.  But charging up the meter might not be as easy as it seems.

—description from the publisher

The Council of Anderian is a competitive yet highly interactive board game that blends strategy, intrigue, and roleplay. Each player leads one of the Great Houses of Anderian, competing to earn prestige and be the first to reach 7 victory points by the end of the year.

There are five paths to victory: politics, religion, war, economy, and intrigue. Each path dominates a different phase of the game, forcing players to adapt their plans, read their rivals, and seize opportunities at the right moment.

Every player holds a Council position that grants unique strengths tied to a specific strategy. The Marshal excels during the war phase, while the Inquisitor thrives in intrigue, for example. The Council itself is led by a player: the Regent, who assigns positions to the other players. On top of that, each player receives a secret Personality Trait. Some will be driven to support the Regent, while others will bide their time and wait for the perfect moment to betray and overthrow them. This is where shifting alliances, negotiation, and uneasy cooperation come to life.

And when alliances become too strong, there is always a darker solution: assassination. Secretly eliminating a key ally can shatter a dominant coalition and throw the balance of power back into chaos. But death is not the end. If your character is killed, you simply draw a new Trait card for your House and continue the game as your heir. You stay in the struggle, but you will need to rebuild your alliances, regain influence, and win back the support of the factions if you want to reclaim your place at the heart of the Council.

—description from the designer

These new terrain tiles are used in addition to the terrain tiles of the base game. This will make your game board bigger and you will have more sources of income. Follow the game setup as usual. A creature will also be placed on these terrain tiles.

This is a board card game that will give you and your loved ones an ocean of positive emotions!

It doesn't require any special skills or memorization of complex rules. However, it will stimulate your mind and help develop logic, imagination, resourcefulness, strategic thinking, and even fine motor skills.

The basic principle of the game is very simple. Cards are stacked so that honey flows from card to card. Each honey flow must connect with another honey flow!

The mission of the game is to build a Honey River, an entire engineering structure in the form of a honey river slowly flowing through the vastness of a beehive. Each meandering stream must precisely connect with the others, creating a single honey flow.

If you manage to complete the Honey River to the end of the deck, you win! Together, as one team! You've created your own unique hive with honey rivers for your bee colony. Congratulate each other!

If the deck runs out of cards and there's even one open honey flow left on the board, you lose! And again, everyone loses. The honey has leaked out of the hive, and the bees are very unhappy with you... Try playing again!

—description from the publisher (translated)

Это настольная карточная игра, которая подарит вам и вашим близким океан положительных эмоций!

Она не потребует особых навыков и запоминания сложных правил. Однако сумеет пробудить ваш разум и поможет развить логику, воображение, сообразительность, стратегическое мышление и даже мелкую моторику рук.

Основной принцип игры очень прост. Карточки складываются друг к другу таким образом, чтобы мед перетекал из карточки в карточку. Каждый медопоток должен соединяться с другим медопотоком!

Миссия игры — соорудить Медовод, целую инженерную конструкцию в виде медовой реки, медленно текущей по просторам пчелиного улья. Каждый её извилистый ручей должен точно соединяться с другими, создавая единый медовый поток.

Если у вас получилось собрать Медовод до конца колоды, то вы выиграли! Все вместе, одной командой! У вас получился свой уникальный улей с медовыми реками для пчелиной семьи. Поздравьте друг друга!

Если карты в колоде закончились, а на игровом поле остался хотя бы один не замкнутый медопоток, то вы проиграли! И опять же — все вместе. Мёд вытек из улья и пчёлы очень вами недовольны... Попробуйте сыграть еще раз!

—description from the publisher

In this 2-player board game, you immerse yourself in 16th- and 17th-century Europe, a time when old certainties were crumbling and new ideas spread at an unprecedented pace. Thanks to the revolutionary invention of the printing press, knowledge was no longer confined to elites but rapidly reached a much wider audience.

This flow of ideas fueled unrest across a continent already torn by religious and political tensions, such as during the Protestant Reformation. The Catholic Church watched with growing concern as heretical and innovative pamphlets stirred minds and challenged centuries-old authority.

In Ink & Inquisition, you choose your role in this ideological battleground.

Play as a philosopher, spreading forbidden knowledge, protecting controversial books, and reshaping the thinking of Europe. Or take on the role of an inquisitor, enforcing orthodoxy: track down heresy, exert influence in major cities, and suppress dangerous ideas before they take root.

Call upon influential figures from this turbulent era, such as Desiderius Erasmus or Galileo Galilei, or seek the support of powerful popes and monarchs determined to crush intellectual dissent at its source.

Do you dare to rewrite… or control the future of Europe?

—description from the designer

Immerse yourself in a centuries-old tradition where flavor, patience, and skill become art.

In Ates, each player becomes a master confectioner, competing to create the finest ate (a type of fruit paste) using select ingredients, traditional tools, and techniques passed down through generations. From the careful cooking of the fruit to the perfect cut, every decision counts in achieving the ideal balance of texture, color, and flavor.

Will you be able to master the process and become the ultimate ate artisan?

Ates is a resource management and placement game, as well as an order delivery game where each turn your ingredients move down the board. You must either deliver your order or discard your products.

—description from the publisher

In this lively dice game for 2–4 players, you dive into the world of Faultopia (Slothtopia) and take on the roles of diligent, slightly sleepy sloths sent out to gather ingredients for the grand village feast. Village elder Papa Faulus has chosen you to collect the island’s rarest treats and turn them into an unforgettable meal.

You compete to be the first to assemble valuable food bundles by reaching the island’s scenic lookout points and collecting ingredients there. The first player to gather three food bundles (or five for a longer game) triggers the final round.

On your turn, you roll dice and cleverly combine the results to move along different island paths. If you use all your dice efficiently, you can keep going and push your luck to reach even more valuable ingredients. But be careful: unused dice or failed combinations can abruptly end your turn or even set you back.

Reaching a lookout point earns you a rare ingredient for your food bundle, and that path resets for the other players. Good timing, smart decisions, and a bit of risk-taking determine who ultimately proves themselves as the master chef. Optional events add extra surprises and keep the game dynamic, while special action cards can give you an advantage or slow down your fellow players.

—description from the publisher

This board game was manufactured and released in Greece in the early 1990s by ΜΙΚΑ toy company and can be played by 2, 3 or 4 players.

This game is based on a fictional story of a star fleet, which after a difficult mission in the depths of space, found itself scattered and pursued by enemy ships. The star fleet consists of 4 teams, blue, red, yellow and orange. Each player takes on the role of a squadron commander in a team of 4 spaceships and his mission is to manage to unite his ships at some point in space.

The one who will be the first to place his 4 spaceships in four adjacent hexagons on the board is the winner.

You've just arrived in the West looking for a better life, and like many before you, you've landed a job washing dishes at a busy restaurant. To escape the dish pit and get promoted, you need to load the dishwasher efficiently, score the most points, and prove you're the best!

The player who makes the best use of the available space, the one who combines events the most effectively, or maybe the one who puts the final item into the dishwasher wins. This is for you to find out.

This game can be best described as a distant relative of tetris, only competitive with a common play grid, and some events that might act as game changers if played with the right strategy.

—description from the designer

Vengeful Dragons is a dark fantasy card game designed for players who crave a blend of high-stakes strategy and aggressive, "take-that" interaction. In a realm where ancient power has been scattered, players assume the roles of fallen dragons seeking to reclaim their lost glory. By amassing magic-infused treasures and fortifying their hoards against the inevitable greed of their peers, players must prove their tactical superiority. The game's aesthetic and mechanical depth elevate the chaotic energy of traditional party games into a more sophisticated, moody competitive experience.

The gameplay begins with each player establishing a board presence consisting of a unique Dragon Card—each possessing a distinct ability—and a single starting Treasure Card. From a starting hand of five cards, players must navigate a diverse deck featuring Magic, Relics, Traps, and Raiders. Unlike many turn-based games, the inclusion of Magic and Relic cards allows for instantaneous play, creating a reactive environment where a player's fortunes can shift in a heartbeat. This "instant-speed" mechanic ensures that all participants remain engaged, even when it is not their active turn.

Strategic depth is primarily found in the tension between offense and defense. Players must decide whether to commit resources to expanding their own hoard through Raider cards—stealing essential treasures from rivals—or to prioritize the protection of their current assets with Traps. Defense is managed through the clever placement of Traps and the utilization of Relics that provide passive buffs or active deterrents. Success requires reading the table and baiting opponents into triggering hidden Traps, effectively turning a rival's aggression into their own downfall.

The path to victory is a race toward atmospheric dominance. The first player to successfully navigate the gauntlet of dark sorcery and end their turn with five Treasure Cards in their hoard is declared the winner. By blending accessible rules with complex tactical layers and a serious dark fantasy tone, Vengeful Dragons offers a robust experience for those who enjoy friendship-testing competition and the satisfaction of a perfectly executed, ruthless strategy.

—description from the publisher

This is the 2026 expansion duo for Tramways.

London includes 8 additional Citizens to pre-populate the Residences on the map... because you can't build new Residences!

Washington
A 1910 law restricted the height of buildings to the width of the adjacent street plus 20 feet. Energy and money have been focused on respecting this square plan imagined by Pierre Charles L’Enfant. Gain extra rewards for curved track and for delivering passengers to Leisure destinations.

—description from the publisher

Young and old can play together. The questions are split into two difficulty levels, so everyone has an equal chance to win. Players collect planet cards during the game. Whoever gets three different planet cards first and answers the final question correctly wins.

The Next Stop is a board game developed and compiled to teach our children some of our unique South African elements.

It is a fact that South-Africans are a proud nation that cherishes our people, environment and unique South African events!

While you play the board game The Next Stop you will learn things about our beautiful country and you can enjoy the game with your family and friends.

The Next Stop board game will bring about laughter while your team member tries to explain the word chameleon with charades; you’ll have to use some brain power when you need to answer a general knowledge question such as ‘Name South Africa’s eight animal records’. Do you and your children still remember South African proverbs? So much more fun and facts are locked up in this family board game that will ensure entertainment for the whole family.

Come travel with us throughout South Africa. Compile a team and choose one of six routes to travel on. You will move through ten towns to get from the starting point to the finish point. You will make a pit stop at some unfamiliar towns such as Warrenton, Daniëlskuil, Ficksburg and Bethulie and you will also move through the more familiar cities such as Durban, Cape Town and Mossel Bay. But be careful, you can get a penalty card and be sent off to SANPARKS Kruger National Park to spend two nights there or you can get a flat tyre and sit out a round of play.

Your journey will be full of challenges and setbacks.

Deep in the jungle, on the Isle of Uku, the mythical Mo'o are a problem. In an effort to banish the Mo'o, the village's Kahuna has fallen--leaving an open seat for the next strongest tribe member. Are you fierce and clever enough to become the next Kahuna?

Kahuna Uku is a savagely strategic card game where only the most cunning player will emerge victorious. Choose your opponents wisely, or there may be blood.

To become the Kahuna, be the first player to play out of your three-card sets. Build the discard pile. Pressure the table. Force mistakes. And when someone thinks they're safe, remind them that they're not.

—description from the publisher

The trick-taking game reimagined.

A trick-taking, deck building spy game of hidden agendas and tactical foresight.

In play, Spytails combines trick-taking with deck-building. Each agent is represented by a unique hand-carved wooden meeple, crafted with care to match their secretive persona. Players move through a series of operations where they must forecast their success, play cards strategically, and respond to shifting situations at the table. It is not just about having strong cards, but about using them at the right moment, reading your opponents, and adapting your plan as the round unfolds. The deck-building element allows players to refine their options over time, adding more control and strategy as the game progresses.

To win, players aim to earn the most Influence by the end of the game. This means managing your operations wisely, making strong forecasts, disrupting opponents when it benefits you, and building a deck that supports your overall plan. The player who handles these hidden agendas and tactical choices most effectively will emerge as the top agent and win the game.

—description from the publisher

The old king is dead, and the throne stands empty. In a court filled with intrigue, seven ancient families compete for ultimate power, forging alliances and betraying one another in a relentless struggle for dominance. Through cunning strategy, political maneuvering, and timely coups, players attempt to seize the bloodstained crown and secure their legacy as ruler.

The Usurper: Rusty Crown (篡位：锈色王冠) is a fast-paced card game of shifting power. Players take on the roles of rival factions manipulating influence, loyalty, and ambition. Every decision—whether to support a coup, betray an ally, or quietly build strength—can change the course of the game.

Take care of your Tamagotchi and help it grow in this fun and fast-paced card game!

Earn Happiness Points by feeding your Tamagotchi, playing with it, giving it gifts, cleaning up after it (yes, even the poo!), and keeping it well-behaved.
Sabotage other players to slow them down while making sure your own Tamagotchi stays happy and healthy.
Evolve your Tamagotchi into a cooler, more mature version!

The player with the most Happiness Points at the end of the game wins!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Fields of Fate: The Wild Wood
description:
The road ends at the treeline.
Beyond it lies something older than the frontier. Something watching. Something waiting.

The Wild Wood is a narrative campaign for Fields of Fate, designed around escalating tension, uncertain outcomes, and battles where victory is never guaranteed. This is not a campaign about holding the line, it’s about crossing it.

Your force begins at the edge of civilisation, drawn in by rumours of a missing caravan. What starts as an investigation quickly becomes something more dangerous. The deeper you push into the forest, the less certain the ground beneath you.

Each scenario builds on the last. Early success gives momentum, each loss stiffles your chances of making it out alive.

The campaign culminates in a branching finale shaped by your actions. Will you hunt down you enemy and end the threat at its source, or be forced into a desperate last stand?.

Inside the Book:

     36 pages, a full 21 pages bigger the Blackspire Pass campaign. More depth, More tension, more more more. 
     4 linked narrative scenarios
     A branching final act with two distinct endings
     Campaign map and thematic locations
     Scenario-specific rules and objectives
     Narrative outcomes that carry between battles


Requirements:

     Fields of Fate (core system required)
     Fantasy miniatures and ruleset of your choice
     A standard tabletop and terrain


The Wild Wood draws on the feel of classic campaign play. Uncertain, atmospheric, and driven by the outcomes of your battles.

Step beyond the treeline.
And see what waits.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Enemy Mine: A scenario for Torpedoes and Tides
description:
Enemy Mine is a scenario for coastal forces wargames rules Torpedoes and Tides. 
It features a German S-boat flotilla that is surprised by British MTBs. 
Can it escape without losing any boats?

The scenario is written for Torpedoes and Tides, but can be used with any other set of rules if the ship stats are modified.

—description from the publisher

Questing Knight is a strategic Print & Play RPG. The game utilizes a compact 16-card special deck (included in the PDF) paired with a standard 52-card poker deck and a single six-sided die. It combines the accessibility of traditional playing cards with the depth of a tactical fantasy adventure.

The core engine revolves around forming hand card combinations to trigger powerful Knight skills. Players must carefully manage their cards to launch critical strikes against the monsters. Every card played from the poker deck acts as the energy or maneuver behind your knight’s special abilities, demanding constant tactical decision-making.

Combat is point-based struggle. Success rewards players with glory points, but the monsters are not passive targets. Each boss has unique behaviors, and failing to time your attacks correctly will result in fierce counterattacks, forcing players to balance aggressive scoring with careful defense. The Jokers serve as game-changing wildcards that can shift the tide of battle in an instant, adding a layer of unpredictability to every session.

As part of the elite Knighthood, you must venture out to defeat 8 powerful monster bosses. But honor is not shared - only the knight with the highest score will be crowned the Strongest Knight.

boardgame: Fuller's Ambush: A scenario for Torpeodes and Tides
description:
Fuller's Ambush is a scenario for coastal forces wargames rules Torpedoes and Tides.

It features an action by Canadian coastal forces legend Capt. Tom Fuller set in the Adriatic. Fuller is up against a German convoy escorted by S-boats - will he prevail?

The scenario is written for Torpedoes and Tides, but can be used with any other set of rules if the ship stats are modified.

—description from the publisher

Misfatto is a fast-paced deduction game that builds on the classic mechanics of Clue, but it offers quicker gameplay through new mechanics and a unique 'Case' card deck that changes the rules every time you play.

In this game, everyone is their own author, and there are no winners or losers. Imagination knows no bounds, and with a total of 72 symbols, boredom is never an issue, and there's hardly any repetition. Using the rolled symbols, a coherent story is invented and told. This educational game brings the whole family and friends together around the table.

Züribrätt is a board game released in 2021 that simulates a night out in Zurich. Players move across a board, past well-known bars and clubs, completing tasks on their way home. It's a local version that supports the nightlife scene, often focusing on tasks and interaction. The initial print run sold out quickly.

The knights must avoid the dangers in the forest and find the right tools to reach the castle. They must also cross a river and find the correct entrance. The troop of knights who reach the castle and the king first wins.

boardgame: Böast: A game of exaggerated pride
description:
Böast is a party card game for 3-6 players built around the Old Norse and Celtic traditions of competitive boasting at feast halls.

Each round, players secretly combine three cards -- a Deed, a Witness, and a Reaction -- to construct the most outrageous boast they can. The rotating judge (the Skald) reads each combination aloud and awards the point to the best one. First player to 7 points wins the title of Greatest Skald of the Age.

The game draws on real historical traditions: the Norse heitstrenging and sumbel (ritual oath and boasting ceremonies), and the Celtic curadmír -- the champion's portion, claimed through competitive boasting at feast halls. Flyting, the formal competitive insult tradition of the medieval Norse and Anglo-Saxon world, provides the adversarial spirit.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Endearment: A Royal Tour Mini Expansion
description:
A mini expansion for Endearment. Base game required to play.

The A Royal Tour mini expansion for Endearment features 24 new Setting cards with scenes from the royal residences in London during the early 1800's. We were captivated by the interiors and exteriors of the royal residences of the Regency era, the hand colored engravings by William Henry Pyne, as well as various scenes of London. They are truly exquisite and serve as an important illustration of many historical sites during this era.

boardgame: Amsterdam: The Meet or Beat Card Game
description:
Amsterdam is a fast-paced, family-friendly card game for 2–10 players played with a standard 52-card deck. The core rule is simple: meet or beat the top card on the discard pile — or pick up the entire pile.

The game features three wild cards that add real strategic depth. The 2 (“Make It Low”) resets the pile so the next player can play any card. The 3 (“Mirror”) becomes whatever card it covers — play a 3 on a King and the next player needs a King or better. The 10 (“Clear”) wipes the entire discard pile from the game and gives you another turn.

Cards exist in three layers — hand, face-up, and face-down — played in that order. Players must clear each layer before moving to the next. Many players win each round, but there’s only one loser, and they deal next.

Amsterdam is the only casual card game with an official tournament scoring system. Play sets of 10 rounds with position-based scoring — 1st out scores 0, last place takes a penalty. Lowest total wins.

The game originated in a small apartment in Estes Park, Colorado over 25 years ago and has spread organically through family game nights, camping trips, and annual ski trips. It is a refined, structured version of the game commonly known as Palace, Karma, Shithead, Shed, or Castle — with consistent rules, named wild card mechanics, and three levels of play (Beginner, Intermediate, Expert).

—description from the designer

boardgame: The Pendragon Cycle: Rise of the Merlin
description:
Before Camelot, before the crown, one man’s visions ignite a civilization’s rebirth. The Pendragon Cycle: Rise of the Merlin. Play the official board game of the all new, 7-part epic cinematic series.

Explore the sacred isle filled with enchantments as you race to discover which character, location, and weapon hold the secret of the Grail. Use cunning deduction and your character's special power to unmask the truth before your rivals do.

—description from the publisher

Minna de Quest is a cooperative trick-taking game where the players attempt to defeat a demon lord.

In each round, the lead player will determine which of several monsters in a market the players will fight - that player, however, does not participate in the battle. In general, the color of the monster determines the lead suit, and it's strength determines the sum of cards in that color the players must play.

The monsters have different levels, and some levels provide rewards. Additionally, defeating monsters grants experience points to the player who played the highest card of its color, and only players who have reached a certain xp threshold will participate in attempting to defeat the final boss.

The final boss is one continuous trick until players drop out by playing a card of the same suit as the previous player. The goal is for the sum of the players cards that they play to exceed the value of all of the undefeated monsters.
The Wings of Doom expansion brings divine interference, new playable monsters, and more meddling villagers to your game of monstrous mayhem.

In Flip Angel, players create a 2x3 grid of Angels and Fallen Angels in order to score the most points.

During each of the six turns, the players will summon an Angel or a Fallen Angel from a pool of cards, usually by paying the corresponding Ether: light Ether for Angels, dark Ether for Fallen Angels. The cards are double-sided, with an Angel on one side and a Fallen Angel on the other. At various points of the game, cards will be flipped over, changing the abilities present in the tableau.

Cards also have half sun or moon icons on their border. Each completed icon will be worth points at the end of the game.

Once the players have played six cards, the game is over and the player accumulating the most points is the winner!

Helendra is a magical forest populated by land, water, and flying animals and their respective habitats. This forest is constantly changing, and we all participate in shaping it, but the game is not cooperative; whoever achieves their hidden objective first wins. To complete the objectives, players must play cards from their hand in the forest, overlapping the corners, which will trigger different abilities.

São Paulo feeds millions of people every day. Behind every great meal is a chef who sourced the right ingredients, assured ingredient freshness, and served exactly what the customer ordered — under pressure, let alone the competition, every single week.

In On the Pass, you are that chef.

On the Pass is a competitive kitchen management game for 2–4 players set in Brazil's most vibrant food city: São Paulo.

Each week (round) a special event is triggered as the city pulse and you can perform actions to benefit you or hinder competitors. Then, buy fresh ingredients from the market, welcome your customers, and race to earn enough Fame Points to be crowned São Paulo's greatest chef.

Choose your restaurant
Each player runs one of four distinct restaurants (Pasta House, Burger Joint, Salad Bar and Sweet Spot), each with a culinary specialty that earns bonus Fame Points other chefs simply don't receive based on the customers served. No two players may choose the same restaurant.

Freshness is the heartbeat
Each of the seven (7) ingredients has its own shelf life. Any unused ingredient that expires is discarded. Managing your pantry is the strategic core of the game.

Serve, score, repeat
Each round, a mix of Regular and Special Customer cards arrives at your restaurant. You seat customers at your tables (up to three, or four with the Expansion upgrade), match their dish orders to your available ingredients, and serve. Regular Customers keep the coins flowing. Special Customers bring bigger fame rewards and pay more — but, of course, they bring more demanding orders. Fail to serve and they walk, keeping their money and eventually affecting your fame as a renowned chef.

Action Cards give you tactical advantages: You might outsmart your competitors and source ingredients at a lower cost due to a good relationship with the supplier.

Special Event Cards hit the majority or the whole table: a Food Festival floods everyone with extra customers.

Upgrade your kitchen
Throughout the game, you may upgrade your restaurant: deploy a Reservation System, expand your dining area, or choose a different one. Your rivals are watching the same upgrade options. Timing matters as much as planning.

The race to fame
The first chef to reach the Fame Point threshold (dependent on player count) triggers the final round. All players complete that round, add their end-game bonus points, then the chef with the most Fame Points wins. 
In case of a tie, the player with most coins remaining is the winner. If the tie remains, then most customers served during the game. If there is still a tie, players share the victory.

On the Pass plays in 60–90 minutes, scales cleanly from 2 to 4 players, and rewards both careful planners and bold opportunists. Every turn calls for real decisions that must be wisely chosen — and every wasted ingredient stings just a little.

Pokerhontas is a portable poker variant without chips. Players draw and discard cards while building strong hands — but discarded cards remain in play and can decide the outcome. The best hand wins unless the strongest combination is found in the discard pile.

Digging around in your uncle’s creepy basement, you find an old wood-paneled television set. You turn it on but it doesn’t seem to get any reception, just a blank red screen. You turn a knob and the screen turns blue. You smack the side and get static. You turn the knob and it’s still static, but in a different pattern. So weird. You turn it off and go to unplug it from the wall…but…it wasn’t plugged in? You hear a menacing laugh just before you hear a door slam and the lights go out.

Dead Channels is a trick taking game, but one that oscillates between Must-Follow and Must-Not-Follow. Players also will need to shift the direction they splay their cards as the mode of play shifts.

The goal is to win exactly 2 tricks at 4 players (or exactly 3 tricks at 3 players).

Deviations from that goal will be rewarded with points. Points are bad. The player with the least points after 4 hands (or 3 hands at 3 players) is the winner.

Falsities & Forgeries turns gaslighting into an art form by elevating everyday manipulation into an elegant, high-stakes heist. In this game, you are a Master Forger trying to steal originals and sell forgeries to the gallery, while the rest of the players act as art critics trying to appraise the legitimacy of your exhibit. During a round, every player takes a turn taking the stage as the 'Curator,' tasked with presenting a narrative exhibit to the rest of the table.

The mechanics are built entirely on improvised deception, where players have total creative freedom to curate a story about themselves, someone they know, or any world event. Players can use prompt cards as suggestions to help craft their stories, choosing to tell long detailed stories or shorter anecdotes and hot takes about anything. Ranging from personal experiences to celebrity drama, historical mishaps, ghost sightings, or even cold case files. The Curator must weaponize these facts or fabricate a masterpiece to control the room.

To win, you must pull off your heist by convincing the critics to throw out the originals and accept the fakes into the collection. The Curator only scores if they can gaslight the table into voting incorrectly. However, if the gallery spots the forgery or recognizes the truth, they earn points for their sharp deduction. Ultimately, you're either selling a priceless truth or passing off a brilliant forgery.

—description from the publisher
family: Theme: Fine Art and Art Museums

description:
God Gauntlet is a mythological card game all about chaos, sabotage, and racing to the top to prove who is the most egotistical. Play as gods from different pantheons, use unique abilities, and climb the Gauntlet while ruining your friends’ plans every chance you get.

Every match is packed with wild swings, brutal setbacks, and ridiculous moments. To win, players must land exactly on 21, the top space of the God Gauntlet, to earn a trophy. Overshoot it, and you fall back down, but not before triggering a chaotic event that can completely flip the game. From rock-paper-scissors showdowns to trophies changing hands in an instant, the tide can turn at any moment. The first player to collect 3 trophies wins.

Use number cards to climb the mountain, power cards to disrupt the table, and traps to catch your opponents when they least expect it. Chain cards together for clever combos, charge certain gods’ abilities with matching card types, and look for the perfect moment to sabotage someone’s run to the top. Whether you are making a big push, setting up a brutal reversal, or throwing the whole table into chaos, every turn gives you a new way to stir the pot.

—description from the designer

boardgame: At the Sharp End 2: A Guide to Campaigns with Chain of Command 2
description:
At the Sharp End is the addition for Chain of Command that allows the gamer to play and design Pint-Sized Campaigns.

This 56 page document is the revised and expanded edition designed to accompany Chain of Command 2.

It is divided into two parts, the first providing the rules for running Pint-Sized Campaigns with a streamlined and more user-friendly system. The second half focussed on a seven-point plan to build your own campaign with advice and tips from the game’s designer.   
This expansion also includes rules for gaming with reduced-strength platoons.

—description from the publisher
family: Series: Pint Sized Campaigns for Chain of Command

description:
A game for Collectors, created by Collectors.

In The Way We Collect, you are a minimalist but ambitious collector competing to build the most impressive collection of toys. Over a series of rounds, players secretly bid coins in simultaneous sealed auctions to win lots of toys, then carefully arrange their acquisitions on a limited 3×3 shelf — where every card placed must share at least one characteristic with its neighbours.

Toys are defined by four attributes: Color, Typology, Material, and Condition. When you move a group into a new Collection, matching characteristics with existing ones triggers powerful combos, earning you bonus cards. At the end of each round, you can also sell cards from your collections to refuel your budget for the next auction.

The game ends when the draw deck runs out. Players score points based on their ranking in each Collection category — but beware: toys left in the Attic cost you 2 points each.
Oh, and the box itself? It's part of the collection — every copy is unique.

—description from the publisher

Garw is a drawless connection game for two players: Black and White. It is played on the intersections (points) of an initially empty square grid (board). The top and bottom edges of the board are colored black; the left and right edges are colored white.

A hill is three like-colored stones in a 2×2 area. A mountain is three like-colored stones orthogonally adjacent to the same point.

Black plays first, then turns alternate. On your turn, perform exactly one of these actions:


     Place a stone of your color on an empty point. After your placement, for each pair of diagonally adjacent stones of your color there must be a hill or mountain that includes both stones.
     Flip an enemy stone to your color, provided it is adjacent to all three pieces in a mountain of your color.


Passing is not allowed, but, if you have no legal moves available, your turn is skipped.

You win if there is a chain of orthogonally connected stones of your color touching the two opposite board edges of your color.

To make the game fair, White will have the option, on their first turn only, to swap sides with Black instead of making a regular move.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Wörter sind nur halb so schlimm!
description:
Wörter sind nur halb so schlimm ("Words aren't that bad") is an educational game. The game aims children with writing difficulties. 
The box contains material for various games, e.g. "Animal-Letter-Match". On their turn, the player takes an animal card from the draw pile and places it in the middle. The player who matches the animal card with the correct starting letter first wins the pair. 
The player with the most pairs wins.

Kennst du die Tiere? contains 36 cards showing animals. The cards must be sorted by weight of the animal, from lightest to heaviest. Each player has a pile with animal cards. At their turn, the player turns up the top card of their pile and places it in the row. Thereby, the player tries to find the correct position of the animal, guessing their weight. 
Then, the player to the left decides whether or not they agree with the position of the new card. If they agree, they continue their turn by placing their card from the pile.
If they disagree, they say "Stop!" and explain, where they would place the card instead. 
If the player disagrees correctly, the previous player has to take back the card and put it in their drawing pile.

The game ends, as soon as one player has successfully placed all their cards from the pile. This player wins the game.

Kennst du die Uhr? contains 36 cards showing times. The cards must be sorted in ascending orders starting by 1am. Pictures featuring analogue clocks can be used twice. Each player has a pile with clock cards. At their turn, the player turns up the top card of their pile and places it in the row. Thereby, the player tries to find the correct position of the clock.
Then, the player to the left decides whether they agree with the position of the new card. If they agree, they continue their turn by placing their card from the pile.
If they disagree, they say "Stop!" and explain, where they would place the card instead. 
If the player disagrees correctly, the previous player has to take back the card and put it in their drawing pile.

The game ends, as soon as one player has successfully placed all their cards from the pile. This player wins the game.

Spaß am ABC contains cards showing letters. The cards must be sorted in ascending orders starting by A. 
Each player has a pile with clock cards. At their turn, the player turns up the top card of their pile and places it in the row. Thereby, the player tries to find the correct position of the letter.
Then, the player to the left decides whether they agree with the position of the new card. If they agree, they continue their turn by placing their card from the pile.
If they disagree, they say "Stop!" and explain, where they would place the card instead. 
If the player disagrees correctly, the previous player has to take back the card and put it in their drawing pile.

The game ends, as soon as one player has successfully placed all their cards from the pile. This player wins the game.

Gewinn mit 4 is a card game for two players. Each player receives the cards in their respective colour (blue or red). Player A places a card of their choice, then player B plays a card of theirs and puts it to the left or right or above or below a laid-out card and so on (maximum 8x8)
The player who achieves a row of 4 cards in their colour vertically or horizontally first wins.

The hostile oil rig holds all the answers to your best friend’s fate.
You start a secret investigation to bring the truth to light.
With everything on the line, will you sink or swim?
- description from the publisher

It is a mystery solving game that combines three main elements:

     A modern mystery story.
     Finding details from a "Hidden image" picture.
     More or less classic puzzle solving. The puzzles range from single to four card ones, and from light to quite heavy.


Players can use hints to help with the solving, but it costs them Reputation cards. Giving a wrong answer also makes the players lose a Reputation card.
The more Reputation Cards the players have left at the end, the easier it is for them to follow the "Hunch" found on the remaining cards, and ultimately choosing the correct option in the "Final Choice"
The high age recommendation comes from a few graphic details in the story. These can be skipped by parents playing with their children (12+), as long as they are aware such exist in the game.

Sind wir schon da? is a compilation of 50 cards, each card contains a fun game that can be easily played in the car, e.g. "I spy with my ... little hand". 
A player chooses a object that can be seen regularly during a drive (a stop sign, letter boxes etc.). Whenever they see the object, they clap their hands. The other passengers try to guess the object. The player who guesses the most objects corrctly wins.
family: Misc: Games Played While Doing Something Else

description:
Discover Catmandoo, a simple and original observation game!
Play the same cards, without consulting each other, to win the game.

A fantastic game of intuition to get to know your loved ones better!

This is a family game of observation, listening, and intuition, with very simple rules, that immerses you in a beautifully illustrated, poetic, and dreamlike world: the world of Catmandoo, where "dissonance" has unfortunately crept in.

To restore harmony, you must listen carefully to the Chage player, who describes their Vision of the Mahatmatou card in one word. The Chadhu players must then choose, from their own cat cards, the one that best matches the Chage's description.

If all the players manage to unite their cat-kras by playing the same cat cards, then harmony will return to Catmandoo, and the game will be won!

Basho Sumo Fury is a card and dice sports-simulation game that allows you to replicate Sumo Wresting bouts.  The game uses charts, cards, and 6 sided dice.  Each sumo wrestler has a card that has their ratings as well as their winning move chart.  The player pits two sumo wrestlers against each other, compares their ratings, and then rolls dice and consults the various charts and tables to find out the result.

The game includes the rules, charts, a primer on sumo wrestling, and a glossary of terms.  This game can be used to run your own sumo tournaments.  The designer has also created expansions of more sumo wrestler cards that are available on his website.

"Dachshunds excel at finding connections.
When colors align, a flow emerges; 
when numbers multiply, the scene comes alive.
This is a hand-management card game 
where the joy of gathering invites your next choice.

Avoid finishing last and collect cards (Dachshunds)
 to earn points. 
At the end of the round, the player with the lowest
 score finishes last and loses a life. 
The game continues until someone loses all their lives,
 at which point the game ends and all remaining
 players are the winners."

-from the publisher

Compete to finish your pancakes first by playing four types of ingredient cards across two ""Kitchens."" With a hand of three cards, you can play as many as you like—but be careful! Playing too many might let your opponent snatch your progress. Scores change every round, so mastering the three unique Event Cards is the key to finishing your pancakes when the points are highest. First to 5 points wins!

-from the publisher

boardgame: ドコドコニャンコ！（Where is my cat?）
description: “Where is my cat?” is a fresh take on memory games with a twist of deduction. Memorize the cat illustrations on both sides of the cards to find matching pairs and collect as many "♥" as possible.

Troca ou Não Troca is a board game adaptation of a segment from an 80s children's TV show called "Domingo no Parque," in which the host would make trade offers to a children who had absolutely no idea what was being traded.
In each round, a new player will act as the host, and one of the players will be the recipient of the offers. The host gives a gift card to the recipient and shows another one to the other players, asking the recipient if they want to trade what they have for the new gift card or keep the old one, without knowing what each one is. The card they choose not to keep goes to one of the other players.
In each major round, a new player will act as the auctioneer, and one of the players will be the recipient of the bids. Once everyone has received a card in this manner, a trading round follows, during which players can negotiate trades with one another.
The game ends when everyone has acted as the auctioneer once, and the player with the most valuable gifts wins the game.

Okimba is a drawless connection game for two players: Black and White. It is played on the intersections (points) of an initially empty square grid (board). The top and bottom edges of the board are colored black; the left and right edges are colored white.

A naked diagonal is a pair of like-colored, diagonally adjacent stones with no other like-colored stone adjacent to both.

Black plays first, then turns alternate. On your turn, place a stone of your color on an empty point. There must never be more than one naked diagonal on the board. You cannot pass your turn unless you have no legal moves.

You win if there is a chain of orthogonally connected stones of your color touching the two opposite board edges of your color.

To make the game fair, White will have the option, on their first turn only, to swap sides with Black instead of making a regular move.

—description from the designer
ROLL THE DIE, PLACE THE DIE, ONE MUST DIE!

Pukka Gen is an expansion for Chennault’s First Fight (ATO Issue 12) that was included as a subscriber bonus in ATO issue 18.  It includes 16 counters and two pages of optional reinforcements for both sides and a few optional variant rules.  The reinforcements include…

Two British Spitfire squadrons
An extra Indian Infantry Division 
A British Tank Battalion 
Two Japanese Infantry Divisions
family: Series: Evolution of Airpower in World War 2

description:
Chennault’s Fist is an expansion for Chennault’s First Fight (ATO Issue 12) that was released in 2016 to commemorate the 75th Anniversary of the Flying Tigers first seeing action. It includes 14 counters (and two blank counters) and two pages of optional reinforcements and rules among which are an Allied preemptive strike. The reinforcements include…

3 Squadrons of Hudson light bombers
3 Squadrons of Boston light bombers
3 Brigades of a British International Defense Force
2 Squadrons of Japanese Army Fighter Squadrons (1 each Ki-27 and Ki-43)
1 Squadron of Japanese Army Bombers (Ki-21)
family: Series: Evolution of Airpower in World War 2

description:
"You are renowned perfumers.
To create a fine perfume, harmony of scents is essential.
If a specific scent is too strong, or if too many types are mixed, it degrades into a foul odor.
Who will create the ultimate perfume and claim the fame?"

from the publisher

boardgame: 蒼碧の選定 (Sapphire and Emerald: The Selection)
description:
"GMless murder mystery for exactly 6 players. Set in a sealed mansion in rural 1994 Japan, players investigate a killing that occurs during an ancient family ritual. Each player receives a personal handout with unique knowledge, goals, and secrets. Gameplay proceeds through timed investigation and discussion phases, supported by a smartphone-based clue discovery system. The game culminates in a simultaneous-action phase where every player's choice shapes the final outcome. One scenario, one experience — once played, it cannot be replayed."

-from the publisher
Flip over cards to train and earn victory points!

boardgame: ハッピーバースデータートル!! (Happy Birthday Turtle!!)
description:
"​In ""Happy Birthday Turtle!!"", timing is everything.
1.Pick your room & power card secretly.
2.Reveal! Lowest numbers resolve first.
Don't overshoot the goal or you'll fail!
​Can you be the most enthusiastic party planner?"

-from the publisher

boardgame: 鉱物コレクターズ (Mineral Collectors)
description:
"Collect minerals and titles to become the “Collector King”!
The first player to reach 15 Collector Points (CP), or the game ends after 5 rounds.
There are three ways to play.
• Level ★ is a memory matching game suitable for young children.
• Level ★★ is a game that combines memory matching with Collector Title tiles.
Difficulty ★★★ is a set-collection game that emphasizes strategic maneuvering.
It’s a set-collection game based on memory.
We recommend the third way to play.
That’s the one we’ll introduce here.
Starting with 8 cards in your hand,
you perform 4 actions on your turn:
1. Draw
2. Search
4. Trade at the Shop
5. Earn a Title
Repeat this process
until 5 rounds have passed, or someone reaches 15 CP or more."

-from the publisher
This game is real time Dice matching party game. Players can roll dice as many as they want until other team declare their completion.

boardgame: 妖精の森のお菓子屋さん (The Pâtisserie of Fairywood)
description:
"Step into the magical Fairywood as a pâtisserie owner, collaborating with whimsical fairies to collect ingredients and complete legendary recipe books. This strategic game features a unique blend of dice worker placement and a "must-follow" mechanic, where the starting player's choice of action row constrains the options of those who follow. Manage your fairy workers efficiently to earn the most Victory Points and be recognized as the forest's finest pastry chef."

-from the publisher
publishers: Japanese Twixt Association (日本ツイクスト協会)

description: Slide Puzzle, Nagoya Attack, Tensei for miracle.

"Black Belt is a serious two-player karate dueling card game created by a game designer with a 3rd-degree black belt in karate.
Its defining feature is the intense back-and-forth mind games and tense matches that capture the feel of a real karate sparring bout.

Players face off as Red and White, using decks made up of 18 technique cards.

Victory Condition:
The first player to claim two Ippon tokens wins.

Scoring System:
Depending on the technique used, attacks are judged as either Waza-ari or Ippon.
Two Waza-ari count as one Ippon, so a player can also win by scoring a total of four Waza-ari, or through combinations such as two Waza-ari plus one Ippon.

Actions:
Players roll up to three dice to determine whether their techniques succeed.

Comeback Mechanic:
There is also a dramatic one-shot comeback rule that allows a player to knock out their opponent with a single blow, though it is extremely difficult to pull off."

-from the publisher

"This is the simulator of the Boardgame Cafe's staff.
The turn player is a staff in the Boardgame Cafe. The others are customer.
Now the customers come and request for you.
Three request cards are revealed. Then, the number of the customers, the playing time for one game, and the special request.
Then, the turn player can talk with others regarding to the customer. The customers can make any additional details.

At last, the turn player offers the recommend to the customers.
The customers feel good, they give the points the turn player.

In this game, It's just simulation for the Boardgame Cafe's staff. The winner is nothing. Just enjoy the communications."

-from the publisher

boardgame: ミミックが あらわれた！ (Mimic Attacks!)
description: A game of psychological warfare and physical action where two players clash as the Treasure Hunter and the Mimic.

"In this game, a player who knows the secret code gives hints to help others guess it. You score more points as more players find the right answer—but be careful! If everyone guesses correctly, you "burst" and get no points."

-from the publisher

boardgame: ドッターコネクター (Dotter Connector)
description: Dotter Connector is a cooperative drawing game where you express a secret theme drawing only dots, and your team tries to guess it by connecting the dots. 2 players try to show the selected theme only with drawing dots, and other players will guess what the drawing show by connecting them with some lines.
publishers: みすたーあんどみせすげーむず (MR. & MRS. GAMES)

description:
"A new sensation in sci-fi fleet combat, combining balance and area majority through the power of magnetic levitation.
Fear not the myths. Seize control before the fleet collapses!"

-from the publisher

Kindling is a fast, clever, set collecting card game where strategy, surprise, and a little sabotage are needed to survive. Simply, gather six Sticks and a Spark to win — but with every player out for themselves, the bonfire isn’t the only thing that is guaranteed to get heated.

—description from the publisher

"○ Objectives of the game
 Player = Apprentice Santa collect the four types of gift chips and try to get the highest score. However, it is not enough to just collect them. The player who has the most extra presents will lose points. Collect gifts in a well-balanced manner and use your cards to win.

○Game Preparation
 1. 40 Christmas gift chips (48 for 5-6 players) are arranged by type according to the guide.

2. Shuffle all cards face down, and place them as a deck near the Christmas gift chips. Deal one card to each Apprentice Santa to be his hand.

○How to play
・The player who looks the most like Santa is the starting player and takes to turn clockwise.

・On your turn, do one of the following A or B.
 A. Play a card.
 Select a card from your hand and put it face up. Perform the effect and place the used card on the discard pile.

B. Draw a card
 Draw one card from the deck and add it to your hand.

○Game end
The game will end immediately if any of the following occur
 - The cards in the deck become 0 (the game ends when the last card is drawn).
 - Any of the present chips in the chip place becomes 0.

○Scoring calculation
At the end of the game, the Apprentice Santa separates each of his gift chips by type, looks at the number of gift chips in the chip table, and calculates the score according to the following rules.

Most common gift tip　-1 point
Second most common gift tip　+1 point
Third most common gift tip　+2 point
Least present tip　0 point
Empty box chip　0 point

○In case of a tie
 If there are the same number of present chips in the yard, the order will be moved up.
 Example: In the case of 3 red chips, 3 blue chips, 2 yellow chips, and 1 green chip
→ Red and Blue tie for 1st place, -1 point each. Yellow for 2nd place, +1 point. Green for 3rd place, +2 points.

The player with the highest score is the winner!
 *In case of a tie, the player with the lowest number of empty chips wins."

-from the publisher

boardgame: ザ・ワークアウトマッチョ (The Workout Macho)
description:
"Try to shout the most described training equipment as soon as possible.
But be careful, we must perform macho pose in case there is a silver macho."

-from the publisher

boardgame: The Heir of Charlemagne, 1806 in Germany
description:
The Heir of Charlemagne, 1806 in Germany, Series 3 rules
One campaign
Eight scenarios
Dozens of maps

Nearly 1,700 uniform images!

Fourteen historical scenarios may be played individually or refight the historical campaign of 1806: The Collapse of Prussia.

Schleiz
Saalfeld
Jena
Auerstädt
Erfurt
Halle
Prenzlau
Lübeck
The Heir of Charlemagne, 1806 in Germany, requires ESR Napoleonics or a similar Napoleonic wargame to play.

Players create and debate tierlists of characters in categories silly and serious.

Players each get a hand of 5 separate ranking cards: Star, A, B,C,and F tier. Then cards are revealed from a central character/category deck, each card in that deck has a character on the top and a category on the bottom. Players as a group reveal 5 cards from the character/category deck, then a 6th card is drawn and rotated. The first 5 cards are the characters getting ranked, and the 6th card is the category.

Players then place their ranking cards face down under the characters ranking them creating their secret tierlist Star being the best at something, F being the worst. Then as a group everybody debates and creates a group tierlist. Once the group tierlist is decided everybody reveals their secret tierlist. The closer a players secret tierlist is to what the group decided the more points that player gets. After points are awarded players take back their ranking cards they then reveal new characters and a new category to begin again.

The first player to 18 points wins.

Place the queen bee piece in the center of the board and place random pieces on the pink hexagonal spaces to start the game.

The player who connects the illustration they chose during setup for the longest chain wins.

Draw a piece from the bag and move the corresponding piece on the board to place the piece from the bag in its place. You may not look inside the bag when drawing a piece.

—description from the publisher
publishers: ふつかめのだいまじん

description:
"A fantasy strategy board game featuring poker-style hands, suits with a rock-paper-scissors relationship, tactics and magic, where players battle over hit points."

-from the publisher
publishers: ウェブ企画習己堂　(Shukodo Works)

description:
"• What is the setting?
In an ancient forest, small spirits called “Jellu” begin to gather.
You become a magical piper, gathering and combining Jellu that move with the rhythm to create the Jellu King who will rule the forest.

• What is the goal?
Succeed in rhythm, collect tokens,
and complete the puzzle board to achieve a higher score.

• How do you play?
Place cards precisely in sync with the app’s rhythm,
and strategically place earned tokens on the puzzle board."

-from the publisher

A crime scene. A murder has been committed; and it’s up to you to catch the killer.

You’ll go through documented witness statements and examine the evidence left behind. Your job is to read everything carefully — every detail matters. Everything you need to solve the case is already there.

Connect the dots, solve the puzzle, and earn your reputation as the detective who cracked the case.

There are currently two episodes available, with a third in development—and many more to come.

—description from the publisher

4Score Baseball is a quick-play baseball game that takes into account all facets of team play. Four different statistical categories are available for both hitters and pitchers. A flexible scoring system and team sheets makes this the perfect companion game for quick-play baseball.

Roll the dice and cross reference the game cards for the teams and seasons you are playing to see the outcome.

Scoring continues through the end of the game to determine a winner.

At the beginning of each chapter, you receive cards in hand that your partners cannot see. You must therefore describe the content of your cards together to determine where to play them on a timeline.


     Discuss the images you have in your hand.
     Place your cards in the correct order.
     Draw a card and discover what happens next in the story.


Finish the story together on the right order to win the game !

boardgame: Zero Hour: Instructions for the Wargaming of Armies on the Offensive
description:
Command battalions across the battlefields of the early 20th Century!

Step onto the battlefields of the early twentieth century, where the age of rifles met the dawn of mechanised warfare, and every order could mean victory or disaster. 
Zero Hour recreates the brutal ebb and flow of the Great War and other conflicts of that era, where commanders wrestle not only with their enemies but with the chaos of modern warfare itself. 
Designed for battalion-level engagements of around 100 models, typically fought across a 6’x4’ table, Zero Hour captures the scale, tension and attrition of industrial-age combat. 
Each game unfolds as command structures crumble, assaults falter, and the initiative shifts from side to side in a desperate struggle for control.

Inside the rules you’ll find:

     A dynamic Orders system that puts command and control at the heart of every decision.
     High Command assets including devastating barrages, choking gas attacks, and the primitive aircraft of the era.
     Points values for creating up a battalion size force, suitable for any army from this period, including support units of artillery, heavy machine guns, armoured cars and tanks.
     Support for larger brigade-level engagements, allowing multiple battalions and multiple players to join the fray.
     Options for both open battle and attack-and-defend games, from balanced encounters to desperate stands against overwhelming odds.


Zero Hour brings the dilemmas of early mechanised warfare to life. It is fast, brutal, and unrelenting. So ready your troops, Zero Hour is upon you! Let battle commence!

Scale: Developed with 28mm in mind, but can easily be played at smaller scales

—description from the publisher

Collect spotty bunnies and discover the eggs in this counting game.

Spin the carrot spinner and choose a bunny card showing the same number of spots as the number on your spinner. Turn over the card which will show a number of eggs on it - the more eggs the better! The winner is the player with the most eggs at the end of the game.

Frozen Realms is a 4X deckbuilding tabletop game set in a fantasy world. Players choose a race and faction, build their decks, recruit units, and expand across a modular campaign map. The game combines strategic map control with turn-based battles resolved on a separate battle map.

Each turn, players receive resources from their provinces and structures, recruit units, research technology cards, move their armies, attack and conquer provinces, and engage in battles. They can also construct buildings within their territories and acquire new cards from the market, shaping their strategic and tactical options.

During the game, players manage their provinces, develop infrastructure, and recruit armies while navigating logistics and positioning on the campaign map. Deckbuilding determines available actions, including spells, tactics, and upgrades, while technologies unlock additional strategic options as the game progresses.

Combat takes place on a dedicated battle map, where players command their units in turn-based encounters. Card effects, unit abilities, and positioning all influence the outcome of battles. The game integrates multiple layers of decision-making, including resource management, army composition, and tactical execution.

The objective of the game is to eliminate all other players by removing their control over provinces. A player is eliminated when they no longer control any provinces. In cooperative or solo modes, players face an automated Chaos opponent, and victory is achieved by capturing the Heart of the Chaos.

The Guess List is a clever twist on the classic guessing game, where wit and wordplay are put to the test. On your turn, you’ll give clues to help others guess a famous character — but there’s a catch: your clues can only start with one of the five letters rolled on the special letter dice!

Think describing Sherlock Holmes is easy? Maybe — but what if your only options for the clue must begin with B, M, T, P, or E? Suddenly, your choice becomes a puzzling conundrum.

Will you say “British… Mystery… Trenchcoat… Pipe… Elementary…”?

As play continues and the dice are rerolled, clues add up — and the sooner a player answers correctly, the more points they score.

The Guess List — it’s who you know!
