Risk It All is a fast-paced push-your-luck dice game where every roll tempts fate and every decision could cost you everything. Step into a twisted Wonderland and play as iconic characters like Alice, the Mad Hatter, the Cheshire Cat, or the Crimson Queen; each with unique abilities that bend the rules of the game.

Roll, set aside scoring dice, and decide: bank your points… or risk it all for even greater rewards. Push too far, and you’ll bust—losing everything you earned that turn. Use Coins, special abilities, and powerful Impossible Things cards from the ever-changing Madness Marketplace to manipulate dice, disrupt opponents, and swing the game in your favor.

With evolving strategies, asymmetric characters, and a blend of chaos and control, Risk It All delivers high-energy gameplay where fortune favors the bold—but only the clever survive.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Fields of Fate: Fantasy Edition – Blackspire Pass
description:
A Solo Narrative Campaign for Fantasy Wargaming.

The Vale is about to fall.
A Greenskin horde surges through Blackspire Pass. The fortress of Ironhold lies in ruin. What remains of the realm must stand—or be swept away.
You must take command, and test your fate.

This is a supplement for the Fields of Fate system.

A 4-Game Narrative Campaign
Fight a connected series of battles where every result matters:

     Hunt the enemy scouts
     Ambush the advancing horde
     Defend the walls of Ironhold
     Deliver the final, decisive counterattack
     Win or lose, the campaign evolves with you.
     Built for Solo Play


Designed for use with the Fields of Fate system:

     Enemy actions driven by dice
     Unpredictable thematic behaviour
     No need to control both sides
     Plays like a real opponent.


Use What You Own
Any miniatures
Any terrain
The campaign adapts to your collection, not the other way around.

The Crimson Court is a strategic card game of shifting loyalty, hidden influence, and calculated betrayal set in a twisted Wonderland divided among rival kingdoms.

Players take on the roles of powerful figures aligned with the suits—Hearts, Spades, Diamonds, Clubs, and Moons—each vying for dominance in a fragile court where alliances are fleeting and every decision carries weight. Play your cards carefully to gain Hearts (influence), manipulate the balance of power, and outmaneuver your opponents through clever timing and deduction.

But beware—the Court is never stable. Players may fall, linger as spirits, or rise again, and control of the Crown can change everything. With layered player interaction, evolving board state, and constant tension between risk and reward, The Crimson Court delivers a richly thematic experience where only the most cunning will prevail.

—description from the designer

Escape from the Silken Web is a cooperative dungeon crawler game based on Eastern Mythology where communication is forbidden, and players either win or lose together.

Each turn, play as Tang Sanzang or one of his disciples, using unique skills and equipment to clear seven dungeon levels without a single word. And collect artifacts for reputation, or you may choose to sacrifice them to survive at the same time, but if a threat returns to you unsolved, the entire team is stuck in this place for good......

—description from the publisher

A simple 5-in a row game played on a hexagonal board.

On a player's turn, they can either play a piece in an empty space, or play on a space occupied by a weaker piece, returning the weaker piece to it's owner.

Each player has 10 small pieces, 5 medium pieces, and 4 big pieces. The bigger the piece, the stronger the piece.

Lavandula is a cozy yet competitive board game, where players harvest lavender and use resources to craft items and complete goals.

Each turn, you must move spaces on a rondel, then (in any order) may share with an adjacent player for mutual gain, move any adjacent bees to produce honey, and activate the space. The game ends immediately when one player crafts a certain number of cards. Players then score points from their crafted items, completed bouquet, secret goal card, and leftover resources. The player with the highest score wins.

—description from the publisher

Pick n’ Roll Basketball is a tactical board game that recreates the intensity of basketball on the tabletop. Control your team, execute plays, manage your players, and roll the dice to dominate the court.

Each game becomes a strategic experience where positioning, decision-making, and timing are everything. Will you push the fast break, or build the perfect play to break through the opposing defense?

The game uses a hexagonal board representing the court, allowing precise movement and realistic basketball situations. Player miniatures move, set screens, drive to the basket, and take shots, recreating classic plays.

During the game you will be able to:

Move players around the court
Set screens (Pick & Roll)
Drive to the basket
Attempt 2-point or 3-point shots
Defend and steal the ball
Manage your bench
Use special cards to change the course of the game

Each turn represents a possession where strategy and dice determine the outcome of the play.

Strategy, Dice, and Decisions

Each player has unique skills and statistics represented on team cards. Custom dice determine the outcome of key actions such as: shooting, defense, rebounding, steals, assists…
But luck is not everything. Player positioning and tactical management will make the difference between victory and defeat.

—description from the designer

Traitors Aboard: One Piece is a social deduction game with hidden identities for 3 to 8 players, ages 10 and up—perfect for a fun evening with family or friends.

Play as either a Crew Member or a Glutton. The Crew Members will try to fill the ship’s basket, while the Gluttons will do everything they can to secretly devour the supplies and deceive the rest of the crew. But beware! Time is running out, and innocent players may be eliminated…

Players must rely on strategy, bluffing, and negotiation to achieve their goals. Heated discussions, sharp questioning, and the sharing of information, whether true or false, are all ways to coordinate with allies or mislead them at the perfect moment. Participants will need to show insight to identify their teammates, outwit their opponents, and use the items on board the ship tactically to secure victory.

—description from the publisher

“This is like Fishing, but completely different.” - Ordinary seaman Ronald after the first game

As in Fishing, your caught cards score points, then you use them to try to catch even more — but blowfish, two trump colors, and new buoys create a fresh game experience.

In Fishing 2, you try to catch as many tricks as possible in 8 rounds because each card you catch is worth 1 point. You use your caught cards as bait for the next round — but if you did not catch enough tricks to do so, you first fill your hand with fresh cards from the ocean stack.

You win Fishing 2 by scoring the most points. How many fresh cards will you need to succeed?

Fishing 2 is a standalone game, not an expansion for Fishing!

—description from the publisher

Hi there, and welcome to an exciting new chapter of Galactic Cruise. While the scientists, engineers, and architects are hard at work on Earth, you and your fellow cruise directors will be stationed here on the new, state-of-the-art Galactic Cruise space station. This will be your base of operations, where you’ll be carefully crafting specialized trips, upgrading your fleet, and making sure that our esteemed guests have the time of their lives! I have it on good authority that the cruise director who impresses the board of directors the most will receive the coveted Employee of the Year bonus!

When you are the active player, you choose one of the seven actions around the outside of the space station. These actions include building developments, advertising to guests, discovering new excursions and adding them to your itineraries, upgrading your ships and player board, and sending guests on the vacations of their dreams! All players will take this action simultaneously, but as the active player you will also gain a bonus, the potency of which fluctuates as the space station rotates.

Once a player reaches the requisite number of victory points, the game comes to an end and final scoring commences. The player with the most points is crowned cruise director extraordinaire and will receive the Employee of the Year bonus (and the victory!).

Are you ready to leave some guests starstruck?

—description from the publisher

You are kobolds in service to a Dragon who has been asleep for many years. While the Dragon has been sleeping, you… borrowed its collection of valuables to decorate your own homes and show off to your friends. But now the Dragon is waking up! You must hurry to return all the stuff you borrowed or face the Dragon’s infamous temper.

The Unheist of Hearthburn House is a semi cooperative game for 3-6 players. Players will take turns with their teammates to move around the mansion, place items back in their spots, and sabotage other teams. The game ends when the dragon wakes up!

Players determine their score based on the number of their teams' items in the correct spot. Each player's total score is determined by adding the score for each of their teams together, and the player with the most points wins!

—description from the publisher

Ronin’s Revenge is a cooperative dark Oriental fantasy boss battler, designed to bring the brutal tension of a souls-like experience to the tabletop.

Combat is the heart of the experience: each confrontation pits the heroes against merciless bosses in battles where stamina management, positioning, timing, and reading the enemy matter far more than pure luck. Your choices shape the pressure of the fight, turning every action into a calculated risk — attacking at the wrong moment can open you up to a devastating counter, while precision, perfectly timed defense, and well-prepared counterattacks can completely turn the tide of the battle.

But Ronin’s Revenge is more than just memorable battles. In an epic, non-linear campaign full of consequences, the group must vote on which path to take as each hero pursues their own objectives, loyalties, and personal interests. This opens the door to political intrigue between the characters, since not everyone wants the same outcome and personal endings depend on the truths the group uncovers along the way (EVEN SOLO). Yet no matter how intense these internal tensions become, everyone must stay united in combat — because the threat rising before them is far worse than any conflict among allies, and letting this evil prevail would come at a terrible price for all.

With solo and cooperative modes for 1 to 4 players, campaign progression, character advancement, and deep customization through items and powers, Ronin’s Revenge combines reactive storytelling with demanding combat that rewards learning, discipline, and mastery of the battle’s rhythm. Every defeat teaches a lesson. Every choice leaves its mark. And every victory must be earned blow by blow.

—description from the publisher

Pour some tea, welcome dear friends, and settle into the sweetest kind of game night! So Happy You’re Here! is a whimsical celebration of friendship and the everyday magic we share. By relying on plenty of positive interaction and shared goals, this game brings to your table a wholesome experience of true coziness, full of small joys that linger long after you are done playing. Enriched with inviting hand-painted artwork and high quality components, every turn is a comforting dive in the 'cottagecore' atmosphere for consummate players and novices alike.

Every turn, play a card from your hand matching a card on the roof to gain an effect - grow flowers in the garden, move your lantern or the lizard token, give cups of tea and slices of cake to other players, collect adorable bird tokens. You can also gain points by playing positive affirmation cards to encourage other players and foster your friendships with shared objectives.
At the end of the game, the player with the most points - the one who brought a little extra magic to the table - is declared the winner!

—description from the publisher
Everything you need for a 5th player in a teal color.

Blood, sweat, and tears have led you to this moment.
The moment you've trained for, taking punches and pushing your limits your entire life. Your trainer's words, drilled relentlessly over time, are now etched in your mind—as he shouts them once more from your corner: "A world champion is the boxer who never gives up; who pushes past his limits, gets up when he can't take it anymore, and keeps fighting until he wins!

KO is a game that captures the raw intensity of a real boxing fight.
Your Fight Deck represents your stamina, so you'll need to manage it wisely. Chain together simple combos to corner your opponent or driven him against the ropes, and boost your hits with dice. 
If your punches land hard enough, you can knock him down
—but he might get back up by discarding cards or risking it all with the KO Die.
Aim for the face, break his defense.
The tiles forming his face will show the accumulated damage.
When the bell rings, you'll have a chance to recover energy or heal the injuries on your face by discarding cards.
Your goal is clear: Knock your rival down and score the KO

—description from the desogner
family: Organizations: Board Games Association of Creators in Spain (LUDO)

description:
Join Sonic and seven other iconic characters in a fast-paced race to complete your goals and escape the level the fastest! Beware the villainous Dr. Eggman and his Badnik Robots, who will stop at nothing to slow you down. Dodge traps and opponents, and use your puck to knock out the competition while gathering Rings for your ultimate escape. Be the first player to complete all of your Target cards, have exactly 2 rings of each color, and exit the level through the ramp to win!

—description from the designer
family: Video Game Theme: Sonic the Hedgehog

description:
The crowd roars as the horses speed down the homestretch neck to neck fighting for position along the rail in an exciting display of premiere high speed horse racing. This game captures the exciting outcomes from the main track sections along a linear track, front stretch, clubhouse turn, backstretch, far turn, homestretch, and the finish. The game does not use normal space movement as the outcomes will place them into the next section of track according to a horses result per a modified die roll from the horse/jockey’s card. The track also has modifiers along the top of the track which are add/subtracted from the die roll and those modifiers will be different throughout the length of the track depending on the space they are on. Although the board track is visually dirt they will also be racing on turf and synthetic under dry, wet, or mud conditions.
 A heat race consists of six races with nine horses of varying characteristics entered into the competition. Odds on each horse are pre-determined before each race, bets placed by the player/s. A race lasts six turns and a turn may incur one or more events along the way to the finish, and there is a good possibility of photo finishes which involves photo finish reviewing resulting with a horse winning by a nose, short head, or long head.
Get Ready for the Most Exciting Two Minutes in Racing as the Saying Goes!

—description from the designer

In Oddball, you and a friend will compete against each other in a totally-not-made-up sport. You’ll start by drafting a team of 6 unique and, honestly, kind of weird athletes, then battle it out head-to-head in a series of electrifying Faceoffs. Be smart and play your cards right: an Athlete’s Power will help them win the Faceoff, but their Speed could help them win the ball to go first in the next Faceoff! You've got to remember that it's not always the biggest or fastest that'll win you the game: Each Athlete has a unique ability that can turn the tide of the match, or produce unexpected results!
 Now imagine that there are literally millions of these oddballs, each with unique combinations of appearance, statistics, and Abilities. No two Oddball Athletes will ever be the same!

READY TO PLAY BALL?

-description from publisher

This Cracker Jack prize is probably the smallest published word board game ever.

The contents are a 0.9 inch square plastic board with holes in a 3x3 grid, and attached tiles with a peg on the bottom and a letter on top (one of TIMECARD). The tiles are snipped off before the first play.

To play the game, the tiles are mixed up face down. On a turn, each player picks a tile, looks at it, and places it somewhere in the grid.  The first player to spell a valid English 3-letter word on the grid in any direction wins.

There is also a solitaire game, which invites the player to spell as many 3-letter words as they can think of using the 8 letters provided.

Horrified: DUNGEONS & DRAGONS Ravenloft is a standalone game with four new mechanically-unique monsters to challenge: Carrionette, Gulthias Tree, Baba Lysaga and the Dark Lord of Barovia Strahd von Zarovich. In their quest to defend Barovia, each player gets to take on the role of a hero with one of 5 iconic D&D classes. 
 In Horrified, players work together, alternating between the hero phase, where one player gets to move around the board, guide villagers, pick up valuable items, and work to defeat the monsters; and the monster phase, where the monsters wreak havoc, attacking both heroes and villagers alike.  
 It’s up to the heroes to strategize, adapt, and solve each monster’s unique puzzle before they’re overwhelmed with terror!

—description from the designer

Battlesticks is a light strategy party card game where players collect, combine, and battle a variety of imaginative sticks, each with unique strengths. Inspired by childhood stick fights, the game blends simple mechanics with tactical decision-making, as players build their collections while disrupting their opponents.

On each turn, players draw, play, or use action cards to gain new sticks, enhance existing ones, or interfere with others. Sticks can be modified or combined to increase their power, creating opportunities for stronger plays and strategic timing. Certain cards can trigger battles, where players compete directly using their best sticks.

The objective of the game is to win stick battles and collect rewards. As the game progresses, players must decide when to commit their strongest combinations and when to hold back. The player with the most rewards at the end of the game wins.

—description from the publisher

Neocatics Bit Cards is a fun TCG based on cats and computers. This is a battler VS battler charged battle till both of yours are KOed. Tired of limited move options? Introducing Recodes. Access your battlers full power with recodes a special card type that gives your battlers Access to new moves to help the battle in your favor! We also have a fair share of computer jokes in the game.

As an independent starship captain operating in a frontier system, you trade goods between distant settlements, navigate dangerous space lanes, and outmaneuver rival traders competing for the same opportunities. The Trade Union patrols the system in the name of order, while pirates lurk in the void looking for easy prey. Success belongs to the captains who can read the situation, take risks at the right moment, and seize opportunities before their rivals do.

Each turn you will trade goods, travel between worlds, and adapt your plans as the board changes around you. Markets shift, events disrupt the space lanes, and pirates or patrols may challenge your ship along the way. Action cards can be played at almost any time, allowing players to protect themselves, interfere with opponents, or dramatically change the outcome of an encounter. Every decision has consequences, and the galaxy rewards players who think ahead while staying flexible.

Star Trader plays in about 20–30 minutes per player and supports 2–6 players, making it perfect for game nights where you want meaningful strategy without an all-night commitment. With a modular star map, secret goals, and a large deck of action cards, every game unfolds differently and creates new stories of bold trades, narrow escapes, and last-minute victories. If you enjoy interactive strategy games where timing, risk, and rivalry drive the action, it’s time to take command of your ship and make your fortune among the stars.

—description from the designer

OSHAKA Trick is a trick-taking game where you face the dilemma of whether to score your acquired cards or take a small penalty to prevent massive penalties later. Winning a trick is not always beneficial, and you will carry a heavy burden of judgment. Survive the hellish 13 tricks and aim for victory!

Humanity is venturing into the galaxy for the first time.

We travel along the starlines — natural corridors carved by the gravitational pull of stars and stellar masses. Ships ride these pathways at a fraction of the energy direct travel would demand.

By anchoring relay stations at stable Lagrange points, each faction builds their own expanding network — pushing deeper into unexplored space, making contact with alien civilizations, and learning from worlds beyond our own.

The future of humanity will be shaped by the connections we build.

How to Play:
StarLines is an engine builder that is played over 7 turns.  Each turn, you will play and purchase cards that represent relay stations.  Each relay station card has up to 4 lines on it pertaining to the four actions.  These lines have numbers on them which increase the value of those actions.

On your turn:


     Choose the order in which to play your cards. The order determined which line connections will activate.
     The top line on your cards pertains to RECRUITING Astro Meeple. You will get 1 Astro Meeple for each number that is connected to your player board on the top line.
     The second line on your cards pertains to EXPLORING planets. You will send 1 Astro Meeple to a planet for each number that is connected to your player board on the second line.
     The third line on your cards pertains to RECHARING your batteries. You will draw 1 extra card for each number that is connected to your player board on the third line.
     The last line on your cards pertains to BUILDING relay stations. You get 1 coin for each number that is connected to your player board on the last line. These coins are used to purchase more relay station cards.
     Discard the cards you played.
     Draw cards equal to your current Battery Level.


Scoring:
At the end of the 7th round, you will score points for each of your cards, and for planets that have end game scoring.

—description from the publisher

Cleariosity Numbers is like Cleariosity except that instead of spelling words with letters, players make equations from numbers. It has a deck of 60 transparent plastic cards. Each card has 3 numbers (0-9) on it, and each number is in one of the 9 positions of a 3x3 grid. (When multiple cards are stacked, the grid is apparent.) At the start of the game, the cards are dealt out evenly among the players; any leftover cards are left face-up in the middle of the table. Each player keeps their cards in a face-down draw pile.

To start, all players simultaneously place the top card from their draw pile face up in the middle of the table, all facing the same direction. Players then look to find cards that will make an equation if they are stacked together. When they see one they place their fingers on the cards, which are then stacked to see if the caller is correct. An acceptable equation:

     Appears with 3 numbers in a straight line, horizontally (left to right only), vertically (top to bottom only), or diagonally (left to right only).
     Is a valid arithmetic equation of the form X (+-*÷) Y = Z.
     Has none of its numbers covering or covered by another number. (Numbers not used in that equation can cover other numbers.)


If the equation is correct, the declarer collects the cards making the word and keeps them in a separate winnings pile; if not, the cards are separated and that player may not guess again until after another player guesses. Play then continues with the remaining cards. If no one can see another equation, all players add the top card of their draw piles. Play continues until all cards have been played and no one can find any other equations. The player who has collected the most cards wins the game.

Different levels of play are allowed using the color of the dot in the lower left corner of each card.  

     For using addition only, use cards with a green dot (24 cards). 
     For addition and subtraction, add cards with a yellow dot (12 more cards). 
     For addition and subtraction and multiplication, add cards with a purple dot (12 more cards). 
     For addition, subtraction, multiplication and division, add cards with an orange dot (12 more cards).

Talisman 5th Edition Expansion.

Talisman Sagas: Crown of Blood expansion infuses the Talisman core game with spine-tingling Gothic horror. Players can choose to play as vampire slayers Mina Harker or Professor Van Helsing from Bram Stoker's Dracula. Then embark on a dangerous journey with fresh enemies, events, and magical objects. Compete to reach Dracula's Castle and be the first to defeat the Count to win! Or play the core game without the Dracula's Castle overlay, and use Dracula as a playable character and the Fanged Toad as the antagonist. This extension also introduces Ongoing Event cards, a fresh game-changing mechanic.

Includes:
3 Character cards
1 Dracula reference card
30 Spell and Adventure cards
1 Dracula's Castle board overlay
4 highly detailed miniatures (Investigator Mina Harker, Professor Van Helsing, Count Dracula -- and 1 alternate antagonist, the Fanged Toad)
Rule book

Scrambit has 96 letter tiles, and a 6-sided die whose 1-side is replaced with a picture of the White Beetle dog mascot.  To set up the game, the letter tiles are randomized and laid out on the table face down.  (Fewer than 96 tiles can be used, but at least 60 tiles is recommended.)

In their turn a player first rolls the die, and then can reveal up to that many tiles. If the dog side of the die is thrown, the player chooses any number from 2 to 7. When they stop revealing tiles, if they can spell a valid word (of 2+ letters) using all their revealed tiles, they may remove them and keep them in a personal scoring pile; otherwise they turn all the revealed tiles face down and end their turn. If the player spells a word using the full number of tiles rolled on the die, they may take another turn. The game ends when all the tiles are claimed or when everyone agrees no more words can be made, and the player with the most tiles claimed wins.

The game obviously has a memory component, to spell words with previously revealed letters.  It also has a push-your-luck element: if you have revealed letters for a short word, you may reveal another tile to make a higher-scoring word, and possibly get another turn if it makes the maximum tiles you are allowed that turn, with the risk of getting a letter you cannot use to spell a word with and ending your turn with nothing.

The rules have several strategy tips, such as: 

     If you know you cannot spell a word with letters you have revealed so far, you may want to stop revealing, to avoid letting other players know more letter locations. 
     If you roll the dog side of the die, and you can remember the location of letters that spell a word, you can choose that many letters to spell that word and get another free turn.

This set has 3 instructional cards, and 33 letter cards, one for each letter and an extra one for AEPLRST. Each letter card has the upper case on the front, lower case on the back, and a Spongebob Squarepants character on both sides.  The instructions have a variety of activities for parents to set up for their child, each of which is a challenge which the child wins by completing successfully.

The activities include various ways to help with identifying letters, learning letter sounds, basic spelling, word families, and word reading.

Become the builders of the greatest Tower known!
Use elephants to transport resources, spend gold to upgrade your deck and acquire influence as you compete to gain the highest score.

Assemble a deck of cards, tactically position resource tiles on the Tower of Babel, and upgrade your warehouse to unlock a range of rewards.

—description from the publisher

The Legendary Box Includes:
The Base Game (In retail box inside the box)
The Fall of Man Expansion (In retail box inside of box)
Acrylic Resource Tiles Upgrade Pack (223 Tiles)
First Player Token
Innovations Mini Expansion
Ropes and Rollers Mini Expansion
Dual Layer Player Boards
Elephant and Wagon Minis
Fall of Man Expansion Boards Upgraded to Dual Layer and Lenticular
3D Towers
Extra Premium Stand Up Bags
all inside a Lenticular Cover big box.

In All True Believers, players are members of a mysterious organization called The Order, working together to complete missions and advance the group’s agenda — getting recognized as a tax-exempt religion. But hidden among them are Defectors who seek to undermine the cause.

Each round, the Eye of the cult selects a player to be their Hand. Then, a Mission card is revealed. The Hand places two Virtue cards into a tablet and passes it to the Eye. The Eye selects one card to keep and one to place on the Mission. If the Virtue matches one of the requirements listed on the Mission card, the Mission succeeds. If not, the Mission fails.

These Virtue cards can also be used to enlist Disciples. You do this by discarding the number of Virtues listed on the Disciple card you wish to take. Disciple cards give you special advantages in the game.

Before every Mission, there will be a Call for Disciples when these cards can be used. It is also a time when Shame cards can be played. If a player accumulates three Shame cards from others, they cannot be a Hand for a designated period in the game.

Three times a game, all players but the Defectors will close their eyes. The Defectors will then place a Crisis of Faith card in front of another player in order to sabotage and sew suspicion.

Should more Missions be successes than failures, the True Believers will prevail. If not, the game goes to the Defectors.

Strategy, cunning, trust, and deception all play important roles in All True Believers by Pillbox Games.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Spongebob Squarepants Making Cents Learning Cards
description:
This set has 1 instructional card, and 35 money cards, each of which has 1 or more American coins and/or bills on the front, their total value on the back, and a Spongebob Squarepants character on both sides. The instructions have a variety of activities for parents to set up for their child, each of which is a challenge which the child wins by completing successfully.

The activities include various ways to help with identifying coins and their values, figuring coins to add up to values, deciding which groupings are more valuable, and activities using actual coins.

Funspell has a 10x10 grid board and 100 tiles, including 97 letters with their values, 2 blanks, 1 star tile.

To start, the tiles are placed face up randomly into the spaces of the grid. The game has two phases: drafting (though the term didn't exist then) and scoring.  During the drafting phase, a player in their turn moves the star tile into an occupied space that is adjacent orthogonally or diagonally -- a chess king's move away -- and takes the tile moved onto.  This is continued until either all the non-star tiles are claimed, or the star tile is isolated, not adjacent to any occupied space.

Then all the players arrange their captured letters into words of 4+ letters.  Each word's starting value is the sum of the values of its letters, which range from 1 for common letters, 2-5 for most of the others, and 7-10 for the rare letters; the blank tiles may count as any letter, but have a value of 0.  If a word has 6-7 letters, its value is doubled; if 8+ letters, it is tripled.  The value of unused letters is subtracted from the total. The player with the highest total score wins.

Flagomino is a simple domino game where players use country flags as game pieces. Play cards into the play area combining the colors on cards. Learn about country flags and their history while you play!
Get points based on how many cards you have managed to place together without breaking the color rules.

•••

Флагомино – простая игра-домино, которая поможет вам развить свою эрудицию и познакомиться с государственными символами и даже отчасти историей европейских государств. Вам предстоит выкладывать на поле карты в виде различных флагов, соединяя их друг с другом по цветовому принципу. Уже после первой сыгранной партии вы будете знать об истории флагов чуть больше, чем прежде, а через несколько игр, при должном желании, станете настоящим экспертом! "Флагомино" – прекрасный способ увлекательным образом освоить непростую для запоминания информацию вне зависимости от вашего возраста!

boardgame: A Driving Charge: Breaking the Jitra Line, 1941
description:
During the invasion of Thailand and Malaya, a fast moving Japanese column is heading for the British defensive line at Jitra.  
Due to monsoon damage, the British are desperate to delay the Japanese in order to improve their defences. 
This pint-sized campaign looks at both the British attempts to slow the Japanese advance and the ultimate attack on the main defences at Jitra.

This is the first Pint-Sized Campaign designed to be used with the Far East Handbook, and is an excellent introduction to the theatre, its terrain and the troop types involved.    
Six scenarios are included with the campaign typically running between four and seven games before a conclusion.
family: Series: Pint Sized Campaigns for Chain of Command

description:
TFTC – an adventurous card game for geocachers!

Are you ready to dive into the exciting world of geocaching? TFTC is a fast-paced and tactical card game where players create geocaches, competing with each other to collect the most points. However, geocachers’ preferences can change quickly, so you have to be attentive to catch the right moment exactly – but if you don’t succeed, you can always slow down your fellow competitors a little.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Legacy of Man: The Ancients Expansion Evolution
description:
Finally a Legacy style game for up to 8 players!

Legacy of Man: The Ancients Expansion Evolution combined with Legacy of Man: Evolution allows players to create a massive map over a 12 game period with a constant influx of new items and a different objective every game.

Create your map and get closer to victory with each tile placed, battles won, and objectives completed. Collect resources, hire units, build your empire, and with the expansion; work with your ally (if you choose), to become the strongest nation throughout time.

This expansion is solely intended to increase player count, it does not add or subtract from the original game or campaign. The same items from the base game are included minus additional dice and shared area of the map.

—description from the publisher

Nakasendai no Ran is a two-player game themed around the series of battles connected to Hōjō Tokiyuki’s uprising in July 1335, in the second year of the Kenmu era. Players take the role of either Tokiyuki’s forces, who led the rebellion, or Ashikaga Takauji’s forces, who seek to suppress it. The game features many famous commanders from both sides who took part in the conflict. The cards used in the game offer a variety of strategic options, allowing these warriors to demonstrate their abilities to the fullest.

This game is included as an insert in the board game magazine Reki-Bodo (歴ボド).

1 counter sheet (60 counters)
1 map sheet (A3 size)
1 card sheet (16 cards)
1 rulebook booklet (including 8 pages of game rules)

In this chaotic fantasy themed battle card brawl, players use 42 hero cards and 46 weapon and spell cards to build their defence and attack against multiple opponents.

Take it in turns to use hero cards to act as shields, launch weapons, or use special abilities. Stack Attack Cards into combos to deal increasing damage. Build your attacks, but don't neglect your defence!

Attack your opponent's defence pile until they have no cards left, then attack then while they're vulnerable to send them out of the game. Watch out for special abilities that might ruin your strategy and turn the tables!

The last player standing is the victor of Murder Hobo Tavern Brawl.

—description from the designer

boardgame: All Hands on Deck!: A Bridge Command Crew Drafting Game
description:
All Hands on Deck! is a card drafting game set in the universe of Bridge Command - an immersive theatre, collaborative science fiction bridge command game, based in Vauxhall London.

Players draft crew onto their ships using a card drafting mechanic (like Sushi Go or 7 Wonders), to fulfill the requirements of missions. Players complete missions to earn victory points.

Each crew card is based on a unique character from Bridge Command, with game abilities that reflect their personalities or roles. The game is available to purchase exclusively via the Bridge Command bar.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Dog Star Shippers: Orders from Old Earth
description:
When the 10-year-old signal beamed the schematics from Sol, the four factions set their differences aside to follow its instructions and build the Ancible — an FTL comm array direct to the homeworld, Earth!

Independent shippers like you and your partner are teaming up to finish assembling the megastructure. But the factions are in conflict again, and someone is hiring mercenaries to stop construction.

Can you two survive the System and finish the Ancible before the fighting tears things apart?

With this Co-op Expansion, you and your partner take on missions and discover bases together. As you do, you'll complete the construction of the Ancible Parts, face mercenary threats, and share a Reputation as a pair of scrappy ship owners.

The expansion includes new Rescue Missions, new For Hire ships with the option to hire as wingmates, and new more powerful ships. You'll also have to deal with Incidents affecting construction. But you're under a tight schedule to complete construction, and you can't let these new dangers slow you down!

The Dog Star Shipper's Order's from Old Earth Co-op Expansion adds an exciting episode to the Dog Star Shippers experience.

—description from the publisher

Become a haenyeo, the free-diving women of Jeju Island, and explore the depths of the sea to gather valuable marine life while helping protect the ocean.

In Haenyeo: Sea Divers of Jeju, players secretly choose Dive Cards to determine how deep their divers will descend each round. Careful planning is essential, as actions can only be performed when divers remain connected to the surface through a chain of placements. Dive deeper to collect rarer resources and pearls, but risk losing your connection if gaps appear.

Over 8 rounds, players compete to collect marine life, manage their resources, and clean up ocean pollution. Eggs can be spent to gain additional divers or exchange for valuable items, while collected trash contributes to end-game scoring in unique ways.

With its simultaneous card selection, spatial positioning, and resource management, the game offers a mix of strategic planning and tactical decision-making. The player who best balances diving efficiency, resource collection, and environmental care will earn the highest score and win.

—description from the publisher

Piggy Yellow is a cooperative party card game about reading each other’s minds through color.

Each round, one player becomes the leader and gives a clue word related to a shared theme—without directly mentioning any colors. The leader secretly chooses the color that best matches their clue, while all other players try to guess it by selecting a color card from their hands.

After six rounds, all cards are revealed. If every player has successfully matched the leader’s color in each round, the team wins together. If even one round fails, it’s back to the table for another attempt—along with plenty of laughter and debate about who saw what color.

With simple rules but surprising depth, Piggy Yellow challenges players to think alike, communicate indirectly, and discover just how differently (or similarly) they perceive the world. The special Piggy Yellow card adds a playful twist, acting as a wildcard when you’re unsure.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Intelliwar: Accession To The Throne – Flag & Arrow
description:
Flag & Arrow is a 6-unit expansion for the Intelliwar: Accession To The Throne.

Flag Bearers let adjacent units reroll a die. Archers, on the contrary, reroll a die if there are no adjacent units to them. Both unit classes have unique movement patterns.

Also, the expansion includes new Exploration Tokens (with new actions) and 4 new Exploration Token Placement Cards.

—description from the publisher

Can you count to 10? Then you can play TOTAL 10!

TOTAL 10 is a fast-paced, addictive card game for 2 to 8 players in which everyone manages a hand of exactly 10 cards. On your turn, draw a card from the central board and discard one from your hand — but watch out: other players can intercept your discard at any moment!

Your goal: be the first to announce "TOTAL 10!" by combining all 10 cards in your hand into sets of different colors, each adding up to exactly 10. No repeated colors allowed.

The twist? You must balance quick mental math with real-time reflexes — the interception rule means no one ever fully relaxes, even when it's not their turn.

The game is played over 5 rounds. The player who calls TOTAL 10 scores 10 points; all others score negative points equal to their uncombined cards. After 5 rounds, the highest score wins.

Simple rules, immediate pick-up, endlessly replayable. Created by the family behind Crack List, TOTAL 10 brings together all generations with pure numbers — no language barrier, no reading required.

—description from the publisher

The hottest game of the year has hit the arcade! Button Masher! You’ve got a fistful of quarters, the tournament is about to begin! Fight!

Kicking it old school! Arcade fighters have never been this dicey! Throw your dice, match for combos and more dice, then land that KO before your opponents do!
Each turn players are simultaneously rolling dice to set aside as fast as they can to land the correct combo. But be sure to set the dice in the right order of it doesn’t count. 
Set aside 3 of a kind to add another die to your pool then keep rolling. Crazy characters fill the page and taunt you with each missed roll. If you get the KO first you max out the points, but if you’re the last of the group you’ll get nothing! 
Mind the depleting health bar as well, if you fail too many times your game will be over. No Continue? screens here. It’ll be GAME OVER!
Race to the top of the sheet and you could be the winner! Scores are tallied at the end, even a player who was KO’d themselves might still come out on top!

—description from the designer

In Three Cards, strategic actions play out in order as coins jingle, cards grow stronger, and Victory Points pile up higher than your waving flag. Score the biggest and sail off into legend in this forgotten pirate game.

The game is played in 12 rounds with 3–5 players or in 9 rounds with 2 players. Each round, players select one card from their hand and reveal them at the same time.

The actions are then resolved in priority order to purchase new, more powerful cards, gain coins, or score Victory Points (VP). The player with the most VP at the end of the game wins!

—description from the publisher

Beam Bam Gym is a strategic card game about women’s artistic gymnastics.
Build routines across apparatus and maximize your score with competition-inspired mechanics.
A fun and educational bilingual (FR/EN) game for ages 8+.

—description from the publisher

Beam Bam Gym est un jeu de cartes stratégique sur la gymnastique artistique féminine.
Composez des enchainements sur les agrès et optimisez votre score avec des mécaniques inspirées des compétitions.
Un jeu accessible dès 8 ans, éducatif et bilingue français/anglais.

—description from the publisher (French)

Feel the vibe of Joy City as the ultimate invitation to THE CLUB is yours. Now, complete your TASKS and beat the others to get there first.

As you go, you must balance your JOY to stay on the fast track, while avoiding DOUBLE TROUBLE, which slows you down. There will be twists and turns along the way, but you can use CHANCE cards, outsmart all, and claim the dance floor for the win!

Join the thrill and have fun!




  ✼ GAMEPLAY ✼

Experience a modular tactical race that reinvents the classic 'pick-up and deliver' genre with a push-your-luck movement system. DOUBLE TROUBLE challenges players to optimise their JOY tokens to scale their movement range. Players must carefully manage the DOUBLE TROUBLE threshold that can jeopardise a lead in the final sprint.

The game features a high-interaction JOY Maker piece that functions as a community-controlled obstacle and a modular 5x5 grid ensures no two city layouts are the same. The game rewards those who can adapt their strategy to shifting board states.

Success depends on both luck and how you manage your momentum. You face a constant choice: Will you burn through the city at top speed and risk DOUBLE TROUBLE? Or do you adopt a more calculated strategy by smartly utilising METRO shortcuts and Lake-side CHANCE cards?

Check out the videos. One for the story and the other to help you understand the mechanics and how to play.
  
—description from the designer

A Word game for Literary Nerds.

Welcome to the most prestigious (and frustrating) literary circle in history. In Non Sequitur, you and your fellow Authors draft cards from the Modifier Deck—forcing you to write Works that adhere to absurd, often conflicting requirements.

One round, you might be required to compose a Haiku about an Avocado that must contain an Oxymoron, be presented as a Bard, and end as a Question. Fail to follow a single rule, and the Editor slaps you with a Non Sequitur penalty!

Outwit your peers, cleverly play your Synonym and Antonym cards, and earn enough Over Achiever Points to have your bizarre brilliance published. It’s a hilarious, high-pressure battle of wit where logic is your enemy and chaos is your muse.

—description from the publisher

You and your team are stranded. Beyond rescue, beyond help, and beyond hope. You must choose an environment—from the desolate Desert to the relentless Jungle—and fight for every breath against a world actively trying to kill you.

Hazardous is a tense, cooperative survival game where every decision is a matter of life and death. The environment is a modular deck of threats, and every turn brings you closer to the final escape... or total collapse.

Key Features:

Deck-Driven Doom: Two cards from the Threat Deck are flipped each round to form a unique Hazard. Will you face the crushing force of Wind and Terrain, or the desperate risk of Wildlife and Heat?

The Catastrophe Timer: Manage the Hazard Tracker, a central meter that increases as new threats emerge. If the tracker reaches Level 10, the environment overwhelms you, and the game ends in an immediate loss.

Specialized Survival: Strategically scavenge and utilize four vital resource decks (Rations, Scrap Metal, Cloth, and Vegetation) to mitigate threats and craft essential Tools. Fail to pay the cost, and you'll accumulate crippling Injury stacks like Trauma, Fever, and Broken Limbs.

Scrap together your strength, craft a way out, and hold the line against the inevitable. Time is running out, and the environment shows no mercy.
Do you have what it takes to survive the impossible?

—description from the publisher

The year is 4 billion A.D. Humanity is long gone. From the mulch of a dead civilization, Sentient Hyper-Intelligent Worms have emerged. But the universe is drying up. A cosmic predator, The Entropy Crow, is pecking away at the fabric of spacetime. To survive, the Annelid Elite must stabilize the Grand Unified Soil Time Theory (G.U.S.T.T.) and open a Worm Hole to a fresh timeline.

But not every theory of time is created equal. Choose your research path, burrow into the past, tunnel into the future, compete, coerce, and compliment the other research teams in constructing the three time pillars and be the first to activate the new timeline.

History is just dirt, let’s dig.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Diligent Girl as Lady of the House
description:
This 19th-century game is in the collection of the Library of Congress, where they estimated it to be from the 1870s. Both the box and the rules appear in 4 languages: English, German, Italian, and French.

At the beginning of the game six cards are auctioned off, each with a different domestic scene (poultry yard, larder, wash house, diningroom and study,  kitchen, and parlour or drawingroom). Players then take turns drawing chits from a bag. The chits are a 50-50 mix of blanks and items that match one of the domestic scenes (eg a chicken for the poultry yard).

Players also have "counters" (glass discs) that seem to be the score-keeping mechanism. When you draw from the bag on your turn, if you draw a blank chit, you lose a counter. If you draw a chit with a picture, you get a counter & give the chit to the matching scene; if it's yours, you get 2 counters.

Play continues until someone has collected all 8 of the chits that match their picture. That person gets all of the remaining counters. The rules call this "winning," but it seems implied that collecting the most counters is the actual goal.

No neighborhoods, it's your city.

Caffè della piazzetta, Tuesday morning. You sit at the back of the room and order 2 ristretto, for you and your right arm. Your mafiosi are recruited. We must now organize them to take control of different cities. But while you define your strategies, a rival mafia conspires not far from it. The war of influence begins, and in the end, there will be only one winner.

In Città Nostra, you have 6 towers to place the members of your mafia family in the cities to conquer. But beware, each character has his characteristics that can tip a city.

Make the city the playground of your famiglia!

Learning can happen in many ways – even through play! Three different games invite children into the world of letters. By matching uppercase and lowercase letters, kids learn letter shapes, and the player who collects the most pairs wins. In the “The Ship is Loaded” game, players think about the initial sounds of words. The youngest children can play using pictures.

Four fun letter games help children learn to read and write! It’s as easy as ABC! With the letter boards, children can start forming words, progressing from easier to more challenging ones. The other mini-games encourage players to use their thinking skills in many different ways. Exploring letters and words sparks a child’s natural curiosity and remains engaging time and time again.

Practice reading with adorable animals! Literacy opens the door to the world, and learning it can be playful too! Players compare the animals on their cards based on different values. The player who reads the highest value from their card wins all the cards and adds their favorite to their collection frame. Each card includes a fun fact about the animal featured on it!

Farce is an easy, family-friendly word game that takes five minutes to learn and five minutes to play.

Try to guess everyone else's hidden word before they guess yours!

Here's the catch - some people might not have real words!

Collect delightful garden creatures, chain clever card combos, and fend off competitors in a 2–6 player race to 15 points.

Each player begins with 5 cards. On your turn, draw 1 card from the shared deck, then play up to 2 creature cards into your garden (play area).
Every creature has a point value and an on‑play effect; some help you draw more cards, while others let you interact with the creatures in an opponent’s garden.

The game ends when a player reaches 15 points of garden creatures.

—description from the publisher

What is the setting?


In an ancient forest, small spirits called “Jellu” begin to gather.
You become a magical piper, gathering and combining Jellu that move with the rhythm to create the Jellu King who will rule the forest.


     What is the goal?


Succeed in rhythm, collect tokens,
and complete the puzzle board to achieve a higher score.


     How do you play?


Place cards precisely in sync with the app’s rhythm,
and strategically place earned tokens on the puzzle board.

—description from the publisher

London is calling

Connections and society were ever-present in the Regency era, and the Season in London was at the center of it all. Visit special London Settings to vie for valuable connections and complete new objectives for endgame bonuses.

This essential edition of this colorful dystopian-themed game combines the original Euphoria core game with the expansion and includes some revised rules/components, particularly the board layout.

Euphoria is a worker-placement game where dice are your workers. The number on each die represents that worker’s knowledge, which you will compare to the knowledge of other players’ workers throughout the game. The more you send your workers throughout the city and the more workers you add, the more they discover about their twisted society. If they learn too much, they might desert your cause.

—description from the publisher

Rejuvenate in Bath

It seems as though some relative or other is always planning a stay in Bath for the health benefits. You, my dear, are invited to join them! Boost your traits with aid from supporting characters, and collect dowry, social standing and connection tokens for special set bonuses.

The Handsome Headdresses mini expansion for Endearment features headdresses from the Regency era.

Setup: Add these cards to the Dress deck from the base game. These cards are wild, and may be used to activate a Setting and engage in Rivalries as though they match the Setting’s trait type when played, you still gain one point in the Setting’s Trait type, and still use the Setting’s Trait type for your Trait total in Rivalries.

Survival of the Weirdest is an irreverent party game based on the popular Natural Habitat Shorts animations. The game blends fascinating (and often gross) wild animal facts with hilarious, relatable scenarios, challenging players to debate how bizarre biological traits would play out in everyday human situations.

In each round, one player acts as the Judge and flips over a Scenario Card (e.g., "You're on a blind date"). The other players must then select their best (or worst) Trait Card from their hand and argue why their specific animal characteristic is the most fitting for that situation.
​The traits are double-sided and rooted in real-world biology. You might find yourself explaining why a kangaroo’s "epic farting" habit would ruin a romantic dinner, or how a honey badger’s total lack of fear would help you banish the jitters

"As many of you know, there are often original works which the anime, movies, and video games that you enjoy come from.

Hit Plan is a game about the many wondrous franchises, also known as ‘IPs’, and how new content is produced from them.

Would you make a movie? An anime? Or perhaps, new merchandise? 
Play against your friends, battle for the rights to make a new hit product, and deliver hit content to the world!

Players earn points by creating appealing Proposals to acquire IP (Intellectual Property) rights or by using Countermeasures against crime.

Collect 12 points to win the game before the Criminal Organization does.

What You Need to Play

     10x IP Cards (The original franchises for your new content)
     Action Cards (Needed to make proposals for new content or take on the Criminal


 Organization)

     Event Cards (Random events that occur each round)"


—description from the publisher

The game mechanic is draft.
Players create hands from the cards they obtain through the draft to determine the area where the dart lands.
The rules of darts are the same as the rules of the game.

—description from the publisher

A tactical mental battlefield played out on a minimalist grid.
Outsmart your opponent to dominate the board and seize victory through superior positioning.
Players strategically place cards on the board, triggering chains of effects to outmaneuver the enemy in a fast-paced duel.

—description from the publisher

You and your colleagues need to complete the project before time runs out.

—description from the publisher

It’s a very Japanese game!
How many peanuts or rings can you pick and move with chopsticks in one minute?
Plus, with this set, the possibilities are endless!

—description from the publisher
Protect your city, stop the bears! A thrilling co-op tower defense experience.

After successfully defeating his brother Tostig and the Norwegian king Harald Hardrada in the north, King Harold of England made a strategic decision. He left a significant portion of his forces, led by his trusted allies Morcar and Edwin, in the north to maintain control over the region. With the threat from the north suppressed, Harold marched the remainder of his army south, determined to confront the imminent Norman invasion.
The stakes were high, as the fate of the English throne hung in the balance. Harold, along with his loyal troops, set their sights on the battlefield that would soon become known as Hastings. This historic clash was scheduled for the 14th of October in 1066. 
Hastings was no ordinary battle; it would determine the future of England. On one side, King Harold stood with his English forces, battle-hardened from previous encounters. On the other side, Duke William of Normandy led his formidable Norman army, eager to claim the English crown for himself.
The stage was set for one of the most significant battles in English history. Both sides fought valiantly, but tragically, the English forces were eventually overwhelmed. King Harold himself was killed during the intense fighting, leading to the ultimate victory of Duke William and the Normans. 
The Battle of Hastings marked a turning point in English history. Duke William, now known as William the Conqueror, became the new King of England, forever altering the course of the monarchy and shaping the future of the country.

Armies
The battle of Hastings presents a unique and challenging experience for both sides involved. On the Saxon side, King Harald strategically positioned his forces on a ridge surrounded by woods and rivers, giving them an advantageous position. His well-trained army consisted of Theign spearmen, huscarls, and guard huscarls, who were supported by local peasants dedicated to defending their land and supporting their king.
On the Norman side, William brought archers and armored sergeants equipped with spear, shield, and sword. Their advantage lay in the ability to use archers to weaken enemy lines and their cavalry for outflanking the Saxon troops. Both armies had their own set of advantages and disadvantages on the beautiful reconstruction battle map of Hastings.
With 25 units on each side, a total of 50 units in this epic battle board game reenactment, both sides are determined to emerge victorious. The challenge lies in utilizing their respective strengths while addressing their weaknesses.

Game content
The Battle of Hastings 1066 is available as a downloadable package in PDF format. 
It includes high-quality battle maps that can be printed at your local printer store as a poster size of 59.4x88cm or 22x34 inches, or in PDF A4 and US letter sizes to be printed at home. 
Additionally, the package contains 50 military units on the battlefield, game icons, and a multi-language game manual (EN-GER-FR-ES-IT).

Prepare your army and engage in this exciting historical battle.

Gaming time: +120 mn

Experience the thrill of the Battle of Hastings and step into the shoes of either King Harold of England or William the Conqueror. Take command and rewrite history in this legendary battle that forever etched its name among the most renowned conflicts in history. Embrace the opportunity to shape destiny and leave your mark on the annals of time.

—description from the publisher

The Battle of Trafalgar was one of history’s most decisive naval clashes, where Vice-Admiral Horatio Nelson led the Royal Navy to victory against the combined Franco-Spanish fleet.

In Trafalgar 1805, you step into the role of admiral, commanding mighty ships of the line across the windswept seas. Every choice echoes the drama of October 21, 1805, when courage, cunning and discipline decided the fate of nations. 
This game is both a tribute to naval history and a thrilling challenge for players who want to test their tactical skill.

In gameplay, Trafalgar 1805 blends streamlined mechanics with rich historical flavor. You’ll maneuver squadrons, unleash devastating broadsides and even board enemy vessels in desperate close combat. Action Points drive the tempo of battle, forcing you to decide whether to move, fire, repair or gamble on the wind. With squadrons, formations and boarding parties, every turn demands bold decisions. No two battles play alike and the balance between risk and reward makes each game a tense struggle for supremacy at sea.

The game includes a detailed battlemap, beautifully illustrated ship displays and specialized dice that capture the unique firepower, accuracy and boarding capabilities of each vessel. Components are designed to be functional and immersive, from hull and sail damage markers to flags that record captured ships. Whether you are commanding a massive 1st Rate or a swift 3rd Rate, every piece has a distinct role in recreating the naval warfare of the Napoleonic era. These carefully designed elements ensure that every battle feels like an authentic clash of wooden walls and roaring cannon.

Trafalgar 1805 is a print-and-build experience, giving you the freedom to create your own copy at home. All files are provided for easy printing: maps, ship displays, dice stickers and markers. With a pair of scissors, some glue and a bit of care, you can assemble a full fleet ready to fight. 
For step-by-step guidance, we provide tutorials and demonstrations on our youtube channel, ensuring your build process is smooth and enjoyable. 
Once complete, you’ll be ready to command the seas and relive the glory of Trafalgar on your own table.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Naval SITREP: The Journal of Naval Miniatures Wargaming #70
description:
The Naval SITREP is published twice a year by the Admiralty Trilogy Group. It supports the Admiralty Trilogy game system with scenarios, articles on history and technology, and rules expansions & corrections.

The Naval SITREP Issue #70 was published in April of 2026.

Table of Contents

Features

Modeling the Aerial Search of the IJN's Carrier Force in WW II

Chinese Innovation?

CaS Scenario: Old Steel Never Rusts

CaS Scenario: Gunfight at Bien Sur Coral

Harpoon Scenario: You Say Diaoyu, We Say Senkaku

FG&DN Scenario: Otranto Barrier Retreat

DoB Scenario: Venetian Vortex

French Submersible Cruiser Designs from 1921 to 1936

Product Updates

SSModels on EBay - a Review

Rule Changes and Corrections

Persian Incursion Appendix 1 Supplemental

New Aircraft cards for Persian Incursion

Harpoon Rule Change: The VLow Altitude Band and Bombs

Laser Weapons at Sea

Book Reviews

Destroyer Captain: The Life of Ernest E. Evans, by James D. Hornfischer & David J. Hornfischer

Air-to-Air Missiles, Development and Combat Across 80 Years, Vol 1, by Bill Norton

How Carriers Fought, by Lars Celander

Russia's Military Aviation Industry, by Piotr Butowski
family: Magazine: The Naval SITREP – Journal of Naval Miniatures Wargaming

boardgame: On Bloody Ground: The American War of Independence – Mass Combat Wargaming Rulebook
description:
The American War of Independence, being part of the broader American Revolution, in which American Patriot forces, organised as the Continental Army with their French and Spanish allies, defeated the British Empire and American Loyalists forces, ultimately leading to the establishment of the United States as an independent and sovereign nation.

Fight for the independence of America!

On Bloody Ground: The American War of Independence: A 62 page A4 perfect bound mass combat rulebook with common sense rules for movement, shooting and combat, along with a command and orders structure which produces a highly tactical, dynamic and versatile wargame, that is great fun for both seasoned gamers and beginners alike.

On Bloody Ground - The American War of Independence includes six complete army lists to enable players to build The British Army, Loyalist Forces, The Hessians, The Continental Army, The French Army and Spanish armies.

Turn sequence:

     Charges
     Movement
     Shooting
     Combat


—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Series: On Bloody Ground (WiP Games and Miniatures)

description:
The Kamogawa River in Kyoto has shaped seasonal scenery that reflects Kyoto’s culture since ancient times. Clear water, long riverbanks, traditional townhouses, and elegant bridges—it is a cherished strolling place for both locals and visitors alike.

In Walking in Kamogawa, you take a leisurely walk along the riverbank, capturing the shifting scenery into a beautiful, continuous panorama. By carefully selecting and overlapping River Cards, you will build a unique vision of the riverside. Will you focus on the playful Shiba Inu, find the best spots for tea and dango, or seek the perfect view from the bridges?

Let us begin this relaxing journey. Connect the sights, savor the atmosphere, and create a masterpiece of your own along the Kamogawa.

Each player starts the game with four cards in hand. On their turn, a player must play one of their cards and apply its effects. This might force a player to draw cards, or it might allow them to steal a card from a teammate… the game incorporates numerous effects. Some cards even allow you to play during other players' turns.
If a player plays the last card in their hand and it's not a weekend, they draw four new ones. The hunt for the weekend is on!

As you'll have gathered, Burn Out is a very dynamic, fast-paced, and cunning game that guarantees 10 minutes of intense tension and intense focus, with a burning desire to play again and again. The rules are learned in two minutes, and the atmospheric text contributes to the immersive experience. Tested with children, the theme poses no problem, and they keep asking for more.

Deep in an ancient forest, six spirits have stirred from slumber... Your goal is to restore all spirits to calm harmony by carefully rotating and flipping the cards in your hand. You win when all six spirits are in their spring state after resolving a turn.
this represents the forest fully calm, all spirits showing their outer nature.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
In Tower of Gods, our heroes will climb the tower’s staircases and walkways, defeating Lovecraftian gods and monsters that guard treasures of gold and gems.

For each of the 15 rounds of the game, one player rolls the dice and all players draft 2 dice.
At the same time, players may take a turn and use these dice to attack monsters or explore staircases and walkways between rooms.

Each monster requires a specific combination of dice and/or wild cards to be defeated. Once defeated, it will grant a gold reward (which is higher if you are the first to defeat it) and allow you to earn an additional reward, which can be: more money, a life, a ruby, a diamond, a wild card, or an extra die.

Rubies accumulate to grant us extra gold, and diamonds allow us to purchase upgrades.

At the end of the game, we’ll have to count all the gold we’ve earned; the player with the most gold is the winner.

—description from the publisher

Rumour has it, ghosts have been stirring up all kinds of mischief in this mysterious little town, and curious thrill-seekers are pouring in to catch a glimpse of the paranormal action. Sensing a golden opportunity, you decide to jump on the trend and open your very own spooky bar! Sensing a golden opportunity, you decide to jump on the trend and open your very own spooky bar. Ghoulish drinks, eerie vibes, and ghostly encounters? Yes, please! Just don’t let things get too spooky... If the haunt level gets out of hand, your terrified guests might run out screaming – without paying their tab!

At each of your bars, 5 guests are hanging out, ready to enjoy some drinks and maybe take home a spooky souvenir—ghosts trapped in bottles! Each round, everyone picks and plays 2 action cards at the same time. What you can do with them depends not just on your own cards, but also on what the others play! The game is over when the last guest leaves one of the bars. 
Whoever has the most money at the end wins!

—description from the publisher

A music-based party game for 2–10 players (ages 16+) that challenges players to place 420 hit songs on a musical timeline. It focuses on three eras—Vinyl (pre-1985), Cassette/CD (1985–2004), and Streaming (2005–2025)—with a 30-minute playtime, where the first to collect 10 hits wins.

Fatal Fight is a cooperative deck builder board game with a scrolling beat'em-up video games theme.

The game is played in three stages, all ended with a boss. In the rounds before the boss, new enemies will enter the scene from anyof the six entry points, and players will work together to defeat them, and then suffer damage from those that remain. Players can pick up items from the stage to use as throwing weapons or healing.

Players start with a small deck of skills, but will level up after each stage, putting in new skills in their deck, thus levelling up their characters.

The game comes with 6 wildly different characters to choose from.

Rails and Cargo is a solo roll-and-write game where it’s just you, a pencil, and a map full of untapped potential. Your task may sound simple - restore broken rail lines between cities, build factories, produce goods, and deliver them where they’re needed most. But this is not an easy ride.

This is a think-and-write experience: no luck, no dice-driven chaos - dice will be rolled only during setup. Every decision is entirely in your hands. You’re racing against time, with a limited number of turns to plan, optimize, and execute your strategy. Each action you take and its result is your choice, not chance. Only the most efficient planners will meet the delivery goals and earn victory.

With Rails and Cargo you’ll get three Control Panel sheets in varying levels of challenge - pick the one that suits your journey. Plus, you’ll receive three unique map sheets to explore:

Poland - Start your engines in Warsaw, then stretch your railways across vast plains to reach cities like Kraków, Gdańsk, and Wrocław. Long distances and wide-open spaces make efficiency the key—can you keep your cargo moving while covering so much ground?

Spain - From the bustling heart of Madrid, your trains must climb over rugged mountains and descend toward vibrant coasts. Deliver goods to Barcelona, Valencia, and Seville, balancing tricky routes with limited space. Every track drawn feels like a puzzle piece in this sunny but demanding network.

Germany - A dense web of cities radiates out from Berlin, with major stops like Munich, Hamburg, and Frankfurt waiting for your cargo. Shorter routes mean more connections, but also more competition for space - this map rewards sharp planning and clever timing to keep industries supplied.

No dice, no luck - just pure strategy as you sketch out the most efficient rail lines to deliver goods across Europe!

—description from the publisher

Cross a continent of striking contrasts, where coral reefs shimmer beneath turquoise seas and ancient red earth stretches beyond the horizon. Wander through cosmopolitan harbours, dense eucalyptus forests alive with birdsong, rugged mountain ranges, and small outback towns shaped by resilience and story. This expansion for The Wayfarer invites you to chart your own path across Australia with a richly illustrated map of the entire continent.

Australia unfolds as a land of immense scale and striking contrasts. In the far north, ancient escarpments tower above wetlands and billabongs in Kakadu National Park, while the tropical waters of the Great Barrier Reef shimmer with coral gardens and marine life. Deep in the heart of the continent, the sandstone monolith of Uluru rises from the red desert, glowing at sunrise and sunset. Along the southeast, the forested ridges of the Great Dividing Range give way to fertile valleys and cool-climate vineyards, while dramatic coastal cliffs line the Great Ocean Road. Wildlife roams freely from kangaroos in open bushland to seabirds wheeling above rugged shores. In cities such as Brisbane and Perth, modern skylines meet relaxed outdoor living, shaped by Indigenous heritage and multicultural influences. From reef to rainforest, desert to alpine peaks, Australia offers journeys defined by space, light, and unforgettable natural beauty.

—description from the publisher

One Page Expedition: Expansions contains two variants of play: Patron's Will and Strategy over Luck.

In the Patron's Will variant you will get to complete quests given by patrons. These quests give points for setting camps in a correct way.

In the Strategy over Luck variant you will not use dice to make your camps, instead you will use a sheet with a Sector Table that makes you plan your moves ahead.

—description from the publisher

A mozi legyen veled! ("I Am Your Movie") is a fast-paced party game centered on film knowledge, creativity, and performance. Players take turns completing different types of movie-related challenges, testing both their knowledge and their ability to communicate ideas in entertaining ways.

Each round introduces a new task, which may include recognizing films from quotes, acting out scenes, describing plots, or recreating soundtracks using voice or gestures. These varied challenges require players to think quickly and adapt their approach depending on the category, combining trivia knowledge with expressive skills.

The game emphasizes social interaction and humor, as players interpret clues and attempt to guess correctly under time pressure. With multiple categories and a wide range of film references, it offers a dynamic and replayable experience suitable for groups and casual gatherings.

Choose a book. Flip to a page. Score 1 point per word!

It’s a bookmark for each game, with some fancy art on one side, and a little book challenge on the backside. Choose a book (this is the most important part!) and then flip to a page to see how many instances of the words you can find on that page.

Don’t expect these to be available outside of Button Shy campaigns and Patreon. They’re fun little promos.

—description from the publisher

The Terror transports you to Paris, 1793 – the bloodiest days of the French Revolution. It’s a time of revolutionary intrigue, paranoia, betrayals, and sudden turns of fortune.

The game tells the story of the Terror. It can be set up in minutes, moves fast and hits hard round after round. No slack, no bloat. Just screaming good fun!

The Terror is a “take that” strategy board game with a rich historical theme, in which strong, simple gameplay builds into a fun and frantic battle of wits. To survive in Paris in 1793, you must adapt, deceive, and outmanoeuvre your rivals.

To survive the festival of killing players must collect allies from powerful factions, and avoid association with citizens whose factions have fallen out of favour.

Citizen cards represent leading figures from the five major factions, ranked from one to three stars. The value of these cards will be determined not just by the stars, but also on the ultimate fate of the faction to which they belong.  

As the game progresses, citizens are sent to the guillotine. The more members of a faction who are executed, the more disgraced that faction becomes; and the more dangerous it is to be associated with them.

For example, by the end of the game, 3-star Maximilien Robespierre might be worth either: 6 points, 3 points, or minus 3 (depending on the fate of his faction, the Montagnards).

At the end of the game, factions that have suffered the most executions will rank lowest. Their allies will not just be worth less, they will count against you.

To win, you must manipulate the course of the Terror—protecting factions you are associated with, while pushing rival factions toward the guillotine. You influence events by taking citizen allies, sending others to the guillotine, and by playing Terror Cards. These allow you to execute rival citizens, swap citizens with your opponents, or make secret moves (the mask card) that change the balance of power. 

Beware. Not everyone belongs to a revolutionary faction. The Bourbons, the royal family and their supporters, are already doomed. In Paris in 1793, the taint of association with them could cost you dearly.  There are five Bourbon cards in the deck, and you do not want them. 

Finally, each player receives a secret Conspiracy Card, which carries a hidden objective to pursue during the game. If you succeed in your conspiracy, you will gain additional points at the end of the game. 

The Terror is meticulously researched and beautifully crafted from genuine 18th century paintings and etchings, officially licensed from 19 of the world’s great museums.

—description from the publisher

Of Soil and Stones is a cooperative tile-placement game for 1–4 players who, in the roles of architects and builders, will help a young King and Queen build their Kingdom. To succeed, you must play all the tiles as Meadows and Fortresses, or flip them over to build the Moat. A tile can also be placed on top of another, but only if its value is equal to or between the two values it covers. Will you manage to play them all and defend yourselves against enemies?
Of Soil and Stones is a 10 scenario-based game, in which you’ll discover a gripping story and new, increasingly difficult challenges.

The hamsters are taking a vacation. Everyone wants to hang out on the hammock!

Carefully place your hamsters on the hammock and try to hang on without falling out. If the hammock tilts on your turn, all other players can send one of their hamsters for a rest on a fluffy beach towel.

The first player with all hamsters on the hammock and resting on beach towels, wins the game!

—description from the publisher

Sitting Bull, leader of the Hunkpapa Lakota, occupies a key place in history for his role in the Battle of Little Bighorn in 1876, where the Lakota and Cheyenne nations defeated the Seventh Cavalry.
