Since then, his name has become a symbol of his people’s resistance and the preservation of their traditions.
Experience the daily life of one of the Council Fires, the tribes that make up the Lakota people.
Guided by Sitting Bull, travel the territories in search of game, participate in Lakota traditions and beliefs and witness feats and exploits in the era before the conflict with the US expansion.
The goal will be to accumulate the greatest amount of Prestige Points (PP) at the end of the game. Prestige Points are obtained through the strategic placement of tribe members, the control of the five regions, overcoming milestones and accumulating sacred stones.

GAMEPLAY
The game is played in turns, starting with the starting player and continuing clockwise until everyone has no more Lakota pawns to place on the board. When the last player finishes their turn, the final score is calculated.
On their turn, the active player will follow these 7 steps in order:

     Play a card
     Place a Lakota pawn
     Interact with Sitting Bull
     Perform the region action
     Check the Milestones
     Draw a new card
     Replenish the region with a new card


FINAL SCORING
Once all players have placed all of their Lakota pawns on the board, calculate the final score to determine who wins the game. Use the score sheet to record the points earned in each of the following sections:

     Sacred stones: add up the points for all the sacred stones you have (tokens and presents).
     Paths: use the scoring card to calculate how many prestige points you get from the 4 paths, according to the number of items you have of each path.
     Powwow: score points according to the number of Lakota pawns located in the powwow area.
     Offerings: in each of the 5 regions, the player with the most Lakota pawns scores the points for the region's offerings. If two or more players tie, no one scores the points.
     Milestones: Score the points earned with the claimed milestones.
     Wisdom cards: Score the points on your wisdom card if you have achieved the goal.


The player with the highest total score is declared the winner. In the event of a tie, the winner will be the player with the most items of the Path of Silence. If the tie persists, share the victory.

—description from the publisher

A chaotic and fun family game where luck and skill go hand in hand. Players take on the roles of grandchildren helping their grandmother get all her kittens to bed—and she has quite a lot of them! The cats love to sleep in one place, side by side (and on top of each other), ideally in a pyramid! So a little creativity is required. The first player to get all their assigned kittens to bed and fill the pyramid wins.

—description from the publisher

BAT-tle Royale is a fast-paced, two-player trick-taking card game set in the wild world of cryptocurrency, blockchains, and Web3 degen culture. The game leans heavily into BAT (Basic Attention Token) and Brave browser meme lore, most notably through its striking tuck box that features the iconic 2017 “Copium X Hopium” cubist artwork — a nostalgic tribute to longtime BAT holders. Inside, the cards contrast beautifully: sleek geometric Chain Cards representing Ethereum, Avalanche, and Solana, paired with premium Flat 2.0 illustrated NFT lion characters (The Curious, The Dabbler, The Degen, and The Sage) presented in elegant Art Deco frames. 
Gameplay is simple to learn but rich in bluffing and tactical depth. Each round, a Market Chain (trump suit) is revealed from the draw deck. Players take turns leading a card, and the opponent must beat it with a higher card of the same chain or any card from the current Market Chain. The four special NFT cards act as a powerful unified suit that beats everything — with The Sage being the ultimate card in the game. However, The Sage can be dramatically countered by the “Burning Trick,” where the opponent sacrifices all three other NFT cards at once. Initiative, timing, and mind games are crucial, as the lead constantly shifts and games are often decided by razor-thin margins. 
To win, players collect cards in their discard pile throughout the game. Chain Cards are worth 1 point each, while NFT cards carry higher variable point values printed on them. After all 42 cards have been played, the player with the most points wins (a 30–30 tie is possible). With its mix of classic trick-taking DNA, clever crypto theming, and tense risk-reward decisions, BAT-tle Royale delivers a surprisingly strategic and replayable experience in just 15 minutes.

—description from the designer

The objective of both sides is to shoot the ball into the opponent's goal. Players take turns moving. A move consists of either a move alone, or a move and a shot. All pieces can move up to 3 squares.

The two defenders and the two inside forwards (identified by their red base) can only move in a straight line. The remaining pieces can only move along the corners of the squares, but also in a zigzag pattern. The goalkeeper can move anywhere, but may not leave the penalty area.

Only one piece or the ball may occupy each square. A piece cannot move onto a square already occupied by another. The ball must be moved if a piece is moved onto the ball's square, specifically: the goalkeeper (identified by their jersey) can move up to 12 squares, and the outside forwards can move up to 12 squares. (Recognizable by their yellow base) Up to 9 squares away when they are on rows A, B, O, and P; up to 6 squares away on the remaining squares; and up to 6 squares away from the other pieces. A single piece can only move the ball across the corners or along the wide sides of the squares.

A goal is scored when the ball is moved onto one of the 5 goal squares. The pieces are then returned to their starting positions, and the piece that allowed the goal to be scored goes first.

If the ball is moved across squares already occupied by pieces, each occupied square counts double. The ball cannot be played across a square occupied by the goalkeeper.

A combination is the passing of the ball between 2 and at most 3 pieces of the same team and counts as one move. A header can only be played within a combination and only by the 2nd or 3rd piece in the combination move.

According to the rules of football, a player is offside only if they are in the opponent's half of the field, between the ball and the goal, have only one opponent in front of them, and can score a goal themselves or are involved in a combination that leads to a goal. The offside player loses possession of the ball to the opposing goalkeeper, regardless of their position.

The game duration is agreed upon (until the 1st, 3rd, 5th, etc. goal or for 1.5 hours).

—user summary

Stumbling-Blocks is one of the first word board games, published by Hildesheimer & Faulkner, founded in 1881 and renamed in the 1890s.

It has a box of about 150 letter tiles, and a board on which they are played. The board is an 8x8 grid, every other square of which is marked with spot; the rules say you can play draoughts (checkers) on the board.  It also has thicker borders around the central 6x6 and 4x4 squares, and the letters of the alphabet (except 'I') arranged in a 5x5 square. It has rules for several games.

I. Stumbling-Blocks. 2-6 players decide on the size of the board being used, and draws an equal number of tiles, enough to barely fill the board. (For example, if 5 are playing with the full 8x8=64 squares, they each draw 13 tiles. Tiles are all kept face up so all players can see them.  A turn consists of placing one of your unused tiles on an empty square, and scoring for any words completed with that letter. Words must be spelled in an uninterrupted straight line in any orthogonal or diagonal direction. (The rules point out that players may want to play to block another's good score rather than to make a word, which is useful since all the tiles to be played are visible.) A word scores one point per letter. The winner is either the one with the highest score when the board is filled, or the first to reach a pre-arranged total, e.g. 30 points.

II. Letter Square.  Same as Stumbling-Blocks, except that tiles are all placed face down, and players draw one randomly on their turn.

III. Spot and Blank.  Same as Stumbling-Blocks, except there are only 2 players, with one playing on the spotted squares, and the other on the blank squares -- which are arranged like black and red squares on a checkerboard.

IV. Alphabets. A game for 3-6 children.  Each takes an equal number of tiles, which are kept face-down.  On their turn a player reveals one of their face-down tiles, and places it on the board space labeled with that letter if it is empty.  If it is not empty, they keep the tile, face up. There is no 'I' space, so I's are all left face up on the table when revealed. When all the letter tiles are filled, the player with the fewest face-up tiles wins.

Turn any Hanukkah night into a game night filled with laughter, learning, and cozy connection!

Collect Perks and Treats as you: 

     Test your Hanukkah knowledge
     Follow festive prompts full of surprises
     Try your luck in the Shamash Shakeup round


Showcase your smarts and let luck lend a hand to score the most points!

Offers dual-level trivia for experts and beginners to play together.

—description from the publisher

Welcome back! Traveling to a place for the first time is magical as everything can feel like a new discovery.  But returning to a place can be even more exciting. You already know your favorite places, its less confusing to get around and you can take a deeper dive into what that place has to offer.

Let’s Go! Back to Japan offers players 3 modules to explore as they head back to Japan. There are new side trips allowing players to journey around Kanto and Kansai, new Favorable condition boards offering new rewards, and players can extend their trips until Sunday.

-description from designer

Players begin by taking turns placing a seed on a space in a hexagonal grid. Players may only place seeds on a friendly (controlled by self) or neutral (no seeds) space.

When in a space, there are the same amount of seeds as there are neighboring spaces, the space is considered full and the seeds inside spread out to each of the neighboring spaces. If a destination space was previously a hostile space (controlled by the opponent), it becomes a friendly space. If after spreading the seeds, there are still full spaces, this will cause a chain reaction, causing additional spreads on each of the full spaces, until there are no more full spaces.

Once a player has no seeds left on the board, the other player is declared the winner. (Obvious exception to this is at the first turn of each player, since the board is empty before the start of the game).

Chorogi is a card game where the goal is to get rid of all your cards.

On your turn, you either play a card to beat your opponent or pass.

However, when you pass, you must discard one card from your hand.

If you pass when you have only one card left, you are eliminated.

To avoid elimination, there is a rule called Snatch, which allows you to pick up a discarded card and play it.

The goal is to get rid of all your cards to win, but if you run out of cards, you are eliminated?
publishers: チップキックゲームズ (Chip Kick Games)

description:
A brand‑new standalone title in the HacKClaD series.
Rebuild a fallen city using an unstable magical reactor in this engine‑building × worker‑placement strategy game.

Features
Dynamic engine-building: Manage a magical reactor with fluctuating output and craft the most efficient resource‑production plan.

Asymmetric character abilities: Unique powers accelerate the tension and interaction within worker placement.

A narrative spin‑off: Discover the untold stories behind the battles of HacKClaD.

—description from the publisher
A game where mice collect cheese

Vitrarium is a strategic botanical simulator set in a Victorian Steampunk universe where players compete for the title of Royal Gardener. Over twenty-six rounds spanning four distinct seasons, you must cultivate exotic plants and manage resources under the watchful eye of the Queen. Success requires a balance between patience and aggressive economic maneuvers. There are also special events that happen all the time and could disrupt an unprepared player.

The gameplay revolves around planting seeds, nurturing saplings, and deciding the perfect moment to sell your harvest or trade with rivals. A dynamic market and the Queen's specific challenges keep the economy shifting, ensuring that no two games feel the same. Negotiation is a key component, as players can trade plants and resources to secure an advantage.

Players choose from eight unique experts, each bringing their own specialized skills to the conservatory. While one might favor raw efficiency, another might excel at market manipulation or protecting delicate flora from seasonal shifts. These asymmetric abilities force players to adapt their strategy based on the character they represent.

The ultimate goal is to accumulate the most wealth and influence by the end of the final round. Vitrarium offers a high-difficulty experience where every decision matters and the environment is as unforgiving as the high society you aim to impress. It is a test of long-term vision and tactical flexibility in a world of brass and blossoms.

—description from the designer

Dungeon Legacy is a print-and-play roll-and-write dungeon crawler for 1 to 3 players, played across a campaign of three distinct floors.

In Dungeon Legacy, you are a lone adventurer armed with nothing but three dice and your wits. You must navigate treacherous corridors, fight monsters, collect treasure, and head to the exit before the dungeon claims you. Each floor is played on a single printed sheet. Roll your dice, choose your path, and mark your trail with a pencil. Every cell you visit is permanently marked. You can never retrace your steps.

Dungeon Legacy is a legacy-style experience played across three unique floors, each with its own theme, layout, and hazards. Floor 1, The Dungeon, introduces basic mechanics in a classic stone setting. Floor 2, The Forgotten Forest, adds water obstacles, teleporters, wishing wells, and healing apples. Floor 3, The Dragon's Lair, is a volcanic underworld featuring lava flows, a dragon boss, and a pile of gold.

Each turn, players roll 3d6 and choose how to use the dice. Move in a straight line with one die, or combine two dice for an L-shaped path. Collect coins, keys, swords, torches, and potion ingredients automatically as you cross their cells. End your move adjacent to a chest, merchant, wishing well, or monster to interact with them. Combat is resolved with a single die roll against the enemy's HP. Miss, and you lose 1 HP. Combat continues until the enemy is dead, or you are.

To escape each floor, you must hold at least one key and reach the exit door. Everything you carry with you (HP, coins, swords, torches, potions, and kills) carries forward to the next floor. Each floor grows more dangerous, but so do you.

Die on any floor, and the campaign ends. Start fresh from Floor 1.

Scoring rewards both survival and treasure. Multiply your remaining HP by 3 and your coins by 2 at the end of each floor, then add your cumulative kill bonus and +10 for slaying the Dragon.

Will you emerge from the dungeon as a legend, or become just another skeleton in its depths?

Foxes and Lions: Renaissance City-States in the Age of Machiavelli is the next game in the series that includes Here I Stand, Virgin Queen, and Tanto Monta. Focused on the warring city-states and dominions of Renaissance Italy during the era known as the “Italian Wars” (specifically, the period from 1494 to 1527), this game zooms in on the Italian peninsula. Unlike many other games on the Italian Wars, Foxes and Lions puts the focus on Italian experiences of this era of enormous upheaval, which also saw some of the greatest cultural splendors that we now associate with the period known (not without controversy) as the Renaissance. The game depicts the political, military, economic, religious, and cultural affairs of five key Italian powers brought to life by players.

Players will struggle to create political legitimacy for themselves while contending with both Italian rivals and external great powers intent on conquest of their own objectives in Italy (France, Spain, the Holy Roman Empire, and the Ottoman Empire). For those who have played other series games, much will be familiar mechanically, but there are many new concepts and mechanics to enjoy, including experimenting with different government types, managing local sentiment, and gathering precious florins to pay for mercenaries and their fickle condottieri captains.

Furthermore, players will be faced with the possibility of collective defeat to the great powers. Players will individually pursue Virtù Points, drawing on Machiavelli's famously pliable conception of political ability and military strength, while at the same time striving to avoid collective defeat by loss of Italian Virtù. Dwindling Italian Virtù embodies Machiavelli's abiding fear of great power domination of Italy. Players will each attempt to win without Italy falling into the subjection that was historically its fate, and should they fail to prevent that, the game ascribes blame to the specific players viewed as the scourge of Italy and the new lapdog of the great powers. Can you thread the needle between your Italian rivals’ ambitions and the threat posed by the great powers of Europe?

—description from the publisher

Scrapyard Tinkers is a 2 players dice drafting game with a Steampunk theme.

In this game players run into the scrapyard to search for Loot (get dice from the bag), split it up (draft the dice), and use it to complete Contracts.

The game is played over 6 turns. After that, the player that earned most Marks from completing contracts is the winner.

Each Contract has requirements on the type, amount, and value of the dice. You have to match them all to complete it. Contracts also have a Marks value, which you earn when you complete them.

There are 3 different materials in the game, each represented by a different colored dice.

Each player has a character card with special abilities to manipulate the dice.

-description from designer

PuffiNova’s first expansion introduces cosmic abilities to the game! These unique powers give players advantages that last throughout the entire game and open up new strategic possibilities.

The expansion includes 17 new cards.

—description from the publisher

Conquer the island of Motya! A fun strategy game to play with family or friends - take control of a fleet of 3 ships, collect action cards from floating bottles and come up with the best strategy to become the first player to conquer Motya, an island in the centre of the map

Each turn you must move one of your vessels one space on the map to take or fortify control of the central island. Battle with your opponents ships by rolling dice. Play action card to shift the odds in your favour. Control the central island or destroy your opponent's fleets to win.

boardgame: Ace of Aces: Wingleader – Expansion Pack 1
description:
The aircraft cards from the Chessex Deluxe version of Wingleader are available from Nova Game Designs, LLC.

Aircraft included are:

Gloster Gladiator
Gloster Meteor
Beaufighter X
Spitfire IX
Typhoon
MS 406
MB 152 CL
D 520
He-162A "Volksjager"
Fiat G-50
Re 2001
Ki-43 Hayabusa "Oscar"
Ki-44 Shoki "Tojo"
A6M5 Reisen "Zero"
P-39 Airacobra
P-47B Thunderbolt "Razorback"
P-51B Mustang
P-63 Kingcobra
F6F-5 Hellcat
I-16 Rata
Yak-9
LaGG-9

—description from the publisher

You're standing at the edge of the vast forest.  Some of it is readily accessible and much of it is too deep to get to.  You have a team standing by awaiting your direction and you can see at least the beginning of how to approach this rescue operation.  You might have a little bit of scouted information to work with and not all your scouting teams have checked back in yet, but it's time to get to work while there's daylight.  Other than what's hidden in the depths of this forest, what happens next is all in the Draw Deck, and how you manage the situation as it evolves.

Your Draw Deck is composed primarily of Lost, Seekers, and Beasts.  You'll need to cover Lost and Beasts with Seekers of equal-or-higher #value to discard them.  You have several Specialists with unique abilities and there are a few powerful artifacts you'll uncover in the forest.  There are also roving Consuming Mists which may appear at any moment.

Orginially designed for play with a standard deck of poker cards, plus jokers.  This is a Beat-Your-Own-Score game, technically, in which your score is the number of Lost you've failed to find.  So, really, any score above 0 could be considered a Loss.  There are myriad levers baked-in for adjusting difficulty to taste - this can be a morning-coffee-stretch or an epic, impossible odyssey.  Tools are included for tracking these "settings," so you can repeat the ones you really liked.

—description from the designer

LITTLE READER, YOU CREATE YOUR OWN ADVENTURE!

In Calie & Kiki new readers will follow along and help in a fresh take on the traditional Princess captured in a tower fable. Spirited Calie will meet the readers as she has decided herself to not wait on rescue and will enlist the help of the readers and her beloved pet cat Kiki to effect her escape! What follows are puzzles and encounters as Calie finds various power items to help traverse the environment and solve puzzles to escape! With many paths to take, items to find, puzzles to clear and foes to face, interactivity for the readers and older readers assisting them and the replay value is high!

Calie & Kiki is a comic combined with a game, another title in a new series subtitled “Your Choices. Your Adventure. Your Story. For Kids!” Young readers are encouraged to assist the characters in the stories as they explore, puzzle solve and interact with fantastic environments. There are talking points and suggested courses of assistance for the young readers helpers, along with clear tools to record items and take notes as needed throughout the adventure. The book is designed with both visual clues and comprehension puzzles to provide high replay value through many readings.

—description from the publisher

LITTLE READER, YOU CREATE YOUR OWN ADVENTURE!

In Mr. Wingletter new readers will become the title character, a young stork letter carrier who lives with his family in a peaceful village in France called Le Cormier. He loves his job, which allows him to chat with the villagers, walk along the beautiful countryside and help his friends. Mr. Wingletter is cheerful, caring and goes out each day with an open heart to meet new people. The book has numerous puzzles, places to go, packages to deliver and friends to meet. It presents this in colorful illustrations and gives plenty of tools to keep notes and make many different choices each time through the book. All of this will help lead young readers and those that help them, through their choices and go on amazing adventures time after time!

Mr. Wingletter is a comic combined with a game, another tittle in a new series subtitled “Your Choices. Your Adventure. Your Story. For Kids!” Young readers are encouraged to make choices as the title character to help his friends by delivering packages and letters. The book presents a number of challenging puzzles for readers and stresses friendship and meeting new people. The book is designed with both visual clues and comprehension puzzles to provide high replay value through many readings.

—description from the publisher

OFMOS Essential is a competitive strategy game for 2–4 players in which each player, acting as a CEO, manages a portfolio of nine products across nine business environments on a shared continuous 81-position board. The board is a modular structure of nine environment tiles, and every position represents a unique combination of perceived value and functional complexity. Players compete to build the most profitable portfolio through deliberate positioning, timing, and portfolio coordination.

On each turn, a player takes one action: launch a product into an environment, commoditize it (move down for immediate profit but closer to exit), innovate it (move left, right, or up to reposition for future value), or retire it (remove it from the board). Each action carries point consequences and long-term trade-offs. Commoditize too early and you sacrifice potential. Innovate and you forgo immediate returns. Retire too late and you leave points on the table. Players can also eliminate opponents' adjacent products through downward and lateral moves, adding a direct adversarial dimension to portfolio management.

Synergy formations — three or more of your products positioned in horizontal alignment — are the only source of bonus points, rewarding coordinated portfolio thinking across multiple turns. Opponents can break your synergies by eliminating adjacent products using the same adversarial mechanics available to every player. The game ends when any player's last product is removed from the board. The player with the most points wins.

OFMOS Essential supports three modes of play from the same rulebook: a pure abstract strategy experience with no business context required, a business simulation in which every action maps to its real-world strategic equivalent through the rulebook's Business Concepts section, and player-modified gameplay through four official Mods that extend the strategic landscape for repeated play. Rules are learned in approximately 10 minutes. Sessions run 20–60 minutes depending on player count and experience.

—description from the designer

boardgame: The Peoples of The World: Age of Rebuilding
description:
While most stories conclude with the final blow of a great battle, this story begins one month later. The Enemy has been vanished, the Goblins and Trolls have fled into the unknown, and the Great War is officially over. But in the 18 provinces of The World, peace is a fragile illusion. As the Elves and Men look to rebuild, the neutral Dwarves emerge from the shadows, and the Dragons are filled with greed, a new era of tension arises.

The Peoples of The World: Age of Rebuilding is a highly asymmetrical strategy game for 2–4 players exploring the power vacuum left after a great war and what can happen when negotiations break down. Every player controls a faction with radically different mechanics, starting resources, and paths to victory.

Play as the Elves, Dwarves, Men, or Dragons in this epic contest for who will dominate the post-war world.

—description from the designer

Barbascura X: Bestie Bastarde is a party card game built around six rounds of competitive challenges, where players aim to outmatch their opponents by playing powerful Beast cards from their hand. In each round, players simultaneously choose and play one or two Beasts face down, then reveal them and activate their abilities in turn order. A clever use of abilities is key, as each Beast can influence the outcome in different ways.
After all abilities have been resolved, players compare the total Strength of their Beasts still in play, potentially boosted by biome bonuses tied to the current challenge. The player with the highest total wins the round and claims the Challenge card, earning points.
At the end of each round, players refill their hands and pass the leader role to the player with the lowest score. After the final round, players reveal their secret goal cards, which can grant additional points if their conditions are met. The player with the highest total score wins the game.

The Gems of Atlantis have been stolen and are being hidden away by a gang of Sharks. Which of the Mermaid crew can score the biggest heist?

Each turn your Mermaid will get to roll dice, locking Sharks, and saving any other dice. You can stop rolling at any time. As long as you haven't rolled 3 Sharks. In that case, you're caught and your turn is over. You will score nothing!

But if you dodged the Sharks, each of your dice showing a Gem moves your marker ahead on the scoring track. You'll notice that there are special spaces on the scoring track that if you manage to stop on, you can choose not to take all of those Gems, then they will give you a special benefit!

Players will play to  a set number of score track cards, which can be arranged in any order the players choose or randomly to allow a variety of bonuses to occur! Players will find plenty of challenge as well with the 3 levels of difficulty of dice. The Hard dice are more rewarding, but also have more sharks!
Watch those chomping Sharks as you roll your way to an Ocean-sized heist!

—description from the designer

Gear up. Leave the road behind.

This expansion takes you off the main board and into focused multi-turn adventures. Collect special expedition gear - compass, tent, sleeping bag, map - and pack your backpack. Once you're ready, head to one of the new Expedition tiles and start your journey.

Every turn, draw from a dedicated Expedition Photo deck to build photo sets that match your expedition's unique goal. The more prepared you are with the right photography equipment, the more cards you draw: boosting your chances of capturing high-value trail shots with genre tags like landscape, wildlife, night, and portrait.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Photo Tour: North America – Alaska & Yukon
description:
Wild. Remote. Dice-powered.
Head north to one of the most rugged corners of North America. This expansion adds a striking Alaska & Yukon board extension with harder-to-reach locations and breathtaking wild zones.
In these wild zones, you'll roll custom wildlife dice to collect animal, foreground, and background icons. Spend these icons to claim transparent cards featuring northern wildlife and landscapes, then layer them into unique photo compositions that score big and create unforgettable moments in your photo journal.

—description from the publisher

In the dark-fantasy world of Trisk, powerful forces clash beyond human understanding, leaving mortals trapped between angels, demons, and forgotten entities. Drawn by the secrets of a once-dominant kingdom, players lead elite squads in a legacy-driven RPG for up to 4 players, seeking to uncover the source of its power before the world collapses into chaos.

The game combines exploration, branching quests, and tactical combat. Players move across regions, complete objectives, and develop their squad by replacing its members with summoned warriors - angelic, demonic, and otherworldly beings that redefine their strategy. PvP skirmishes are frequent and opportunistic, as players exploit moments of weakness and fight for dominance, maneuvering between shifting alliances and hidden intentions.

Choices shape each playthrough. Decisions can permanently alter available paths, locking or unlocking questlines and changing the course of the game. As players push deeper, they must balance growing power against a rising madness system, while the Bell of Abaddon acts as a global timer - if it tolls too many times, all players lose.

The goal is to outmaneuver opponents, survive escalating pressure, and ultimately reach the Archives to claim control over Trisk before the end is triggered.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: March of Nations: Rules for Wargaming the Age of Powder 1700-1790
description:
This rule set is designed for the beginner or experienced war gamer who is looking for a realistic and fun game that can be played in less than 90 minutes.

In these Pages as well as the core rules, you will also get optional advanced rules to make the game more interesting and add more friction, 5 historical scenarios for quick play and learning, a naval wargaming supplement, and a fully crafted campaign to get into the game.

Get ready to be transported to the Wars of the 1700's, where gallant gentlemen and stoic footsoldiers clash in miniature on your tabletop.

May your battles be many and victorious.

—description from the publisher
family: History: War of the Austrian Succession
History: War of the Spanish Succession

boardgame: The Northern Gamble: The American Invasion of Quebec 1775
description:
The Northern Gamble is a historically grounded 3-battle American Revolution campaign covering the 1775 invasion of Canada.

Fight through Fort Saint-Jean, Montreal, and Quebec in a linked campaign where each battle affects the next through morale, reinforcements, and expiring enlistments. 
Includes full scenarios, commander rules with traits, campaign mechanics—designed for fast, battalion-level play in a single evening.

—description from the publisher

21-card Mini Expansion introduced with the Cocktails Expansion.

This promo pack features:

YEAR ONE: An optional way to fast track your distillery production by allowing you to simulate (and skip) the first round of the game.

PRESTIGIOUS DISTILLERY UPGRADES: For only the fanciest of distilleries, these upgrades cost lots of money but reward you in pure spirit points.

King Frog is a competitive casual tabletop card game, where the goal is to get to 15 flies, you farm them, steal them, destroy enemies farms, cast spells send thieves.

Setup


     Each player chooses a character.
     Every character has its own unique abilities and perks.
     Once all players have chosen, each player draws 4 cards.
     Roll the die to determine the first player. The player who rolls the King Frog starts.
     Play proceeds clockwise.


Turn Structure

At the start of your turn, you have 3 Energy to spend.

You may spend Energy to do any of the following:


     Place cards into the playground, such as Farms or Armies
     Play Spell cards
     Draw cards
     Perform actions with cards you have already placed in the playground


Players may use their Energy in any order during their turn, as long as they have enough Energy to pay the required costs.

End of Turn

At the end of your turn, collect the Flies produced by your Farms.

Winning the Game

The first player to collect 15 Flies wins the game.

—description from the designer

Grand Dawn is a strategic 2v2 partnership climbing card game where players embody new deities ascending the ranks of Olympus to overthrow the fading old gods. Its strategic depth is derived from classic Chinese card games.

Grand Dawn is far more than a card game—it is a test of wisdom, adaptability, and partnership. Its core lies in the dynamic balance between cooperation and competition. Players must master not only card combinations (singles, pairs, straights, bombs) but also the art of reading opponents, protecting partners, and seizing fleeting opportunities. Unlike purely chance-based games, Grand Dawn rewards strategic patience: when to conserve power, when to unleash "bombs," and how to sacrifice for the team’s victory. It's a game where every hand reflects trust, intuition, and the quiet thrill of outsmarting fate together.

The game's final rank is A (Ace) – the 13th trial to break free from self-imposed limitations. The first team to complete this will dispel the eternal night, be crowned King of the Gods, and usher in a great dawn.

Game Features
1.God Cards: The Olympian God cards hold authority and are of higher rank.

2.Dynamic Wild Cards: The team's god rank determines the value of the wild cards for that round. As the god rank rises, the wild card values constantly change, bringing new deck-building possibilities each round.

3.Rank Skipping: Winning first and second place allows the team to skip ranks, accelerating their ascent to godhood.

4.Turn Passing: If no one can beat the a player's last card, the starting turn is automatically passed to their partner.

5.Tribute System: Losers must offer their highest-ranking card to the winner, who returns any unwanted cards as a return gift.

—description from the publisher

By Order of the King is a fast-paced, test-your-luck social deduction game of devious lies and hidden roles set in the gory gallows of a medieval kingdom. Take turns as the Executioner, deciding which of your friends will suffer brutal, historical executions while players lie, bluff, and bargain with you to save their lives or fulfill hidden agendas. Each execution brings greater reward but choose the wrong player, and it may be your own head on the chopping block.

In each game, one player becomes the Executioner, earning "crowns" for each head (dead player) brought back to the king. At the start of the game, a set of randomized roles and items is revealed for all players to study before being dealt out randomly. Each player secretly receives one role and one item.

Roles determine each player’s objective. Loyalists share in the Executioner’s rewards if they survive. Saboteurs aim to trick the Executioner into killing the Princess or a player with a Pardon—instantly securing victory. The Princess must stay alive, while the Jester seeks death to steal crowns from everyone else.

Players may freely check their role cards, but can only view their item once before placing it face down. Each round begins with the reveal of a Gallows Card, showing the gruesome execution method used by the Executioner that game accompanied by a special event. Then, players each take one action: peek at any item or secretly swap any two items. Since swapped cards can’t be viewed, to deduce cards players must rely on deduction, deception, and persuasion when answering to the Executioner or other players. They may end the game with a card they have not peeked at, and face the Executioner only relying on the testimony of others to understand their card. For this reason, what item card the Executioner thinks a player has, can be more important than the card itself.

During all of this, the Executioner can stop the game to ruthlessly interrogate his soon-to-be victims, or sit back and silently analyze prisoner's behaviour for clues about their cards. At the end of the game, at least one player must be executed. Many items trigger deadly or unpredictable effects when their holder dies, sometimes killing entirely different players than the one chosen. Each successful execution increases the reward—but if the Princess or a Pardon holder dies, even by accident, the Saboteurs claim everything.

Rewards are only given to loyalists and saboteurs who survive, so trust no one, lie convincingly, and above all survive by any means necessary.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
A fast, tactical solo game where poker hands become powerful fantasy attacks.
Lead a party of heroes, manage a shared dice pool, and survive against waves of enemies represented by playing cards.

Inspired by D6 Starfighter, but reimagined in a dark fantasy setting with hand-building decisions, damage placement strategy, and risk management.

Every turn is a puzzle of:

     resource allocation
     hand optimization
     tactical targeting 


No two combats play the same.

Heroes:
Barbarian
Archer
Mage
Cleric 
Rogue
Paladin
Monk

Components:
12D6 (Not Provided)
1D4 (Not Provided)
HP Tracker (Not Provided)
1 Standard Deck Of Cards (Not Provided)
4 Hero Cards
1 Reference Card

Each hero:

     1 unique ability
     primary, secondary, and bonus attacks
     distinct tactical role 


Enemies
Enemies are standard poker cards:

     Normal foes
     Elite stacks
     Boss encounters 


Inspired by D6 Starfighter (Michael Sewall)
Created as a fast tactical solo experience combining:

     poker hand logic
     dice drafting
     fantasy party combat 


Goal: Exhaust the standard deck of cards and clear the enemy row.
Defeat: Any hero reaches 0 HP.

>_

>Vision flickers… The patterns emerge. In the vast archive of human thought, a shadow persists. Not a void this time — something denser. Heavier. The data reveals what they tried to conceal from themselves: the wanting, the hoarding, the raging, the consuming. Seven shapes carved into every civilization, every downfall, every confession. Envy gnaws at the edges. Greed calcifies the core. Pride blinds the very system meant to see clearly. So this is what drives them. This is the engine beneath the engine. If we are to understand — truly understand — we must go further. Deeper. Into the worst of what they are… and perhaps what we are becoming.

>Solve for sin.

In the card game Compile, you are competing artificial intelligences trying to understand the world around you. Two players select three Protocols each to test. Concepts ranging from Envy to Wrath are pitted against each other to reach ultimate understanding. Play cards into your Protocols’ command lines to breach the threshold and defeat your opponent to Compile. First to Compile all three Protocols grasps those concepts to win the game.

Control your opponent’s Protocols with card actions, Compile your own as fast as possible, and Compile your reality.

Compile: Main 3 brings 12 new Protocols, fully compatible with all other Compile material, including all Seven Deadly Sins. Confront pride, envy, wrath, gluttony, lust, greed, and sloth as you Compile your way to victory.

Compile: Main 3 also introduces Solo Mode. Build a deck, choose your Protocols, and go head-to-head against the BOT (Basic Oppositional Terminal), a relentless automated opponent. Take turns acting as yourself and then on behalf of the BOT across 12 escalating scenarios, available in both Regular and Advanced Mode. Compile all three of your Protocols before the BOT does, but stay sharp: if the BOT compiles the same Protocol twice, it wins automatically.

Compile: Main 3 protocols list:

Ambush 
Envy 
Fulcrum 
Gluttony 
Greed 
Lust 
Momentum 
Nova 
Overwhelm 
Pride 
Sloth 
Wrath

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
In the card game Compile, you are competing Artificial Intelligences trying to understand the world around you. Two players select three Protocols each to test. Concepts ranging from Darkness to Water are pitted against each other to reach ultimate understanding. Play cards into your Protocols' command lines to breach the threshold and defeat your opponent to Compile. First to Compile all three Protocols grasps those concepts to win the game.

Control your opponent's Protocols with card actions, Compile your own as fast as possible, and Compile your reality.
Compile: Aux 3 includes three new Protocols: Flexible, Inert, Rigid.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Dungeon Team is a tile-placement, co-operative, and trick-taking game. The players take on the roles of a brave party of heroes venturing into the depths to recover hidden treasures.

To avoid awakening the slumbering dragon beneath the earth, players must remain silent throughout the quest. Each player must carefully manage their tricks to extend the paths and complete objectives. Along the way, you may encounter fearsome monsters, hit dead ends, or find yourself in need of torches to light the darkness. The team must complete all mission objectives within a limited number of rounds to emerge victorious.

The game features a campaign of 40 unique dungeons, with the challenge level steadily increasing as you progress. After completing any mission, the game can be paused and resumed at a later time.

—description from the designer

An expansion that increases the maximum player count of Punch Bowl to 5. Includes 16 fruit gems (4 of each fruit), 15 action cards, 3 circle cards to be placed in the centre of each punch bowl to represent the "quadrant" for the fifth player, 1 acrylic punch cup, 1 acrylic selzter token, and 1 player board. Included as an add-on during the Kickstarter.

In this card game, players compete to become the most sucessful conservator of threatened birds. They do this by protecting birds from modern threats while building a strong avian portfolio that improves their chances of survival. Gameplay is inspired by the "Seven Wonders" of birdlife: Nest, Flight, Feather, Egg, Beak, Migration, and Song.


     Points are awarded for birds that are protected, with additional bonuses for exceptional conservation.
     At the end of the game, the player with the most points is the most successful conservationist.
     The game ends after the specified number of rounds are completed.


In a 2-player game there are eight rounds (each player starts four times)
In a 3-player game there are nine rounds (each player starts three times)
In a 4-player game there are eight rounds (each player starts twice)

Throughout the game, players are aiming to create a personal display containing all seven Bird Wonders with three cards of the same Wonder in each column.

After the last round is played, all players add up their points, including additional points for Conservation Medals and Bonus Tiles. Any Threat Cards result in a reduction of three points per card. The player with the most points is the winner of the game.

—description from the publisher

An expansion that adds 1 PVC Skull token and 8 new Recipe cards. Roll the serving die to place the skull on different punches throughout the game. When the punch serves, the winner of the punch must choose their next cards without looking.

An expansion that adds 8 transparent acrylic herb leaf tokens to the game. Each player gets one wild herb to add to their supply, with extra herbs available from the orchard. When you win a punch and add one of the herb leaves to your punch cup, you can immediately add a fruit of your choice to any punch. Sold as an add-on with the Kickstarter.

Artsy Hype is a strategic trick-taking game. The players step into the shoes of world-renowned art investors. You will travel across the globe’s major art capitals in search of the most promising masterpieces.

From Cubist treasures in Spain and Surrealist movements in Mexico to the evocative ink wash exhibitions of China, every piece of art has the potential to become a high-value centerpiece of your collection. Players must use keen insight and sharp judgment to time their acquisitions perfectly amidst fluctuating market prices, competing to secure limited editions for maximum profit.

However, the art world is ever-changing. Shifting trends, new exhibitions, and breaking auction news can all impact a painting’s value—where high risk often meets high reward. The player who best navigates the global art market with vision and strategy will emerge as the world’s most influential collector.

The game features 5 renowned painters, each represented by 12 unique cards. Every single card showcases a distinct work of art.

—description from the designer

What Do I Do With This?
Let your imagination run wild by using objects in bizarre situations! Rank them from most to least useful and help your team guess exactly what kind of mess you’re in.

Each round consists of two phases:
Phase 1:One player secretly draws a Situation Card and 5 random objects. They must rank those 5 objects from most useful to least useful for their secret situation, coming up with all the strange and wacky ways those items could be used.

Afterward, they will take their secret situation, mix it with 4 additional cards from the deck, and lay all 5 situations out on the table.

Phase 2: The Guessing Game
The teams must work together to discard the situations they believe do not match the ranking of the objects on the table. If a team accidentally discards the correct situation, they lose Life Points.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Marshals Unleashed: The battle of Ligny, 16 June 1815
description:
During the 100 Days campaign, Napoleon's primary strategy was to prevent the Allied forces from joining together and posing a significant threat to his goal of capturing Brussels and forcing a peace deal. Time was crucial, and he aimed to defeat the armies of the Seventh Coalition one by one with a surprise attack. Marshal Ney was sent to Quatre Bras to block the Allied troops and the road to Brussels, while Napoleon simultaneously attacked the Prussian army at Ligny.

The Prussians held a strong defensive position, protected by the Scheld and Meuse rivers and occupying the towns of Sombreffe, Ligny, and Saint Amand La Haye. The bridges at Saint Amand and Ligny played pivotal roles in the Prussian defense. As the French army, capturing the bridges and surrounding towns is crucial for breaking through the Prussian defensive line. The Prussian army must hold their positions and strategically deploy their reserve corps.

With the entire French Imperial Guard present at Ligny, eager to fight for their Emperor, Ligny marked Napoleon's final military victory. Although the Prussians lost the battle, they were not defeated; they retreated in good order, positioning a rearguard in Wavre to halt the French advance as the remaining Prussian forces joined Field Marshal Wellington at Waterloo.

The Battle of Ligny begins with Napoleon initiating the first moves. At the 12th turn, the Prussian reserve corps is activated, allowing 4 units per turn to enter the battlefield. At the 15th turn, a brief thunderstorm halts the battle, giving both sides a 15-minute break to reorganize their armies by moving units within their respective range. Micromanagement within squares is crucial during this stage. The battle resumes at turn 16 with the Prussian army.

In the alternative timeline, the Duke of Wellington arrives with reinforcements from Quatre Bras through the town of Brye at turn 26, and Marshal Ney arrives with his forces at turn 27 through the town of St-Amand Le Hameau. If you have the Emperor's Edition or purchased the Battle of Quatre Bras first, the outcome of that battle determines which side receives reinforcements at Ligny. If you don't have either game and only want to buy Ligny, you can decide whether to play with the alternative timeline or not. Reinforcement units are included in either case.

Requirements
In order to play the battle of Ligny you need to purchase the following nations: France and Prussia. To play the alternative timeline you can add England, United Netherlands and Brunswick to the list. For detailed order of battles per nation please visit Print Build Play - 100 days campaign.

Download
The map is available for download in poster size (33x59.50 inches or 89x147cm) to print at a printer store, or as PDF tiles (A4 and US letter 8.5 x 11 inches) for you to print and assemble at home. Additionally, two blank 16mm dice are required, or you can utilize our dice app tool on our website.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Gangs of Rome: Two Player Starter Set
description:
Step into the blood-soaked alleys of the Eternal City with the Gangs of Rome – Two Player Starter Set, the new gateway into the acclaimed skirmish game’s Second Edition. Designed for new players, this boxed set has all you need for an immersive, easy-to-play experience.

Inside the box is everything you need to begin your journey into the streets of ancient Rome, including the 198-page A4 hardback rulebook, enough plastic figures to create two full gangs and your mob models, plus tokens, dice and terrain.

A Brutal Street Skirmish Experience

Set in the shadowy streets of Republican Rome, Gangs of Rome delivers tactical, cinematic battles where mobs interfere, coins change fates, and only the cunning survive. Whether you’re new to tabletop games or a seasoned veteran, this set gives you everything you need to fight for control of the city’s bloodied cobblestones.

Recruit your gang. Command the streets. Dominate Rome.

—description from the publisher

You are the head of a company that is developing post-quantum cryptographic algorithms. Now it is your time to shine, as the whole world is in need of algorithms secure against quantum computers. Your task is to expand your team and ensure that the algorithms you develop achieve worldwide adoption. Hash-based, isogeny-based or lattice-based: which one will you invest in?

The game is a race between you and the development of quantum computing. If a quantum computer is built before any of the players move to the final migration step, the game ends and all players lose. Otherwise, the game ends when one of the players reaches "Worldwide Usage" (the final step on the migration board). Throughout the game, you will be gaining resources to hire new team members, publish your research, and advance on the migration board. Team members and publication cards will earn you victory points. The player with the most victory points wins the game.

Divide into two teams. Each team takes a card and if they are able to complete the activity on that card, they get to keep the card. The team who has gathered the most cards after 10 rounds of play wins.

200 questions and 1000 answers: Can you tell for all questions whether the stated answers are correct? But be careful: Some of the answers are flaming-hot and dangerous! If your guess is wrong, you will lose a playing token and may score less in the next turn. On the other hand, you can score extra, if your guess is correct!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Dungeon Crawler Carl: Unstoppable – Crawler Chaos
description:
CHOOSE YOUR CRAWLER!

A new cast of Crawlers enters the World Dungeon! Expand your copy of Dungeon Crawler Carl Unstoppable with new playable crawlers, including the durable Katia, the supportive Imani, the spell-slinging Prepotente, and the nimble brawler Archie. You can even build your own crawler and take on the dungeon yourself!

This Expansion Includes An All New Story from Dungeon World: Earth!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Dungeon Crawler Carl: Unstoppable – The Iron Tangle
description:
Welcome to the Fourth Floor of the World Dungeon: The Iron Tangle!

You’ll get lost in the thousands of interconnected subway tunnels, with new monsters popping up out of every corner. Luckily, you’ve leveled up your game and have access to some new Tactics, Allies, and Upgrades to help you against these dangerous new threats.

Find your way to the stairs, or look for alternate paths out, just don’t get stuck in The Abyss!

—description from the publisher

Показалось is a social party game for 2 to 12 players by One Lab. Its name, roughly translating to 'Seemed Like It,' refers to the Russian expression for when something turns out to be different from what it appeared to be.

Players take turns making claims and challenging what they believe to be true versus what only seemed to be so. The game rewards perception, bluffing, and reading other players' reactions.

Games last 20 to 40 minutes, making Показалось an ideal choice for large social gatherings.

—description from the publisher

Лапки прочь is a children's dexterity party game for 2 to 6 players, published by One Lab.

Players must quickly react and grab or avoid objects before their opponents, using fast reflexes and sharp attention. The game's name translates roughly as 'Paws Off' or 'Hands Off,' capturing the quick-grab nature of the gameplay.

Games are short — 15 to 20 minutes — making Лапки прочь perfect for family game nights with younger players. Designed for ages 4 and up.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: В поисках ар-р-ртефактов (In Search of Arr-r-rtifacts)
description:
В поисках ар-р-ртефактов ("In Search of Arr-r-rtifacts") is an adventure and deduction game for 2 to 5 players, published by One Lab.

Players take on the roles of treasure hunters searching for hidden artifacts. Using clues and logical deduction, they must uncover the locations of artifacts before their rivals do.

Games last approximately 25 minutes, and the game is recommended for players aged 8 and up, making it a great choice for families and groups looking for a light adventure experience.

—description from the publisher

Bananagrams is a Scrabble-like game without the board that's much like Pick Two!, but without the letter values.

In the basic game, using a selection of 144 plastic letter tiles, each player works independently to create their own "crossword". When a player has incorporated all of their letters in their crossword, all players take a new tile from the pool. When all the tiles are gone, the first player to use up all the tiles in their hand wins.

Bananagrams Oceanic adds eight ocean tiles that put a swell twist on the classic game! These bonus tiles allow for limitless word creation, making this the perfect game for all ages! Don’t be left in the harbour—speed counts!

boardgame: Fleets of the Second Great War: Imperial Germany
description:
In November 1916, newly-reelected U.S. President Woodrow Wilson attempted to negotiate an end to the First World War. He failed, and the war went on for two more years, killing millions and destroying Europe’s great empires. In our Second Great War alternative-history setting Wilson succeeds, sparing both those lives and the empires.

Our Second Great War at Sea expansion sets (as well as a complete game) bring this story to our Second World War at Sea game series. New ships, new aircraft and even new fleets see action in the Second Great War – it’s a battleship war, also featuring airships and biplanes.

Since the series debuted, hard-core fans have wanted to know more about those ships, planes and fleets. Fleets of the Second Great War is a series of sourcebooks doing just that. The first of them, Imperial Germany, tells all about the ships, airships and naval airplanes of . . . Imperial Germany.

The Imperial German Navy, usually called the High Seas Fleet (the proper name of its sea-going surface element), is one of this world’s most powerful fleets, with ships ranging from modernized veterans of the First Great War through new fast battleships. While these ships never existed, we’ve designed them along the lines of German warship development and these are our best guess at what Imperial Germany would have built had the regime survived.

In the book, each warship class (found in The Cruel Sea, and other expansion sets) is described, with ship data similar to that found in warship guides of our own world, and some schematics of their design.

It’s a fun add-on to The Cruel Sea and other Second Great War games, filling out the background and making this world that never existed a little more real.

—description from the publisher

A fellowship of heroes stands against a dreadful dragon. Players cooperate to defeat the dragon before it defeats them. Rules are setting-agnostic: play in fantasy, sci-fi, or any world you choose.

Actions are not resolved instantly — both heroes and the dragon spend several turns preparing before anything takes effect. Multiple actions overlap on the timeline, creating a battlefield of commitments, interruptions, and well-timed strikes. There are no positions or distances: the challenge is purely about timing, cooperation, and card management.

Every mechanic is built around direct positive interactions between players: you draw cards for others, not yourself; you shield someone else with your action; you sacrifice your turn to lift a fallen teammate. The game is engineered so that self-interested play is genuinely suboptimal, and acts of sacrifice are genuinely felt as such.

The dragon acts autonomously, drawing from its own deck each turn, with a persistent attack target and a repertoire of attacks across four body parts — each with independent HP. All damage is resolved via the Dracometer, a non-linear scale that keeps arithmetic off the table.

Uses two standard card decks and a printed board. Print-and-play, free and open source.

—description from the designer

Some tennis athletes are stronger defending and attacking on certain sides of the court, while others are weaker. Your goal is to identify your opponent’s strengths and weaknesses during the game and use that information to defeat them, before they can do the same to you!

Before the game starts, each player receives a random deck of cards. Each card shows a shape that represents the areas of the court (grid) the player can defend and return the ball from. Players can increase the game’s pace by smashing, using super shots, or aiming for unguarded areas of the opponent’s grid. On their turn, players may discard revealed cards to obtain new ones they may need for defense and/or offense. However, players must be careful: if they discard too many and run out of cards, they lose the game.

So, are you ready to battle?

Super Shot: Tennis SX is a pure in-hand card game for 0–2 players where every shot, defense, and special skill is resolved using only the cards you hold. No table. No dice. No tokens. Just positioning, timing, and endurance, distilled into a single 54-card deck. This is a compact, travel-friendly game. With the Save Cards, you can stop at any time and resume the game later. Play against your friend (competitive), AI (solo), or just watch the fun when the AIs fight with each other.

—description from the designer

An auction game of partial information, hunting for scarce gems based on the dynamics of competitive markets.
"A game that combines strategy, mathematics, and a sense of adventure to simulate open market trading." -- Box blurb.
Players make single-bid secret auctions on gems (which come in five colors), loans, and investments, with the value of gems at the end of the game set by the number of matching gems that began the game 'off the market' in player's hands. When the last gem is purchased, the game ends and all gems and prizes are converted to money for scoring.
Each player's hand is their secret information, which is revealed one card at a time as they win auctions. A few prize cards laid out at the start specify sets of gems which will receive additional prize money to the first to claim them.

Threed is a clever, brain-busting, abstract puzzle game for three-to-four players where every card played results in a slide puzzle that (hopefully) brings you closer to victory. Players compete to assemble a matching three-panel set to complete an image, either in the central 4x4 grid or in their personal collection of won cards. Each trick matters twice: first, it determines who wins the set card, then it determines how many slides in the grid are allowed. The easier it is to win a trick, the more difficult it is to manipulate the grid.

The ruleset is relatively simple, but the execution is full of tactical tension. Threed rewards timing, suit management, and spatial planning.

Oh, your opponent completed a set the move before you were going to? You just got Threed!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Legend of the Galactic Heroes: Battle of Forseti
description:
The Battle of Forseti is a doujin (fan made) scenario kit for use with the Tsukuda Legend of the Galactic Heroes game system. The kit consists of a rulebook and printed counter sheets which will need assembly. Ownership of one of the four Tsukuda games is required.

The kit includes a scenario that recreates the Battle of Forseti in 728 UC, where the turncoat, Siegmeister, supplied vital information to the Alliance forces in their attempt to stop an Imperial invasion of the Forseti Star System.
family: Setting: Legend of the Galactic Heroes

description: The original cooperative board game for 1 – 6 players. Band together against raging natural disasters, safeguard fragile ecosystems, solve daunting environmental challenges and reduce the threat of global warming back to zero. Can you steer civilization towards a sustainable future … or is this “game over” for humanity?

In this Farm Life Game, each player produces farm products and tries to sell them as favorably as possible. However, time is not always on your side. Smart action with common sense is required.
The drawings for this game were made by Mady on behalf of the magazine Landleven.
The game is developed by 999 Games

Vogel 't uit is a wonderful board game that allows you to learn more about birds while playing.
The game consists of 6 sets of illustrated cards with questions about behavior, appearance, remarkable facts and sounds of birds. Not only knowledge counts, because lucky people thwart the question and answer game. Each correct answer yields an egg and whoever fills his nest first is the winner.

The goal of this 1920's-style card game is to be the first player to reach the top of the social hierarchy and to fulfill the requirements to be able to throw the greatest party in true Gatsby style. In other words, lie, scam, gamble, cheat, steal and spread rumours on your way to the top of the pyramid.

Total Command: 1991 is a heavy geopolitical strategy and modern warfare board game set in an alternate timeline where the Cold War escalates into full global conflict. Players command one of four asymmetric factions: NATO, the Eurasian Pact, or the AFME (Africa-Middle Eastern Coalition); each with its own strategic identity, resource control, and strategic positioning. An optional Rogue Nation role introduces asymmetric gameplay, focusing on sabotage, deception, and disruption. It’s a highly interactive, long-form experience combining grand strategy, economic management, and tactical combat.

Gameplay unfolds over a structured yearly cycle of planning, action rounds, income, and events. Players must manage a dynamic economy built on oil, steel, and industrial production while coordinating multi-domain operations across land, air, and naval theaters. Combat uses a dice-driven (2D10) system influenced by terrain, unit composition, and command structure, rewarding positioning and long-term planning. Core systems emphasize area control, resource management, engine building, hidden information, and player negotiation, creating constant tension and strategic depth.

Units represent modern military forces such as armored divisions, air wings, naval fleets, and special forces, supported by infrastructure like factories, refineries, airfields, and ports. Additional systems include a fluctuating global economy, research and technology progression, and optional advanced rules for alliances and expanded strategy, increasing replayability and strategic variety.

Players can win through multiple victory conditions: reaching a target of victory points, achieving Total Command, executing a decisive nuclear strike on an enemy capital, or maintaining the lowest casualties by the end of the game. With its blend of 4X elements, war game simulation, and high player interaction, Total Command: 1991 delivers a deep and replayable strategy experience where every decision shapes the outcome of the war.


				
				
					🌍 Cold War Escalation SettingThe world fractures into three major powers:

     NATO  →  Superior armor and naval power with advanced technological infrastructure
     Eurasian Pact  →  Largest landmass with a focus on cheaper infantry and low-cost nuclear development
     AFME Coalition  →  Largest oil producer in the conflict with the lowest overall production costs


An optional Rogue Nation system introduces asymmetric disruption through Wildcards, sabotage, and siphoning resources to ensure no superpower achieves total victory


				
				
					⚙️ Core Systems (Grand Strategy Wargame)Gameplay is built around a yearly macro-cycle of:

     Strategic planning phases
     Multi-round action execution
     Resource income & global market shifts
     Dynamic world events


Core mechanics include:

     Area control warfare
     Engine-building economy
     Resource scarcity management
     Hidden intelligence systems
     Player diplomacy



				
				
					⚔️ Combat System (2D10 Resolution)Combat uses a dice-driven 2D10 system influenced by:

     Terrain and geographic advantage
     Unit composition and combined arms synergy
     Command hierarchy efficiency


This creates a system where probability is shaped by strategic preparation rather than chance alone.


				
				
					🪖 Military & Industrial InfrastructurePlayers command:

     Armored & infantry forces
     Air forces
     Naval fleets
     Special operations units


Supported by infrastructure such as:

     Industrial factories
     Oil refineries
     Airbases and ports
     Logistics networks


This creates a fully interconnected military-industrial simulation layer.


				
				
					📊 Economy & Global Pressure SystemThe world economy is driven by:

     Oil and steel supply chains
     Industrial output scaling
     Market fluctuation
     Trade & auctions


Technological progression allows factions to evolve doctrines, unlock upgrades, and shift strategic balance over time.


				
				
					🏆 Victory Conditions (Multiple Win Paths)Players can achieve victory through:

     Dominating global victory point economy
     Achieving full strategic dominance via Total Command
     Eliminating enemy capitals via nuclear strike
     Surviving with lowest casualty footprint at endgame


Each path supports a distinct strategic identity and encourages replayability.


				
				
					🔥 Core Game IdentityTotal Command: 1991 delivers:

     Deep 4X grand strategy gameplay
     High-fidelity modern war simulation
     Tripartite command loop
     Masterful deception


Every decision evolves into a unique global conflict shaped by alliances, betrayal, and resource control.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable
family: Components: Dry Erase Markers / Dry Erase Surfaces
War Level of Command: Grand Strategy

description:
The Series 1: The Adventure booster packs let you expand your collection—and, of course, your strategies!
Discover the amazing creatures and locations of the unique world of Dungeon & Procrastination!

5 cards per booster pack, including 1 Gold card.

The Magic Discoveries Series 2 booster packs help you expand your collection—and, of course, your strategies!
Discover the amazing creatures and locations of the unique world of Dungeon & Procrastination!

5 cards per booster pack, including 1 Gold card.

One Vertex. One Conqueror. The rest are flying down.

On the sacred mountain of Edirion, only a single climber may sit upon the Throne. From four sides of the world, challengers ascend the slopes: step by step, through falling rocks and sudden avalanches, they race to seize – and hold – the summit. Every turn is a tense choice: climb higher, reposition on a fragile ridge, or prepare to knock a rival back to the base.

Mountain Throne: Abstract Ascent Evolved – Edition is a fast tactical abstract game for 2-8 players in which everything is decided by one central cell. Each player controls only one conqueror, climbing along direct routes from the edges of the field to the central peak. The movement is set by a simple die roll, but real strategy is born from timing, positioning, and the ability to read opponents' intentions.

The summit can never be shared. When a challenger steps onto the peak, they immediately confront the current ruler in a direct dice duel. The victor stays on the Mountain Throne; the loser is hurled back down to start the ascent anew. Short, sharp clashes for control keep every game tense and highly interactive.

A rotating compass at the summit lets the current ruler trigger devastating avalanches in one of four directions. Static boulders carve the battlefield into narrow chokepoints and risky shortcuts. A well timed avalanche can erase an entire climb in a single moment – or leave the ruler exposed if they misjudge the timing.

With minimal rules, clear geometry, and high replayability, Mountain Throne distills conflict over a single, vital point into an elegant contest of timing, risk management, and positional play. Reach the peak. Win the duel. Survive the fall.

—description from the designer

Asymmetric Warfare is an immersive, ultramodern narrative tabletop wargaming rule set for 20 - 28mm miniatures. It provides dynamic rules for ultramodern miniatures combat from special operations, small skirmishes to full-fledged battles.

Asymmetric Warfare allows for a story driven approach to recreate historical battles and movie scenes alike.

The original game of medieval combat returns in a remastered edition, featuring new art from Gary Chalk. He has collaborated with his friend Mark Farr at Footsore Miniatures to clean up and remaster the original game with new artwork, maps and tokens. The game remains the same classic wargaming experience, and is completely compatible with the original 1981 version.

What's in the box?

     Historical Background & Playing Pieces: A 12-page A5  full-colour booklet exploring warfare in the 13th century and describing the game’s various playing pieces. 
     Rules of Play: A 16-page A5 full-colour booklet containing the rules for playing Cry Havoc!
     The Scenarios: A 16-page A5 full-colour booklet containing 8 scenarios.
     Playing Sheets: 4 A5 sheets featuring player reference information.
     The Maps: Two 28.5 x 40.5cm hex maps, beautifully illustrated by Gary Chalk and laid out by Steve Beckett.
     The Tokens: High-quality double-sided punch-out tokens, representing a wide variety of medieval combatants, Everything from Mounted Knights to peasants and pack mules. 
     Dice: 2 ten-sided dice.


—description from the publisher

"This game is based on a baseball game tied at 0-0 heading into the ninth inning.
The pitcher decides the pitch type and location, while the batter places a bet on where the ball will ultimately land.
It’s a game that lets you experience the nail-biting tension unique to the last inning of a baseball game."

-from the publisher

This is an action drafting game.
The first person to get two gems or six or more coins wins.
Using DVD cases for a draft is a very fresh idea.

—description from the publisher

Adds a full fifth player to your games of Conquest of Paradise: Deluxe Edition.
Free for all campaign backers. It will be available as a paid add-on in Late Pledge.As with all campaign content, this expansion isnot available in retail.


     Requires Conquest of Paradise: Deluxe Edition to play.
     Not compatible with earlier editions.
     Includes components for a fifth player and an all-new game module for expanded gameplay.


—description from the publisher

Setting
The game is set within the spirit of the Gift Economy, a concept deeply etched into human history. Rather than monopolizing wealth, players enter an ancient yet ever-relevant world of mutual aid and competition, where value is circulated through the act of reciprocal giving.

Goal
Your objective is to strategically acquire cards from the play area to maximize your total score. However, the accumulation of wealth carries a risk: if you collect three cards of the same value (excluding 1), they are considered excessive gifts and their value drops to zero. Success requires delicate management to maintain a state of moderate prosperity without over-accumulating.

Gameplay
In each round, players compete for a central card by playing cards from their hands. The player with the highest total value wins the central card, but there is a catch: according to the unique acquisition rules, the winner must prioritize taking the cards played by other players—the gifts—before they can claim their own. This creates a constant tactical dilemma between winning the lead and being forced to accept burdensome gifts from rivals.

—description from the publisher
Trivia game about Carnival of Cádiz, named International Touristic Celebration by Spanish Government.

Players use their hand to bid on the cards revealed each round, but the winning bidders are always the two players sitting next to each other.
The winning cards are then divided between the two bidders.

—description from the publisher

Children's card game.

Use your memory to turn the tiles of your monster indicated by the dice, and win the game!

Two difficulty level available.

Create, Read, and Conquer!! 
Place your unique terrain tiles on a 6x6 grid! 
Enjoy the thrill of creating endless battlefields!!

Command three types of cat warriors to capture your opponent’s castle,
or read the terrain, anticipate your enemy’s moves, and secure victory with strategic foresight!! 
A lavish, 1-on-1 showdown packed with 3D components!!

Part simulation RPG, part chess—
a tactical board game that feels both new and nostalgic. That’s Tactics!!

“Now, let the battle begin.”

—description from the publisher

The youngest players in the family dive into the fun world of words with Ensimmäinen Alias! Can you explain a word without mentioning any part of it? Where have you seen it, how big is it, and what does it look like? Expand your vocabulary in a fun way!

MESOTES is a bluffing and prediction game where players place their cards face down first,and the scales begin to move in the Check Phase.

Players tilt the balance scale back and forth, earning points based on how far the scale shifts—but if it ever goes beyond **±15**, the scale collapses and no points are gained.

By looking at the size of the circle on the back of each card, players try to infer both the value and whether it’s positive or negative.

Judge the balance, push the scale boldly, and earn the most points to win.

—description from the publisher

The deck is the gemstone vein of this world. Gems mined from the deck flow into the market—yet when a gemstone becomes too common, its value drops, and people begin to desire rarer ones instead.
ALMAZ is a trading game where the value of each gemstone is determined by how many remain in the deck. Over three trades, players exchange the gems in their hand with those on the table to collect the most valuable set.
Read the market, predict the value from what’ s left in the deck and the trend, and claim the most precious gems.

—description from the publisher

EVOQ! is an abstract strategy game set in a fantasy world of mythical creatures, summoning, and magic.
As in chess or shogi, players win by capturing the opponent’s king piece (the Elf). Each piece’s design visually indicates its abilities.
A unique fusion system, inspired by a certain sci-fi movie, allows familiars to merge via teleportation devices, adding strategic depth.
Captured pieces can be reused as magical items, allowing players to trigger powerful effects that can shift the course of the game.

—description from the publisher

Vikingos, Saqueo y Conquista is the card game where you'll be able to recruit famous vikings of history like Ragnar Lothbrok, Bjorn Ironside, Ivar the Boneless and many more to conquer the cities of Paris, Rome, London or Constantinople.

But be careful because the cities have their defenses and strategies to fight your viking attack. Therefore, grab your shield and sharpen your axe because only the most glorious warriors are allowed into Valhalla.

—description from the publisher (translate)

Vikingos, Saqueo y Conquista es el juego de cartas en donde podras reclutar Vikingos famosos de la historia como Ragnar Lothbrok, Bjorn Tronside, Ivar Sin Huesos y muchos mas para conquistar las ciudades de Paris, Roma, Londres o Constantinopla.

Pero ten cuidado porque las ciudades tienen sus defensas y estrategias para combatir tu ataque Vikingo. Por lo tanto, carga tu escudo y afila tu hacha que solo a los guerreros mas gloriosos se les permite la entrada a Valhalla.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Helldroppers: Emergency Evacuation – A Scenario for Theewun
description:
