Alien robots have invaded a planet and a group of troopers are sent in to find some trapped VIP's and ensure they board a transport off the planet.

Inspired by Helldivers 2 using Theewun rules this scenario takes the player(s) through four stages of the mission: Landing, Linking the Signal, Escorting the civilians, and finally evacuating the troopers themselves.

Each section the difficulty increases with the number and strength of the enemy constantly increasing.

Rules included for gearing up your troopers with weapons, armor, and stratagems.

To add another layer of tension Stratagems are no longer called down automatically, players must roll the correct sequence to get the stratagem.

A copy of Theewun (free version or paid) is required to play this game.

These rules are designed for 28mm scale miniature battles of a squad level size.

They were meant to be played solo, cooperatively, or with a GM and have a strong story-based influence rather than the typical two forces meeting in the middle and fighting to the last unit standing typical to miniature gaming.  
The enemy units’ actions follow a set of predetermined actions based on the current situation.  They were also created to be usable in any genre, be simple enough for new players to pick up quickly, and most of all allow everyone playing to have fun and enjoy the game.

These rules set up the basics of gameplay and give some direction into setting up a game in whatever genre you wish to play in.  
Everything you need is here (well except for miniatures, dice, and terrain) to set up and start playing with minimal tweaking to make things work.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Goblin Raid: A fantasy themed scenario for Theewun Rules
description:
The daily life of the small village is shattered with screams as the goblins appeared.  
Most of the villagers managed to get to the keep for safety, but some are missing.  
A group of young heroes volunteer to search the town for the missing townsfolk even as the last few defenders begin to be overwhelmed by the growing number of goblins and wolves.

—description from the publisher

Quix is a drawless connection game for two players: Black and White. It is played on the intersections (points) of an initially empty square grid (board). The top and bottom edges of the board are colored black; the left and right edges are colored white. Some definitions:


     A naked diagonal is a pair of like-colored, diagonally adjacent stones with no other like-colored stone adjacent to both.
     A crosscut is a 2×2 area with two interlocking naked diagonals of opposite colors.
     A switch is a 2×3 area with four stones alternating in color in the corners and two empty points in the middle.
     A pinwheel is a 2×2 empty area orthogonally adjacent to eight stones alternating in color.


Black plays first, then turns alternate. On your turn, place a stone of your color on an empty point. Your placement must not create a crosscut, and it may only create a naked diagonal if it occupies an empty point in a switch or a pinwheel. Passing is not allowed.

You win if there is a chain of orthogonally or diagonally connected stones of your color touching the two opposite board edges of your color.

To make the game fair, White will have the option, on their first turn only, to swap sides with Black instead of making a regular move.

—description from the designer

Sharenanigans is a fast-paced board game that simulates the ups and downs of the share market in a fun and accessible way. Players take on the role of investors, buying and selling shares across different industries while reacting to changing market conditions.

Each turn, players make decisions about when to invest, when to cash out, and how to respond to market shifts. Special cards introduce unexpected events, allowing players to influence prices, disrupt opponents, and create moments of chaos around the table. Timing and strategy are key, but no lead is ever completely safe.

The game is designed to be easy to learn while offering meaningful choices and strong player interaction. As the market moves and players adapt, every session plays out differently.

The goal is to finish the game with the highest total value by building a strong portfolio and making smart decisions throughout the game.

—description from the publisher

Koorasu Tokasu is an abstract territory-control game set in the Antarctic Ocean.

Players overlap cards to expand pack ice and sea areas, creating habitats for penguins and orcas. By changing the shape and size of connected regions, players can place more of their animal pieces onto the board while limiting their opponent’s options.

The game also features the Polar Shifter, a special device that can rotate or slide cards already in play, dramatically changing the map and opening new tactical opportunities.

The first player to place all of their animal pieces wins. If the deck and both players’ hands run out, the player with the most animal pieces on the board wins.

—description from the publisher

Eliria is a dark fantasy trading card game created in Monterrey, México, by Javier Náñez Pro. Set in a world of magic, strategy, and shadow, the game immerses players in a universe where powerful warriors, mystical beings, spirits, beasts, and reanimated creatures clash in battle. The lore draws from a rich original fantasy setting, giving each card a distinct identity within a broader, evolving world.

Each player builds a deck with no duplicate card names and brings it to the table alongside three 12-sided dice, which serve as counters. Players place up to three combat cards on their side of the board across three aligned spaces, and fill the lower row with summoning and reaction cards. Each combat card has a Potential stat (its maximum power capacity), a Resistance threshold, and one or more attacks that require a minimum accumulated power to use. Summoning cards come in three subtypes — Ephemeral (single-turn effects), Adhesion (linked to an active combatant), and Field (affecting both players indefinitely) — while Reaction cards are played face-down and can only trigger during the opponent's turn when their activation condition is met.

Victory is achieved by destroying all of the opponent's combat cards on the board, or by forcing them to draw from an empty deck. A player also loses if they enter the Power Phase with no combat cards in play. Each turn flows through four phases: Initial (discard reaction cards, draw one), Movement (activate, play, or reposition cards), Power (all combatants gain one power point up to their Potential), and Attack (declare one attack per combatant, only against the directly aligned opponent card). The alignment rule and the three-card rule create a positional puzzle that shapes every decision on the board.

What sets Eliria apart is its emphasis on reactive, adaptive play. Since no two decks can share duplicate card names, every deck is inherently unique. The interplay between face-down bluffing, reaction traps, and power accumulation means that no single strategy dominates — players must read their opponent and adapt in real time to come out on top.

—description from the designer

Face Off is a Print & Play board game that lets you step into the shoes of legendary historical figures and race through time to prove your mastery of the past!

Travel through different eras, answering era-specific questions as you advance along the path.

Your identity is a secret: others must guess who you are based on your actions and answers. If they guess correctly, you’ll lose valuable ground and be sent back in time.

The first player to reach the end and answer the final question  is crowned the victor. Face off against your rivals and see if you have what it takes to navigate time and secure your place in history!

—description from the publisher

The Last Tribe: A Tactical Arena Card Game

The Last Tribe is an intense tactical card battler for 2 players that bridges the gap between traditional CCG mechanics and dynamic 2D arena positioning. It’s not just about the power of the cards in your hand; it’s about where you stand on the battlefield and how you manipulate the flow of time.

Key Features & Mechanics:
• • LIFO Reaction System (The Art of Countering): Unlike standard card games where effects trigger in the order played, The Last Tribe utilizes a Last-In-First-Out (LIFO) system. When your opponent strikes, the window of opportunity opens. By playing a reaction, your defensive maneuver or dodge resolves before the incoming attack. This creates high-stakes mind games: Can you step out of range before the blade falls? Can you land a fatal counter-blow while the enemy is mid-swing?

• • Spatial Strategy (Manhattan Distance Grid): The arena is a living weapon. Movement is governed by Manhattan distance, making positioning the ultimate resource.
Strategic Beacons (Flags): Control vital points on the map to expand your hand limit and augment your ability Range.
Positional Dominance: Every attack has a precise reach. A single step to the side can turn a devastating strike into a wasted card.

• • Action Economy & Color-Coded Synergy: There is no traditional "mana" pool. Every move costs an Action Point, forcing players into a tense balance of aggression versus preservation. 
Do you exhaust your actions to pressure the enemy, or hold back to react during their turn?
Red Cards (Offense): Brutal strikes and summoning loyal units to the frontline.
Blue Cards (Control): Spells that warp the battlefield with status effects like Slow, Root, or Disarm.
Yellow Cards (Agility): Dynamic movement and evasive dodges.
Black Cards (Augments): Free-action modifiers that enhance damage or range without depleting your turn's momentum.

• • Asymmetric Warfare: Take command of unique Heroes—the last bastions of their tribes—each with their own vitality and reactive instincts. Deploy units and guardians to block paths, protect your Hero, and corner the opposition.

The Last Tribe is a game of perfect information and lethal timing. When the Hero’s health hits zero, the tribe falls. Will you be the one left standing?

—description from the designer

boardgame: Dragon and Republic: A naval campaign system and fast-play rules for the Sino French War, 1884-85
description:
A campaign system and tactical rules for pre-dreadnought naval warfare.

A mini campaign covering the Sino French War , 1884 to 1885.

Also included is a brief history of the naval campaign, a series of standalone wargame scenarios, and the Broadside and Salvo fast play pre-dreadnought rules and a complete set of ship data for the rules allowing the campaign to be fought out over a weekend or a few club nights.

Broadside and Salvo is a set of fast play naval rules for the pre-dreadnought era that allow large actions to be fought out in just a couple of hours.

—description from the designer

Season Ticket Football is a card & dice simulation from the creator of Football Mogul & Season Ticket Baseball. Years of effort went into making a game that is both fast and fun while delivering accurate play-by-play results and statistics.

Gameplay: Most plays are resolved with a single roll.


     Pick an Offensive and Defensive Play (or roll on the Play Selector to let the game choose)
     Roll the dice to find the key player and rating for this play.
     Check the blue die against the specified Player Rating (e.g. "RB1 Rush") to get the result.


Each team card includes all the necessary player ratings. There's no need to buy or print hundreds of cards just to play a game of football.

—description from the designer

Face Off is a Print & Play board game that lets you step into the shoes of legendary historical figures and race through time to prove your mastery of the past!

Travel through different eras, answering era-specific questions as you advance along the path.

Your identity is a secret: others must guess who you are based on your actions and answers. If they guess correctly, you’ll lose valuable ground and be sent back in time.

The first player to reach the end and answer the final question  is crowned the victor. Face off against your rivals and see if you have what it takes to navigate time and secure your place in history!

—description from the designer

The year is 1345. You and three other peasants are in a struggle for safety in the trying times. Four old friends offer gestures of favor, intricate and purposeful and unique. Whoever can best utilize these favors will shore up their keep, safe and sound for the days to come...

Keeps is an elegant 36-card microstrategy game that takes place over any number of 15-25 minute rounds, where two to four players maneuver to have the most coinage at the end of the round.

Each player has a draw pile which hides either one of three characters (the Count, the Fool, and the Friar) or an even mix of them (the Mask). The piles move during the game, so players should track and anticipate which cards might be where. As no two cards are the same, the best players will grow to know each card and when it might be more or less useful.

"Keep" cards can be played in front of any player for extra effects, such as the ability to draw from any pile, or to take an extra action at the end of the round. Some Keep cards are worth coins, and the player with the most coins in their keep when the round ends gets a Victory Mark. First to three Marks wins the game!

—description from the designer

Welcome to the most chaotic worksite in town!

That Brick Game is a fast, physical stacking game where the dice calls the shots and you do the heavy lifting.

Stack bricks, avoid hazards, and sabotage other Builders to make sure you’re left with the smallest pile of rubble when the final brick is laid.

"1. What is the setting?
The game takes place around a table where players draw on paper and use theme cards to create monsters together. No electricity or devices are needed.

2. What is the goal?
The goal is to create unique monsters by connecting theme-based ideas, and then guess which player is linked to which monster through sounds, names, or clues.

3. How do you play?
Players draw monster parts based on theme cards, pass their papers each round, and complete a full creature after five turns. Then, depending on the mode, players guess the monster assignments using sounds, names, or hints."

"VECT-REVERSE is a two-player abstract strategy game built on the disc-flipping rules of Reversi — enhanced with disc movement and chain mechanics.
After placing a disc and outflanking opponent's pieces, you choose one outflanked disc, move it to an adjacent square, and flip it to your color. This can trigger a chain reaction, creating dramatic multi-step combos.
Victory goes not to the player with the most discs, but to the first player to control 4 Bases — the four corners and the center cluster.
Easy to learn. Deep to master."

-from the publisher

boardgame: 7th Sea: City of Five Sails – Blood and Salt
description:
Bitter rivalries and twisted schemes threaten to tear the City of Five Sails asunder.  Each hero faces a choice: Seek blood for blood, or restore the City to order?  Each villain must decide: seek alliances and consolidate their winnings, or rub salt in the wounds of their foes to prove their dominance.

This 149 card expansion for 7th Sea: City of Five Sails introduces new characters, schemes and other cards themed around academic and mercantile order, or dangerous new heights of violence.  This box also contains City Under Siege, a solo play expansion for you to explore and enjoy between harrowing duels and sneaky stratagems.

—description from the back of the box
family: Versions & Editions: Board Game Versions of Role-Playing Games

description:
Monster Nuki is an Old Maid-style game with the theme of capturing escaped monster plushies.
Retrieve an opponent's card, use the monster's ability, and collect more plushies than other players!

This is Monster Nuki, a special containment facility that transforms dangerous monsters from all over the world into cute felt plushies and accommodates them!
At first glance, it looks like just a cozy toy room, but do not be deceived. Inside these fluffy plushies, terrifying monsters are breathing.
Recently, an emergency situation occurred where plushies that were being quarantined in the facility escaped. You must become the manager of the facility, correctly match the pairs of the escaped plushies, and safely capture the plushies.
However, be careful of the 'Great Old Plushie (Icon)', which is a dangerous Class-1 entity. If you capture this plushie, the plushie leaves a 'Dangerous Mark (Icon)' on you.
It carries a strong penalty, but if you collect a lot, you might wake up to a new power...
Understand the plushies that the opponent has well, capture many plushies, and become the best manager of the [Monster Nuki] facility!

Axia: Slayer Combo is a tactical card-placement and dice-management game where you take on the role of a Slayer, a legendary hunter tasked with restoring peace to the lands of Axia.

After the fall of the walls of Alakat, fearsome beasts and acolytes of ancient lineages roam freely across unknown regions. Only your strategic skill can stop them.

Over the course of 8 rounds, each Slayer builds a 3x3 territory of cards. On each turn, you will select dice from a shared pool, both physical and magical, to wound or defeat the creatures inhabiting your map.

The core of the game lies in its combo system: defeating certain beasts triggers immediate effects, allowing you to perform additional attacks, gain tactical tools, or chain multiple actions within a single turn.

Your score depends on your ability to activate objective cards placed along the edges of your territory. You will only earn points by completing entire rows or columns, which can be combined with points from defeated beasts and unused tools.

Will you rise as a legendary Slayer, or remain a mere apprentice in the chronicles of Axia?

—description from the publisher

Ice Scream is a hidden-role game in which players are seagulls trying to blend into the crowd by repeating different sounds at the volume indicated by a die. Their goal is to identify the Seagull Leader, but they must also watch out for the Ice Cream Seller, who hides among them and tries to discover the leader first.

Only the Seagull Leader can secure victory for all the seagulls by finding the Ice Cream Seller.

The game is a free Print and play provided by Zacatrus via Zacanews.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Four Crossings of the Chishui River: victory through quagmire
description:
Four Crossings of the Chishui River: victory through quagmire simulates the most critical turning point of the Red Army's strategic transfer (the Long March) in the first half of 1935—the battles of the Four Crossings of the Chishui River and the Crossing of the Jinsha River. Historically, through proactive mobile warfare, the Red Army maneuvered amidst 400,000 enemy troops, annihilated a total of enemies equivalent in size to them own and regaining strategic control.
In the game, card-driven mechanics and the Red Army's covert deployments shape a large but sluggish White Army, contrasted with a small yet agile Red Army. The White Army must continuously tighten its encirclement to restrict the Red Army's movements, while the Red Army must seize opportunities to break the enemy's siege when the White Army is at its most exhausted.
Both sides face opportunities and risks, and victory or defeat may hinge on a single decision.

Dice Tiny Town Duel is a 2-player roll-and-write game using dice and a pencil.

Two players compete over 9 rounds to build the finest village. Both players share the same three dice, but each chooses how to use them by placing Selection Cards in front of the dice they want to claim. The result of the dice determines the shape of the tetromino or pentomino block drawn on your sheet, and the building and nature drawn within it.

Each round follows the icon on the Round Track. On Sun and Moon rounds, the first player places cards openly, and the second player cannot place a card of the same color across from the opponent's. On Dark rounds, both players place their cards face-down at the same time, free from the color restriction.

On Dark rounds, you can draw an extra nature or building icon and send an invitation to a visitor you want in your village.

Two pentomino cards can only be used once in the whole game, so use them at the right moment. Spend star tokens to reroll the dice or shift their values, and earn more stars by completing full rows or columns on your village sheet.

After 9 rounds, compare villages. The player whose village earns more Prestige points — from buildings, nature, star tokens, and satisfied visitors — wins.

Build a village more charming than your rival's!

—description from the designer

boardgame: Vampire: The Masquerade – Palermo Conspiracies: Plagued Faith Story Expansion
description:
During Palermo's vibrant Feast of Santa Rosalia, a mysterious plague begins afflicting Kindred who feed amid the throngs of pilgrims and revelers. As panic grips the undead, the Archons must trace the plague's elusive origin, navigate fragile alliances, and decide how far they'll go to contain the spreading doom.

This expansion includes:

     5+ hours of new content
     New Informant (Mercenary) smart miniature
     New Technician (Ally) smart miniature
     Influence cubes
     NPC Tokens


—description from the publisher
family: Versions & Editions: Board Game Versions of Role-Playing Games

boardgame: Vampire: The Masquerade – Palermo Conspiracies: Undead Monastery Story Expansion
description:
In Palermo's Capuchin Monastery, where death and the underworld intertwine, a disturbed tomb shatters an ancient seal once forged by the elusive Hecata known as the Capuchin. As restless spectres rise and chaos spreads, the Archons must uncover the culprit, defend sacred cemeteries, and take a difficult decision to restore balance.

This expansion includes:

     5+ hours of new content
     New City Guide (Mercenary) smart miniature
     New Street Boss (Ghoul) smart miniature
     Influence cubes
     NPC Tokens


—description from the publisher
family: Versions & Editions: Board Game Versions of Role-Playing Games

boardgame: Vampire: The Masquerade – Milan Uprising: Playable Character Expansion Pack
description:
Four new Archons are ready to join the fight against the Camarilla, with their highly detailed smart miniatures.

Domenica Amato (Lasombra)
A criminal boss, and a masterful schemer and manipulator. As a Lasombra Kindred she has found her true calling as an apex predator, and as an Anarch she is determined to bring retribution to those of her clan who betrayed her sire to join the Camarilla.

Aristide Demiana (The Ministry)
A consummate storyteller, liar and media influencer. In his hands the truth is as malleable as clay, and he sees the reputation of any Kindred as something easily made, and easily broken. He has travelled to Milan to boost the morale and the image of the Anarch Movement in their time of need.

Jemilah Ianni (Hecata)
A former nurse exposed for speeding unwary and unfortunate mortals to their deaths. As a Kindred she has continued her work, but now with greater artistry, and in the name of her new family, the Hecata clan. Her family have sent her to aid the Anarchs against their mutual enemy Prince Prospero.

Romeo De Carlo (Tzimisce)
An avant-garde artist, a committed punk, and talented in the art of rogue taxidermy. He relishes in the shock of others, and exhibits grotesque tableaus to the delight of his devoted followers. He believes deeply in the Anarch cause and hopes to make his mark in Milan.

—description from the publisher
family: Versions & Editions: Board Game Versions of Role-Playing Games

boardgame: Vampire: The Masquerade – Milan Uprising: Allies & Ghouls Expansion
description:
Add new Allies and Ghouls to your Milan Uprising game, with these highly detailed smart miniatures:

     The Food Delivery Driver (Ally)
     The Paramedic (Ally)
     The Taxi Driver (Ally)
     The Blood Addict (Ghoul)
     The Enforcer (Ghoul)
     The Punk (Ghoul)


—description from the publisher
family: Versions & Editions: Board Game Versions of Role-Playing Games

description:
Eva discovers a suitcase. Inside: dozens of photos. All of her. Someone has been watching, and now you need to find out who, and why.

The Stalker is a story-driven at-home escape experience by Owner Unknown. Work together to unravel a disturbing obsession through mysterious physical props and online elements that blur the line between fiction and reality. A thrilling, immersive challenge for 2–6 players who love suspense and deep storytelling.

—description from the publisher

In Card Trip, we plan a road trip across Europe. Try to fill your days as efficiently as possible with different attractions. Attend a cycling event in France, hike through nature in the Dolomites, stroll through Prague, and finish with the tulip festival — it’s all possible in this Card Trip. Make sure to schedule enough rest moments between busy days and plan the most memorable trip this way.

Each player creates their own Card Trip of 16 cards, in which the different locations must be alternated. You cannot visit two locations of the same type in a row, so you’ll need to mix them carefully to make your week as interesting as possible. Your energy per day is limited, so between activities you’ll need to rest at the campsite before starting a new week.

To add cards to your trip, you must always make sure the card you play has a higher number than the previous card in your week. Visiting the campsite (a card with the campsite side face up) resets your week, allowing you to start fresh on a new adventure. Keep a close eye on your energy as well: each week you can have a maximum of 7 stars, after which you’ll need to return to the campsite.

All players simultaneously select 2 cards from their hand and place them face down in front of them. Once everyone has chosen, the cards are revealed and added to the week. The remaining cards in hand are then passed to the player on the left. So make sure to pick the best locations for yourself before you have to pass your hand along. After 8 rounds, the game ends and it’s time to count the points. Each location type has its own way of scoring, and the player with the most points has created the best Card Trip and wins the game!

-description from publisher

Enter the fartastic world of Fart Masters, where strategy meets total chaos! Choose your fart champion—Boom Bum Bash, Dusty Puff, Dr. Fartzz, Stinky Samurai and outplay your opponents in a battle of fartactics.

The person who “farted last” starts the game. 
On your turn, you play your fart champion token on any empty spot on the board.

The next player must also place their fart champion on any empty tile but must flip any other token on the board.
Player to make a sequence of their 3 fart champion tokens first on the board WINS!!
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: Vampire: The Masquerade – Milan Uprising: Mystical Milan Story Expansion
description:
In the Mystical Milan story expansion, the player coterie is invited to explore the mystical heritage of Milan and the great inventor Leonardo da Vinci. The coterie must compete against the clock with several rival groups of Kindred looking for the same lost arcane knowledge.

This expansion includes:

     5+ hours of new content
     The Burglar (Ghoul) smart miniature
     The Investigator (Ally) smart miniature
     8 Tokens


—description from the publisher
family: Versions & Editions: Board Game Versions of Role-Playing Games

description: This ten-card expansion adds and extra puppy card, and extra bird card, a purple suit of four cards, and a gold suit of four cards.

boardgame: Vampire: The Masquerade – Milan Uprising: Prospero's Legacy Story Expansion
description:
In the Prospero's Legacy story expansion, more secrets from Prince Prospero's past are revealed by old rivals who arrive in the city to claim his legacy. The player coterie must battle with these new enemies while searching for the means to defeat them.

This expansion includes:

     5+ hours of new content
     The Hacker (Ally) smart miniature
     6 Sabbat Control cubes
     6 Sabbat Control minis


—description from the publisher
family: Versions & Editions: Board Game Versions of Role-Playing Games

description:
Frontiers: Whelming the West is a Wild West settlement deck-building game by Greg Finkelstein, Wade Green, and Pat Lahey. It combines the strategic depth of deck builder games with the thrill of combat and the fun of designing strong combinations. In Frontiers, players lead settlements against each other, spending gold to recruit Persons, Buildings, and Items to build their fortitude up or strike their opponents’ fortitude down. When you gain 75 or more Fortitude, or bring your opponent’s Fortitude to zero or below, you win!

The game features deep, engaging mechanics that keep every game feeling fun and fresh. Each player starts the game with the same cards and competes to build their decks from a shared pool of new cards. With 53 unique card types, and each card having multiple abilities to combine with other cards in your deck, no two games are the same. Compete to acquire the cards you want while denying your opponent the cards they're looking for in this fun-filled, wild west brawl!

—description from the designer

boardgame: Vampire: The Masquerade – Milan Uprising: Unending Dream Story Expansion
description:
In the Unending Dream story expansion, the Kindred society of Milan is swayed by the arrival of a new religious cult of vampires intent on establishing an earthly paradise, regardless of the attention it might bring from the Second Inquisition agencies. The players' coterie must find a way to neutralize the cult's powerful and charismatic leader.

This expansion includes:

     10+ hours of new content
     The Vagrant (Ghoul) smart miniature
     The Real Estate Agent (Ally) smart miniature
     10 Cult of Nephilim cubes
     6 Tokens


—description from the publisher
family: Versions & Editions: Board Game Versions of Role-Playing Games

description:
DOG House is a 4-player fixed team, trick-taker that uses 2 sets of double six dominoes and a 6x6 grid.  Scoring will take place for winning tricks and for scoring majorities on a 6x6 grid.

Pieces will be placed on the board by the losing team each trick that matches the coordinates on the grid of the domino they played and one of the colors present on that domino.  Trick winners will either score victory points or claim control of one of the six colors on the board for scoring.

Work with your partner to maximize your trick points and clever board placement to have the most majorities in the rows and columns of the board to stay out of the DOG House and lead your team to victory.

Goal:
Be the fastest player to spot and shout a valid word using the pool of shared letter tiles. Win tiles by calling out the longest or alphabetically highest word. At the end of the game, the player with the most Winner tokens wins.

Components:
• 72 Single Letter Tiles
• 16 2-Letter Bigram Tiles
• 10 “Won a Round” (Winner) Tokens

Setup:
• Shuffle the tiles face down as the draw pile.
• Place seven letter tiles face up between the players as the pool.
• Everyone can see all letters at all times.
• Decide how many rounds to play (three recommended).
• Place the Winner tokens aside.

How to Play:
1. Everyone studies the same exposed letters.
2. When you see a valid word, shout it out!
3. That word becomes the current leading word.
4. Other players have five seconds to beat it with a word that is: longer, or same length but alphabetically higher.
5. When the five seconds expire, the best word wins.
6. The winning player collects the tiles used in their word.
7. Replace the used tiles with new ones from the pile.
8. Continue until all rounds are complete.

 
The Five Second Rule Examples
After the first word is shouted, a virtual five second countdown begins.
During those five seconds, anyone may shout a better word.
Example 1: With these letters:
F W S M N D A
• First shout: FARM
• Within five seconds: WANDS
• WINDS wins because it is longer.
If no one beats the first word within five seconds, the first caller wins the tiles.
Example2: With these letters:
N J I L O E E
• First shout: EEL
• Within five seconds: OIL
• OIL wins because it is alphabetically higher.

Age Based Word Lengths:
To keep the game fair for mixed ages:
Age Group    Minimums
Kids (under 10): 3 letters, no time limit
Teens (10–15): 4 letters, 10 seconds
Adults (16+): 5 letters, five seconds.
Players may shout any valid word, but only words meeting their age minimum count. In a family game, the youngest player always grabs their winning letters before an older player who made another word. The older player can only claim any remaining letters matching their word.

End of Round Scoring:
• Each tile has a point value.
• Add up the points on your tiles.
• Highest total wins a "Winner" token. In case of a tie, both players will get a token.
At the end of the round, start another round with all tiles face down.

Perfect Yell Bonus: Grab a Winner token when using all eight letters.

Winning:
After all rounds have been played. the player with the most Winner tokens becomes the YELL A GRAM Champion. In case of a tie, play another round.

Valid Words:
In this game, just about all words are valid including first names, places and countries. Acronyms are not valid.

—description from the designer

Headliner is a music–themed card game of building bands, chasing hype, and stealing the spotlight. Draft the right mix of talent, style, and sound to become the act everyone came to see. Own the stage in this travel-sized showdown full of bold personalities and rock n’ roll attitude.

On your turn, you will draft a new band member into your hand from one of five columns. Then, you'll drive your van to a venue in town with hopes to hit the stage once you have earned the matching icon requirements. Finally, if you've got the right card combination, you hit the stage and headline at the venue! Headlining will earn you cash that you can use to get new band members in the future, but ultimately gets you closer to winning the game.

Once you headline, though, your band breaks up! You must discard all band members except for one, then work your way back towards performing again soon with a new (and might I say better) band.

The first player to earn $100 cash will win the game and go on a nationwide tour!

—description from the publisher

Each new year, a tournament takes place in Zal'Janna - the heavens themselves. Mortals who choose to compete have their souls transferred there, and those who win are imbued with godlike abilities upon returning.

Card-driven, Modular Board, Miniatures Skirmish Game.

     Epic wargame content with a compact TCG twist.
     Custom decks, battlefield, and modes.
     Card stats determine movement/interactions, not dice!
     Win by eliminating other players in multiplayer mode or the boss in solo mode.


—description from the designer

boardgame: Tamashii: The Final Amendment – Celestial Bodies Pack
description: Add six fresh, highly asymmetric Bodies to your pool, each offering unique mechanics, skills, and upgrade paths to expand your gameplay options.

boardgame: Tamashii: The Final Amendment – Tamaposhii Expansion
description: This expansion introduces unique companion allies who join you on your journey through the city. Complete special missions to earn their trust, and they will become permanent companions to assist you in combat!

Craft the Crown is an economic strategy game where players must craft their way to a fortune. Players can draw any one of 56 unique crafting recipes, which they can only play if they own the required ingredients.

Each turn, players can take as many actions as they'd like: 

     Craft new Elements, Tools (one-time use disrupters) or Sites (passive upgrades) from other Elements
     Buy or sell from a shared Market
     Use Tools to help yourself or disrupt others
     Discard cards you don't want, or reserve them for later
     Trade or negotiate tactics with other players


Players must balance short-term gains against long-term strategy. With hidden hands, shifting economies, and player-driven deals, adaptability is key. Will you be the first to amass 50 Gold, or will you forge the Crown to win instantly?

—description from the designer

boardgame: ステラ・フロンティア (Stella Frontier)
description:
"Join a planetary colonization crew and work together with your teammates to complete your mission!

Adapt to the ever-changing environment and push forward with development before you run out of air.
To succeed, you’ll need dice, a deck of cards to control them, and seamless teamwork.

Build a deck that leverages each crew member’s strengths and take on the unknown planet!"

-from the publisher

boardgame: なみコレ ～波打ち際のお宝コレクション～ (NamiKore: Seaside Treasure Collection)
description:
"""NamiKore"" is a set-collection game inspired by the joy of collecting seaside treasures with my child.

[Setting]
Players become children visiting the seaside, exploring the shore to find treasures and small sea creatures.

[Goal]
Collect sets of seaside treasures and creatures to score the most points.

[How to Play]
On your turn, collect cards from the shore as the tide shifts between low and high, changing what’s available. Build your collection strategically to maximize your score.

[Features]
Have you ever discovered seaside treasures or spotted little sea creatures along the shore?
- Adorable cards featuring pearls, crabs, and more
- Simple rules — quick and easy for anyone to enjoy
- A dynamic shore that changes with the tide
- Enjoy a gentle seaside atmosphere as you play

Find and collect seaside treasures, and create your very own Seaside Collection!"

-from the publisher
publishers: つきあかりゲームズ (Tsukiakari Games)

description:
"It’s a puzzle game where you fit two mirror-image pieces into a transparent case.
It offers surprising difficulty and a brilliantly satisfying solution."

-from the publisher

"It’s a puzzle game where you fit six identical pieces into a transparent case.
Think carefully about how the pieces can fit together.
When you play Snake Pit, you might just notice something new."

-from the publisher

"It’s a puzzle game where you fit seven identical pieces into a transparent case.
It’s a highly challenging puzzle, so take your time and experiment with different arrangements.
When you play Fancy Form, you might just discover something unexpected."

-from the publisher

"Setting:
This is an abstract card game with no specific setting.
Goal:
Win tricks, collect number cards, and make your total as close to 10 as possible.
How to Play:
Players play number cards to win tricks based on the current color rule: on red, higher numbers win; on blue, lower numbers win. The trick winner takes either all red or all blue center cards, places 1 face up and the rest face down, and tries to keep their total at 10 or less. At the end of each round, the player closest to 10 wins the round and gains an Angel card. If you win a round while already holding an Angel card, you win the game."

-from the publisher

"Construct Mana Forges to supply Mana to all of Elementica.
Generate Mana from elements that are compatible with your Mana Forges!
Expand your Bases to earn Design Tokens!
Use the Design Tokens to take your actions!

Elementica is a game of building Mana Forges, expanding your influence, collecting tokens, and balancing actions with mana production."

-from the publisher

A fun and colorful game of observation and speed

Footsteps on the stairs!

We lost track of time playing with our funny little monsters. Our parents came up to our room to check if everything was tidied up as promised, but there were still toys scattered all over the floor!

Who will be the fastest to pick everything up? And what have the others already picked up?

Lay all the cards face up on the table to simulate the mess in your room.

When everyone is ready to clean up, choose a player at random to flip over a card face down, revealing 5 monsters.

These are the 5 monsters you’ll have to find during this round. But watch out for traps!

—description from the publisher

Welcome to Scrap Hounds, a solo Mech skirmish game of gritty survival set on a post - apocalyptic future world of your choice. 
Scrap Hounds is a miniature agnostic skirmish game for one player (or two if they want to share their models) that features the following aspects: low model count, flexible setting, thrilling combat, and resource acquisition / management. 
The game features our Rugged 3D6 mechanics, and you can use almost any fantasy or sci -fi miniatures in Scrap Hounds, and the rules will work in scales ranging from 6mm all the way up to 28mm.
 The game was play-tested using mostly 10mm and 15mm figures, but player preferences will vary. 
The rules work best on a 2’ x 2’ (60cm x 60cm) board or play mat, but players using 28mm figures might want to expand to 3’ x 3’ (90cm x 90cm).

boardgame: 2d Delves: Solo & Co-Op Fantasy Skirmish Adventures
description:
2d Delves gives you the tools to build a party of fantasy heroes and guide them through amazing adventures!

This solo / co-op procedural fantasy system allows you to tell incredible tabletop stories with ANY models in your collection as the stars. 
Along with those models you will need: 

     a handful of six sided dice, 
     chits numbered 1 - 20, 
     a tape measure marked in inches, 
     a map or small collection of terrain, and 
     paper and pencil to track the action. 


Our intuitive AI rules assign your enemy models simple roles that govern their reactions to your heroes’ actions. 
Play out story-driven skirmishes right from this book, or adapt your favorite dungeon fantasy adventures.

Explore the depths of your imagination with 2d Delves!

Your task: House the black cats (black dice) and white cats (white dice). But these cats are moody! You'll need to deftly coax them into the cat house as their tempers change.

You will write the values rolled on two 10-sided dice into the rooms (cells) of the cat house. These values become your score in MP (Meow Points). Be careful, as the MP value can change drastically depending on the cats' moods.

The values on the dice represent the moods of the four cats. You must skillfully manage their moods as you place them into the cat house (= write the values in the cells).
The game ends at the conclusion of the round in which at least one player has filled in all the cells on their sheet. At that point, all players calculate their scores.
First, calculate and write down the sum of each column and each row in the scoring area. To calculate your MP (Meow Points), you will multiply the total of a column by the total of a corresponding row. Multiply the total of Column A by the total of Row 1. A positive score indicates the cats are in a good mood. A negative score indicates they are in a bad mood. Repeat this process for the remaining pairs (Column B × Row 2, Column C × Row 3, and so on). 
The player with the highest grand total of MP wins the game.

—description from the publisher

Create the most mayhem in this zany demolition derby game!
Each player controls a demolition derby car.

Players will do 3 things: Vroom Zoom & Boom! 
VROOM: Customize your weapons and racing gear, so you can blow up your friend's ride. 
ZOOM: Using pathing cards, players will simultaneously program wacky paths around the demolition dome to collect flags and power ups.
BOOM: Crash into your friends and blow each other up using the customized weapons on your car.

Score points by collecting flags around the demolition dome, firing weapons at other cars, or crashing into each other. The game ends when the first player is eliminated. All players count their mayhem points, and whoever scores the most wins!

-description from publisher

`"A 10-second search and scare by the faintest of lights.
Players are divided into the ‘Paranormal Team’ and the ‘Explorers’, engaging in a battle of wits and a search in the darkness.
The Supernatural Team hides a ‘Sealed Vial’ and a ‘Stuffed Toy’ somewhere in the room and places footprint markers as clues.
They plunge the room into complete darkness and invite the Explorers in.
The Explorers search the room by torchlight. Each turn, they can search the area illuminated by their torch for ‘just 10 seconds’.
If they find both items within 5 turns, the Explorers win. If they fail to find them, then you..."

-from the publisher

Is the real enemy truly a WEREWOLF?

Could it be that CULTISTS worshiping Cthulhu are merely masquerading as werewolves to secretly preparing a ritual in the darkness to summon their master?

""CTHULHU WEREWOLF"" transcends the typical mafia-style game. It's a battle of deception, psychological warfare, and strategic decision-making.

The GM secretly selects either ""Werewolves"" or ""Cultists"" as the enemy, without revealing it to the players.
The unchosen enemy does not appear in this game.

     Werewolves: Seek to massacre the villagers.
     Cultists: Disguise themselves as Werewolves, hoping to be sacrificed for Cthulhu's summoning ritual.
     Villagers: Must deduce who among them is hiding their true identity, and what their real role is. If the enemy is 'Werewolves', eliminate them all. If the enemy is 'Cultists', stop the summoning ritual.


—description from the publisher
publishers: Team Blue Flower / チーム青い花

description:
Blasters & Bulkheads utilizes the popular Goalsystem game engine to allow players to run Space Opera skirmish battles in a far-flung future. 
Build a crew of adventurers and ne'er do wells, or command akill-squad of Galactic Hegemony enforcers.

Rules for combat, campaigns, character advancement and more allow for story-driven campaigns, and four pre-built scenarios allow players to jump right into the action. 
Written for both 15mm and 28mm miniatures, B&B requires only a handful of figures for each side. 
Complete character creation rules let players build exactly the type of fighters they want, and rules for the mysterious Essence allow for the existence of strange powers.

Prime your blaster, draw your energy sword, and battle for the fate of the galaxy's future!

Let the Yetoid win!

boardgame: 霧の郵便屋 (Deliveries in the Mist)
description:
"Deliveries in the Mist

In Deliveries in the Mist, you are a novice wizard courier magically clearing paths through a forest shrouded in seasonal mist. With the goal of becoming the most respected messenger by bringing happiness to the villagers, you will dispel the fog, build road networks, and deliver mail. Strategic route planning and clever cooperation with other couriers are key to succeeding in this mystical land.

Players manage their deliveries under a strict environmental constraint: the forest is treacherous, and all paths must follow the shortest route. The gameplay revolves around these core actions:

BUILD: Place road pieces to connect isolated villages and expand the network.

PICK UP: Receive mail and prepare for your journey to the next destination.

DELIVER: Transport mail through the established paths to earn Happiness Points (Victory Points).

At the heart of the game is a unique Shared Incentive system. When delivering mail, you may use any player's road pieces as long as they belong to the shortest path. However, using another's road also grants that player Happiness Points. To win, you must cleverly utilize your rivals' infrastructure while building roads so essential that others cannot help but use them."

-from the publisher

"You are the owner of a Japanese restaurant. You go out to sea to fish, display your catches in your restaurant, and serve them to your customers. This game is not just something to ""play,"" but something that ""lasts.""

In this game, you will always catch fish. However, you may not always catch them in perfect condition. Large catches can be displayed in your restaurant as fish prints. However, displayed fish cannot be served as dishes. By recording your catches, decisions, and events of the day, you will build your own unique restaurant story."

This individual is a liar, I say!
These words might be the key to victory, or a trap that leads to ruin in this strategic bluffing game where a well-timed skill card can change your fate!

Lords and Ladies, this evening we invite you to partake in a humble aristocratic diversion: 'Liar Seven'—a game conceived of, by, and for the nobility.
The telling of extravagant lies is the very cornerstone of noble etiquette.
As, indeed, is the art of unmasking them.
A few cards are all that stand between you and your destiny.

Lose, and your fortune is forfeit.
Win, and your coffers shall swell.

Does that not sound like the most delightfully perilous pursuit, of the kind only our privileged class may partake?

There is not a true noble alive who would not leap at such an opportunity. Go boldly, elegantly forth, as the curtain rises on Liar Seven!"

-from the publisher

boardgame: Tianqi A Spacetime Mystery Spanning 400 Years
description:
"Background
1626, Ming Dynasty: The empire teeters on the brink of collapse. Omens of doom and dark rumors plague the capital. When a prominent merchant family is brutally murdered, the Jinyiwei (the Imperial Secret Police) are dispatched to investigate—only for a world-shaking, mysterious explosion to erase all clues and silence the truth.

2018, Present Day: A curious reader happens upon a historical mystery novel titled Tianqi (Apocalypse). Soon, they realize that just like the missing author and editor before them, they have unwittingly stumbled into a vast, centuries-old conspiracy. As past and present collide, it becomes clear that the mysterious power behind the ""Great Explosion"" of 1626 has transcended time to prevent the truth from ever being revealed.

Goal
Your objective is to reconstruct the fragmented narrative into its correct chronological order and crack 27 intricate puzzles hidden within the pages to uncover the ultimate secret.

How to Play
This is an immersive puzzle-solving book. Players must carefully analyze the text, investigate historical clues, and interact with physical props included with the book to unlock secrets and progress through the branching mystery."

-from the publisher
publishers: 万物皆谜 (Beijing YiYuanWanXiang Culture Co., Ltd.)

description:
"Background
Long ago, the Lu Shen (God of Fortune), who governed the merits of the mortal world, fell from the divine Kunlun Realm after losing a sacred artifact during a ranking trial. Reborn as a Nine-Colored Deer named Bensheng in the border forests of the Chaoyun Kingdom, he possesses the mysterious power to manipulate fire, yet is feared as an ""omen of disaster"" by his own tribe.As the mortal kingdoms of Yongle and Buzhou descend into chaos, war, and environmental collapse, Bensheng must embark on a journey across parched deserts and ancient ruins. Accompanied by a loyal companion from the Crow Tribe and a mysterious old woman who harbors her own secrets, he seeks to reclaim the Time Hourglass and the lost Five-Colored Seeds to restore the balance of the four seasons.
Goal
Players must reconstruct the fragments of divine memory and solve over 20 ""Mortal Choice"" puzzles integrated into the narrative to help Bensheng find his lost artifact and find his way back to the heavens.
How to Play
Dunhuang: Nine-Colored Deer is an immersive narrative puzzle book. It combines a deep, philosophical story with interactive puzzle-solving. Players will use physical props, observe the stunning Dunhuang-style illustrations, and use logic to overcome obstacles (""Mortal Choices"") that determine the fate of the characters."

-from the publisher
publishers: 万物皆谜 (Beijing YiYuanWanXiang Culture Co., Ltd.)

description:
"Background
Welcome to the ""Mad Chemistry Lab,"" where test tubes are your weapons and pH isn't just a science—it's your life! In this fast-paced competitive card game, players take on the roles of chemists engaged in a volatile battle of reactions. Forget standard HP; here, your survival depends on maintaining your acidity and alkalinity balance while your opponents try to destabilize your beaker.
Goal
Every player starts at a neutral pH of 7. Your goal is to use strategic card combinations to push your opponents to the extremes: either a burning pH of 0 or a corrosive pH of 14. The last chemist standing with a stable solution wins! 
How to Play
Volatile Reactions: Play Strong/Weak Acids and Bases to attack. Opponents can neutralize your moves with opposite substances, but beware—neutralization generates ""Heat"" in the public Temperature Monitoring Area. Laboratory Equipment: Deploy realistic lab tools like Beakers, Glass Rods, and Catalysts to buff your actions, increase reaction frequency, or protect yourself from ""Overload"" accidents. Dynamic Hand Size: Your strategic options are limited by your current pH state. The further you drift from neutral 7, the fewer cards you can hold, simulating the instability of extreme chemical states. Interaction & Traps: Set hidden ""Trap"" cards like Label Error or Discovery of Impurities to sabotage enemy experiments, or deal with unpredictable Lab Accidents that rotate between players. "

-from the publisher
publishers: 万物皆谜 (Beijing YiYuanWanXiang Culture Co., Ltd.)

description:
Battle Box - Napoleonic Wars contains all you need to play a game in the age of black powder in a small and neat tin box that makes it the perfect wargame to take on the move: 24 unit pieces in red and blue to represent armies of the period (each piece is an infantry brigade, cavalry regiment or artillery battery), a battlefield generator in 18 terrain cards to create a 6x4 grid where armies fight and die, 6 thrilling scenarios to jump into the period, 3 dice and 1 simple set of rules to read and play in under 30 minutes while still capturing the flavour of the Napoleonic Wars.

The battlefield cards are shuffled to create a random battlefield - or they are laid out as described in the 6 scenarios provided with the game - and players roll for random army composition; meaning that as a player you will have to adapt your strategy to the terrain and forces on the field.

Once the battlefield, objectives and opposing forces are determined, players act as Commanders In Chief of their armies by issuing orders (March, Fire, Assault) to each troop on the battlefield with the goal of destroying the enemy army (when their morale fails them) or achieving the scenario objectives.

The game introduces command friction in the form of Command Tests of increased difficulty. Players should focus their first orders on the most critical part of the battlefield, as there is no guarantee of being able to issue orders to all your troops (specially if they are spread all over the battlefield!).

Additional rules for multiplayer and solo modes are included, as well as optional special unit rules.

-description from publisher

Chess Grand Prix is a fast-paced racing board game that transforms classic chess movement rules into an exciting competitive race experience.

Players take control of racing cars on a specially designed 50-space track inspired by a chessboard. Instead of traditional chess gameplay, movement is driven by dice rolls that activate chess piece abilities—Pawn, Knight, Bishop, Rook, Queen, and King—each offering unique movement patterns on the track.

The goal is not checkmate, but speed, strategy, and consistency. Players must complete 3 full laps while collecting points through racing positions, Pit Stop cards, and special action cards.

Strategic decisions are crucial: players must balance risk and reward, choose when to use advantage cards, and manage mandatory Pit Stop visits that can change the course of the race.

Key Features:
Chess-inspired movement system (all piece movements are integrated into racing mechanics)
Dice-driven tactical racing gameplay
Card-based strategy system with 64 action cards and 24 Pit Stop cards
Mandatory Pit Stop mechanic introducing risk management
Dynamic track with special spaces and tactical shortcuts
Point-based victory system rewarding both speed and strategy

Objective:

Complete 3 laps of the race track while collecting the highest total score from race positions, cards, and bonuses. The player with the highest score at the end of the final lap wins the Chess Grand Prix.

Design Purpose:

Chess Grand Prix is designed as an engaging way to teach chess movement rules through gameplay, especially for younger players. By integrating chess mechanics into a racing environment, players naturally learn how each chess piece moves while enjoying a competitive and fun board game experience.

—description from the designer

In this fast-paced card game, players become hens pecking for grain to lay more eggs than their opponents. On their turn a player can either draw a card to collect grains or exchange their collected grains into eggs. The player with the most eggs at the end of the game wins.

boardgame: Knights Out: The Trial of the Line
description:
Knights Out is a ritualistic, card-driven tactical duel for 1–2 players. The game is entirely Free-to-Play and requires only a single standard 52-card deck and two tokens to play.

The Trial of the Line
In the sunless depths of the Iron Canyons, the Silent God watches from a rift in the sky, demanding a sacrifice of weight and will. You are a Knight cast upon a crumbling five-card bridge over the abyss. Your objective is not glory, but survival: you must earn the God’s Favors by paying The Toll and casting your opponent into the void as an Offering.

Knights Out reimagines the standard deck of cards as a visceral combat engine. Players "purge" the deck down to values 2–10 to create a high-tension environment where every card matters.

No specialized components required. Use your Spades to push, your Hearts to block, and your Clubs to advance.

Each round, players secretly commit to a sequence of three actions. Success depends on reading your opponent's intent and anticipating the "Whiff"—the moment your strike hits empty air because your foe has already moved.

Face the Hollow Knight, an adaptive AI that reacts to your proximity and reveals an "Omen" (a face-up card) before you program your turn. It turns a standard deck of cards into a challenging, puzzle-like boss fight.

Zero Cost: Grab any deck of cards and start the ritual.

Travel-Friendly: Requires very little table space; perfect for dueling at a tavern or on the go.

boardgame: H.D.P.: Hasta Donde Puedas – Lo Mejor de: El Cine
description: Promotional micro expansion with new cards, related to movies.

boardgame: Print & Crawl: The Print & Play Dungeon Crawler
description:
Print & Crawl is a co-operative print-and-play fantasy dungeon crawler for 1–4 players. Players guide heroes with distinct roles through dungeon maps using included paper standees or their own miniatures. They fight monsters on a square grid, deal with traps and room conditions, and find new equipment. Heroes take turns moving, attacking, and using skills, with combat resolved using six-sided dice. Across a multi-scenario campaign, heroes grow stronger, unlock class skills, and take on tougher challenges. Each scenario has its own objective, and players win by completing it before the party is defeated.

—description from the designer

Uglylicious is a fast-paced, competitive card game set in a delightfully chaotic flea market where players become collectors of items that are… let’s say, “full of character.” One minute you’re admiring a vintage lamp, the next you’re fighting over a garden gnome like it’s priceless art. With eager customers and even eagier rivals, you’ll need sharp reflexes and a strong tolerance for questionable taste to come out on top.

Players take turns flipping cards onto a central pile, building a tempting stack of potential treasures and terrible decisions. At any moment, anyone can slap the pile to grab cards—but timing is everything. Jump too early and you’ll look ridiculous (and possibly get penalized); wait too long and someone else walks away with your dream collection of slightly cursed knick-knacks.

The cards you collect form your personal hoard, mixing valuable objects with absolute chaos. Some cards reward you with points, others push you to collect sets, and a few exist purely to ruin your day—like grumpy customers, kleptomaniac gnome thieves, or cats with a personal vendetta against your inventory. Luckily, a loyal dog might help you fend off disaster, because nothing says “professional vendor” like relying on a big slobbery bodyguard.

When the cards run out, players count up their points to see who built the most impressive (or least disastrous) collection. Victory goes to the quickest, cleverest, and possibly luckiest player—proving once and for all that beauty is in the eye of the beholder… especially when you grabbed it first.

Isomer: Chemical Connections is a puzzle game embedded in the natural world. Players use real molecular modeling pieces to build three different 3D molecules over the course of the game.

The tactile experience really feels like chemical bonding, and there are millions of ways to connect the atoms. The best connections depend on the available tiles and cards and your atoms (you don't always get what you want!).  You may literally never make the same molecule twice...

To play, simply grab your atoms and bond them together. It's that easy! Except for the pesky timer...Score points based on how well you incorporate the various Functional Groups, Structures, and Properties into your molecules.

—description from the publisher

Earth Under Siege: Warzone is a small unit skirmish game for 1-4 players. Play as the invading Xethan aliens, or as the defending allied forces of Earth.

Play in either solo/co-op mode, or vs mode against other players. Compete in 1v1, 2v2, and 1v2 battles.

Build a custom army, assemble a Tactics deck from your chosen Elite units and your Strategy. Kill the enemy and capture Command Points to earn Momentum, a valuable resource that determines the flow of the battle.

Pick a side, and get in the battle!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Pete's Plaice: Tea by The Sea
description:
The sun blazes.  The overheated crowds shuffle along the promenade.  The seagulls squawk.  Nothing sounds better than getting into the shade for an afternoon tea, by the sea.

Your afternoon tea arrives, and as the tea brews, you cut your scone into two halves.  However, which topping do you choose first?  Clotted cream or Strawberry Jam?

During your turns of the base game of Pete's Plaice, you will now have the opportunity to build your afternoon tea scones.  But which menu description is correct to match your served scones?  You will have the chance to flip the menu to your favour throughout the game.   But once the last card is played, only one way will be correct.  
  
The Tea By The Sea expansion adds a competitive, sweet twist to the game.  Please note, you need a copy of the base game to play the Tea by The Sea expansion.

-description from publisher

boardgame: Montes de La Rioja. El juego de cartas
description:
A card game themed around the mountains of La Rioja, Spain. Roll the dice, take cards from the main camp, and play them in your individual camp. The first player to climb two mountains of different colors wins the game!

—description from the publisher

En este juego de cartas descubrirás algunos de los más famosos montes, lugares y personajes mitológicos de las sierras riojanas.
Para ganar, deberás ser el primero en ascender a las cumbres, usando los alimentos y refugios tradicionales del lugar.
Pero cuidado: ¡las leyendas y los mitos populares de la región también formarán parte de la partida...!

—description from the publisher (Spanish)

For years, monsters have emerged from the Cave of Ederoth, spreading across the surrounding lands and turning once-stable regions into chaotic battlefields. Efforts to eliminate them failed, so the kingdoms adopted a different approach: instead of fighting monsters, they began investing in them. By supplying resources such as wheat, meat, herbs, potions, and magical stones, kingdoms profit from monster activity as these creatures battle one another and bring back gold. Players take on the role of investors seeking to build the most successful monster-based economy.

In Dungeonomics, players invest resources to gain returns from monsters active on the field. By placing investments on specific monsters, players earn gold when those monsters win battles and remain in play. However, monsters can be defeated and removed, causing any associated investments to lose their value. Players must balance risk and reward when choosing where and when to invest.

The game is played over four rounds. During each round, players allocate resources, anticipate the outcomes of monster battles, and adjust their strategies based on the shifting state of the battlefield. As monsters clash and are eliminated, new opportunities and risks emerge, shaping each session differently.

At the end of the fourth round, players total the gold earned from their investments. The player with the most gold wins the game.

—description from the designer

This four-player chess variant is played on a square 14×14 board that excludes the four central squares. Each player controls a set of sixteen standard chess pieces, and additionally nine fairy pieces (of seven distinct types).
The game can be played in partnership (two opposing teams of two) or all-versus-all.
Each player's eight pawns are divided into two groups of four: one group moves forward along files, the other along ranks. For the remainder of the pieces, movement and capture work as per usual. There is no castling in Quatrochess.
When a player is checkmated or stalemated, his king is immediately removed from the game and his remaining men become the property of the player delivering the mate or stalemate (all-versus-all game), or of his teammate (partnership game). The last surviving player (or team) is the winner.

In Kami, the players take the roles of artist trying to get the best combinations of flowers and origami figures to get the maximum amount of points among all players.

The game starts with one isolated tile in the middle of the board, players take turns to add tiles to the board, they must place them in specific places and orientation to make them match with the tiles already on the board while trying to maximize the amount of points they can obtain from them. Matching origami figures, using different flowers and creating chains of the same flower will award the players with points.

The game ends when, at the end of a round, any player got the goal amount of points (sometimes 50 points, sometimes 30 points. After that the player with the most points win the game. If there is a tie, the tied players plays sudden death rounds until there is a winner.

—description from the designer

Fury of Dracula (Fifth Edition) is a one-versus-many deduction game inspired by Bram Stoker's classic novel: Dracula. One player controls Dracula and up to four players assume the roles of his hunters. During the game, Dracula will attempt to elude the hunters as he spawns new vampires across Europe, while the hunters try to find and destroy Dracula, thereby ending his reign of terror.

What's different about this edition: The fifth edition core gameplay is consistent with the gameplay in Third/Fourth edition but includes an all-new hunter to play as (Jonathan Harker) and a "Quick-Play Variant".

—description from the publisher

Shinobi Spy & Supply is a small card game about 2 competing shops that sell all the gear Japanese fantasy spies need for espionage! However, when your clientele are Ninja, there’s literally spies around every corner. Will your shop be the most successful?

The game ends when the small deck runs out. Before then, use the multi-use cards to serve customers in your shop for points and to chain bonus actions together. Use the back of the double-sided cards to spy on your opponent for even more points, and resource discounts towards serving customers!

You won't only score points for your shop (tableau), but also your opponent's shop (tableau), based on the ninja spies you've hired. This leads to a knife-fight-in-a-phone-booth feel, where every action that you take may open up opportunities to your opponent and vise versa.

Gain the advantage by playing synergistic cards to your tableau, spying on your opponent at the right moment, and preventing your opponent from getting that card they need. With multiple ways to score, there's multiple strategies and paths to victory in an otherwise very tactical game.

—description from the designer

boardgame: ヒットアンドウィン (Hit and Win)
description:
• What is the setting?
No theme

• What is the objective?
Guess the hidden hand or win tricks to earn more victory points than anyone else.

• How do you play?
A trick-taking game where you guess the hidden hand.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: フォレスト＆フォーチュン 第2弾 氷晶の深淵 (FOREST & FORTUNE: Abyss of Ice Crystals)
description:
The game takes place in a magical forest, specifically in a frozen region known as the “Abyss of Ice Crystals.” Adventurers explore the forest in search of treasure while facing monsters, magical artifacts, and unpredictable events. Goal: Players compete to collect the most valuable treasure by exploring, overcoming monsters, and using magical artifacts strategically. How to Play: On their turn, players roll dice to explore the forest and reveal cards. They may encounter monsters, gain treasure, or trigger events. Magical artifact cards can be used at key moments to influence outcomes. The game features player interaction, including negotiation and interference, and randomized monster setup for replayability.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Guarding the Relics Ancient Chinese History in the National Museum of China
description:
Background
History is not just cold text; it lives within the artifacts that survived the passage of time. Relics of China takes you into the National Museum of China, the ""Vault of Chinese Civilization."" This immersive board game and book set covers the vast timeline of Chinese history—from the prehistoric era to the Qing Dynasty—using nearly 100 world-class cultural relics as its core.

Goal
Your mission is to become a ""Guardian of Relics."" Through strategic exploration and historical reasoning, players aim to collect and protect significant artifacts, correctly identifying their historical periods and cultural significance to build a comprehensive ""Museum of History.""

How to Play

Card-Based Strategy: Players use Relic Cards, each featuring a real artifact from the National Museum. The game integrates historical facts with mechanics like ""Chronicle Matching"" and ""Relic Protection.""

Interactive Narrative: The set includes a 300-page historical adventure book. Players must read the ""Field Journals"" and solve puzzles to unlock special artifact abilities or discover hidden treasures.

Educational Combat: Engage in battles where ""Historical Logic"" beats luck. Use specific artifact traits to counter opponents' moves, simulating the challenges faced by real historians and museum curators.

Multiple Modes: Supports solo exploration (Narrative Puzzle mode) and competitive play (4—8 players) for families and history enthusiasts."

—description from the publisher
publishers: 万物皆谜 (Beijing YiYuanWanXiang Culture Co., Ltd.)

description:
Background
Welcome to the ""Brain Gym""! In a world where colors and emotions are inextricably linked, follow the adventures of Mood Cat and Color Monster. This interactive puzzle book explores the fascinating science of color psychology—how red makes us feel energized or why blue brings a sense of calm. It's not just a book; it's a journey into the spectrum of the human mind.

Goal
Help Mood Cat restore order to the world of colors by solving a series of mysterious cases. Along the way, players will learn about basic color theory, the emotional impact of different hues, and how colors are used in art and daily life.

How to Play
Interactive Puzzles: The book features 30 unique cases across 6 themed chapters, ranging from ""The Logic of Color"" to ""Color and Emotion."" Observe & Solve: Players must observe colorful illustrations, use provided clues, and apply their growing knowledge of color psychology to crack each mystery. Science-Based Fun: Each puzzle is grounded in psychological and physiological science, making it a perfect educational tool for young thinkers and families.

—description from the publisher
publishers: 万物皆谜 (Beijing YiYuanWanXiang Culture Co., Ltd.)

description:
Win the Trick, Collect the Bits!

This is a trick-taking game where the strongest card on the table takes it all. Your mission: aim for the win when the ""1""s are piling up!

But watch out—playing the weakest card can flip the entire power structure. When is the perfect time to trigger a reversal? Despite its simple rules, you'll find deep tactical gameplay in deciding exactly when to push for a win.

—description from the publisher
publishers: むこちゃん開発局 (Muko-chan Game Studios)

boardgame: Boo Boo (SUPER PIG) GO TO CITY
description:
This is a board game where players move animal pieces to reach a goal in a big city. Each round, players roll three dice and compete based on the strength of the "hands" determined by the combination of dice rolls, advancing on the board according to their total points. Special rules are included as strategic elements, such as the build action where players swap dice in a bag, and helper cards that allow the last-place player to try to make a comeback. The components include four types of special dice and lane cards, and the game progresses by repeatedly checking the rank of hands and moving. The goal of this game is to be the first to reach the destination by using a combination of luck and strategy.

—description from the publisher

"Day after day, the hero never shows..."
Tired of being left in the ""backlog,"" the final bosses have decided to take matters into their own hands and battle to determine who is truly the strongest.
Experience a trick-taking game set in a video game spin-off world!

—description from the publisher

Behold the supreme summoner—or succumb to the darkness.
After a failed raid on the demon folk, young summoners find themselves trapped within a 30-day magical barrier.
Will you maintain your ""Noble Form"" through contracts with powerful spirits, or will you be dragged down by the Demon King and transform into a ""Wicked Fiend""?
A trick-taking battle for your very soul!

—description from the publisher

A thrilling Nezumi Kozo (phantom thief, Meeple) stealth battle unfolds with just 1 to 5 cards and a limited 5x5 tile area!

This is a analog game (card game) where you evade persistent Okappiki (police, Pone) on rooftops and aim to distribute all your koban (oval gold coins) before other Nezumi Kozo.
An ultra-simple WabiSaby card battle unfolds with 1-6 hand cards, mixing face-up and face-down states!

Bring a new level of strategy on the dohyō with the Yoisho expansion deck!

Adding a draft mechanic shared between both Rikishi(sumo wrestlers) to overturn the course of the Basho(tournament)!

Increase the interaction with the board with the Flip and the Add Die cards!
Recover cards will make Rikishi push beyond their limits!
Stall and Cancel cards will help you trick your opponent into a misstep!

Following the destruction of the Saladin by the Warspite, the scattered remnants of humanity are embroiled in war.

While focusing on the offensive set by the Medinan Star Force, the Olympian Defense Force Space Arm has the Xing Cheng Celestial Navy moving into its flanks. Xing Cheng asked the government of the Federal Republic of Altiplano to become a formal ally. Altiplano, situated closer to the Olympian operational areas, and currently at peace with them, put a different offer on the table.

Armed neutrality.

Altiplano’s Guardia Espacia de Altiplano is a professional service in transition. Having inherited a mishmash of orphaned warships from the Loss, now all at the tail ends of their service lives, the Guardia Espacial had planned on transitioning to home-built hulls, like the Ricardo Robinson and Independencia classes.

With a conflagration of fusion torch warships spreading through the Ten Worlds, the need to protect shipping from opportunist captains and “pirates” is growing. Altiplano has taken up the challenge, reflagging freighters to put them under their own escorts.

Threading the needle of neutrality in a multi-polar interstellar war is far from easy.

It’s the only course Altiplano has.

21 ships, 14 scenarios, and new fiction for the Ten Worlds!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: 一月一日月曜日（Ichigatsu tsuitachi getsuyobi）
description:
This set contains two Japanese kanji word games.
“Speed Game” is a fast 2-minute speed game where players race to combine kanji cards and form words.
“Kanji Narabe” is a calm 15-minute placement puzzle, creating words by connecting kanji vertically and horizontally.
Both games highlight the fun of kanji readings and meanings, and are enjoyable for language lovers and learners alike.

—description from the publisher

Kuku Doubt is a bluffing game that combines multiplication with deduction and mind games.
On your turn, you declare the result of multiplying two hidden cards.
The next player must then choose one of three actions:

     Accept
     Doubt
     Guess the Pair


If a challenge succeeds, the losing player loses 1 Life.
The last player remaining wins the game.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: 生け花クリエイターズ（Flower Arrangement Creators）
description:
• What is the setting?
You are up-and-coming ikebana creators.
• What is the goal?
A competitive mode where you can achieve your own unique vision for flower arrangement.
A cooperative mode where everyone can achieve their own unique vision.
• How do you play?
You place 3D-printed flowers into a vase, trying to fulfill the requirements of the cards in your hand.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: ポイズンポーカー(Poison Poker)
description:
• What is the setting?
You're a poker player who doesn't know much about the game.
But you're skilled at handling poison.
You secretly slip poison into the cards.

• What is the goal?
Let's aim for the highest score.

• How do you play?
This is a competitive version of The Gang.
Players bet chips on the final ranking and receive points equal to the chips bet, depending on the final ranking.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Dinks & Donkeys: A Roll & Write Fishing Game
description:
Choose a spot on the lake map, pick a cast direction, then roll dice to determine your cast distance — draw a straight line from your spot to that exact point on the map. Roll dice again to check for bites; rod type gives modifiers to those casting/bite rolls, while your lure and cast location change bite probabilities and likely species. On a bite, roll to determine fish species and size, then enter the fight: depending on your drawn cast distance and the fish’s size you must roll a number of dice to reel it in without snagging or breaking the line.
