Catches award XP to buy or upgrade gear (which modify future dice rolls). Use TIGHT LINES luck points to re-roll dice, retrieve lures, or prevent line breaks. Play on pre-made maps or generate lakes by rolling dice on a template — the game centers on dice resolution and the drawn cast line as the primary tactical elements.

Deck Rogue Reload is a roguelike-inspired cooperative card game designed for high replayability.
The goal is to build the strongest possible deck, defeat the enemies you encounter, and ultimately take down the final boss deep within the dungeon.
On your turn, the rules are simple: you can play as many cards as you like. However, when your deck runs out, you do not shuffle it—your discard pile simply becomes your new deck as it is. Because of this, the order in which you play your cards is extremely important.
As the game progresses, enemies become increasingly powerful, so players must carefully choose how to use their cards.
Can you make it to the end?

—description from the publisher

Will you win her heart with a sweet date—or steal it with a bold pickup line?
Fight it out to become the “King of Love” or the “King of Pickup”!
Romance × Competition

Game Overview
Go on dates with different girls and work your way toward a heartfelt confession!
But watch out—other players might interrupt you with sneaky pickup attempts or a dramatic “Wait!” at the worst moment!
With simple rules and desire-driven gameplay, it’s easy to jump in and chase your goal!

Game Details
Don’t you want a cute girlfriend…?
In this game, the first player to win a girl’s heart claims victory!
You can win in one of two ways:
Build up your relationship through dates and reach a confession to find true love
Successfully pick up two girls and become the “King of Pickup”
But beware—someone might interrupt your big confession with a “Wait!” at the last second!
And if you try to pick up a yandere girl… you’ll be haunted by the dreaded “Yandere Instant Death Card.”
Picking someone up might just be a life-risking move!

How to Play

Choose a Date Spot
On your turn, pick a date location. Try to choose somewhere your target girl loves!
But other players might interfere and stop you from going where you want!

Date or Pickup?
After choosing the location, decide: go on a Date or attempt a Pickup!
If you’re at her favorite spot, your date becomes a powerful Double Date with double effect!

Pickup Attempt
If you choose Pickup, success or failure is determined by chance—regardless of her affection level!
Read the lines on the YES/NO cards out loud for maximum drama!
If successful, you take the girl home, and other players can’t target her anymore.
However… she’ll come back later as an annoying obstacle during future pickup attempts!
“Wait!” Interruption
When another player is about to confess, you can interrupt with a dramatic “Wait!”
But it comes at a cost—you must sacrifice one of your own pieces.
Are you willing to pay the price just to mess with someone else’s love!?

Confession Victory
If you’re the first to reach a confession, you become the “King of Love”!
Read the girl’s confession line out loud and enjoy your True Ending!

Pickup Victory
If you successfully pick up two girls first, you become the “King of Pickup”!

—description from the publisher

This set of rock–paper–scissors and number cards includes three games.
Game 1: “JANKEN SUZI” — A twist on a shedding-type card game. Players play rock-paper-scissors with the card on their left while connecting the numbers on the card on their right. When one player loses, both adjacent players also lose; players aim to win while helping their neighbors. No one can go out early, and it is not zero-sum. The game is designed as a microcosm of the world for children.

Game 2: “JANKEN STRAIGHT SUZI FLASH” — Players use rock–paper–scissors played on discarded cards to take cards from others, building their hands and forming sets.

Game 3: “JANKEN SUJITTER” — A cooperative memory-style game where each player flips one card at a time to form sets. Matching identical cards is a miss, while completing a consecutive sequence earns the set. In addition to individual scores, there is a shared rating based on total mistakes. It can also be played solo.

—description from the publisher

Inspired by a rising movement of solo and cooperative games, Slugfest! offers players a full and immersive super skirmish game experience.

This D20-based rule set features detailed, but accessible character creation, comprehensive power and skill lists, exciting combat, and a detailed campaign system where your battles become issues in a comic book series featuring your characters as the stars! 
This book comes complete with three mini-series worth of story material and dozens of sample characters.

All you need to play are a couple of twenty-sided dice, a standard deck of playing cards, and a handful of appropriate miniatures. You will also want terrain or maps to model the environments of your super battles.

Whether you play solo or with friends, Slugfest! will bring the comic book experience to your tabletop. Excelsior!

Battle of Solomon is a card game that recreates the fierce fighting between Japanese and American forces in the Solomon Islands and New Guinea theater during the middle stage of the Pacific War. Players take the role of either the Japanese side or the Allied side and compete by building a deck unique to their faction.
To win the game, players must capture key objectives such as Guadalcanal and Port Moresby. At the same time, each side faces its own geographical advantages and disadvantages. The cards represent land, naval, and air units, and supply is a vital part of the game because it is needed to deploy and maintain these forces on the battlefield.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Jagged Shards: Skirmish Protocol is a fast, lethal tabletop wargame where fractured timelines collide on the surface of Mars.

A failed attempt to destroy a deep-space anomaly shattered reality itself—forcing ancient Martian constructs and the human colonists into the same space.

Command a small strike force in brutal, high-stakes engagements where every decision matters—because every battle decides which reality survives… and which is erased forever.

Turn Sequence:
A battle lasts five rounds, each divided into ordered phases.
Jagged Shards uses an I-Go-You-Go system per phase:

     The player with Initiative completes the phase with all models
     The opposing player completes the same phase
     The game advances to the next phase


What is needed to play:
To play Jagged Shards, you will need:

     A4′×4′ battlefield
     6–10 terrain pieces (Light, Heavy, Obscuring)
     A measuring tape (inches)
     A D100 (two ten-sided dice)
     Models mounted on appropriate base sizes (see Chapter 6)
     Tokens for Wounds, Objectives, and status effects
     The rulebook

Overtake 1 is a competitive racing board game inspired by the excitement, strategy, and intensity of Formula 1–style motorsport.

Take control of a racing team and lead your two drivers to victory through a dynamic and unpredictable circuit. Every turn matters as players must balance speed, tire management, fan support, and tactical decisions to outmaneuver their opponents.

The game combines dice-driven movement with strategic card play, where Event Cards can instantly change the race — from lucky boosts to unexpected crashes or even the appearance of the Safety Car. Weather conditions evolve throughout the race, forcing players to adapt their tire strategy at the right moment or risk losing valuable positions.

Each team features a unique ability, allowing different play styles and strategic approaches. Meanwhile, Player Boards help track tire condition, fan support, and tire type, adding depth and control to every decision.

With simple rules but layered gameplay, Overtake 1 delivers a fast-paced racing experience that captures the spirit of top-tier motorsport competition.

Will you play it safe… or risk everything to overtake?

—description from the designer

boardgame: Aetherpunk28: a post apocalyptic Wizard skirmish game
description:
In a time almost forgotten, the world was whole. From their seats at Avantir, the council of Wizards shared their gift of magic with the world, founding the great magical colleges. It was a time of wonder, prosperity, and progress. In their hubris, the council failed to consider the costs of their actions. They failed to realise that their powers were finite, until, rather predictably, it was too late, and they were too dependent on magic to function without it. With magic beginning to deteriorate, so too did the bonds between the colleges.

Aetherpunk28 is a post apocalyptic Wizard skirmish game where colleges of Wizards battle one another in the ruins of their world, fighting over what little magic is left after the end of the world.

Aetherpunk28 is a skirmish game, where players build Colleges (squads) of Apprentices, commanded by a powerful Wizard. The game uses a unique skill check system where a model must roll a 6 or higher, on any sized die from D12 to D4 (good luck with that) depending on the model’s skills and abilities to complete actions. But this is a game about magic after all, managing your finite Erudite is key to victory. 
Will you use it to power up your troops, or unleash devastating spells upon your foes? 
The choice is yours, but watch your supplies carefully, as magic is powerful but fleeting.

But what would a game be without units? 
A firm favourite from the game’s first edition is the humble Slogger, the humble backbone of many a college. The eagle-eyed fans of the game’s first edition may notice a few changes to the profile. The stats listed correspond to the die rolled when moving, making a ranged attack, fighting in melee, defending themselves against incoming attacks, and finally, how long they’re willing to stay in the fight once the shots and spells start flying. Willpower is not only a model’s health, but dictates how well they can act rationally, react to enemy movements and cast spells. Once a model’s willpower begins to decline, they’ll rapidly lose effectiveness in battle.

But of course it’s not just Sloggers that you’ll be able to command, there’s mounted scouts, arcane engines (ancient and magical tanks), Golems, zombies and even walking fortresses!

The actions and what you do with your units is just as important as the units themselves. Take a sneak peak at how actions work in Aetherpunk28 and maybe even spot a target for your friend to charge whilst you're at it!
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Grandstand is a head-to-head poker duel played across a shared grid. Players take turns placing cards into a central tableau, building intersecting hands that score in different directions; one player claims rows, the other claims columns.

Every placement shapes multiples hands at once, creating constant tension between offense and denial. Each turn makes position of the card just as critical as card value. Will you risk giving your opponent what they need for that perfect hand?

Jokers introduce moments of disruption by allowing players to break patterns, shift outcomes, and seize control of the board. Side Pots give players additional goals to compete for every chip.

With a shared play space and opposing scoring goals, Grandstand transforms familiar poker hands into a tactical battle of timing, placement, and control.

—description from the publisher

Full Ascension set required for this expansion

Step into another reality!
A new portal into the Dreamscape has been opened! The world of Vigil is warped by dreams and nightmares come to life. You must wield the power of the Dreamborn to battle back the forces of Delirium to save the realm.

•Use Insight to access powerful new Hero abilities to roll the Delirium Die.
•Recruit legendary heroes who have been warped by Delirium into new forms.
•Playable as an expansion to your Ascension game!

Contents:
• 99 Center Deck cards
• 6 Delirium Die cards
• 30 Insight cards
• Expansion Rules

Note: Ascension Center Deck Sets include every center deck card from the original set, along with unique card types, rules, and components needed to play. However, these sets do not include starting cards & the always available cards, honor tokens, a game board, or the original box and therefore require an appropriate base set which contain those necessary game components.

In the mountain city of Altera, the world’s conflicts are no longer settled on the battlefield, but at the Ascension Festival. Each year, the great factions gather in a shared belief: that ambition, ingenuity, and unity can shape a better future than war ever could.

Altera is a simultaneous-play, bag-building worker placement dice game. Each round, players draw and roll their dice, then strategically deploy Emissaries to shape their path to victory.

Where you send your Emissaries determines which of the three Guilds you gain Reputation with. At the end of each round, the player with the most Reputation in a Guild earns Prestige—then returns to the bottom of that Guild’s track, opening the door for others to rise.

Over seven rounds, you’ll build your engine, outmaneuver rivals, and earn enough Prestige to win and ascend to the High Council.

-description from designer

Futsal Face-Off is a fast-paced card and dice game that captures the intensity and momentum of futsal, where players take on the role of team managers making tactical decisions from the sidelines.

Build your team, choose your formation, and decide between playing it safe with strategy cards or taking risks with chance cards, all of which influence your scoring range, the dice values needed to score a goal.

Each turn, you roll to find the back of the net as the game swings back and forth, mirroring the highs and lows of a real match. The objective is simple: outscore your opponent by making the right decisions at the right time and capitalising on your key moments.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Pioneers of Pagonia: The Board Game
description:
As a pioneer, you lead the expedition to the uncharted lands of Pagonia. Starting from the shores where your ship first anchors, you build a thriving settlement, explore the surrounding wilderness, and establish stable supply and production chains.
Every decision has lasting consequences. Only those who plan ahead and build reliable structures step by step will successfully unlock the full potential of Pagonia. Whether managing resources, expanding your workforce, or optimizing your production lines — there is always another challenge waiting around the corner.

Based on the acclaimed video game Pioneers of Pagonia by Envision Entertainment, this board game brings the experience offline with a unique deck-building card mechanism featuring action cards upgradeable up to three times. With over 60 different buildings and professions, combined with a variable game setup, endless hours of engaging gameplay are guaranteed.

The planned Conflict expansion will raise the stakes even further, introducing watchtowers, fortresses, and units for a more confrontational experience — all brought to life with atmospheric original artwork from the video game.

—description from the designer

Taş Kağıt Makas - Güçler Tersine is a fast-paced, competitive card game that transforms the classic hand game into a chaotic, real-time tabletop experience.

The deck is played face-up, creating constant tension as players race to identify the winning symbol between the top card and the center card. The fastest player to react correctly claims the pile — but hesitation or mistakes can cost you the game.

Special “Reverse Power” cards flip the rules of the game, turning everything you know upside down and forcing players to adapt instantly.

With simple rules, quick rounds, and high-energy gameplay, Rock Paper Scissors is perfect for families, parties, and players who enjoy reflex-based challenges.

—description from the designer

You are the head of a Faction, leading a mission 3.5 billion light-years from Earth to explore and chart the distant Alcyoneus Galaxy.

During the game you will build a Starship to explore the galaxy, expand your Space Base to gain points and benefits, make a contraption to give you resources, come up with Innovations to bend the rules of the game, fulfil Achievements and reach end game Goals to score points. There are many paths you can take to victory.

There are five actions, each with three options depending on how much you wish to pay. If you can't afford to pay for an action you can use the free option, but this will let all the other players perform a reaction.

The game ends when all the sectors of the galaxy have been explored, putting the players in control of the length of the game. Finish with the most points and you win.

—description from the designer

Scout is a tactical card game where players build their own football squad by managing transfers and completing hidden formations.

Each player receives a secret formation card with two possible squad structures. Throughout the game, players reveal footballer cards and place them into the transfer market, or strategically acquire entire rows to strengthen their team.

Timing and decision-making are key:
Will you wait for better players to appear, or secure a row before your opponents take it?

The first player to complete one of their hidden formations — with the exact number of goalkeeper, defenders, midfielders, and forwards — wins the game instantly.

With simple rules and meaningful choices, Scout offers a clever mix of planning, risk-taking, and competition in a fast-paced football-themed experience.

—description from the designer

Aklım Sende is a two-player social guessing game about connection, intuition, and how well you truly understand each other.

Each round, players are given a question — but instead of answering for themselves, they try to predict what the other person would say. Both players secretly write their answers, then reveal them at the same time.

If the answers match, they earn the card. If not, the moment is lost.

After a series of questions, players compare how many matches they achieved and discover their “relationship score,” revealing just how in sync they really are.

Simple, personal, and often surprising, Aklım Sende creates meaningful moments, laughter, and unexpected insights between players.

—description from the designer
Expansion for Deckers. More infos to be announced.

Players battle their Pokémon by rolling dice to determine their moves' effects. In addition to customizable energy dice, the player's Pokémon is a die as well.

Players take turns choosing a move from their movepool, then rolling their dice to see if they have the required energy for the move to succeed. Additional effects, positive or negative, are determined by which orientation their character die lands in. Effects may add bonus damage, affect future dice availability, and more.

The player who reduces their opponent's Pokémon to 0 HP wins the battle.

Crack Rimes is the categories game… but in reverse. No starting letters: here, each player holds cards with a rhyme sound and must come up with an answer from the current list that ends with that sound. A word, a phrase—anything goes, as long as it rhymes.

Each player receives 8 cards (blue deck). On their turn, they give a valid answer that matches one of their cards and place it down. The first player to get rid of all their cards wins the round.

ACTION cards spice things up: Reverse to change the direction of play, Stop to block an opponent, Swap to exchange hands, and +1/+2/+3 cards to force others to draw. The game is played over 3 winning rounds.

—description from the publisher

Crack Rimes, c'est le petit bac… mais à l'envers. Pas de lettres de départ : ici, chaque joueur a en main des cartes portant une rime, et doit trouver une réponse de la liste du moment qui se termine par ce son. Un mot, une phrase, tout est permis — l'essentiel, c'est que ça rime.
Chaque joueur reçoit 8 cartes (paquet bleu). À son tour, il propose une réponse valide qui correspond à l'une de ses cartes, et la pose. Le premier à se débarrasser de toutes ses cartes remporte la manche.
Les cartes ACTION pimentent la partie : Inverse pour changer le sens du jeu, Stop pour bloquer un adversaire, Swap pour échanger sa main, et les cartes +1/+2/+3 pour infliger des pioches. Une partie se joue en 3 manches gagnantes.

—description from the publisher (French)

Take your Pagonia experience to the next level. The Conflict Expansion introduces a more confrontational dimension to the game, while keeping the beloved deck-building mechanism at its core. Defend your settlement against roaming wild boars, thieves, and bandits — and keep a close eye on your rivals. Fortresses and watchtowers strengthen your defenses, while strategically deployed units help you outmaneuver the competition in the race to dominate Pagonia. The same deep planning and production chain management you know — now with higher stakes, new threats, and fiercer competition.

—description from the publisher

The world of the Norse gods has descended into chaos. Everyone is fighting over the BigWig – the ultimate artifact, granting control over all worlds and all times.

BigWig is a dynamic trick-taking card game for 2–6 players in which all players secretly choose cards simultaneously. Each round, a scoring card determines the current trump colors and the value of the trick. Players compete to win these scoring cards, but ties cause rewards to slip beneath the altar, building tension for later turns.
Special Holmgang duel cards allow the player with the fewest points to challenge opponents directly, potentially winning not only a contested scoring card but also any unclaimed rewards hidden under the altar. In addition, powerful special cards introduce rule-bending effects, reversals, and tactical surprises.
Hand management is key: players can sacrifice cards to draw new ones using their personal “Mímir’s Well” card, carefully balancing short-term losses against long-term advantage.

The game ends after the fourth Holmgang duel, and the player with the most victory points claims the BigWig – and dominion over space and time.

BigWig combines classic trick-taking with modern twists such as hidden simultaneous play, shifting trump rules, and cinematic duel moments. It rewards clever timing, memory of played cards, and knowing when to step back to rebuild your hand instead of fighting for every point.

—description from the publisher

The beautiful peninsula of Greenwood is run by a circle of druids and advertised as a sanctuary for magical animals and ideal vacation spot. But that version of Greenwood is a lie.

In Greenwood, you play as Primal Spirits and lead your companions to save Greenwood.

On each turn, you face the difficult decision of whether to use the abilities of your own companions, to use the abilities of other players’ companions, or to send one of your companions into the forest to improve your wayfinding and rescue mystical creatures.

In the advanced game, each clan has a unique ability – a small additional rule that has a major impact on the game. What makes this special is that players can use the unique ability of any clan currently in play, not just their own.

The game is played over the course of 3 nights and whoever scored the most victory points wins.

—description from the publisher

Unlock! is a cooperative card game inspired by escape rooms that uses a simple system which allows you to search scenes, combine objects, and solve riddles. Play Unlock! to embark on great adventures, while seated at a table using only cards and a companion app that can provide clues, check codes, monitor time remaining, etc.

Unlock! New Game Adventures (a standalone follow up from Unlock!: Game Adventures) includes three separate scenarios for you to explore, each set in a different well-known board games universe:

Heat : un scénario de Thomas Cauet, illustré par Vincent Dutrait
7 Wonders : Scénario de Thomas Cauet, illustré par Paul Vérité
Carcassonnes : Scénario de Thomas Cauet, illustré par Cyrille Bertin


     Heat, a scenario illustrated by Vincent Dutrait
     7 Wonders, a scenario illustrated by Paul Vérité
     Carcassonne, a scenario illustrated by Cyrille Bertin


Note: Unlock! requires a free application to be downloaded from the App Store or Google Play. Once downloaded, an internet connection is not required during game play.

—description from the publisher's website
family: Game: Heat – Pedal to the Metal

description:
Celebrated as a modern classic, Ostia combines mancala-driven resource management and action selection with a race across the sea routes of ancient Rome.

Amass a mighty fleet of merchant ships and establish a sprawling collection of ports across the ocean. Gather vital trade goods, exotic beasts, and the support of the nobility at home to secure your place in the halls of history.

Crafty Games is extremely proud to offer this thoughtful upgrade to the original, folding in the first two expansions and deluxe content, with multiple upgrades for faster, smoother play.

The Ostia Mariner Edition includes all of the following in one "big box":


     Ostia Base Game
     Merchants Expansion
     Patronus Expansion
     Deluxe Content
     Custom Storage Solution


The Ostia Mariner Edition does not include the Pirates Expansion or other material not listed above. It also does not include double-layer boards as this publisher is prohibited from including these items.


This updated edition improves playability without altering gameplay. Among the improvements are:

? REFERENCE CARDS: One of the first things we knew we wanted for the Ostia Mariner Edition was a Reference card for each player. The game isn’t difficult to play, but the large number of actions and their interactions could stymie Harbor board decisions, especially for new players. That’s why our jumbo-sized* Reference cards focus an entire side on the steps of your turn, including detailed breakdowns of every action you can take. The Reference card’s back side focuses on how the game can end and how you score, so you always have a handy guide for what you’re trying to accomplish and how much time you have left to get it done.


     The Reference cards are the same height as the Harbor and Asset boards, letting you set it right alongside your play area.


? ORDER / DELIVER ICON: This is arguably the most extreme change we made to the game, and the one that might trip up certain players the most. We replaced and renamed one of the six core action icons — originally called Order and represented by the Amphora in the action frame because we thought it was unnecessarily confusing to have the same icon representing both an action and a resource. Deliver also seemed to fit what you were doing with this action more thematically than the original presentation.

? MAINBOARD: You’ll notice several things here. We used a much cleaner font for all the city names. We labeled the Local Sea and Distant Sea on either side of the dotted line (there’s another set of these labels at the bottom of the board). We also adjusted the colors of the Initial Port / Port tiles & spaces to aid low vision players. This cutaway reminds us of another change we desperately wanted to make but couldn’t, as it would have upset the custom insert: we wanted to increase the size of the Discovery tiles, as the icons on those can be very hard to read. Instead, our graphic designers spent a good deal of time spreading out those icons to help with their recognition. We showcase that a few graphics down.

? ASSET BOARDS: With the Asset boards — previously called the Construction / Shipbuilding Track boards — we aimed to simplify at every turn. We decluttered the background texture, which previously made everything hard to read. We moved the Triumph icons into both the tracks and added the immediate icon to them so it was obvious when and how you gained them. We also swapped the positions of the Shipbuilding and Construction tracks to match their presentation everywhere else in the game (in the rules, on the Harbor boards, and elsewhere).

? TILES & TOKENS: Our graphics team had a light touch on the tiles and tokens, and yet they touched nearly all of them. This is because our playtests identified that the immediate and endgame icons consistently improved a player’s understanding of how and when benefits are gained and used. We placed these icons nearly everywhere they applied.
Update! Since we first previewed the tiles and tokens (with the graphic above), we’ve revisited the Discovery tiles. Now, they include both the immediate and endgame icons next to the relevant benefits. You can see the final Discovery tile designs in the base game rulebook. We’ve left this original image alone so you can see the component’s evolution.

? ORDER CARDS: The Order cards had a strong design already. The primary issue with them was how the second optional cost and corresponding bonus Amphora benefit were presented. Consistency was needed on both sides of the card to graphically link each cost with the benefit across from it. This also gave us the opportunity to increase the size of the secondary costs, as those were very small on the original cards. As with most components in the game, we also added immediate and endgame icons where they were applicable.

? FREE ACTION: Here we see the action and upgrade portions of the Purchase segment of the Harbor board. Placing a building here upgrades the Free Action to trade gold for other resources. The original iconography for the Free Action matched nothing else in the game, least of all the other Action icons, so we developed something new. This altered icon resembles the Administrate icon but has enough differences to call out that the Free Action has its own special rules.
We also added reference for the Free Action in the Harbor board’s upper left, so you always know what the base action does before it’s upgraded. This reference is a little out of frame in the graphic above, in case anyone is wondering why it isn’t visible here. We wanted to focus this section on the new icon, but also mention the reference because it’s another small quality-of-life upgrade we’re proud to include.

? SCOREPAD: Finally, we have an improved scorepad. The original was way too busy. None of the split cells in the top three rows were necessary, so we eliminated all the breaks there. We also faded the art in the lower right corner. Once we’d cleared the field, as it were, we focused on making the scoring criteria in the left column as obvious as possible. Adding the Triumph icon to the first three scoring rows was a no brainer, as was updating the next three rows to match the backs of those components. We spread out the set icons and topped the pad off with the endgame icon that’s now so prevalent everywhere final scoring is applied.

—description from the publisher's website
family: Versions & Editions: Big Box Versions of Individual Games

description:
What tasty treats will your furry and feathery friends prefer today? Explore Regency England visiting scenic farms and estates and gathering adorable animals in this darling game for two to four players.

On your turn, you will pick which scene you visit in order to catch the attention of the animals there. Others may visit the same scene where a rivalry will ensue. Using your food cards to win the rivalry, you can have the animal come home with you. Some animals may grant Little Darling points and the game ends when someone earns 10 Little Darling points. Victory is acheived after bonus points are granted for whoever has the most food tokens of each category.

Little Endearment is the perfect introduction to Endearment for families and children as young as 5 years old.
family: Versions & Editions: Junior Versions of Grown-Up Games

description:
Players take on the roles of dogs trying to survive an outbreak. Some are infected and trying to keep it secret. Every turn is a risk.

GOAL
Be the last player remaining in the game.

OVERVIEW
Avoid drawing Virus cards and if drew one, keep it hidden and try to get rig of it. ... or try to infect other players
You must draw at least 1 card.

WIN
The last player without 2 revealed Virus CARDS wins

LOSE
A player with 2 OR MORE revealed Virus CARDS is out

Details
You can continue playing with 1 revealed Virus.
Players can check each other forcing to open their hand.

-description from designer

Expansion #13 - Vehicles #1 for the Warfighter - Vietnam series by Dan Verssen Games introduces new tactical elements with additional military vehicles and specialized skills tailored to jungle warfare. Set during the Vietnam conflict, this add-on challenges players to lead U.S. infantry through guerrilla tactics, ambushes, and search-and-destroy missions against Viet Cong and NVA forces. The box set enhances gameplay by reflecting the psychological and strategic complexities of dense terrain combat.

Batalla de temazos is a music party game where teams compete to prove their knowledge and quick thinking by naming songs within different categories. From holiday classics to hits from specific decades, every round turns into a lively musical showdown.

The game is played in two teams. In each round, players start with the “Battle” phase, where teams take turns singing songs that fit the given category. Each team has a limited time to respond, and the round continues until one team repeats a song, makes a mistake, or takes too long—giving the other team the win for that phase.

But winning the battle isn’t enough. In the “Finale” phase, two players from the winning team must silently think of the same answer to a given prompt. If they match, their team earns the point; if not, no points are awarded.

The first team to win 5 rounds takes the victory. With singing, teamwork, and a bit of mind-reading, this game delivers a fun and energetic experience for any group.

—description from the publisher

Competid para ver quién puede nombrar más canciones dentro de una categoría específica: ¿Villancicos?, ¿Españolas de los 2000? ¡Lo que sea!

Luego, justo cuando creáis haber ganado...¡dadle la vuelta a la tarjeta e intentad adivinar lo que está pensando alguien de vuestro equipo! ¿Listos para el concierto de vuestras vidas?

—description from the publisher (Spanish)

Calienta, que imitas is a lively party game all about acting, gestures, and creativity. Players must communicate without words, using only mime and body language to help their team guess a wide variety of characters, objects, and situations—ranging from simple ideas to more challenging and imaginative concepts.

The game is played in teams. On each turn, a player chooses a difficulty level (Easy or Expert), then performs one of the prompts on the card while their team tries to guess it within 30 seconds. Each correct guess earns a point, and the first team to reach 10 points wins.

The Easy side includes accessible prompts suitable for all ages, while the Expert side offers more complex and creative challenges. For a faster-paced experience, players can use the Quick Mode, where teams have one minute per round to guess as many cards as possible, scoring different points depending on the difficulty.

With over 500 acting prompts and plenty of laughter, this game is perfect for groups looking for an energetic and entertaining challenge.

—description from the publisher

¿Cómo de bien podéis expresaros sin palabras? Solo gestos, mímica y mucha imaginación. 

Más de 500 opciones para imitar desde una jirafa hasta Las meninas de Velázquez, ¡tú eliges la dificultad!

Pide a tu equipo que elija una letra aleatoriamente (A, B o C) y lee en alto la pista de lo que vas a representar. Los demás tendrán 30 segundos para adivinarlo. ¡Buena suerte!

—description from the publisher (Spanish)

Es mi cumple is a fun party game designed to celebrate a birthday while putting friendships to the test. Players compete to prove who knows the birthday person best, uncovering funny stories and surprising details along the way.

The game is played in two teams. In each round, the birthday person reads a question about their preferences, habits, or past experiences. Both teams discuss and choose their answer within a limited time. If only one team answers correctly, they score a point. If both teams get it right, a tie-breaker question is played, where teams must give the most accurate or fitting answer.

The first team to reach 5 points wins. With laughter, memories, and a bit of bluffing, this game guarantees an unforgettable birthday celebration.

—description from the publisher

¡Diversión asegurada hasta soplar las velas!

¿Sabréis a qué temperatura le gusta el agua de la ducha? ¿El nombre de sus padres?

Este juego es el regalo perfecto para descubrir quién conoce de verdad al cumpleañero, sacar historias del pasado y echarse unas risas que harán que este cumpleaños no se olvide fácilmente.

—description from the publisher (Spanish)

Mini juegos to-go is a portable party game that combines four different game modes into a single deck of cards, offering a variety of fast-paced and engaging challenges. With 200 topics to guess—100 objects and 100 people—it’s designed to be played anywhere, anytime.

Each card can be used in four different ways. In Who am I?, players guess their identity by asking yes-or-no questions. In Don’t Say It!, teams describe a word without using certain forbidden clues. In Guess in 3 Clues, players try to identify the answer with as few hints as possible to score more points. Finally, in Say as Many…, teams take turns naming items within a category until someone makes a mistake or hesitates.

Depending on the chosen game mode, players compete individually or in teams to score points by guessing correctly, describing effectively, or thinking quickly. The winner is determined by reaching a set number of points or winning rounds first, making each session dynamic and replayable.

—description from the publisher

4 juegos en 1! Disfruta de la diversión en cualquier momento y lugar: solo coge una tarjeta, elige uno de los cuatro modos de juego y ¡empieza a jugar!

    1 ¿Quién soy?: Adivina con preguntas de Sí o No.
    2 ¡No lo digas! Descríbelo sin usar los 3 conceptos.
    3 Adivina en 3 pistas: Léeselas a tu equipo una por una. ¡Cuantas menos necesiten, más puntos!
    4 Quién sabe más… Competid por equipos para ver quién puede decir más cosas que entren dentro de la categoría subrayada.


—description from the publisher (Spanish)

The rocky river ahead has claimed many boats, but this year will be your year! You’re sure of it. But can you guide the boat well enough to reach the rank of Captain?

Each turn a card numbered between 1 and 10 will be revealed. The players have to decide if the next card will be higher or lower. One of the cards is removed each round so there will not be perfect information leading players to push their luck to dodge the rocks. 
 Starting as a lowly swabby you can earn a promotion each time the number of cards in the round has reached 5-8 revealed cards. Players will use their scoring cards to show this turning them a quarter turn with each promotion. If a player can stay in the round until the 9th card is revealed, they will earn two promotions which is a half turn on their scoring card.

Players will find strategic depth figuring out what their vote will be as they are revealed simultaneously. Guess wrong and the player is out for the round!

Do you push your luck to get the promotion on your boat or is it better to hang back and wait to see if your opponents wipe out?
Only the luckiest can reach the rank of Captain earning their 4th rank!

To increase the challenge and variability swap in up to 4 effects cards to add more chaos to this addictive game! Players can add in one or more each time to find new and intriguing ways to play!

Win or lose, you’ll make a splash!

—description from the designer

A Greek board game released in the 70's themed after the attack on Pearl Harbor.

The game is played with two players and contains 2 battlefields, the allies and the axis, 26 cards, 1 roulette and 4 pawns - miniature airplanes. One player takes on the role of the allies and the other of the axis. The players move using roulette. The game begins by placing the miniature airplanes, one player on number 1 and the other on number 16. Each battlefield has numbers and each time the player lands on a square with a number, he follows the instructions written in the instruction booklet, which indicate which of his opponent's planes depicted on the board he destroys and accordingly places a card in the corresponding square.

The winner is the player who first manages to destroy all of his opponent's planes and place all 14 cards on his opponent's battlefield.

In Dogs with Shoes, players compete to collect adorable dogs and outfit them with matching shoes — four shoes per dog makes a completed pup. Dogs come in seven breeds, and shoe cards must match a dog's breed to be played on it, though the versatile Cool Dog wildcard accepts shoes of any type.

On each turn, a player takes exactly one action: play a dog card to their play area, attach a matching shoe to one of their dogs, send a Robber to steal shoes (or even unshod dogs) from an opponent, launch a Firework to scare a rival's dog and prevent it from receiving shoes, calm one of their own scared dogs, buy a card from the central market by discarding one, flush the entire market for new options, or simply draw a card. Guard cards can be played reactively to block incoming Robbers. Hands are limited to five cards, and most actions involve playing a card and drawing a replacement.

Completed dogs with exactly four shoes are permanently safe from Robbers and Fireworks. Scared dogs cannot receive shoes and are especially vulnerable — Robbers can strip multiple shoes from a scared dog in a single action. The deck composition scales with player count, adjusting the number of breeds and cards to keep the game balanced.

The standard game ends the instant a player completes their third dog. A scored variant awards points for partially and fully dressed dogs, and a quick variant for larger groups reduces the target to two completed dogs.

—description from the publisher

The Wild Vengeance of Witches is a miniatures agnostic skirmish adventure board game for 1-3 player (Solo or Co-op). You lead a coven of witches against relentless townsfolk, seeking revenge for them kidnapping your familiars.

Customize 6 unique Witches, fight endless swarms of enemies, and play through 2 narrative-driven scenarios. Includes a 20+ page printable rulebook, custom maps, tokens, and cards. Every success is an objective fulfilled. Every failure fuels your Cauldron, turning bad luck into powerful, vengeful magic.

To win, you must complete your objectives before the endless swarms of townsfolk overwhelm you.

For fans of tactical combat, narrative stories, puzzly dice-chucking, special abilities, and cute familiars.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Dice Tiny Town Duel: Animal and Split Shape
description:
Dice Tiny Town Duel: Animal includes 2 modules.


     The Animal module adds a new scoring element: animal points. Each game, you draw and use two cards among 8 animal cards. Recommended for experienced players who are already familiar with the base rules. This module is also compatible with "Dice Tiny Town (2023)".



     The Split Shapes module adds new shape cards to your hand. Once per game, each player may use a shape split into two pieces instead of a single connected shape. Recommended for those who want the flexibility to fill in tight spaces.


—description from the publisher

A team (2 X2)Civil War game encompassing the entire war. Many aspects are covered including economics, blockades, Rebel raiders, hostile Indians, terrain, weather, rail river and canal movement,and political considerations. Neutral states play a significant roll.
Based loosely on Axis and Allies system with unit upgrades and promotions available.
20 decks of historical cards pour tactical and strategic considerations into the mix.

—description from the designer

Eu Vi! - Capivara is a lively spotting game that invites players to look closely, think quickly and react before anyone else.
As each Character Card is revealed, everyone searches the Board Cards to find the capybara and other striking animals inspired by Brazil’s rich wildlife.

Easy to learn and great fun for all ages, it is a game that brings children and adults together around the table. Spot it first, call it out, and score points as the race unfolds.

boardgame: Tic Tac Go: Tic Tac Toe on a whole new level
description:
Tic Tac Go is a fast-paced 2-player strategy game where rock-paper-scissors rules combine with Chess move rules on a Tic Tac Toe 3X3 game board. It’s Tic Tac Toe on a whole new level. The simplicity of Tic Tac Toe with the thinking required in Chess. Players move rock paper and scissor chips onto the game board from their stacks of unplayed chips, following rock paper scissor stacking rules, by stacking rocks onto scissors, paper onto rocks and scissors onto papers. On each turn, players can choose to stack a chip from their stack of chips onto the board, or choose to move chips already on the board.  Players can move their own chips which are already on the board to new locations using Chess Move rules. Rock chips move like chess knights. Paper chips move like a chess rooks. Scissor chips move like chess bishops.
Goal is to get 3 of your own chips in a row to win, but with a twist. Three in a row of the same chips, ie, 3 rocks, or 3 papers, or 3 scissor chips in a row is a soft win, like Check in Chess. Your opponent can cancel your soft win with a strategic Chess move. If a soft win is cancelled, the game continues until someone wins with a noncancellable soft win, or with a Rock Paper Scissor hard win row.
A hard win is a Rock Paper Scissor row, with a Rock chip, a paper chip, and a scissor chip in the same row in Rock Paper Scissor order. This is a noncancellable win. Like Checkmate in chess.
When a player moves a chip already on the board to stack upon the opponent’s chip using a chess move, the chip under the moved chip becomes active again. This newly active chip can change the entire game. Memory is the key to winning. Players must try to remember which chips are covered by a stacked chip.
Players can also strategically move their chips between board positions using a castling move, similar to castling in chess, to exchange board positions between two chips on the board. This type of move does not uncover any chips, so its safer than moving a chip onto an opponent’s chip.
The game is simple to learn, but with surprising depth.

An expansion to the modern-era Warfighter game that provides modern United States Marine Corps soldiers, equipment, and skills.

     13 soldiers cards
     7 NPS (non-player soldier) cards
     7 SQS (squad soldier) cards
     12 weapon cards
     7 equipment cards
     10 skills cards, including the 'Gung Ho' skill that is only usable by USMC soldiers


Back of Box:
Adds 56 Cards to your loadout!  This expansion gives you all the necessary Soldiers, Weapons, and Equipment to deploy these elite Warfighters.

The game box says "c. 2023" but the game was first shipped in 2026.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Warfighter: Multi-Era Expansion #4 – Skill and Gear Cards!
description:
Contains cards expanding different Warfighter base games:
- Modern (Daylight) - 35 cards
- WWII (both Europe and Pacific) - 39 cards
- Fantasy - 20 cards
- 'Nam - 18 cards

From the box:
"Let's Move!"
Adds 112 Skill and Gear Cards to your Loadout!

The box says "c. 2019" but the expansion was first released in 2026.

An expansion with a new set of missions, objectives, locations, and enemies.  The set also includes a few British soldiers and their equipment.

     Player cards (soldiers, equipment, ...) -- 20 total cards
     Adversary cards (missions, enemies, ...) -- 36 total cards


From the box back:
"Roll in!"
Adds 56 cards to your loadout!  This expansion gives you all the necessary Soldiers, Weapons, and Equipment to deploy these elite Warfighters!

The box lists "c. 2023" but the expansion was first released in 2026.

An expansion with a new mission setting (Middle East) and US soldiers to help complete it.

     25 player cards
     31 adversary cards


back of the box:
"It was a war without a front line!"
Adds 56 Cards to your loadout!  This expansion gives you all the necessary Soldiers, Weapons, and Equipment to employ these elite Warfighters.

The box lists "c. 2023" but it was first released in 2026.

El Delirio is an intellectual dispute for two players. It consists of a 120-square board. This is the Level 1 version for the year 2026, an absolutely attractive and easy-to-play version. At the start of a match, each player leads a total of 21 pyramids: one red pyramid (Leader), three black pyramids (Objectives), and 17 colored pyramids. The goal of the game is to capture the rival's red leader pyramid or any of the three black ones. The first player to achieve this goal wins the match. The rules can be learned in just 10 minutes, and no further theory study is required.

—description from the designer

Modern Day #75 - Winter Pack Focused on winter warfare, this Battle Pack introduces snow-covered terrains, cold-weather gear, and the logistical challenges of fighting in freezing conditions. Players will manage camouflage, snow hostiles, and visibility issues while adapting to icy battlefields. Scenarios range from mountain assaults to Arctic bases that must be eliminated.

56 cards that allow missions to be performed in cold climates.
- 5 mission cards
- 5 objective cards
- 16 location cards
- 22 hostile cards
- 5 event cards (shuffled into the hostile deck)
- 1 skill card
- 2 equipment cards

Box says "c. 2025" but the first edition was released in 2026.

boardgame: Bite Me: Stomach Wars – The Expansion Pack
description: 14 new cards, adds rotten food & up to 2 more players

This is a building-type game where you pay the necessary food costs by discarding cards from the table and then play new cards from your hand.

The cards on your table will change, with various creatures coming and going, creating a dynamic ecosystem!

Furthermore, discarded cards can be picked up by other players, making them difficult to discard.

Enjoy this compact ""scrap and build"" game with just 54 cards.

—description from the publisher

Players are summoners of elemental, be called ""Elementalians."" The goal is summon spirits to defeat your opponent.
This is a ladder climbing game. Players have some cards in their hand, and the goal is play all the cards in hand asap. To play card, your play must stronger than previous. Player can use ""BOOST"" or ""Fusion"" to make their play stronger, but any time they use ""BOOST"", they also gains ""HEAT"" and need cooldown first if they are overheated.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: じゃんけんリズム (Rock-Paper-Scissors Rhythm Game)
description:
・A party game for 10 or more people that’s guaranteed to get everyone laughing!
・Just line up the cards in two rows and take turns making the same hand gestures
・It looks easy, but no one can get it right—making it a huge hit!

This game involves making the “same hand gesture” as the one shown on the rock-paper-scissors cards in a row, all in time with the rhythm.

It looks incredibly simple, but when you try it, you’ll experience the strange sensation of no one being able to get it right. Even though the rules are super simple, the fact that no one can do it means players take turns, creating a lively and fun atmosphere—it’s a brand-new party game!

It supports a wide range of players from 2 to 15, but you can accommodate even larger groups by using two or more sets!

If you just want to have fun without thinking too much, this is the game for you!

・Rock-Paper-Scissors Cards… 36 cards (63×88 mm)
・Metronome System URL (Adjustable difficulty)
・Instruction sheet (1)"

—description from the publisher

"19,000 units sold in Japan in just one year! The super-popular meat game!

Play memory and karuta with yakiniku cards!
Even though no one can remember where the cards are, somehow everyone manages to play!
It’s a mysterious game that doesn’t teach you anything about cuts of meat or anything else—it’s completely useless!

This is a karuta game where you play memory and Go Fish with yakiniku cards.
At first glance, you might think, “There’s no way this works…” but when you actually play, everyone somehow manages to get the hang of it.
It’s a strange game that doesn’t teach you anything about meat cuts or anything else—it’s completely useless!

You can even mix and match with previous editions!

・30 meat cards
・2 instruction sheets
(63 x 88 mm cards)"

-from the publisher

"What is the setting?
The game is set in a whimsical, dream-like world where dogs and cats form a marching band. The original conductor has fallen ill, and the players are asked to step in as substitute conductors to organize the band’s formation for an upcoming marching band competition. Depending on the chosen mode, the players either work together to achieve a high score or compete against each other.

What is the goal?
The goal is to place cards to form a 5×5 grid and score points by creating specific card combinations (“hands”) in each row and column.

In Competitive Mode, players compare their scores, and the higher score wins.
In Cooperative Mode, players combine their scores and aim for a high total score.

How do you play?
Players take turns placing one card adjacent to already placed cards, without breaking the 5×5 grid shape.
One player is responsible for columns (vertical scoring) and the other for rows (horizontal scoring).
As cards become surrounded on two sides, they are flipped face up.
When all cards have been placed, the game ends and scoring begins. Points are calculated for each row and column based on instrument combinations, with bonus points awarded for sets sharing the same attributes (such as costume color or animal type).
An additional diagonal scoring rule applies, and optional special cards can be added in competitive mode for more advanced play."

—description from the publisher
publishers: ふつかめのだいまじん

boardgame: ピラミッドバトル（Pyramid Battle）
description:
• What is the setting?
Players are apprentice workers building a pyramid using numbered stones.

• What is the goal?
Be the first player to complete your pyramid following the placement rules.

• How do you play?
Players take turns choosing one stone from a shared set, place it on their pyramid in the correct order (smaller numbers on top/left, larger on bottom/right), and pass the remaining stones to the next player. If you cannot place a stone, you discard it. The first to complete the pyramid wins.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Lord of the Rings: Ascension
description:
The Lord of the Rings: Ascension is a deckbuilding game for 1–4 players that takes you on an adventure through Middle-earth across three interconnected Ascension sets. It features the beloved mechanics of the Ascension Deckbuilding Game, reimagined to capture the wonder of The Lord of the Rings, from its legendary heroes and villains to the corruption of The One Ring.

The fate of the world hangs by a thread. Will you rally the Free Peoples—Elves, Humans, Dwarves, and Hobbits—or turn toward the Shadow of Mordor? Recruit heroes, acquire powerful cards, and shape your strategy as you build your strength, all while confronting the quiet, ever-present pull of The One Ring. The game is fast to learn and difficult to master; each choice carries weight, and the path to victory is never without cost.

—description from the publisher

Beetle Racing is a fast-paced, strategic racing game where five unique beetles compete for the crown. In this game, you aren’t just cheering for a beetle; you are a secret supporter using tactical card play and specialized insect abilities to ensure your beetle reaches the podium.
Players take turns playing Action Cards to move beetles forward. If a beetle lands on its matching color tile, it triggers a Matching Event, allowing you to use its special power or take a coin. The race ends immediately when two beetles cross the finish line. Will your chosen beetles take 1st and 2nd place, or will your opponents' strategies leave you in the dust? Strategic, unpredictable, and exciting—get ready for the wildest race in the insect kingdom!"

—description from the publisher

"The Master Artist hosts a three-day Art Creation Camp, inviting artists from around the world. This time, perfection is not the goal; what matters is the keen insight of your soul’s creativity.
Each day, players create works based on available materials and the day’s theme, gaining diverse perspectives by observing each other’s creations.
Beyond creation, the Master sets a mysterious task: each day, three prompt  cards remain unaddressed by any player. This challenges not only creativity but also observation and intuition in an artistic detective game.
Can you discern the unseen and identify the absent inspirations? This is a test of creativity and insight; only disciples truly attuned in mind and art can earn the Master’s recognition."

-from the publisher

Inspired by classic scifi cinema, Earth vs the Invaders throws you into the front lines of humanity's desperate struggle against otherworldly horrors!

This asymmetric wargame pits brave soldiers, tanks, and scientists against three terrifying enemy factions: radiation-mutated insects, sinister flying saucers, and an all-consuming alien blob.

Designed for 15mm miniatures, the game's dynamic reinforcement system keeps both sides guessing as the battle escalates from local response to full military intervention.

Grab a box of WWII miniatures and some toy bugs.

The fate of the Earth hangs in the balance!

Features:

     Three different Invader factions: the Giant Insects, the Flying Saucers, and the gelatinous Culture!
     Six scenarios inspired by classic 1950's films. Play out the entire invasion of Anytown, USA!
     A Reinforcements system that lets you adapt to your opponent in real time.
     Campaign rules in which you unlock more powerful units as you progress.
     Rules for large 2v2 games. Earth vs the Invaders is the perfect convention or club night game!


Optional unit cards are also available for sale. The included unit stats are all available in the book, these cards make it more convenient to reference.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: ダーティ ディール ゲーム (DIRTY DEAL GAME)
description:
"Setting
DIRTY DEAL GAME is a tense negotiation game set in a world of risky deals and fragile trust.
Players take on the role of partners trying to achieve a shared goal… but each player secretly has the option to betray the other.

Goal
Work together to achieve mutual success — or turn the tables and win alone by betraying at the perfect moment.
If both players succeed, you both win.
But if only one succeeds, that player becomes the sole winner.

How to Play
Players negotiate, make decisions, and take actions while carefully reading each other's intentions.
Every round, you must choose whether to cooperate or act in your own interest.
The game is simple to learn but creates deep psychological tension, where trust, timing, and mind games determine the outcome."

-from the publisher

This puzzle game, which won the International Puzzle Design Competition 2025, involves fitting four identical pieces into a box with a partially blocked entrance.
“Fudge” is a type of confection similar to caramel, known for its crumbly, melt‑in‑the‑mouth texture.
The name is inspired by the feel of solving the puzzle.

—description from the publisher

You have four pieces of different shapes and a 3×3×3 box.
The puzzle includes two challenges: fitting them into the box, and fitting them into a 2×3×4 space as well.
Each challenge has only one unique solution.

Modern Day #66 - Norway and Denmark This expansion focuses on the strategic contributions of Norway and Denmark to modern NATO operations. Players will navigate Arctic warfare scenarios and urban missions using state-of-the-art equipment such as Norway's HK416 Carbine UGL and Denmark's AX50 Rifle. It emphasizes these nations' skill in operating in harsh environments.

"Fjor Raet Frykt Ingen!"
A 56-card expansion that adds soldiers and equipment representing modern-day Norwegian and Danish elite forces.  As allied nations, you can use a mix of soldiers from both nations and share equipment.  The Norwegian soldiers have access to the new 'nordic' skill that gives experience from surviving the cold.  The Danish soldiers have access to the new 'push forward' skill, allowing them free entry into hostile locations.

It is the year 1551, and feudal Japan is in the midst of war - the Sengoku period. Across the land, the ambition and power of five daimyō are rising. Take on the role of one of them, overthrow the old shogunate, and unite Japan under your rule! A new area control game for 1–5 players, the third entry in a series following Bretwalda and Principes.

To unify Japan, players will learn new arts, build mighty structures, collect rice taxes, recruit armies of Samurai and Ashigaru, and of course wage wars. However, the key to victory will be weaving a political network of alliances with previously neutral clans. In this way, players will not control just their own clan, but an entire coalition supporting their cause. Once their power has sufficiently matured, victory can be secured in only one way — by taking control of central Kyoto.

Modern Day #70 - Biohazard Hostiles! This alternate-reality Battle Pack throws players into a Middle Eastern outbreak of the undead. Featuring specialized units, improvised weapons, and fortified defenses, players must survive waves of mutants while managing dwindling resources. Scenarios range from evacuations to desperate last stands, blending survival horror with modern military tactics.

A 56-card expansion that allows you to fight zombies in the post-apocalyptic United States.
- 4 soldier cards
- 1 NPS card
- 1 SQS card
- 3 weapon cards
- 6 equipment cards
- 5 skill cards
- 4 mission cards
- 4 objective cards
- 10 location cards
- 18 hostile cards (zombies)

Modern Day #68 - Sweden and Finland Set in the Nordic regions, this expansion highlights Sweden and Finland's military capabilities. Players will deploy advanced weaponry such as the Swedish AK5C Carbine and Finnish 7.62 KK 62 Machine Gun. Scenarios emphasize winter warfare, border defense, and joint operations, reflecting the nations' commitment to regional security and self-reliance.

A 56-card expansion with soldiers and equipment based on modern-day elite forces from Sweden and Finland.  The Swedes have access to the 'Alpine' skill.  The Fins have access to the 'Arctic Trained' skill.  Both can share equipment and mix their forces.

Modern Day #74 - Missions & Objectives This expansion focuses on Missions and Objectives for the Middle East and Jungle locations, offering players a variety of scenarios that test their strategic planning and adaptability. Players will navigate complex operations ranging from high-stakes extractions to critical supply runs under fire. Each mission presents unique challenges, requiring precise coordination, resource management, and decision-making. Newly introduced Modifiers keep gameplay dynamic, forcing players to plan ahead to successfully complete their objectives.

A 56-card expansion with many new mission and objective cards for the Middle East environment.
- 20 mission cards
- 26 objective cards
- 6 hostile cards (referenced by objectives)
- 4 action cards (4 copies of "Let's End This")

Trivia Heads combines fast-paced questions, a fresh format, and quirky artwork to create a social, high-energy trivia experience. Whether it’s game night at home, a pub with friends, or a spontaneous trivia session, Trivia Heads brings people together and makes them argue about facts they swear they know.

Taking turns, one player acts as the Question Giver while another becomes the Active Answerer. The Question Giver draws a double-sided question card and reveals the category. The Active Answerer must then choose from three difficulty levels, deciding how confident they feel about answering. The harder the question, the greater the potential reward.

Correct answers earn Victory Cards, which provide points, sabotages, or lifelines. Play sabotage cards to hinder opponents or steal questions, and use lifeline cards to improve your chances of success or increase your reward.

The first player to collect 10 points from Victory Cards wins! Play individually or in teams, and adjust the target score to suit your group - Trivia Heads is as flexible as you need it to be.

"The duty of stubborn resistance"
A 56-card expansion providing elite soldiers and equipment from Belgium and the Netherlands.  Belgium soldiers have access to the "Coordinated Fire" skill, and Netherland soldiers have access to the "Make Do" skill.  Both skills allow XP to be spent for temporary combat advantages.

Modern Day #71 - Belgium and Netherlands Highlighting two key NATO allies, this expansion features combined arms operations across the lowlands of Belgium and the Netherlands. Players will command iconic units like Dutch Marines and Belgian Commandos, along with advanced equipment such as the SCAR-H PR Rifle and the AX762 Rifle. Scenarios focus on managing limited supplies and achieving multinational coordination.

boardgame: The Company Rules: Rules for Science Fiction Planet Forces
description:
The Company Rules, TCR is a game set in a plausible future.

Small number forces attack each other in small actions centred around resources.

Scale

     Each square= 300 metres.
     Each model represents 1 real soldiers.


Game setting

     Small raiding force versus defence force.


   
Game scenario generator

     An “Approach” grid. Both players manoeuvre up and down a grid  in order to try to arrive at an advantageous level (5 minutes).


What is needed

     10 D6 in total. 
     5 by 3 feet gridded table, 6" squares. 
     template or scenery, placed by both players.


Motivation mechanism
Each base (usually about 9 per player) acts independently.

Action/moving mechanism
TCR is a fast game. 
Each base may only carry out 1 action per own turn. 

     Shoot,
     move,
     put up blockers,
     launch mine.


Typical force composition

     Typical force= 10 foot bases (20 figures).
     Most PP games use about 100 figures a side. 


Army lists:
1. Authority
2. Precinct
3. Distance
4. Free staters
5. Indies
6. Bonkers
7. Creepers (The aliens)

PeterPig offers a complete range of associated figures and items to play TCR.

Mister Vampire has hidden all the candies. The players are searching for them. When a player finds the right candy, they move one step along the path. Everyone tries to be the first. Be careful – watch out for the black cat! By placing the third black cat behind the Vampire, he raises his head to see which player was unlucky.

The game ends when one player reaches the last space on the path. That player wins the game. The game also ends if only one player remains. That player is declared the winner.

—description from the publisher

Relive Alexandria’s golden age as the intellectual capital of the world, in the era of the Great Library and Lighthouse, through a fully asymmetrical competitive game built on constructive, positive interaction.

In a world guarded by the Ptolemaic Army and thriving with Harvesters, Crafters, Architects, Merchants, Priests, Philosophers, and Seafarers, lead any faction in a truly asymmetrical journey where each pursues victory along a different path.

In this living society, every action you take fuels Alexandria’s growth. A miner striking iron grows richer when he trades his ore through a merchant to the weaponsmith, who forges powerful arms that strengthen the Ptolemaic Army and protect all citizens. Your choices may uplift everyone at the table, however, the Player who contributes the most to elevating Alexandria to its greatest glory will be remembered as its true architect.

To achieve this, you must discover your unique actions and draft the dice that best let you trade resources, grow your population, acquire luxuries, unlock and upgrade your faction’s powers, and earn the most Victory Points across 5 Rounds.

—description from the publisher

Rush Hour Duel is the 2-player edition of the classic traffic jam logic game! Challenge a friend to a head-to-head race to escape the gridlock. Use quick thinking and swift moves to slide vehicles out of your way before your opponent reaches the finish. Includes 40 brand-new challenges designed to keep both kids and adults engaged in the ultimate puzzle showdown.

Includes:
• 1 game board
• 24 vehicles (12 per player)
• 40 tasks and solutions
family: Versions & Editions: Two-Player Versions of More-Player Games

description:
Battle Box - Age of Sail is a complete naval wargame within a small tin box that fits in your pocket, perfect for a quick game on the go or even full naval campaigns. It includes ships, battle mat, dice, narrative scenarios, and cards for in-game effects such as critical hits and special ship rules.

The set of four ships, each of a different colour, are used to represent generic men-of-war and merchant ships. The status, crew quality, ship upgrades, and critical hits on each ship are tracked by means of cards and a token matching the ship's colour.

The rules are perfect for fun and short battles at sea during the 18th and 19th centuries, such as French privateers attacking an East India Company convoy, or a British squadron intercepting the Spanish treasure fleet. Easy to learn but with a tactical depth that makes strategic choices decisive.

Players roll for initiative and take turns activating each of their ships, with advantages for ships sailing in line. The wind might change depending on the initiative roll, forcing players to adapt to the new situation. When a ship is activated, it can sail on the hex grid provided with the game, fire broadsides (devastating critical hits might happen too, such a bringing down a mast), and perform crew tests to repair the ship, maneuver for better positioning or board enemy ships. The game continues until the enemy ships strike their colours or the scenario objectives are met.

—description from the designer

GRIZZO is the most feared and most successful grizzly on this side of the Yukon River. Legend has it that not a single salmon slips through his paws when he’s fishing. Challenge accepted! Bold and fearless, you compete to see who can snatch the most and the best salmon. So sharpen your claws and get started, but steer clear of the stinky carcasses!

Whoever ends up with the juiciest haul wins.

—description from the publisher

In Leaves, players race to complete a stack of autumn leaves by selecting one of the three cards in their hand and combining it with the eight face-up cards on the table. Leaves are layered according to their shared features, and because every card is unique—defined by its leaf type, color, and number—players must quickly spot the correct arrangement.

All players play simultaneously. In each challenge, everyone works from the same display of eight face-up cards, trying to be the first to use one card from hand to complete the stack correctly. The first player to do so wins the challenge, and the first player to win three challenges wins the game.

-description from publisher
publishers: Play With Us Design (玩聚設計)

description:
"Freedom is not Free"
A 56-card expansion recreating the battle between US Marines and Iraqi forces.  It provides a new Middle East setting and USMC forces to support the player.

     4 soldier cards (USMC)
     2 TS cards (USMC soldier cards with no action cards)
     3 SQS cards (USMC squad soldiers)
     4 skill cards
     2 equipment cards
     1 weapon card
     4 mission cards
     4 objective cards
     10 location cards
     5 event cards (shuffle into hostile deck)
     7 hostile cards


—description from the publisher

"Todo por la patria!"
A 56-card expansion providing soldiers and equipment for elite soldiers from Spain and Portugal.  Spanish soldiers have access to the "Bounding" skill, allowing them to suppress hostiles as they enter a location.  Portuguese soldiers have access to the "Contact Front" skill, allowing them to react to hostiles appearing in their location.

—description from the publisher

With this expansion, you will be able to send Time Travelers to the future, so that they can prepare the land for your coming and return as knowledgeable Scholars.
And you will be able to turn back the time on some actions you did, by unlocking the action space. That way, you will be able to use the action a second time in the same round.
And with this expansion, not only will the Competencies be at different places like before, but there will also be more Competencies than you can fit onto the Innovation display. In some games, you’ll therefore have to adapt yourself to doing without the one Competency you usually rely on.

Contains new factions, competencies, palace and innovation tiles, book action and round bonus tiles, and a new city tile.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: All Time Wrestling: Gail Kim Expansion
description:
Add Gail Kim as a playable wrestler.

Contains 2 profile cards, 1 player board and 16 attack cards.

You will require ATW: Women's Edition

By skillfully laying out your string,you collect shells for your necklace and clean up the beach. If your shell necklace is also mirror-image(symmetrical), your points are doubled. The person with the most points wins the game.

Each player receives one (1) string. Mix all the shells, shards, and crabs, and drop these items randomly into the center of the table so that they lie in a colorful jumble. Spread the items apart so that they no longer touch each other. These items form the beach.

You play as many rounds as there are players. The person who was on vacation most recently gets the shell with the pearl and a leftover string to collect items with. At the end of the round, this shell and the string are passed on, so that everyone has been the starting player once.

As soon as the last piece of glass is removed from the center of the table, the round is over and you begin scoring. The person with the most points wins the round. Whoever wins the most rounds wins the entire game. In the event of a tie after a round or at the end of the entire game, the players with the same number of points or wins present their shell necklaces. The others may choose one shell necklace as the winner, and the owner of that necklace wins the round or the game.

—description from the publisher

Players collect tokens by moving their capybara pawns around the board. Tokens can then be exchanged for cards – one card from each of the four categories: Care, Food, Toy, and Playmate.
The first player to collect a complete set of 4 different cards wins the game!

—description from the designer

Diables is a mid-heavy eurogame designed by Dani Garcia for 1–4 players in which you lead a colla in the spectacular Mediterranean tradition of the Ball de Diables.

Manage your troupe of performers, coordinate different types of fireworks, and adapt to evolving events to stage the most breathtaking street performances. Carefully plan your actions to captivate the crowd, build momentum across the parade, and earn the highest prestige.

A game of Diables is played over 3 rounds, each divided into 4 phases:

Performances: Players take turns sending their performers into the streets to carry out actions and engage the crowd. This is the core of the game, where key decisions are made.

Passage of Time: The time marker advances, intermediate scoring is resolved, and carts are refilled with new fireworks.

Advance the Parade: Crowd cards move forward along the streets, and performers return to players’ boards, ready for the next round.

Attract the Crowd: New crowd cards are added to each street, bringing fresh opportunities and challenges.

Balance timing, resource management, and positioning to outshine rival colles and deliver the most unforgettable performances.

—description from the publisher

An expansion for Aeon’s End that consists of milestones from The Beyond the breach Gamefound campaign. It contains:

Mages:
Qu: the changeling
Inco: the outsider

Nemesis:
The timeless

10 tresures
2 gems
2 relic
3 spell

A social deduction game designed for everyone. Reverspell brings a touch of the arcane to your next game night with an easy-to-learn system that gets non-gamers and veterans bluffing in minutes. Ditch the heavy rulebooks and dive into a world of vintage mystery, deception, and high-energy social play.

—description from the publisher

Pueblos: México is a vibrant journey through the heart of Mexico, where you and your friends become travelers discovering the soul of its most enchanting towns. From cobblestone streets to bustling markets, you'll collect souvenirs, snap unforgettable photos, and earn anecdote cards that tell the story of your trip.

On your turn, you'll take two trips across the shared map. Each move lets you place a new traveler or guide an existing one to a new location. Upon arrival, you collect a souvenir—a hand-painted ceramic, a colorful textile, or a treasured snapshot.

When a town's souvenirs run out, claim it as a stamp, then reveal a new town and refill it with tokens drawn from a bag—some face-up, some face-down, adding a touch of mystery to your adventure.

Complete a traveler's route to return them to your board and gain an Anecdote card—each one unlocks surprising new ways to score points at the end of the game, turning your journey into a personal story.

But not every moment goes as planned. Share a location with another traveler or stray from your route, and you'll earn bad experiences that cost you victory points at the final tally.

The game ends once every traveler has finished their route. The player with the most points wins, having felt the most warmly embraced (apapachado) by Mexico's folklore, flavors, and friendly spirit.

Plan your dream itinerary, choose your path wisely, and lose yourself in this folkloric adventure filled with the colorful villages and unforgettable places you'll want to visit for real one day.

—description from the publisher

Mangia e Vinci: Menu Race is a light card game where players compete to complete Italian menus by collecting the right food combinations.
Two menu cards are revealed at the start, and players draw or pick cards to match the required sets while managing their hand and timing their moves. Simple rules make it easy to learn, while strategic decisions add depth. Espresso cards provide a twist, offering bonus points when used to complete a menu.
Players score 50 points for completing a menu, or 100 points if it is finished with an espresso card. The first player to reach 150 points wins.

—description from the publisher
