My Name Was is a competitive roll-and-write game for 1‑100 players set in a zombie apocalypse. You wake up in your wrecked car. The groan of the horde grows closer. Your goal: complete missions, scavenge gear, rescue survivors, and take down the Mega Zombie threatening your last refuge.

Each round begins with a Day card that sets the tone: it tells you how zombies will behave at night, in which direction you'll pass your map, and what resources you must pay. You then roll six action dice and assign them to three zones—Experience, Interior, or Exterior—to move, search, assemble items, fight, or improve your skills.

Here’s the twist: during the Night phase, you don't draw zombies on your own sheet. Instead, you pass your map to an opponent (left or right, as the Day card indicates). That opponent draws the advancing horde on your terrain, growing zombie clusters according to the day's modifier and the dice. Meanwhile, you draw the undead on someone else’s map. The Zombie Control track lets you tweak this exchange—making your opponent’s life harder while easing your own.

After the horde spreads, you get your map back and suffer attacks from any adjacent zombies. Roll wound dice. Manage your infection. Use knives to avoid bites or medicine to stop the infection track.

The game ends after eight rounds, or sooner if a player completes nine missions or all but one survivor falls. You win by having the most completed missions—or by being the last conscious survivor with at least five missions done. Watch your back. The person who draws your zombies is sitting right next to you.

My Name Was turns the roll-and-write genre into a tense, interactive thriller where your rivals literally hold the pen over your grave.

—description from the publisher

Regular Human Climber is a co-operative print&play trick-taking game about climbing, where you each control one limb and pull off wild moves together.

It is played in short rounds of 3 steps each. You start each round by playing cards in the 'Playing a Trick' step, then you use the played cards to move in the 'Moving Your Body' step and finally you check if you can stay on the wall in the 'Staying on the Wall' step.

You win if you stay on the wall with both hands at the TOP. You lose if you fail to stay on the wall at any point, or if none of you have any cards left.

—description from the designer

More than a club, Barca is an icon in the world of football. Now the honour – and challenge – falls to you: can you lead them to new glories? Camp Nou has cleared space for the Superclub trophy, but it is no easy feat, and the Catalans have little patience for mid-table

MIMIC is a social deduction game where one hidden player (the Mimic) tries to blend in while others use clues to identify them within six rounds.

Players secretly receive either the same location or the Mimic role, then take clue cards from a 3x3 grid each round to hint at the location without making it too obvious.

The group wins by correctly identifying the Mimic and protecting the location, while the Mimic wins by avoiding detection or correctly guessing the hidden location.

—description from the publisher

Treacherous Trolls is a chaotic card game where players compete to collect sets of sapphires, rubies, and emeralds while sabotaging each other with traps, theft, and clever tactics.

Each turn, players draw cards and play treasures into their vaults or use action cards to disrupt opponents and protect their own progress.

The winner is the first to gather five of each gem!

Villains is a fast-paced card game where players take on the role of captured supervillains competing to be the first to escape by discarding all their cards.

On each turn, players match the top discard card by villain/colour, ally, or action, strategically chaining multiple cards of the same type to clear their hand quickly.

Action cards add twists, but can’t be used to win, so clever timing and use of allies are key to outplaying opponents and escaping first

boardgame: Alfred the Great vol. 6: The War of the Five Kings 892 AD
description:
Alfred the Great vol. 6: The War of the Five Kings 892 AD is a Medieval England Operational level  board-wargame for 2 players.

King Alfred must have believed that by 892AD the lands of the Saxons were secure, as the implementation of his military and structural reforms meant that his vision of a united England, was achievable if not in his lifetime than by his son the Atheling prince, Edward. He would in fact be facing the greatest challenge of his long and successful reign as later in the year, a war broke out that involved five Kings and stretched across most of middle & southern Britain.

By 892 AD Vikings had been in the Daneland areas of East Anglia, Mercia, and Northumbria for 27 years. These local warriors comprised a formidable army, and formed alliance with another Viking Army that pillaged Frankia and arrived on the shores of Britain in two divisions led by the legendary Hasten.

Alfred, who showed a mind that thought both strategically and tactically, not only reformed his Fyrd (local militia) but had also created the Burh system of defense in depth. The southern Welsh kingdoms, who for so long were a natural foe of the Saxons and who had battled vigorously with the Saxons of Mercia, had through strategic necessity and Alfred’s diplomatic entrees, become firm allies with Alfred in what could only be described as a diplomatic master class for this greatest of Saxon kings.

The Viking strength was in their professional and aggressive soldiery skills. Their weaknesses were often in the re-supply of their fighting men from overseas. However, with the local Viking Army drawn from the Daneland region of Britain this was changing the re-supply dynamic and creating new challenges for the Saxons and Welsh.

Each copy of War of the Five Kings is composed of the following:

One 10 page set of Rules
2x Combat Boards
672 single sided Units   
3x Player Aid Sheets
3x 11 by 17-inch Map Sheets

Also needed for play is at least one six-sided die (although using two or three speed play of the game).

-description from designer
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Series: Warrior Kings Campaign (High Flying Dice Games)

description: Allianz Arena hums like a well-oiled machine. The German champions demand excellence, and their fans demand trophies. Can you continue the proud traditions of this powerhouse of European football? Can you take them further even, adding to their brimful trophy cabinet with the coveted Superclub trophy? Take on the challenge.

Curilemu is a card game set in the universe of the video game of the same name, themed around the rich mythology of the Chiloé Archipelago.

The game features 160 unique cards with various abilities. At the start of the match, each player is dealt a hand of 7 cards. These cards are divided into two main categories:

     Common Cards: During each turn, a player must play 2 Common cards.
     Trap Cards: These can be played at any time during the game, including during an opponent's turn, provided the specific conditions for their ability are met.


There are two ways to achieve victory in Curilemu:


     Successfully discarding all cards from your hand.
     Playing the powerful "El Coleccionista" card.


—description from the publisher

Play as a nature spirit trying to save the sacred tree from the flames of Onibi... But you are not alone in your mission. Animal guardians and the Kodama come together to lend a hand.

The Living Forest 2nd Edition combines the core game and Kodama expansion from the 1st Edition, now with a complete illustration overhaul.

In Living Forest, you play as a nature spirit who will try to save the forest and its sacred tree from the flames of Onibi. But you are not alone in your mission as the animal guardians have come together to lend a hand around the Circle of Spirits where you progress. Each turn, they bring you valuable elements, so try to combine your team of animal guardians as best as possible to carry out your actions, but be careful: some of them are solitary and do not like to be mixed with others...

You have one of three ways to achieve your goal: by planting 12 different Protective Trees, by collecting 12 Sacred Flowers to awaken Sanki the great Guardian of the Forest, by extinguishing 12 Fires to permanently repel Onibi.

Each turn includes 3 phases:

Guardian Animals (simultaneous push your luck phase) : You draw and turn face up, one after the other, the Guardian Animal cards from your personal stack. You thus form the Animal Guardian Help Line. You can stop drawing cards whenever you want. However, if you reveal a card showing a third solitary symbol, then you must stop drawing cards. This card closes your Help Line.
    Action phase: You play in turn. If your Help Line shows strictly less than three solitary symbols (not canceled by gregarious symbols), you can then perform 2 different Actions. If your Help Line shows three solitary symbols (not canceled by gregarious symbols), you can then perform only 1 Action. The strength of an Action is determined by the number of corresponding Elements visible on the Guardian Animal cards in your Help Line and on your Forest individual board.

End of the turn:

Onibi attacks you: If some Fires remain at the center of the Circle of Spirits and you cannot resist them, then add as many Fire Varan cards to your discard stack as there are Fire tiles at the center of the Circle of Spirits.
    Onibi attacks the Sacred Tree: Add as many Fires to the center of the Circle of Spirits as there are Guardian Animal cards taken this turn.
    The arrival of new Guardian Animals: Complete the Guardian Animal reserve by revealing as many new cards per level as there were cards taken this turn.
    Passing the Sacred Tree: Give the Sacred Tree to the next Spirit of Nature clockwise.
    The Return of Guardian Animals: Move all the Animal Guardian cards from your Help Line to your personal discard stack.

The game stops at the end of a complete turn when one of the Spirits of Nature has managed to collect at least 12 different Protective Trees OR 12 Fires OR 12 Sacred Flowers.

boardgame: Hypersteel Meatgrinder: Infantry Support For Brutal Armoured Combat
description:
Hypersteel Meatgrinder is an expansion to Hypersteel Nightmare introducing new rules to introduce infantry to your games. Including unit design rules and new rules for fully destructable buildings.

     Rules for creating your own infantry units
     Seventeen new weapons
     New rules for scouts and cavalry
     New rules for occupying and assaulting buildings
     Four new infantry-focused scenarios


This PDF also includes all the rosters and tokens required to play the game, and links to separate PDFs of these can be found on the Hypersteel Nightmare website, and in the purchase notes.

This title requires a copy of Hypersteel Nightmare to use.

A pocket battle game (#35) from LPS that was included in ATO Issue 63. This game covers the Benghazi Raid from September 13, 1942, one of several raids conducted as a diversion for Operation Agreement.
family: Series: Operation Topsy Pocket Battle Games

description: LPS Pocket Battle Game 36, initially included with Against the Odds Issue 63. Operation Agreement is a postcard sized game covering the main Allied raid on Tobruk from September 13-14, 1942.
family: Series: Operation Topsy Pocket Battle Games

description: LPS Pocket Battle Game #37, initially included in Against the Odds Issue #63.  Operation Nicety in a postcard sized game that covers the Allied Raid on Jalo Oasis on September 13-14, 1942.
family: Series: Five for Fighting (Against the Odds)
Series: Operation Topsy Pocket Battle Games

boardgame: The Marengo Campaign: Bonaparte vs. the 2nd Coalition
description:
Bonaparte vs. the 2nd Coalition
October 1799-December 1800

These four games explore the major battles of 1799-1800, commanded by Moreau, Lannes and Bonaparte against Suvarov, Melas, and Archduke Johann. Each game shows the approach to the battlefield on the day before battle. The three maps of Italian battlefields can be connected together to create a single campaign in the plain of the River Scrivia.

NOVI  15 August 1799
MONTEBELLO  9 June, 1800
MARENGO  14 June, 1800
HOHENLINDEN  3 December, 1800

Volume XVII in the Library of Napoleonic Battles series.

The Marengo Campaign contains: 
• 15 Player Aid cards 
• Study Folder
• 4 full size 22" x 34" maps 
• 560 die-cut counters 
• Series Rules Folder 
• Charts & Tables
family: Series: The Library of Napoleonic Battles (OSG)

description:
Pizza Otter Double Bingo! is a roll-and-write game in which each player has two sheets and fills in the numbers. Each round, you roll two dice and fill in the numbers in the 5×5 grid on each of your two sheets. You write one number from each die on each sheet, but the numbers must be written in the exact same squares on both sheets.

The game ends when all the squares are filled. A score is calculated for every 5 squares in a row (horizontally, vertically, or diagonally), and the player with the highest total score wins.

—description from the designer

Juicy Fruit Salad (ジューシーフルーツサラダ) is a small box, competitive, family card game that is colorblind friendly and language-independent.

You and your friends are making fruit salads and adding fruit cards of value 1-9 to various fruit salads. The player who makes a fruit salad add up to 10 scores the cards involved.

Cards can be played for their value 2-9 on the front, or as a 1 on the back. You can also flip a card that might make a perfect fruit salad for you to claim, but more likely overfill the bowl (adding up to 11 or more), in which case, the previous player scores the cards.

Fruit cards are worth 1 point, and ice cream cards are worth 3 (unless you have the most ice cream, in which case they score you 0 points). The game starts with 8 salads, 7 of which are ice cream cards, that get taken as they are scored. This leaves one last spot, the bowl card, to add cards to. Don't be caught empty-handed as the game comes to an end. With a tight hand limit of 3 cards, it's always good to draw fruit cards if you can. If your hand of cards and the deck are empty, you may flip or pass.

The round ends when all players pass. The player with the most points after 3 rounds wins.

-description from designer

Full Ascension set required for this expansion

Endless possibilities await!
Step into the Dreamscape, a plane of visions and imagination created in the wake of New Vigil's transformation. As the factions gain insight into the new world's power, they must also be on guard for those who might abuse it for their own nefarious ends. Join the heroes of New Vigil as they explore the exciting possibilities that await them in the Dreamscape!

• New Insight resource and Dreamborn cards allows players to access Dream cards and other powers!
• Use the Dreamscape to access the powerful, new Vision cards as well as Heroes and Constructs!

Contents:
• 98 Center Deck cards
• 35 Dream Deck cards
• 30 Insight cards
• Expansion rules

Note: Ascension Center Deck Sets include every center deck card from the original set, along with unique card types, rules, and components needed to play. However, these sets do not include starting cards & the always available cards, honor tokens, a game board, or the original box and therefore require an appropriate base set which contain those necessary game components.

Tokyo Loop is a card-tiling route-building game with a clear goal and optional scoring cards. You are a city planner tasked with making a new rail line for the metropolis. You must place cards to create a loop around the Central Tokyo card and make as many rail connections to it as possible. At the end of the game, score any unplayed cards multiplied by the number of connections from the loop to Central Tokyo.

—description from the designer

Full Ascension set required for this expansion

Conquer the High Seas!
Avast, me hearties! Captain your ship and explore The Severed Sea. Find treasure, use your crew to acquire powerful cards and defeat terrifying sea monsters in this award-wining, pirate deck building game.

• Captain a ship around The Severed Sea.
• Defeat powerful monsters, raid enemy vessels, and steal buried treasure!
• Beware the ultimate terror of The Deep, Thukal, The Kraken!

Contents:
• 118 Center Deck cards
• 4 Starter Deck cards
• 1 Thukal, the Kraken Card
• 4 Ship dials
• Expansion rules

Note: Ascension Center Deck Sets include every center deck card from the original set, along with unique card types, rules, and components needed to play. However, these sets do not include starting cards & the always available cards, honor tokens, a game board, or the original box and therefore require an appropriate base set which contain those necessary game components.

Ouroboros Solitaire Official Rules

This is an "endless deck-building" card game where you manage limited resources (your hand) while continually challenging enemies that grow infinitely stronger.

1. Setup
Requirements: Standard 52-card deck + 1 Joker
Divide the cards into the following 3 groups to set up the play area:
1. Threat Deck (Enemies): All 26 [Spades] and [Clubs]. Shuffle well and place face down.
2. Player Deck (Your Forces): A total of 10 cards: "A, 2, 3, 4, 5" of [Hearts] and [Diamonds]. Shuffle and place face down.
3. Market (Shop): The remaining [Hearts] and [Diamonds] (6 to K) and the [Joker]. Place all of them face down and shuffle well.
4. Discard Pile: The place for your played cards. Initially empty.
(* In addition to these, allocate one space in your play area for a "Stock" slot.)

2. Card Rank (Values)
• A: 1
• 2-10: Face value
• J: 11 / Q: 12 / K: 13
• Joker: A "Wildcard" that guarantees an unconditional win regardless of the target's value. However, it cannot be combined with other cards and must be played alone. It is always returned to the Market after use (a powerful, single-use trump card).

3. Core Rules
Players must always follow these rules:
• Life: Players start with "3" Life (max 3). Losing all life results in Game Over.
• Drawing Cards: Always draw so your hand has a "maximum of 5 cards".
• Stock: You can choose 1 card from your hand and place it face up in your "Stock" slot (max 1 card). Stock cards do not count toward the 5-card hand limit and can be included in any encounter at any time.
• Play Limit: You can play "1 to 3 cards" per encounter (freely combining cards from your hand and Stock).
• Open Information: You may check the contents of your Deck (draw pile) at any time. However, you must not change the order of the cards.

4. Game Flow
The game repeats the following 4 steps (phases):

Step 1: Draw (Refill)
Draw cards from your Player Deck until your hand has 5 cards.
• If your Deck is empty, shuffle all cards in the Discard Pile to create a new Deck.

Step 2: Encounter (Reveal)
Reveal the top card from the Threat Deck. This is your Target (Enemy) for this encounter. The current target value is displayed directly below the Target card. The number of consecutive encounters required for each Target changes based on the current "Loop Level" (see below).

Step 3: Play (Confront)
Choose 1 to 3 cards from your hand and Stock to play, then press the Play button.
Win conditions are determined as follows:
• Match (Success): If the "Total Value" of your played cards matches the Target's value (1-13), you win.
• Cyclic Arithmetic: If your total value exceeds 13, subtract 13 from it (e.g., Total 14 = 1).
• Mismatch (Failure): If the total value does not match the Target, you lose 1 Life as a penalty. At this point, the total value of your played cards is revealed on screen.
• If you still have Life, you consume the cards and proceed to the next Target (or the next encounter in the Sequence).

Step 4: Swap and Sequence
Only if you win the encounter (Match), perform the following:
• Chaos Market (Swap): You must choose exactly 1 card from those you just played and swap it with a random face-down card from the Market. Place the new card in your "Discard Pile" and shuffle your chosen card back into the Market face down.
• The Ouroboros Sequence: Depending on your Loop Level, you must defeat the target consecutively. If the sequence continues, the Target's value increases by "+1", and you return to Step 1 for the next encounter.
• Life Recovery: Every time you defeat all 26 cards in the Threat Deck and complete a loop, you recover 1 Life (up to 3).

5. Endless Loop
When you defeat the entire Threat Deck (26 cards), shuffle the 26 cards and start the next "Loop". From Loop 2 onwards, target values do not inflate. Instead, the number of consecutive breakthroughs required for a single target card equals the current Loop Level.

• Consecutive Encounters per Target = Current Loop Level
Loop 1: Defeat the target once (e.g., Target 8 -> Create an "8" to clear).
Loop 2: After defeating the target, face a consecutive encounter with the Target value "+1" (e.g., Target 8 -> Create an "8", then create a "9" to clear).
...
The number of consecutive encounters will increase indefinitely until the player faces defeat (Game Over).

6. Score (Survival Record)
Your final score is the "cumulative number of Threat Cards defeated" before you reach Game Over.
Aim to survive as long as possible and defeat as many enemies as you can in this endless loop of despair.

Bapsang Battle is a light card game where players compete to complete a traditional Korean table by collecting the right combinations of dishes.

At the start of the game, two menu cards are revealed, each showing a set of dishes players must gather. Players draw from the deck or pick from the discard pile, trying to collect matching food cards while managing their hand and timing their decisions.

Simple rules make the game easy to learn, while strategic choices add depth. Players must decide which cards to keep and when to complete a menu. Special Boricha cards add a twist, offering bonus points when used to finish a menu.

Players score 50 points for completing a menu, or 100 points if it is completed with a Boricha card. The first player to reach 150 points wins the game.

-description from publisher

EISH, /eɪʃ/, an informal South African exclamation,  used to express a range of emotions, such as surprise, annoyance, or resignation.

In EISH!!, players are Rangers in training bringing tourists out into the bush to spot the Big 5. Come out on top as you skilfully AVOID spotting the same animals as the Lead Ranger while taking breaks to spot cute Dassies, however, be sure not to get caught skiving off together!

Each round, players take turns as the Lead Ranger. Every turn, Rangers report a sighting (by playing a card) from their hand but as the Lead Ranger has first dibs on bringing the tourists to their sightings, other rangers MUST NOT play the same card as the Lead Ranger otherwise they will lose Credibility! The round ends in one of two ways: Either when a ranger loses all their Cred, or runs out of sightings to report. All other rangers will then get a chance to score that round!

The game is played in multiple rounds and ends when a Ranger reaches 13 Points. The Ranger(s) with the highest points wins!

Begin your trek into the bush in this game of probability, mind games, and light strategy!

-description from designer

boardgame: Marvel: Crisis Protocol – X-Men Starter Set
description: A full roster of some of the most iconic heroes and villains from Marvel’s X-Men are joining forces to show their foes the true power of mutantkind! This Starter Set contains all the miniatures, Character Stat Cards, and Team Tactic Cards for the following characters: Cyclops, Wolverine, Storm, Beast, Rogue, Gambit, Magneto, Toad, Mystique, and Sabretooth. Also included are Crisis Cards and all the tokens, measuring tools, and dice needed to play. Players interested in getting into Marvel: Crisis Protocol will find that Starter Sets are the perfect starting place for a single player.

Rookies Ascended is a fast-paced tactical board game for 2-4 players. The goal? Be the first to reach 4 Kill Points. To get there, you’ll need to gear up, outmanoeuvre your rivals, and survive brutal Boss encounters that require cooperation. Until they don't.

Players earn Kill Points which provide various rewards, such as ascending to a new class, gaining more dice, or unlocking additional Gear slots. The first player to reach 4 Kill Points wins the game.

Your story begins here!

You, a Rookie adventurer sipping hot grog in a cozy tavern, are listening to an old bard’s tale of The Pit—a legendary area where only the most cunning survive. To prove yourself worthy, you’ll need to gear up fast: grab your Funky Bucket helmet, pull on your Slimy Dragon Slaying Socks, and unsheathe the Blade of Blunder. Don’t linger too long, because other greedy Rookies are already on the move. In this conquest, every upgrade you find could just make you a bigger target.

When a Boss's health bar drops, loyalty evaporates. Why shield a friend when you can trigger your Ascended Ability to ensure they take the brunt of the attack? Clear your path to land the final blow and snag the loot. Whether you're sniping a kill from an "ally" or charging them head-on. Remember, in The Pit, the monster in front of you is rarely as dangerous as the friend sitting next to you.

Don’t trust anyone and start Clashing!

Three custom d6's display consonants, two display vowels, and another displays four colors and two wild colors. Players take turns rolling the six dice and trying to form words of from two to nine letters in the displayed color using only the letters shown on the dice. A scoresheet enables players to remember the lengths and colors of the words that they have formed. Only one word of each length may be constructed for each color.

A word length is "locked" (cannot be used again) for all players if one player has created words of that length in all four colors. A player receives a bee sting if he/she cannot create a word. The game ends when a player has four bee stings or when four word lengths are locked.

Score one point for each letter used in a word. Score bonuses for locking a word length, having the longest word in a color, and for creating more than five words in a color. Most points wins.

boardgame: Carcassonne: The Four Seasons – The Winter
description:
The Winter is part of the 4 Seasons tile series, with individual illustrations. You can use the tiles to enlarge the game board or as a small mini-expansion.

It's especially cold in winter, so we'll be keeping a close eye on the Waylayers to make sure they don't freeze

Scoring points (base game variation)
For highwaymen and autumn tiles that are vertically, horizontally or diagonally adjacent to the winter tile you have placed, you immediately receive 2 points. 
You receive no points for the winter tile you just placed.

Scoring points (Winter-Edition Variation)
For highwaymen and wild animals that are vertically, horizontally or diagonally adjacent to the winter tile you have placed, you immediately receive 2 points.

Statue tiles
You will find 1 statue tile in each of the 4 Seasons.
The following rule applies to all 4 statue tiles of the 4 Seasons:
If your tile is vertically, horizontally or diagonally adjacent to a statue tile of the 4 Seasons after being placed, you may place 2 meeple on this tile in different areas.

-description from publisher's website

In Cryptid Creek: Build a Legend, players compete to craft the most convincing fake cryptid from mismatched body parts to save their dying town through tourism. Only one player can win, but if the hoax is exposed, everyone loses together.

Players bid on specimen cards from six atmospheric locations (Road, Bridge, Creek, Woods, Swamp, Field) to collect body parts across five categories: Head, Torso, Arms, Lower Body, and Legs/Feet. Each card features distinctive texture tags like Fur, Scales, and Feathers that align with hidden agenda scoring objectives. When players successfully claim a specimen card after winning a bid, they engage in light storytelling to describe their cryptid sighting.

The game balances individual competition with collective risk through a shared "hoax meter" monitored by an NPC Reality TV crew. Players use character abilities and Smoke & Mirrors cards to outmaneuver opponents while managing the town's exposure level. Push too hard for personal victory and the cameras might catch everyone, causing all players to lose despite individual progress.

Cryptid Creek combines strategic bidding and set collection with a unique collective failure condition and storytelling moments, creating constant tension between competing to win and keeping the shared hoax alive.

-description from designer

Placing Orders is a climbing-shedding game for exactly three players, where each player can "place an order" for more cards at the start of the hand, but be careful, because they may be a liability instead of a benefit...

Placing Orders is played with a deck of 52 playing cards. Each hand, the three players are dealt 12 cards. Players look at their hands and then, on the count of three, all simultaneously reveal from 0 to 5 fingers on their hand. Players take as many extra cards into their hand as fingers shown.

Whichever player took the fewest cards starts the first climb. They can play an valid meld, and other players must either pass or play a meld of the same type but higher rank. Or, they can play a bomb if they have one. Play continues in this fashion, with players trying to be the first to shed all their cards, and scoring points based on how many cards remain in the other players' hands.

—description from the designer

What happens when party game designers make a dungeon-crawling RPG? You get all the adventure and none of the homework.

Shake the dice to make new maps each round! Draw your path to outwit opponents, complete quests, & level up your character.

Here's How to Play: 
1. Shake it up!
Put the castle on top of the dice tray and shake it until your adventurer's heart is content. Remove the castle to reveal your brand new, totally random, (probably) never seen before dungeon!

2. Draw your....marker
Using your wits, transparency, and dry erase marker, chart your path through the dungeon. Be careful to use your abilities efficiently to be the first to complete your chosen quest.

3. Adventure, again and again
Slay monsters, solve puzzles, and cross hazards on your way to glory! In just a few minutes, your party will go on an epic adventure, finish quests, and gain new abilities before shaking it up for a brand new adventure!

-description from designer
publishers: Evan and Josh's Very Special Games Company (Very Special Games)

description:
Convoy Escort  is a game of modern naval combat; the focus is on convoy operations. The game abstracts the technical details for various equipment types. Counters represent one unit (like a submarine) or many (up to 100 aircraft) or a group of ships. Unit counters are provided, which are generic, representing types rather than named ships.

Objectives: NATO must escort convoys to European ports to earn Supply Points. Russia must destroy the convoys and NATO warships to prevent the above.

Land combat in Europe is not represented, but depends on Supply Points for determining who will win the war.

This game is available from 'wargamevault' online as a download.

-description from designer

boardgame: Bandido: 10th Anniversary – Chill Bandido Exclusive extension card
description:
This promo card to celebrate the 10th anniversary of Bandido. 
It is part of Spielbox Magazine Issue 2#2026.

It contains one Chill Bandio Card and a rules card in German and English

Rules: 
The game follows the original rules with the following addition: Shuffle Chill Bandido into the deck. He is a Joker and may be placed to remove any misplaced card from the tunnel system, provided that its removal does not make the tunnel system impossible to complete. 
After resolving its effect, return Chill Bandido and the removed card to the box. Afterward, draw a new card.

This family-friendly card game is suitable for 2 to 6 players, ages 8+, and takes around 15 minutes to play. Whether you’re a cat lover or hater, you’re sure to get into character as a Cat Burglar who’s just waiting for any opportunity to leave a hairball on their opponent’s stash!

How to Play:

The game is simple: Steal. Stash. Win!

Everyone takes a Cat Burglar Character Card with a special ability. We encourage you to lean into this and really build your personality. Are you feline sneaky? Or are you totally pawsome?

On your turn, you can choose to move Stolen Goods to your Stash, attack an opponent’s Stash, refresh your hand, or play an Action to either benefit your game or disrupt others.

When all the cards have been played, it’s time to add up your Stash. The player with the most points is the winner and is crowned Best Cat Burglar!

GET YOUR CLAWS OUT – Steal and stash to win Klepto Cats from Ridley’s Games. Become a cunning Cat Burglar and complete the purrfect heist in this quick-to-learn light strategy card game that’s full of cattitude! Are you ready to go feral?

STEAL. STASH. WIN. – Collect sets of stolen items to build your stash and earn points. Use actions like going Cat Nip crazy to load up your loot, Schrödinger’s Box to hide what you have, and play the sardine challenge to score a tasty bonus.

IS THAT A HAIRBALL? – The most fiendish felines take out the competition. Swipe to mess with their plans, overfeed them, and drop funky hairballs and cat barf (bleurgh!) to lower their score. But watch your tail, because they’re coming for your goodies too.

MADE FOR GAME NIGHT – Suitable for 2 to 6 players, ages 8+, and taking around 15 minutes to play, it’s great for families or as a starter on game night! There are 94 cards and instructions inside the compact box that’s made to be taken out and about.

Zeal is a science fantasy card game where four apocalypse cults race to end the world in their own way. Each cult has its own goals, theme, and strategic play style.

Zeal is a competitive deck drafting game that offers a similar experience to a collectible card game, but every card comes in one box. A single box supports a draft for up to 8 players. It also offers other game modes, like cult decks, constructed, and "sealed" deck formats.

In Zeal you will use the characters, devices and spells in your deck to defeat all of your opponents' bases to win the game.

Introducing the Platonium Mines pack - a small expansion with a big impact.

Fresh twists. New decisions. Even more ways to make every playthrough feel unique.

This mini expansion adds Special Plutonium Mines to the game. These new mines feature a special space for an additional, more powerful resource: plutonium. Depending on the mine, it may also provide another benefit, giving you new options for development and resource planning.

Replicants is a confrontation and strategy game in which you play biomechanical creatures, the Replicants, battling each other in an arena.

In a chaotic, violent future,to satisfy the population’s need for entertainment,engineers have created creatures built for combat. They named them...REPLICANTS

These  biomechanical  energy  creatures,  gladiators  of  the  future, battle it out in an arena.
Replicants form a group of individuals. Two, three or four groups of Replicants meet in the arena to wage merciless combat.
A Replicant has two distinct structures. Its mechanical part, called the  heart,  gives  it  the  energy  it  needs  to  perform  its  various functions. Its biological part, called the soul, gives it the ability to move and destroy.
Within  each  group,  a  particular  Replicant  is  called  the  Matrix. 
When combat begins, its energetic activity becomes exacerbated. 
The Matrix rapidly expands and eventually splits, giving rise to a new Replicant.
In  battle,  Replicants  must  destroy  their  opponents.  To  do  this, one Replicant must pull itself up onto another. When it does so, it releases an enormous amount of electricity which annihilates its hapless opponent.
During  these  violent  skirmishes,  the  Matrix’s  replication  process sometimes  reaches  such  an  energy  paroxysm  that  it  has  to discharge an excess of energy. It does so by disintegrating one of its Replicants, which acts as a lightning rod. This discharge is life-saving for the Matrix, but not for the Replicant undergoing it.
This  confrontation  ends  when  one  of  the  Matrix  is  destroyed by  another  Replicant,  or  when  a  group  is  practically  devoid  of individuals.
In this game, you play as a group of Replicants. Whether two, three or four players, to have a chance of winning the game in 
this confrontation, you must destroy the opposing Replicants while minimizing your losses. Can you lead your group to victory?

Two popular classic games with a twist – Carcassonne Labyrinth combines the best of both worlds. For 40 years, Labyrinth has been thrilling families worldwide – and for 25 years, Carcassonne has been an international game night favorite. Now, both classics come together in a completely new game.

Players push tiles into the layout of the maze to shift the Labyrinth so they may find a path that leads to the treasure they seek. Any treasure tiles they collect and tiles pushed out of the Labyrinth can then be used to build the cities and roads of Carcassonne!
 The player who moves the most cleverly and builds the most skillfully wins this creative reinterpretation of two timeless games.

—description from the publisher

Welcome to Studio Showdown, where you roleplay as an Indie Studio CEO and fight for funding at game night:

     Deck-building + sharp resource management (build smart, spend smarter)
     Cashflow optimization that rewards planning, not vibes
     Sabotage cards for peak studio politics (friendly rivalries only, probably)
     Immersive storytelling where your choices shape your studio’s legacy
     The “Pitch Phase” finale: sell your game to the Investor-Judge and steal the spotlight
     Anime-meets-graphic-novel art that looks collector-worthy on the table


Fast setup (about 5 minutes), flexible play length (15 to 60 minutes), learn in 10 to 15

2 to 4 Players can play at the same time.  PLAYER with the HIGHEST CASH balance WINS!

—description from the publisher

It's a perfect day at the beach, and the only thing left to settle is whose sandcastle reigns supreme! Sandcastles is a drafting and tile-laying game for 2-6 players in which everyone building their own sandcastle, tile by tile, over 15 rounds.

Each round, one player reveals tiles one at a time and passes them around the table. Every player must decide in the moment whether to claim the current tile or hold out for something better later in the round — but once you take a tile, you're done for that round, whatever comes next. The tile you claim gets added to your sandcastle, a 5x5 grid anchored by your Starter Tile at the bottom center. Sand tiles build up from the ground following the rules of gravity, while sky tiles float at the top and drop down to rest on your highest sand tiles at game end.

Scoring rewards players for building strategically:
• Windows score based on how many appear in each horizontal row, with points jumping at specific thresholds.
•Starfish score when placed adjacent to others of the same color, and collecting all five colors earns a bonus.
• Shells, shovels, and buckets each carry flat point values, with end-game bonuses for whoever collects the most of each.
• Filling your top row with five sky tiles earns a bonus as well.

The player whose sandcastle totals the most points wins the title of Best Sandcastle at the Beach!

-description from publisher

Swordplay is a quick, fun, strategic and easy-to-learn two-player card game in which players are engaged in a one-on-one swordfight. Players manage a small hand of cards and a deck that runs out quickly, choosing and playing moves in order to try and hit their opponent. Three hits wins the game. Cards represent attacks, blocks, and evades, and the trick of the game is Refresh - a highly vulnerable card that you must play to reset and enhance your deck.

—description from the designer

Under the British Raj, India included 565 "princely states," each with their own semi-independent rulers. At the 1947 founding of independent India almost all of them opted to join either India or Pakistan, but the largest, Hyderabad, balked at becoming part of India. Its ruler, the Nizam Osman Ali Khan, chose independence for his state or, barring that, accession to Pakistan.

Indian leaders refused to allow a large Muslim nation within their borders, leaving land-locked Hyderabad with few options. Muslim activists armed themselves and prepared to resist the Indians. While the Nizam frantically tried to expand his 22,000-strong regular army and Qasim Razwi gathered 200,000 fanatic militiamen, the Indians prepared their Operation Polo to conquer Hyderabad. In September 1948, the Indian Army began a four-pronged assault. Four days later, the Nizam surrendered.

Hyderabad 1948 is a mostly-complete Panzer Grenadier game; you’ll need a set of Panzer Grenadier Fourth Edition rules (from any Panzer Grenadier game – the rules are the same in all of them). You can use the maps from Conquest of Ethiopia or order them separately.

Hyderabad 1948 includes 240 new pieces and 10 new scenarios; it’s a digital download so you’ll have to assemble the pieces yourself.

Note: This product is a digital download. Pieces require assembly.

-description from publisher's website

Iron Protocol is a compact solo experience for fans of sci-fi, heavy machinery, and tactical combat. Take control of a mech unit and push through hostile territory - engaging enemy machines while extracting survivors from the ruins.

The game is built around three core components:

> A regional map, where you navigate devastated suburbs, dodge drones and landmines, and salvage parts from fallen mechs as you search for survivors.
> Tactical battlefield boards, where every deployment is a calculated risk. 
> And at the center of it all, a low-ink mech record sheet that doesn’t just track your status - it drives your decisions, guiding your development as you balance computing power, weapon systems, and armor upgrades.

—description from the designer

boardgame: For the Girls On the Go
description:
Dump the dudes for the night and gather up the girls for a wild ride

Players compete to collect the most cards…


     Take turns drawing a card from the top of the deck.
     Do whatever the card tells you to do!
     First player to 10 cards wins the game.


—description from the back of the box
family: Versions & Editions: Travel Versions of Non-Travel Games

description:
The Hour at Houx: this free scenario is designed as your first step into BattlegroundHD, and more specifically into its World War II setting. It is simple by design  not overloaded with rules or complexity  so you can quickly learn the flow of play while focusing on the heart of the system: infantry combat and tactics.

In BattlegroundHD, detail matters. The rules reward you for thinking like a real squad leader. Cover, movement, suppressive fire, and flanking maneuvers aren’t just flavor; they’re essential tools for success. Use realistic battlefield tactics, and you’ll see them pay off on the table just as they would in history.

This scenario is meant to give you a taste of how BattlegroundHD plays  tense, tactical, and immersive. Whether you’re brand new to the system or just curious about the WWII version, we hope you enjoy the experience and that it sparks your interest to explore more of what BattlegroundHD has to offer.

Anatolian Feast: Afiyet Olsun! is a light card game where players compete to build a traditional Turkish menu by collecting the right combinations of dishes.
At the start of the game, two menu cards are revealed, each showing a set of dishes players must gather. Players draw from the deck or pick from the discard pile, aiming to collect matching food cards while managing their hand and timing their decisions.
Simple rules make the game easy to learn, while strategic choices add depth. Players must decide which cards to keep and when to complete a menu. Special Tea cards add a twist, offering bonus points when used to finish a menu.
Players score 50 points for completing a menu, or 100 points if it is completed with a Tea card. The first player to reach 150 points wins the game.

—description from the publisher

Which country in Europe has the longest coastline? Where do residents have the most mobile phone contracts? This guessing game relies on intuition: correctly categorize countries in exciting categories, constantly having to think outside the box.

Beginning by a random country you have to sort countries in different categories, from less to more. Clockwise each player has to place a country at the right place. If the order is wrong and a players guess the mistake the player which has played the last card gets a penalty. If the order is correct the guessing player gets the penalty.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Welches Land in Europa hat die längste Küste? Wo haben die Einwohnerinnen und Einwohner die meisten Handyverträge? Bei diesem Schätzspiel kommt es aufs Bauchgefühl an: Länder in spannenden Kategorien richtig einordnen und dabei immer wieder ins Grübeln kommen. Wer traut sich, die Schätzungen in der Auslage anzuzweifeln? Zwei bis fünf Spielerinnen und Spieler ab zehn Jahren versuchen in drei Themensets mit je acht Kategorien ihre Länderkarten richtig einzusortieren und dabei die Fehler der anderen zu entdecken. Ein Spiel voller Spielspaß und interessanter Fakten über Europa für die ganze Familie!

—description from the publisher

Randomly place a star token, several elephant tokens, a pawn for each player, and a bean token in each player's color onto a 25 X 25 square grid. Each player also chooses two navigational cards, each of which displays a direction and distance of movement.
Players take turns rolling a die and then allocating the result between the two cards (e.g., roll a 6 and allocate four to one card and two to the other). For each card, the player then moves his pawn the appropriate distance (the allocated number times the number on the card) and direction.
When a player's pawn passes through an elephant token, the player receives an elephant card that will allow him to add obstacles to the grid or to break rules by, say, transporting a token or pawn.
Choose an additional navigation card when you pass through your bean token or, if you do not pass through your bean token, at the end of the round. The round ends when a player's pawn reaches the star token after first passing through that player's bean token. That player receives points based on player count, and the other players receive fewer points based upon how close they came to the star token.
Re-populate the grid as at the beginning of the game and play two more rounds. Then, in addition to the points scored during the game, score a point for each unused elephant card, and score the sum of the numbers on your navigation cards. Most points wins.

The second game in the Pact of the Seven-Armed Orangutan series.
This time you'll explore the home of Armored Hedgehog, an ancient knight and protector of the land.

The current knights don't wage wars. They live in peace. So they have other goals. You will help them fulfill their oaths and bring the land into an age of harmony.

As with the previous title the illustrated book provides a view of the city, while The Traveler contains everything the characters are going to tell you. The stickers, along with a list of keywords and items will help track your progress.

Start with the illustrated book and look for scenes that interest you. The order doesn’t matter. You can choose any page and any scene. If there’s a number next to a person or creature, that means this character has something to say. Grab The Traveler and find the paragraph with the same number. Remember to only read the paragraphs you’ve been instructed to. After reading, you’ll learn what is troubling the character. Try to help them.

boardgame: We Led the Way: Darby’s Rangers – Gela Sicily, 1943
description:
We Led the Way is a narrative-driven scenario book for BattlegroundHD, focusing on the hard-fought opening actions of Operation Husky and the U.S. Army Rangers’ landings at Gela, Sicily in July 1943.

This book places players in the boots of Darby’s Rangers, Italian coastal defenders, and armored counterattack forces as the invasion unfolds from the beaches inland. Each scenario is built from detailed historical research and designed to deliver tense, cinematic tabletop battles where leadership, terrain, and timing matter as much as firepower.

What’s Inside:

     A complete set of linked scenarios depicting the Rangers’ assault, inland fighting, and Italian counterattacks.
     Historically grounded forces, objectives, and special rules tailored to each engagement.
     Unique scenario mechanics reflecting amphibious landings, coastal defenses, exhaustion, limited visibility, and improvised battlefield decisions.
     Hero characters drawn from history and narrative interpretation, adding personality and dramatic moments to your games.
     Clear, immersive layouts that allow each side to play with limited knowledge, reinforcing fog of war and replayability.


Designed for the Table.

Every scenario in We Led the Way is crafted to be:

     Historically authentic without sacrificing playability
     Visually engaging, with terrain that rewards thoughtful table design
     Tactically demanding, emphasizing maneuver, leadership, and mission focus
     Replayable, with asymmetric objectives and evolving battlefield pressure


Whether you’re playing a single evening’s game or linking scenarios into a broader narrative campaign, We Led the Way delivers intense, story-driven battles that showcase what BattlegroundHD does best.

Required:

     BattlegroundHD Core Rules
     US Ranger Force Pack
     Italian Force Pack


—description from the publisher

boardgame: がおー人狼（GAO JINRO -the ROAR game-）
description:
Too Fast for a Werewolf...!?
High-Speed Chicken Race Game: "GAOO WEREWOLF"

【Game Overview】
A super-fast, chicken-race card game settled in just 5 seconds!
Every card you flip tests your "Calmness" and your "Wolf’s Heart."
Make split-second decisions to seize victory and become the Village Chief!

Players: 3–5
Play Time: 5 seconds – 20 minutes
Age: 8+

【Story】
You are the "Jin-ro" (Werewolf), the shy residents of "Jin-ro Village."
Usually, you are peaceful, but when your "Wolf’s Heart" overflows, you transform into a terrifying wolf!
Today, the "Discussion"—actually a high-speed card-playing battle—begins once again to exile the wolves among you!

【How to Play (Simplified)】
1. Setup
Each player holds their own deck face down. (The deck consists of two types of cards: "Calmness" and "Wolf’s Heart.") Make sure to shuffle well before the round starts.

2. Game Start
The Temporary Village Chief shouts, "Let the discussion begin!" to start the round.
Players flip over cards from their decks as fast as they can, lining them up.
The first player to line up 5 cards can grab a "Proof of Innocence Token" to prove they aren't a wolf!

HOWEVER...
If you reveal 3 "Wolf’s Heart" cards, you transform with a loud "GAOO!!" (ROAR!!) and get exiled immediately.

The "First Witness" Bonus: If someone turns into a wolf, point and shout "Exiled!" to contribute to the village's peace. The first person to point it out receives an extra "Proof of Innocence Token."

"Simultaneous GAOO!": This is the strongest roar! If two or more players transform at the same time, the wolves win! Those who roared "GAOO!" get the tokens instead. Even the "Exiled!" shout won't work against a simultaneous roar.

3. Game End
The round ends as soon as someone roars "GAOO!" (transforms into a wolf).
Repeat the rounds until someone collects 5 "Proof of Innocence Tokens." That player is the winner and becomes the honorable Village Chief!

0ops! is a team-based card game in which mages race to hatch secret magical eggs by gathering the right five elements.

Each team has a secret Arcane Egg Card showing the elements it needs. On your turn, draw an Element Card, then play one card into any player’s field. You can help your own team, interfere with another team, or set up a risky chain reaction.

Each Element Card has a hatching element and a 0ops! element. When the 0ops! element reacts with a matching card already in that field, both cards are pushed out and sent to different players. A carefully built field can change in an instant, turning one mistake into a new chance.

The first team to gather all five elements needed for its secret magical egg hatches the egg and wins.

—description from the publisher
publishers: Play With Us Design (玩聚設計)

description:
Vocabularian is thoughtfully designed to be a superior word game. It’s easy to learn and quick to play, with fast turns, and lots of player interaction. Plus, it rewards skill much more than luck.

Put simply, Vocabularian is all about spelling impressive words.

To play, take turns drafting the letters you want from the pool at the center of the table. Once you have at least five letters, start spelling words! Longer words are worth a lot more points. The first player to 50 points wins. It’s really that simple!

—description from the publisher

Stalwart heroes! Vile villains! Monstrous minions! 

Super Skirmish Gaming (SSG) brings together twenty years worth of comic book miniature gaming experience in one exciting new rulebook!

SSG gives you the tools to play out your own comic book series on your tabletop. 
Immersive rules allow you to recreate just about any comic book character you can think of, or build your own hero or villain to your exact specifications. Our rugged 3D6 system provides for fast and furious action that includes all of the trappings of the superhero genre.

All you need to play are a handful of miniatures, six D6s, ten to fifteen chits, tokens, or cards (numbered in sequential order), and a measuring tape or sticks marked in inches. 
SSG’s versatile rules let you play in multiple modes: solo, cooperative, or player-versus-player.

Start telling your own four-color tabletop tales today with Super Skirmish Gaming!

This game includes free paper miniatures, rulers, and initiative chits. All you have to do is print or copy them onto heavy card stock and cut them out!

—description from the publisher

Techno-Fantasy Adventure 2nd Edition (TFA2) gives you the tools to play fast and fun science-fantasy skirmish role-play battles using any figures in your collection!

You can play TFA2 solo, cooperatively, or in a player-vs-player mode, exploring realms of weird sci-fi adventure and mayhem.

You will also need a handful of six-sided dice, chits or tokens numbered 1–20, and a few pieces of terrain or a map.

This 2nd edition still utilizes our Rugged 3D6 action resolution system, but provides expanded player-facing rules, more character creation options, and a more robust campaign system.

TFA2 doubles down on 1st edition’s already versatile character creation, including a new Background system. 
A chapter of foes and allies and rules for creating more of your own devising is also included. 
You bring the figures, and TFA2 will provide the high-intensity skirmish RPG action to tell their stories.

Roll high!

Rehilete a geometric game whereup to 4 players take turns to stack and under stack hexagonal tiles in order to form an hexagon made up to 3 diferent colors, if the hexagon is made by excatly 2/6 parts of each colors is considered "ideal" and gains the maximus of 3 points, 1 point if the hexagon only cointains one color, and 2 points for every other combination. The games ends when all players put all their cards in the game. The most points wins.

—user summary

¿Por qué Rehilete es el nuevo favorito de la familia?

Estrategia en Seis Lados: Con 78 cartas hexagonales, las posibilidades de conexión son infinitas, obligándote a planear cada jugada con precisión.

Dinámica de Alto Impacto: El objetivo es vibrante y claro: ¡Conecta, completa y conquista el hexágono!.

Diversión Didáctica: Diseñado para mentes ágiles de 6 años en adelante, es la herramienta perfecta para desarrollar el pensamiento lógico y la concentración.

Diseño Vanguardista: Fieles a nuestra herencia en artes gráficas, cada carta presenta colores explosivos y un diseño limpio que hace que cada partida sea visualmente increíble.

Ya sea en una reunión con amigos o en una tarde de juegos en casa, Rehilete se adapta a cualquier entorno gracias a su formato práctico y adictivo. No hay turnos aburridos cuando cada pieza puede cambiar el rumbo de la partida.

Como todos los productos de Flink, este juego está fabricado con la calidad que nos distingue, garantizando durabilidad para miles de batallas estratégicas.

¿Tienes la visión necesaria para dominar el hexágono? ¡Consigue tu Rehilete hoy mismo y descubre por qué en Flink reinventamos la forma de jugar!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Grand Prix Glory: A Game of Grand Prix Racing, 1920-1939
description:
Grand Prix Glory is a fun and innovative set of fast play rules covering Grand Prix auto racing from 1920 to 1940.

The pre-Formula One era featured remarkably beautiful cars driven by remarkably brave drivers. 
Grand Prix Glory captures the feel of the era without getting bogged down in mechanical details. 
The game emphasizes the connection between man and machine and features ratings for 23 real life drivers as well as the coolest cars both decades had to offer.

You don’t have to be an expert on auto racing, or even knowledgeable on the subject, and even if you've never driven a car, you can still play and enjoy Grand Prix Glory.

While the actual traveling around the circuit is somewhat abstracted, the essential moments of each track are represented, bringing with each the fun and terror real drivers faced.

The download includes the 52 page rulebook, 25 event cards to be printed onto card stock or other sturdy paper, plus a pre-sized (US poker card) pdf file, and a collection of Circuit Cards each covering real circuits as they were laid out at the time.

Grand Prix Glory is designed for 1-6 players, though more may certainly join in, and is ideal for solo gaming and includes rules for such.

Miniatures are not necessary, though they are strongly encouraged.

boardgame: Squirm: A Game of Vile Space Bugs
description:
In the darkest corner of space, the Hive Mind stirs. It has existed for millenia, spreading its children across the stars. It Squirms, reaching its tendrils into rancid meat and sparking neurons. Shapes, carved from chitin and bone stir and stalk their hunting grounds. 

Squirm is a fast-paced battle royale game with a heavy emphasis on customisation and kitbashing. 
Roll on the mutation index to generate your outbreak's mutations, build your bugs and devour all those who stand before you until you are the last one standing.

This download consists of two files, the core rules, and the mutation index. Each document is a single page, laid out so that it can be cut and folded into an A7 booklet, perfect for tucking in your pocket.

Dozen Roses is a romantic deduction and bluffing card game inspired by the tradition of giving twelve roses as a proposal.

Each player has a secret rose card, and players take turns playing rose cards face up or face down. By reading the visible roses, guessing the hidden ones, and deciding when the timing is right, players try to make the total number of roses exactly twelve.

On your turn, you may declare “Proposal!” if you believe the total is exactly twelve. If the total is right, your proposal succeeds. If not, the result changes depending on the number of roses, leading to different flower-language endings.

The game ends when all players have proposed or when the cards run out. Players who successfully reach exactly twelve roses win.

Arcanixia is a fast strategy card game for 2-5 players build around hidden color wagers and table reads. You race with others to collect a set of 7 different colored Wizards.

The goal is simple: collect all seven Wizard colors. 
The moment you complete the seven-color set, you win instantly.

Each player chooses one of five Heroes, then plays spell cards and makes face-down wagers.
When you wager, you must declare a specific Wizard color (e.g. “Blue Wizard”) and mark it with a matching colored crystal. 
Other players decide whether to trust the claim or call it out, then the card is revealed as a Wizard or a Trap.

Correct reads don’t just decide the moment - they generate Power, a resource you can spend to unleash character abilities and impactful spell effects. 
Spells help you disrupt opponents, protect your progress, and enable comebacks, while the wager system keeps everyone engaged every round.

—description from the publisher

Similar to Monopoly Deal Card Game, but comes with character cards. Each player receives one at the start of the game. They all have an ongoing, different ability (either "On your turn" or "during your turn").
family: Versions & Editions: Card Versions of Non-Card Games

boardgame: Monopoly Deal Wizarding World/Harry Potter Card Game
description: Similar to Monopoly Deal Card Game, but comes with unique character cards with special abilities.
family: Versions & Editions: Card Versions of Non-Card Games

description: Similar to Monopoly Deal Card Game, but comes with unique character cards with special abilities.
family: Versions & Editions: Card Versions of Non-Card Games

boardgame: シェイズオブセブン (7 SHADES OF S:EVEN)
description:
"What is the setting?
In the mysterious world of Farvendre, all colors have vanished.
Players become chosen travelers, seeking to restore the seven lost colors.

What is the goal?
Be the first player to collect and lock all seven colors to achieve victory.

How do you play?
Players move across a 7×7 board, flipping cards to discover colors, effects, and hidden opportunities.
Strategic positioning, timing, and player interaction are key — you can disrupt opponents, steal colors, and turn the tide at critical moments.

With simple rules but deep tactical choices, the game offers a unique blend of luck, strategy, and dramatic turnarounds."

-from the publisher

"It’s a puzzle game where you fit seven mostly simple‑shaped pieces into a box.
Among our works, this is one of the easiest puzzles, but it still hides a small twist."

-from the publisher

"It’s a puzzle game where you fit six identical pieces into a transparent case.
It’s a highly challenging puzzle.
Can you interlock the pieces and form a shape that fits perfectly inside?"

-from the publisher

"It’s a puzzle game where you fit six identical pieces into a transparent case.
It’s a highly challenging puzzle.
Take a close look and see if you can figure out how these pieces could possibly fit inside."

-from the publisher

Terravista is a fast and accessible roll-and-write game for 1–6 players, where every line you draw shapes your path to victory. Designed by Eilif Svensson (Trails of Tucana) and Åsmund Svensson, it combines simple rules with rewarding route-building decisions.

Each player receives an asymmetric map and sets out to build roads connecting olives, oranges, and strawberries to their markets, while also linking matching market pairs for additional progress. With virtually no setup time, the game begins in seconds but offers meaningful choices from the very first roll.

Each round, two dice determine which terrain types can be connected, and all players simultaneously draw a road between adjacent spaces on their own map. Completing connections advances players along shared tracks, unlocking bonus spaces that can trigger satisfying chain reactions of additional roads.

The asymmetric maps and the uneven distribution of terrain combinations on the dice create a subtle puzzle: some connections are easier to complete than others, rewarding players who plan ahead and adapt their routes as the game unfolds.
The first player to complete three connections on all four tracks wins the game.

Terravista includes 12 unique maps across 6 double-sided boards, special dice, dry-erase markers, and multiple game modes, including solo play, point-based scoring, and a tournament variant.

—description from the publisher

A Concordia expansion map where you colonize Roman cities only from the sea. It does completely away with routes between cities. While the ships share sea regions from where they can build new houses in harbor cities of the Roman Empire, the land units offer additional new strategic choices inside a separate area on the board.
It is a double sided board, designed for either 2 to 4 or for 3 to 5 players. 
You win by appeasing the Roman Gods in the usual manner of Concordia.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: キメラトリックス (Chimera Tricks)
description:
"""Chimera Tricks"" is a trick-taking game where players use forbidden synthesis magic to create Chimeras and compete for Grimoires.

The game’s key feature is the Chimera system, which lets you synthesize cards. The non-winning players leave their cards in their play area, and combines with the card played from their hand in the next trick to create a powerful ""Chimera"" with an increased rank and multiple suits. Will you intentionally lose to build up your strength, or win to claim a ""Grimoire"" for points?

The next trick’s reward Grimoire is revealed in advance, so planned defeats that look one step ahead are the key to victory. Even if you claim a cursed negative-point Grimoire, do not give up. If you win with a low-rank card and acquire a ""Crystal,"" you can convert it into positive points. Master your Familiars and synthesis magic, navigate the intricate battles, 
and claim victory!"

-from the publisher

"What is the setting?
The game is set against the diverse and beautiful everyday landscapes of Japan. It uses a unique deck of ""Onomatopoeia"" cards—Japanese words that mimic sounds, textures, and emotional atmospheres—and a deck of ""Photo"" cards capturing local scenery.

What is the goal?
There are no winners or losers. The goal is to explore and celebrate the differences in how each person perceives the world. By matching abstract sounds with visual images, players discover new perspectives and deepen their connection with others through meaningful dialogue.

How do you play?
In each of the three rounds, one player acts as the ""Leader"" and chooses an Onomatopoeia card as an ""audio theme."" Other players (Participants) then select a Photo card from their hand that they feel best fits that sound. After everyone presents their photo, the Leader picks their favorite, followed by a discussion where everyone shares why they perceived that specific match."

-from the publisher

boardgame: おおいそがしオーケストラ (Busy Busy Orchestra)
description:
"“A music fairy lives in this venue.” Upon hearing this rumor, the conductor had a brilliant idea! If the fairy could cast a spell in time with the music, wouldn’t this become the greatest orchestra ever? Conducting while also giving magical instructions… this is going to be busy busy work!

As a 3-minute masterpiece plays, cast spells on the musicians to create the most beautiful music. The conductor conveys the instructions from the score using only body movements and the baton, while the fairy watches and places magic tiles in the correct positions. Work together to clear the challenges that appear one after another and aim for a high score!"

-from the publisher

"25 tiles are used. Tiles are placed to connect matching colors.
Create patterns to score points. The player with the higher score wins."

-from the publisher

"What is the setting? 
BaBaBa (バババ抜き) is a reimagined, modern version of the classic card game ""Old Maid"" (known as Baba-nuki in Japan). It is specifically designed to be highly engaging and thrilling, even for adults who might have grown tired of the traditional game.

What is the goal? 
The ultimate goal is to empty your hand by forming sets. However, the critical losing condition is collecting 3 ""BaBa"" (Joker) cards. If you end up holding 3 BaBas in your hand, you instantly lose the game. Your objective is to avoid drawing that fatal third BaBa while trying to get rid of your other cards.

How do you play? 
The core mechanics are similar to traditional Old Maid, but with two major twists: first, you must collect and discard matching sets of 3 cards instead of pairs (2 cards). Second, there are 3 ""BaBa"" (Joker) cards hidden in the deck instead of just one. As players draw cards from each other, the tension rises—whoever draws and accumulates all 3 BaBas loses the game. This simple but brilliant change creates intense psychological interactions and thrilling suspense.
"

-from the publisher

protocole-zero is a strategic trick-taking game focused on area control and resource manipulation.

Cards are not collected when won: instead, they create value cards in the center of the table, which players compete to control using tokens. These values can be contested, shifted, or temporarily disabled.

"A message from the BOSS:
"Identify the most suspicious person from the seven individuals involved in the case.
Uncovering the truth is not important; the goal is a definite indictment.""

The seven witnesses—are their testimonies true?
Clever alibis, tricks, perjury, accomplices, confessions.
Deduction and fiction, a chain of suspicion.
Who is the most suspicious?!

Which cards will you reveal, and which will you keep?
Bluffing, misdirection
This is a mystery game where you can fully enjoy the interplay and psychological warfare between players.

Game Overview
At the start of each round, one situation card is randomly placed face down beneath each witness card.
The remaining situation cards are shuffled and dealt four cards to each player.
The objective of the game is to use the cards dealt to deduce the face-down cards and find the most suspicious person.

Each turn, players reveal one card from their hand and bet accusation chips based on the revealed information.
The witnesses may become suspects or witnesses to the crime.

At the end of the round, players earn points based on the chips they bet on the witness who is deemed the most suspicious.
The game consists of three turns per round, and ends after three rounds.
The player with the most accusation points wins."
publishers: ラウンドテーブルコミュニケーションズ（Round Table Communications）

description:
"Use bait cards numbered 0–9 skillfully and catch the most fish to win.
Before fishing, each turn you lay three bait cards face-down — then fishing begins!
Reveal the face-down numbers one at a time:
   - The player who played the highest number can take the fish!
   - The player who played the lowest number decides where to move next
Use the golden fishing rod to power up your bait, 
but beware — it also provides information to other players!"

-from the publisher

"A party game where wild illustrations born from everyone’s memories bring the fun to life.
Let’s work together to create a single illustration of one or two characters!
Each player draws a different part—such as the “head,” “eyes,” or “mouth.
”Stack the drawings, and have the guesser figure out what the completed picture is!"

-from the publisher

boardgame: Der König des Waldes: Der Barde Promo Card
description: If the Bard  is played in turn order all players have to pick up one card from their courts (except for the  Bard  and the  Assassin ).

boardgame: ポクポクポクチン（Poku Poku Poku Chin）
description:
"Setting:
Inspired by the real-life situation of not knowing how to perform incense rituals at a Japanese funeral, players secretly watch and copy the person in front of them.

Goal:
Work together to successfully recreate the full sequence of poses without mistakes.

How to play:
Players take turns copying all previous poses and adding one new pose, following the rhythm of “poku poku poku, ding!” while memorizing by watching others."

-from the publisher
publishers: ゲーム工房カコムタク(Game Factory KAKOMUTAKU)

boardgame: チムチムドンドンチムドンドン (Chim-Chim-Don-Don-Chim-Don-Don)
description:
"Set to the rhythm of “Eisa,” a traditional Okinawan performing art, this game challenges players to be the first to find the “Chim Chim Don Don Chim Don Don” route by following the symbols on randomly arranged circular cards.

It’s not simply a matter of finding the route quickly; the following elements add depth to the difficulty and make the strategic gameplay more engaging:
1. Restrictions on finding the route are imposed based on the point values of the cards on the table
2. Finding a route that passes through the central card allows you to steal points from other players.
3. Because there are advantage rules for players who haven’t scored yet, strategic decisions arise regarding whether to take the points now.

The game’s unique gameplay, challenge level, and motifs inspired by Okinawa’s distinctive traditional culture will captivate you. We hope you enjoy the sounds and rhythms of Okinawa.
"

-from the publisher
designers: かわごえ たかひで（Takahide Kawagoe）
artists: かわごえ たかひで（Takahide Kawagoe）

description:
"Shiro and Choco, two playful dogs, are out for another walk in the park. The layout of the park changes every time, and their goal is simple: find their favorite bone before the other player does!

But these dogs are anything but predictable. They may suddenly head in the wrong direction, get distracted by interesting smells, or wander off course. To win, players must carefully plan their routes, remember their walking paths, and cleverly bury treasures to deceive their opponent.

Osanpo Wanko is a two-player race game that blends memory, tactical card play, and a touch of luck. Each round, players simultaneously choose a movement card, then resolve actions based on priority. Movement can be straightforward—or chaotic—depending on dice rolls and the dogs’ whims.

As the game progresses, players can bury treasure cards on the board, including powerful effects and even bones. Remembering what was buried—and where—becomes key to gaining an advantage or setting traps. Players can also build “walk sets” by grouping previously used cards, allowing for strategic multi-step moves in later rounds.

The modular board ensures a different park layout every game, while interactive elements like scent markers and moving companions add unexpected twists.

The first player to collect three bones wins the game—but only if they can keep track of where everything is hidden.

A charming yet competitive experience, Osanpo Wanko offers a mix of strategy, memory, and playful unpredictability."

-from the publisher

boardgame: ハイサイゴーヤーハブシーサー (Haisai-Goya-Habu-Shisa)
description:
"A cultural card game that begins with the Okinawan cheer, “Hīyā Sā Sā!” Players must instantly identify combinations of cards featuring bitter melon, shisa, and the fearsome “habu” snake, then shout unique phrases to snatch the cards from one another! Everyone is sure to find their palms sweating with the thrill of the habu’s appearance, all while enjoying the unique sounds of the Okinawan language. With simple rules that transcend language barriers, this game lets you experience Okinawa’s vibrant culture firsthand. Players from all over the world can enjoy this exciting and thrilling game."

-from the publisher
designers: かわごえ たかひで（Tatakhide Kawagoe）
ハイサイすなかわ（Haisai Sunakawa）

boardgame: ブレーメンにあこがれて (Longing for Bremen)
description:
"Longing for Bremen" is a cooperative puzzle game where players work together to stack and arrange six different animals to match a "Target Pattern."

How to Play
Draw a Movement Card: At the start of your turn, draw a card from the Movement Deck to determine the distance (1 to 3 spaces).

Play an Animal Card: Based on the distance drawn, choose and play an Animal Card from your hand to decide which animal will move that many spaces.

Move: You must move the chosen animal in one direction (left or right) for the full distance.

Core Mechanics
Stacking & Splitting: When an animal moves, any animals stacked on top of it move with it as a single unit. However, any animals underneath it are left behind.

Leaping on Top: If an animal (or a stack) ends its movement on a space occupied by another animal, it hops onto the very top of that stack.

The Rebound: If an animal reaches the edge of the board, it bounces back and continues its remaining movement in the opposite direction.

Leapfrog Movement: Animals can pass over other animals/stacks during their movement without stopping.

Victory Condition
The team wins if you successfully recreate the Target Pattern before everyone runs out of cards. For a higher challenge, players can discuss and agree to discard an equal number of cards at the start, aiming for a "Grand Success"!"

-from the publisher

boardgame: The Illusion of the Time Machine
description:
A simple card game with a time travel theme.

---

Setting:
What if the "organization" that led the revolution was different? What if the "item" that spread across the world was different? What if the "promise" that was made was different? What would the world look like today?
In this game, you and your friends are time travelers, each possessing a "Time Machine" to alter the past. These changes affect the present, but it seems everyone has their own intentions... In this battle to rewrite history, will you be the winner?

---

Goal:
The player with the highest total score in their hand at the end of the game wins.
As cards on the Timeline change, the scoring method for your hand ("the Now") changes as well. The key is to repeatedly alter the past, managing both the Timeline and your hand to ensure the final history favors you.

---

How to Play:
• 5 Rounds: The game is played over five rounds.
• On Your Turn: You can perform the following actions:
  - Draw cards.
  - Discard cards.
  - Alter the Past: Change an event on the Timeline.

• How to Alter the Past:
  - Play a card from your hand that matches either the "Number" or "Color" of a card on the Timeline, and place it on top.
  - You may play two cards at once to change both the Color and Number.

• Special Cards:
  - Butterfly Effect Card: When you draw this card, you can swap cards across different eras.
  - Time Police Card: Appearing in the later stages of the game, this card partially restricts your ability to alter the past.

—description from the designer

"A variation of “Daifugo” where the objective is to be the last player with cards remaining—the opposite of the standard rules.
The game’s unique feature is the dilemma of wanting to pass without playing a card, yet feeling compelled to play one in order to win.
It’s a Daifugo-style game that offers a completely different experience despite being part of the Daifugo series."

-from the publisher

"Use your hand to keep an eye on how your cat is doing today!
The later you play a card, the more powerful its effect becomes!
When you run out of your hand, the day ends, and all your cards return to your hand!
Keep collecting cards to strengthen your hand!
You win when your total victory points reach the target score!"

-from the publisher
