"It’s a hand-management card game about new vs old. Players play numbered cards while trying to keep the right mix in hand, and the highest score wins: New Cards are positive, Old Cards are negative, but only New Cards become negative instead."

-from the publisher
designers: イワムラ タクヤ (Takuya Iwamura)
artists: イワムラ タクヤ (Takuya Iwamura)

description:
Flip Dungeon is a flip-and-write game that pits you against a deadly dungeon, featuring a standard poker deck to drive a sandbox style of game play and dry erase player boards to track your progress.

The game takes place over two acts. In Act 1, you will equip and improve your hero before descending into the dungeon. In Act II, enter the dungeon to explore its passages and chambers. Collect enough keys to enter the Boss Monster's lair for one final, epic battle!

Flip Dungeon is a standalone solo-only game.

—description from the publisher

"Bubble and Shadow is a light strategy board game with a literary fantasy theme, adapted from the novel The moon and sixpence.

In the game, playes will alternately appear as either an emissary of the Magician or a Mortal of the earthly realm, signing pacts with each other and engaging in transactions centered around different prices.

Through mutual speculation and probing, you will approach a delicate balance-everything you possess will determine the final outcome."

-from the publisher

"Paraglyph is a game that uses real characters and symbols from throughout history and across the world.
All player simultaneously create clues to help the other players guess their specific secret word.
Your clue is a string created by combining three characters or symbols.
You earn points when other players guess your word correctly, or when you successfully guess someone else's word.
The player with the most points at the end of the game wins."

-from the publisher

boardgame: 星猫メジャーアルカナ (Hoshineko Major Arcana)
description:
"Work out which cards to discard from other’s hands, to be the last one holding cards and win.
Players try to remember the remaining cards from numbers declared already.

The cards are based on the Major arcana of the tarot cards."

-from the publisher

With a must-follow system and no trump suit, players must judge when to win and when to deliberately lose.
The “Pass” action lets you reveal a card to avoid a trick while setting up future points—but high ranks can make you an easy target later.
In addition, matching “5” cards can trigger cancellations, nullifying strong plays and keeping outcomes unpredictable.
With open information and layered mind games, it offers a simple yet deeply engaging tactical experience.
The game consists of one round per player.
The scores from each round are totaled, and the player with the highest score wins.

"The game is set in the remote mountains of old Japan. To become the lord of the mountains, a fox and a raccoon dog compete in a contest of shape-shifting. The goal is to control various territories. You win if the territories you control are connected in all four directions (north, south, east, and west) or if the total power value of your controlled territories reaches +8. Conversely, you lose if the total power value reaches -8. Each round, three of the 16 territory cards (arranged in a 4x4 grid) are revealed. Afterward, players take turns revealing 3 cards from their 6-card hands to compete for control of the corresponding territories in each of the three colors."

-from the publisher

boardgame: モンスロートインミッドサマーナイツドリーム (Monslaught in Midsummer Night's Dream)
description:
"This game is the new deck building game. The player can select and take the new cards in the hand from the discarded pile at the end of the turn. No random.

The players collects the monsters from the field.
Their monsters are added to the deck.
The Boss round is happened every three rounds.

Each cards has the points. After 12th round, the winner is the player with the most points.

This is the series of ""Monslaught"".
Mainly, two systems are added. ""Enter the battle field"" and ""Token cards""."

-from the publisher

boardgame: 生ハムメロンゲーム (Prosciutto and Melon Game)

description:
Outsk8 is a competitive trick-taking card game inspired by the game of SKATE, where players try to force each other into mistakes, land clean tricks, and survive multiple rounds of escalating pressure.

Each round plays like a battle of momentum and positioning: players duel over suits, timing, and risk-taking while a shared "cardline" evolves into a shifting tactical landscape. Survive the round, manage exhaustion, and avoid bailing under pressure - because too many mistakes will cost you your life in the game.

The last skater standing wins immediately, or players compete across three rounds for survival points and exhaustion control.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Οι Άθλοι του Ηρακλή (The Labors of Hercules)
description:
A rare board game manufactured and released in Greece in the early 70's, inspired by the twelve labors of Hercules.

It can be played from 2-6 players. At the start of the game, players place the instruction cards and the priority cards face down. The labor cards are placed face up. Each player draws a priority card to determine the order in which the players will play. On their turn, each player draws an instruction card and moves based on the written order. When a player's pawn stops at a point on the board that depicts a labor, they take the card with that labor. The winner is the player who reaches the end with the most labor cards in their possession.

Designed for the participants of the 2024 West Coast Rumble, these six scenarios are designed for minimum set up time, resulting in maximum playing time.

Q37 – VRATARNICA, YUGOSLAVIA, 1 October 1944: In the shadow of the Danube's Iron Gates, as WWII's tides turn in the Balkans, the 7th SS-Mountain Division Prinz Eugen faces a critical confrontation. On October 1, 1944, amidst fierce combat at Vratarnica, its desperate attempt to secure a vital bridgehead against Bulgarian forces becomes a brutal test of strategy and endurance.

Q38 – Northwest of LIÈGE, BELGIUM, 11 May 1940: As dawn broke on May 11, the German 4th Panzer Division, invigorated by the fall of Fort Eben-Emael and key Albert Canal crossings, launched a relentless advance northwest of Liège, Belgium. With Belgian forces in disarray and the strategic route to Liège compromised, the stage was set for a dramatic German push that would reshape the battle lines of Western Europe.

Q39 – MARNACH, LUXEMBOURG, 16 December 1944: On a frozen morning in Luxembourg, the 38th Panzer-Pionier Battalion and the 304th Panzergrenadier Regiment clashed with the steadfast defenders of Company B, 110th Regiment, U.S. 28th Infantry Division, in a battle for control of Marnach. With both sides entrenched, the Germans' attempt to break through with flamethrowers and explosives met fierce resistance, setting the stage for a pivotal confrontation on the road to Bastogne.

Q40 – SAINT POUÇIN SUR SIOULE, FRANCE, 19 June 1940: In a daring blitz towards the Loire on a June morning in 1940, the elite SS-Leibstandarte Adolf Hitler regiment led a surprise assault on the French-defended town of Saint Pouçin. Racing through mortar fire and armoured cars, the Germans surged into the town, but a fierce French stand and the destruction of a vital bridge turned the tide, setting the stage for a brutal, close-quarters fight.

Q41 – TRÉVIÈRES, FRANCE, 9 June 1944: During the heated Normandy campaign, the US 9th and 38th Infantry Regiments faced a daunting task: capture the strategically positioned village of Trévières. Hindered by rugged bocage and fierce German resistance, the battle turned into a desperate fight against both nature and machine-gun nests, with heroic leadership keeping the American advance alive as they pushed toward their crucial objective before dawn.

Q42 – BREST-LITOVSK, POLAND, 26 June 1941: At the heart of the northern island conflict, Major Gavrilov's East Fort stood as a bastion of Soviet defiance, enduring relentless German artillery and air bombardment. As the fort's walls crumbled under the weight of Stuka bombs, the beleaguered defenders mounted a valiant last stand, highlighting extraordinary courage in the face of overwhelming odds, ultimately marking a heroic, albeit tragic, chapter in the battle's history.

Players take turns rolling the die. Anyone who rolls a 6 may place a Knulli figure of their color on their starting space—specifically, on the corner space marked with a six. This brings them into the game and entitles them to roll again.
With each subsequent roll, a player's Knulli figure may be moved forward by exactly one space in any direction—vertically, horizontally, or diagonally—provided it lands on an adjacent space displaying the number rolled on the die. A move must be made if possible; if none of the adjacent spaces match the rolled number, the player must skip their turn.
If a player rolls a 6 at any point during the game, they must first bring another one of their Knulli figures onto the board and are then entitled to roll again. Once a player has all four of their Knulli figures in play, rolling a 6 allows them to move one of their pieces to any adjacent space of their choice.
If a player moves to an adjacent space that is already occupied by a Knulli figure — even one of their own — they must "knock it out." The knocked-out piece must then return to its owner's starting space to begin the journey all over again.
To enter the target corner space located directly opposite the start, a player must once again roll a 6. Rolling a 6 allows the piece to complete its journey and take its place within the target area.
The first player to successfully get all four of their pieces into the target area wins the game.

It smells like summer — and someone just raided your fridge.

PINKO! is a lively card game inspired by šaltibarščiai, the beloved Lithuanian cold beet soup also known as Pink Soup. Race to collect the right ingredients, snatch them from your neighbors, and when you've got the winning combination — shout PINKO!

Each turn, you take one ingredient card (from the face-up table or the deck) and optionally place a card from your hand or play an action card. Two winning combinations are possible: collect 7 different ingredients for the full "grandma's recipe" (7 points), or aim for the budget-friendly Student's Pink Soup — just kefir, beetroot, cucumber, and potatoes — for a quick 4 points.

The kitchen, however, is not a safe place. Action cards spice things up: the Blender lets you peek at a rival's hand and steal a card — potentially ruining their winning recipe at the last moment. The Ladle lets you draw two extra ingredients. The Jar acts as a wild card joker. But watch out for the Fly (−1 penalty point) and the Cat (cancels your score entirely) — you'd rather swap them than be caught holding them at the end of a round.

Once a player shouts PINKO!, all remaining players get one final turn — enough time for a Blender to steal a critical ingredient and flip the outcome. Points are tallied, and the game continues for one more round than the number of players. The player with the most points after all rounds wins.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Break System: solo roguelite heist game
description:
Some plans were made to be broken…

Claw your way from low-level operative to master strategist in The Break System, a solo heist adventure. Scout, negotiate, infiltrate, and (hopefully) escape! Every decision shapes your experience. How will you manage your timeline? How will you spend your loot? Do you have what it takes?

You won’t be without resources. The Spring Break prequel story is included with new annotations to guide your early steps. Key moments reveal their mechanics and show how chance and choice shape a mission. You don’t need to study the rules upfront; they’re introduced gradually. Take it slow or jump ahead. In this heist, you call the shots.

Beyond the tutorial, the full Break system offers a self-paced framework for roll-and-write campaigns of increasing challenge. Six-sided dice and tactical decisions keep you on your toes, while you track your progress, building your reputation. Even failure isn’t the end; here, learning beats luck. Adapt your plans and carry on to master the art of the steal.

In The Break System, the end is just a beginning.

—description from the designer

Pöbbkviss 5 is the fifth installment in the Icelandic trivia game series. It includes 1,000 new questions covering a broad mix of topics and formats, including general knowledge, famous quotes, “three hints,” and “fivefold.”

The game supports an unlimited number of players and can be played in different ways. In a typical setup, players form teams while one person acts as quizmaster. Teams write down their answers, and the team with the most correct responses wins.

Krakkakviss is a fun and educational trivia game suitable for any occasion. The series has been very popular in Iceland, selling tens of thousands of copies.

Krakkakviss 5 includes 100 cards with 300 brand-new questions covering a wide range of topics.

- What is the maximum number of points you can score in Eurovision?
- Which vegetable makes people cry?
- Has Iceland always been called Iceland?
family: Versions & Editions: Junior Versions of Grown-Up Games

description:
Stubbakviss is a simple trivia game designed for very young children (ages 3–6). It uses clear, colorful illustrations alongside short questions, making it suitable for early learners and pre-readers.

The game includes 100 cards that support recognition, vocabulary, and basic knowledge through play.
family: Versions & Editions: Junior Versions of Grown-Up Games

description:
Krakkakviss: Halloween is a themed trivia game for children built around familiar Halloween topics such as monsters, ghosts, witches, costumes, and candy.

The game includes 200 multiple-choice questions, making it accessible for a wide age range and allowing everyone to take part. It works well in groups and can be played in a variety of ways.
family: Versions & Editions: Junior Versions of Grown-Up Games

description:
Imagine the craziest challenges! Sometimes in a team - all alone. From super light to really crazy! But beware: every second counts! Feed the talking timer with your bills, because for every dollar you get a second of playing time. Clever householding is mandatory – there is no change! Can you manage to successfully master 5 challenges together? Or do you put another boat on it and show that there is more in you? Feed and launch timers – now it’s TIME TO SCHEIN!

Game idea
It's TIME TO SHAM! Together you try to master the most diverse challenges from simple to crazy – sometimes alone, sometimes in a team. Choose who will take up the challenge and how much time you need! Feed the talking timer with bills, because for every dollar you buy yourself a second for the challenge. As a team, however, you only have a certain number of bills available to pass 5 challenges. Better to spend a little more and play it safe, or gamble with a short time? How well do you value yourself? Now is not the time to lose!

Playing end
If you manage to master 5 challenges, you have won! If you run out of money before, unfortunately you didn’t make it this time. Try it again! Do you have any banknotes left after the 5 mastered challenges? Then you can continue playing with it, to create the bonus victory places on the other side of the box underside!

Content
    1 Timer
    54 Banknotes (play money)
    6 Cubes
    100 Challenge cards
    40 action cards
    1 Challenge-Box
    1 Game instructions

-description from publisher's website

At the slippery party game you little freak you get to know each other your dirtiest secrets. First, you answer explosive questions by ranking yourself on a scale of 1 to 5 without the others being able to see it, and then everyone else is trying to guess your answers. Did you correctly rank the answer on the freak-o-meter? Great, you know a lot about each other! You're right off? Then there's probably more freak in your friends than you thought you'd be... you're playing as a team, so you all win or lose together!

How the Game Plays

On your turn:
1.    Roll the die and move along any route.
2.    If you reach a location, you may stop to search it.  You can look at all cards there.  During that turn you can take any number of cards from that location and leave any number of cards from your hand behind, providing you end your turn with a maximum of 6 cards in hand.

Winning:
Once you have collected 3 matching weapon cards (or 2 and a wildcard) and 3 matching suspect cards (or 2 and a wild card) you can race to the Godfather with your accusation.  The winner successfully frames an opponent.

Police Officer Spaces
Land on a Police Officer space, draw a Police Officer card, and follow the instructions immediately.

Some officers are in the pay of the mob. Others are not and could damage your plans. Either way, the office cards can change the state of the game - fast.

Alibi card
If someone has an alibi card in their hand they cannot be named as the killer.  However you cannot hold an alibi card in your hand and win.  So at some point you have to ditch your protection and go it alone.

The Board
The board contains a crime scene and multiple locations where you can gather and discard evidence, which means you’re constantly watching what someone else is doing.

-description from publisher

Cluck & Gold is a lively card game of matching, bluffing, and surprise interactions, where players compete to hold the Golden Chicken at the right moment.
Players are dealt a hand of cards and take turns discarding matching pairs or drawing from other players. The goal is to reduce your hand while keeping track of the powerful Golden Chicken card, which must be held to win the round.
Special action cards create dynamic gameplay by forcing hand swaps, reversing card flow, blocking actions, or allowing players to secretly peek at opponents’ hands. Chick cards add extra interaction by letting players take cards from others.
The round ends when a player is left with only the Golden Chicken card. That player wins the round and earns a golden egg token. The first player to collect three golden eggs wins the game.
“light, interactive, and easy-to-learn”

—description from the publisher

Through clever planning, tactical shifting, and a bit of luck, players race to arrange all dachshund cards into the correct order. Treat cards trigger the reveal of wag cards, creating twists that change how walk cards can be used. Breeder cards introduce additional player decisions, while solo challenges provide an alternative way to play.

—description from the publisher

In this eighteen-card micro game players arrange the biome cards (mountain, steppe, desert, forest, water) in a row and pick opposite sides to play on and receive a starting family card. The rest of the family deck is shuffled and three family cards are drawn with both players taking turns choosing from. The top of the draw deck determines what hazards are affecting which biomes that round. Turns involve moving families to safe zones or activating a family's special ability (such as a waterskin to counteract drought) in an attempt to grow their tribe. When the deck is exhausted the largest tribe with the most ancestral bonuses wins.

Welcome to Meepletown! While the town sleeps, the ovens are glowing with the sweet scent of fresh dough and warm glaze.

Tomorrow is the biggest Cookie Festival Meepletown has ever seen, and you must prepare your small shop for the big day!

In Ookiee Cookie, you and your Apprentice must build upgrades, trade in the Cookie Market, and arrange your cookies in the most appealing way. To get cookies, you will reach into the Master Bakery — a cloth bag used for random draws.

But be careful: managing a cookie shop isn't easy, and your Apprentice might be a bit clumsy!

How It Plays
Ookiee Cookie features a quick and simple turn structure: Draw one card, Play one card.

You draw from the Festival Deck and must choose one of three ways to play your card:

a. Resolve an Action: Perform immediate effects like trading, grabbing cookies from the bakery, or swapping cookie positions.
b. Build an Upgrade: Pay a cost in cookies to gain permanent abilities and Victory Points (VP).
c. Set a Stabilizer: Place the card in a dedicated slot to help your Apprentice hold more cookies.

The Cookie Market & Supervisors
A Cookie Market facilitates cookie trading. Using the "Go to Market" action, players follow a Pick-Slide-Replace mechanic: take a cookie from the bottom row, slide the column down, and replace the top slot with a cookie from their own shop.

Throughout the game, Supervisor Cookies are drawn from the bag, triggering Event cards and permanently taking up market slots, steadily reducing available options.

The Apprentice Tray
Players can have their Apprentice hold cookies in their tray for more Victory Points, but this comes with a Push-Your-Luck element. Every time cookies are added or swapped on the tray, you must roll the Apprentice Die. Rolling a number sets a temporary capacity limit; any cookies exceeding this limit fall into the Pile of Crumbs, resulting in negative points.

Game End & Winning
The festival concludes immediately when the fifth Supervisor is drawn or the Festival Deck is depleted. The player with the most Victory Points wins.

Points are awarded for:
a. Cookies in the Apprentice tray.
b. Set Collection on shop displays.
c. Upgrades on your shop.
d. Festival Goals.
e. Finally, you lose points for every cookie in your Crumb pile!.

So, get ready and remember: even the greatest plans may turn to crumbs!

—description from the designer

5150: Shipyard

Speed or armor. Firepower or cargo. Stability or Risk.

Across Known Space, ships aren’t just bought — they’re engineered, compromised, overbuilt, under-crewed, refit, and pushed past safe limits. Every Captain eventually makes the same choice: build safe, or build bold.

5150: Shipyard is the structural expansion for 5150 campaign play. This book gives you full control over how your ship is built, modified, strained, and maintained — without changing a single Contact in Space rule.

This supplement is designed for use with the 5150: New Beginnings core rules, available on Wargame Vault.

Inside You’ll Find:

     A complete Structural HP (SHP) construction system tied directly to Hull size.
     Rules for reallocating Thrust, Firepower, Cargo Slots, and Crew capacity.
     Risk Conversions that let you exceed safe limits — at permanent operational cost.
     Crew Strain, System Crew Requirements (SCR), and understaffing penalties.
     Refit, overhaul, and stabilization rules for long-term campaign evolution.
     Military, civilian, criminal, and exotic ship lists fully compatible with GateMax scale.


Your Ship Is Now Part of the Campaign.
After this book, your vessel isn’t just transportation. It is a structural decision engine that shapes your Reputation, your risk profile, your available jobs, and whether your campaign survives the next month.

Overbuild the drives. Strip out crew space for cargo. Convert civilian hulls into gunships. Push an old freighter past its design limits and hope the maintenance bills don’t bury you before the next job pays out.

Every modification has a cost. Every shortcut leaves a mark. Every Captain thinks the ship can take one more upgrade.

Welcome to the Shipyard, Captain.

Smurf up your already smurf-tastic game with this bonus mini-exsmurfsion pack! More smurfs? More fun!

The Smurfs: Hidden Village Mini Expansion comes with 4 new Smurf cards and 1 new Cake card to add extra variety to your games!

—description from the publisher's website

Join a trio of daring dice-head adventurers descending into the heart of a restless volcano in search of glory, treasure, and the secrets at the center of the Earth. Each chapter of your journey brings new obstacles, strange tools, and terrifying creatures. Roll, adapt, and push your luck as the depths grow darker … and trust that friendship will prevail!

Rolling Deep is a Balatro-inspired roguelike dice-building adventure featuring a cornucopia of unlockable content, addicting challenges, and delicious combos. Each round, you roll dice representing your team’s daring actions as you push deeper underground. Decide when to act (scoring a roll) and when to try your luck (rerolling individual dice in pursuit of a stronger outcome).

When you score a roll, its value advances your progress toward a target score, the difficulty required to overcome the current stage of the journey. Each set of 3 rounds is called a chapter. Each chapter raises the challenge, and the final round of every chapter features a boss — a dangerous encounter that alters the rules for that round.

Between rounds, you visit the shop, where you spend coins to acquire upgrades and consumable gear. Some upgrades permanently alter the sides of your dice, changing their values and allowing them to generate coins or reroll potions when scored. Others boost your score, either every roll or when you score specific result ranges, rewarding careful control over how boldly (or cautiously) you push forward.

Your most valuable resources are time and reroll potions. Time determines how many opportunities you have to score your dice and accumulate a total score for the round, while reroll potions allow you to take a chance and — you guessed it — reroll individual dice before committing to a result.

Your goal is to survive 5 chapters and reach the volcano’s core. If you can overcome the final boss, you claim the treasure of the deep and escape, winning the game (and possibly discovering more mysteries to pursue in another expedition). If you fail to meet a round’s target score, you lose. Better luck next expedition!

With each run, strive to complete new achievements that unlock hundreds more cards to utilize and challenges to overcome!

Rolling Deep is the first title in Bitewing Games’ new Table for One Series — a line of solo-only games featuring oodles of challenges with vintage cartoon art.

—description from the publisher

A pirate themed social deduction game where a crew of pirates are trying to deduce who amongst them have succumbed to the evil skeleton curse. Each player serves a different role, randomly assigned from a set tailored by a game master at the start of the game, granting them abilities and their allegiance. Every role on the crew is displayed in the Crew Log at the center of the playspace for all to see, with additional decoy roles added for each skeleton in the game. What player is what role is up to the group to figure out amongst themselves. Every player is also granted a life token in front of them, symbolizing one of the game's seats, and if that player is still alive.

The game is guided by the game master, who ferries the players through the day and night cycle of the game and builds a narrative for the players. During the day, the players will get up and move around to have private conversations amongst themselves or change seats with one another. Where and who they end up sitting next to might play into several of the roles in the game. As a group they can discuss and debate potential leads, share their alibi and attempt to claim a role in the Crew Log, and finally nominate and vote on who they suspect to be one of the evil skeletons. The player who receives more than half; and the most votes will be marked for execution at the end of the day.

Moving into the still tides of night time, all players cover their eyes and fall asleep. The players are then silently woken up by the Game Master in the night order to act out their role abilities; along with the skeletons, who choose a target to eliminate that night. As the sun rises in the sky and the players are woken up to a new daytime, the game master announces the deaths of the night, and flips over their life tokens. All dead players are very much still in the game, still being able to converse and vote with the group, but not use their abilities, nominate for execution, or move from their seat.

And so the cycle continues, until the skeletons match the number of alive pirates left in the game, or the pirates successfully rid their crew of the skeleton curse by the means of execution. Either the skeletons, or the pirates win. There is an extra hurdle for both teams, which are any of the many third party solo sloopers the game master may introduce; all with their own goals and winning conditions. Good luck, Pirates!

-description from designer

Eureka! It’s the discovery of the century! Petey Pickaxe and his friends have found an ocean of gems beneath the soil. Now it’s up to them to mine deep and find treasures untold. But resources are limited and daylight is burning — can they reach the best riches before it’s too late?

Eureka is an addicting solo campaign with quick-playing levels and evolving challenges. Manage your supply of dice wisely to score as many layers (categories) as best as possible! Each round you’ll roll five dice from your supply and assign them to layers on the mine board. Be careful — you won’t get those dice back until you score those layers! The game ends when you don’t have five dice to roll at the start of a round or when you have scored all the layers. Aim for a high score, then move on to the next level!

Once you’ve succeeded in all the levels of one chapter, you’ll be able to move on to the next chapter and discover what new challenges await in the sealed envelope. Each level features specific bronze, silver, and gold scoring thresholds — try for gold across all the levels for the ultimate victory! The score chart also includes extra columns to record your personal best or compete against family and friends.

Eureka is the second title in Bitewing Games’ new Table for One Series — a line of solo-only games featuring oodles of challenges with vintage cartoon art.

—description from the publisher

You have wandered into a world of dreams, and now you must escape from the Dream Phantoms!
Night Parade is a thrilling card game where you must trust your luck and probability as you flip cards.
On your turn, you choose whether to add cards or take a chance and flip cards.
If you reveal a Golden Lantern Card, it's a huge success! You can pass the Phantoms to another player. But if you fail, the Phantoms stay with you...
Avoid collecting too many Phantoms and being eliminated, and try to survive until the very end by passing them on to others!

—description from the publisher

Journey Horizon is a card game where players travel across the world to discover breathtaking landscapes.

The game lasts 4 rounds. In each round, players draft 2 cards and play them to build their journey. The goal is to create a line of 9 cards by the end of the game, scoring as many points as possible and unlocking bonuses.

The game includes 61 beautifully illustrated, unique cards divided into 4 biomes: mountains, desert, forest, and ocean. Each time you add a card to your line, you create new and unique ways to score points.

There are also 3 shared objectives that give players extra opportunities to earn even more points.

Will you create the most memorable journey?

Memo Burger is a fast-paced memory and set-collection game for 2–4 players. As chefs in a busy kitchen, players race to fulfill orders by flipping ingredient tiles from the "Cooking Area," their own hidden hand, or by asking opponents for specific items. The game features a unique "Stacked Burger" mechanic: completed burgers stay on the table face-down, and their ingredients can be "raided" by any player to complete future orders. The first chef to reach 12 points wins.

—description from the publisher

Testphase Liebe is a social card game for 3–8 players focused on honest answers, discussion, and humor.

Players draw question cards, choose one of two prompts, and everyone answers.

The group decides which answer is best and awards a Green or Red Flag. 
Special cards add additional twists throughout the game, offering effects such as stealing cards, granting immunity, interrogating another player, or giving gifts.

Players count their Green Flags as positive points and their Red Flags as negative points. The player with the highest total score is crowned the Best Lover, while the player with the lowest score becomes the Worst Lover.

Testphase Liebe is best enjoyed as a lighthearted, honest, and humorous experience, encouraging conversation, self-reflection, and laughter among friends or couples.

Gregor Hägele will release his second music album, Testphase Liebe, on May 15, 2026. The album explores themes of commitment anxiety and personal growth. As part of the album promotion, he collaborated with HABA to develop the card game, which is also included in exclusive fan bundles.

—description from the publisher

A MIGHTY TOME TO EXPAND YOUR BARONS' WAR EXPERIENCE!

The reign of King John (1199-1217) was a tumultuous turning-point in the history of England. Decades of tension with the kings of France boiled over into conflict which saw Normandy, Brittany and Anjou lost. Meanwhile the fractious and restive rulers of Wales sought to exploit any sign of weakness and the kings of Scotland cast covetous eyes on Northumbria. At centre stage was King John, a figure whose distrusting nature made him as ungrateful to his allies as he was spiteful to his enemies. Resentment, hatred and fear pushed many of England's barons into armed defiance, leading to the Magna Carta, an unprecedented compromise limiting royal power. When it was quashed by the Pope, civil war followed.

The Barons' War: King John brings these turbulent times onto the tabletop. It updates the Feudal European retinue list and introduces six more factions to the game: the light and flexible Welsh, the diverse and colourful Scottish, the aggressive Poitevins and the proudly disciplined Flemish, as well as hard-bitten professional Mercenaries and the cunning and desperate Outlaws.

The sourcebook also provides rules and background for a score of the dramatis personae of the story, enabling you to command your troops with the vindictive King John, the chivalrous William Marshal and the scheming King Philip Augustus - or even Robin Hood and his Merry Men.

Ten historical scenarios allow you to re-tell the bloody story of King John's reign, from bitter clashes in the Welsh Marches to the king's harrying of his enemies. You can recreate the titanic pitched battle of Bouvines or follow the stages of the climatic battle of Lincoln.

Whichever side you take, there will be something for you in the first supplement for the new edition of The Barons' War.

—description from the publisher

Arnie, Barney, SzzKeat, Snoop, and Mantis have gotten into a chaotic mix-up!

Find them in the jumble and help them avoid being confused by Hypnita’s powers.

Roll the dice and quickly collect the cards showing all the characters you need. Too many or too few will earn you penalty points, so look closely!

Whoever has the fewest penalty points after five rounds wins!

—description from the publisher

Suddenly, everything around Arnie & Barney is bone dry.
Where on earth did all the water go?! Now it’s time to collect water drops. But be careful: each container can hold a maximum of 11 drops. Any more and it overflows, but fewer usually won’t be enough to win! So think carefully whether to keep collecting or stop in time before the water spills over and you lose everything.

The first player to secure the most drops three times becomes the “Water Champion” and wins the game!


     easy rules so you can start playing right away
     a fun twist on the classic Blackjack


—description from the publisher

A simple fun game to help improve creativity in kids and help them boost their confidence. A game to make the queen laugh and by preforming silly and fun tasks. Playing Jatra can help kids learn social skills and communication skills, build creativity and imagination, and boost their confidence.

The game comes with a board and activities cards. The player picks the activities card and performs the action on the  cards. Activities could be like Act like a lollipop and make noise like a monkey or find 5 cards on the board. The kids will end up giggling and earning coins as they through the set of activities.

—description from the publisher

Langur Burja, a dice game that keeps you on your toes, is popularly played across Nepal especially during the span of Dashain and Tihar. This gripping game, in its ethos, is very similar to the dice game Crown and Anchor played by British sailors, or Chuck-A-Luck played in America. This game comes with six dice and six boards with two sides. Each die has six faces with different symbols: Jhanda (flag), Burja (crown), Itta (diamond), Paan (heart), Surath (spade), and Chidi (club).

The game is played between a banker, who rolls the dice, and the players, who place their bets on one or more symbols on the board. The players will only win if the symbols they placed their bet on
show up on the faces of the dice rolled.

—description from the publisher

Dharahara is an engaging family-friendly tabletop game that combines elements of strategy, dexterity, and excitement. Participants collaboratively construct a magnificent Dharahara tower, simultaneously guiding their player pieces to ascend to its summit.

Game ends when the tower collapses and the player who was on the top floor before the collapse wins the game. Or the person who has the conqueror cad wins.

GAME PLAY
Begin play by distributing 3 floor cards to each player.
Play proceeds in a clockwise direction.

     Player starts by laying any wall card from the pile on the base card following the position of the yellow marker. Follow by adding a floor card from their hand on the wall they just built.
     Move your player 1 floor up by placing your player in the spot indicated by the crown. Follow the task of the floor card if any. If the card carries a battle symbol then 2 players need to battle saying “tope, khukuri, jaal.”
     The next player follows. Players climb higher one floor at a time. It is ok for two or more players to be on the same floor.

'Momo' is a card game which involves identifying a set that is either all the same OR all different in color, shape, number of momos, and serving plate. With no turns or luck, the first player to first find a set and yell ‘Momo’ gets the cards from the table.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: あなたの1位はチョコミント!?(Is Your No.1 Mint Chocolate!?)
description:
A communication game in which players predict and guess each other’s values and preferences.

In each round, a theme card with five options is revealed. Players secretly rank these options based on their own values and preferences.

Once everyone has finished ranking, players try to predict how the other players have ranked the same options. Correct predictions earn points.

After all rankings are revealed, players compare their answers in detail. Bonus points are awarded for perfect matches and other special conditions.

After several rounds, the player with the highest total score wins the game.

—description from the publisher

Get ready to battle your bros in the ultimate handshake showdown! Pass hilarious handshakes from player to player, and try not to lose your cool as the seriously street greetings get more and more elaborate. One wrong move and you're out!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Warfighter War: The WWII Europe Strategic Combat Card Game
description:
Warfighter War takes the solitaire (also plays great in cooperative mode with one or more other players) Warfighter tactical design to the strategic level. In Warfighter tactical, a unit card is an individual soldier. In Warfighter War, a unit card is a brigade, regiment, battalion, or company. In tactical, each location is roughly 50 to 400 yards. In War, each location is roughly 10 to 50 miles.

In the tactical game, you fought way through streets, buildings, and other tactical locations. In War, you'll command your forces as they capture cities, forests, and other strategic objectives.

The general rule framework of War is based on the Warfighter tactical system that has been in print, and refined, over the past decade. If you know how to play Warfighter, you'll be able to jump right in to War.

—description from the publisher

As a rally driver, you will try to set the best time against the clock on 3 rally stages of increasing difficulty.

Entered in the 2025 Traditional Deck Game Design Contest.

Played with a traditional deck of playing cards and with light blackjack rules, with a rally theme, you will try to nail each sector represented by single numerical cards by avoiding busting and to be as precise as possible. You'll have gears (numerical cards) in 3 different piles to help you reach those sectors right, Jacks and Queens will help you by braking (-1) or boosting (+1), Kings are your clock, and Jokers are your damage trackers.

Bust too many times, and your car will be totaled. Suffer too many time penalties, and you will get disqualified. Try to set a record by having more time bonuses than time penalties !

—description from the designer

ELI Tree is a board game for children AND adults.

2-5 players can participate, and a round lasts a little over half an hour, depending on the players' skill and the luck of the draw. With four players, it's recommended to play in two teams, while with 2, 3, or 5 players, everyone plays against everyone else. The unique idea behind ELI Tree is that every player can move any tree across the board. This means the outcome of the game can suddenly and unexpectedly change. Furthermore, all ELI Trees are the same color.

Thanks to the 40 event cards, the gameplay remains varied and exciting.

Whether you're catching up to the next tree, stealing a tree, changing direction, or using a joker, caution is still advised, as ELI might fall into a deep tree sleep and have to sit out. But don't worry, since every player can move any tree across the board, there's no standstill. The number cards (those playing cards that aren't event cards) indicate how many spaces an ELI can move on the board. The goal of the game is to bring four ELI trees to safety in your own target zone.

—description from the designer

A fast-thinking card game where you are an adventurous cat fighting for the greatest treasure. Choose your class, sabotage your friends' plans, and escape the Dragon's dungeon!

In Cats & Dungeons, players are adventurous cats escaping a dungeon while grabbing as much treasure as possible.

The game is played in real time. All players act simultaneously, placing tiles from their hand onto the shared table. To place a tile, it must be aligned against at least one tile already on the table and form at least one door to door connection. Tiles cannot be rotated, and once placed they cannot be picked back up. Each player also has a Collapse tile that can be played on top of creature tiles to block enemies.

Once a player places all their tiles, they grab an Exit tile from a shared stack. The player who takes the last Exit tile ends the round. But beware! There is not enough Exit tiles for everyone, so you must hurry!

Scoring uses a rock-paper-scissors system. Each cat and creature tile shows a symbol. When two tiles are connected side by side through a door, their symbols duel: wins score the defeated creature's coin value, ties score 1 coin, and losses score nothing. The Exit card also adds its printed coins to the player's total.

The round winner earns a gold chest, the runner up earns silver. Two silvers convert into a gold. The game ends when someone collects 3 gold chests.

Kluster Trio is an incredibly fun magnet game, perfect for family time and game nights with friends! Its triangular magnets allow for more precise control of magnetic fields.
Kluster's rules are extremely simple. However, they allow for many strategic possibilities! Take turns placing a magnet inside the string. But be careful because you collect all the magnets that stick together. The first one to get rid of their magnets wins!
With Elimination Mode, Kluster Trio can be played with up to 8 players!
Kluster Trio also features an Equilibrium Mode for even more twists and excitement!

—description from the publisher

The Marines have made it to Egghead Island, and are after our heroes! Play as fate, and guide Luffy and Bonney through enemies and obstacles in their rush to escape Egghead. Be the first player to help Luffy and Bonney to ESCAPE FROM EGGHEAD ISLAND!

Press your luck by rolling dice and overcoming obstacles. Collect resources and allies to modify your dice rolls, and move toward victory. Your objective is to be the first player to escape from three different Bosses and leave Egghead Island before the Vegapunk Timer runs out!

—description from the designer

boardgame: Things on Strings: Traveler Promo Pack
description: The Traveler Promo Pack adds even more incredibly illustrated Thing cards. We can't stop. Usable in both Things on Strings and the original Things in Rings.

Creativity beats vocabulary in Acrosticism. Players reverse engineer phrases from Word Cards to match revealed Theme Cards. If you can read, you can play.

In each turn, one player serves as the Judge while the others create backronyms from the letters of a Word Card in their hand. The goal is not to search for dictionary words. Players build a phrase that fits the theme using the letters they have available.

How It Works

The Theme: Flip a Theme Card, anything from "a terrible name for a new perfume" to "a motto for surviving a bad Monday."

The Challenge: Acrosticize a Word Card into a backronym that fits the theme. Players have 2 minutes. Each letter becomes the start of a new word.

The Verdict: The Judge selects their favorite entry. The most creative, hilarious, or fitting backronym wins the turn.

Why It Is Different

No drawing. Every Word Card played is gone for good, so players manage a shrinking hand across the game.

Simple to start. If you can read, you can play.

Fast paced. Everyone acrosticizes at the same time, and turns stay tight at roughly 5 minutes per player.

High variety. With 53 Theme Cards and 126 Word Cards, Acrosticism offers 6,678 unique combinations.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Escape de la Casa del Infierno
description:
Escape de la Casa del Infierno ("Escape from the House of Hell") is an Argentine gothic horror and survival board game. Set in February 1989, players take on the roles of characters exploring a haunted house, using tiles, miniatures, and an interactive guide to survive. It is a cooperative/competitive game with a solo mode option and high replayability.


     Follow the clues to locate your missing cousin.
     Search furniture, corners, and rooms to find useful items for escape and survival.
     Flee from or eliminate the sinister creatures that lurk in the dark hallways.
     Interact with hundreds of unique interactive points scattered throughout the cursed house.
     Discover personal documents to uncover the truth about what happened to the people who once lived here.
     Use the Ouija board to contact the spirits of the family.
     Find a way to escape before the storm breaks and night falls.


Key Features:

Theme: Gothic horror and survival with a strong narrative focus.
Components: Includes corridor and room tiles, objective tokens, and miniatures; total weight approximately 1.6–2 kg.
Mechanics: Role-playing elements, interactive game guide, and random tile setup ensuring high replayability.
Players: Cooperative/competitive gameplay with solo mode support.

•••

Escape de la Casa del Infierno es un juego de mesa argentino de terror gótico y supervivencia. Ambientado en febrero de 1989, los jugadores encarnan personajes que exploran una casa embrujada, utilizando losetas, miniaturas y una guía interactiva para sobrevivir. Es un juego colaborativo/competitivo con opción de modo solitario y alta rejugabilidad


     Encuentra los rastros de para localizar a tu prima desaparecida.
     Revisa muebles, rincones y habitaciones para ubicar algún objeto útil en el escape y la supervivencia.
     Huye de ellas o elimina a las siniestras criaturas que se harán presentes en los oscuros pasillos.
     interactúa con los cientos de puntos interactivos únicos diseminados por la casa maldita.
     Localiza documentos personales para revelar parte de la verdad sobre lo que ocurrió a la gente que vivía aquí.
     Utiliza la Ouija y contacta con los fantasmas de la familia.
     Ingéniatelas para lograr encontrar una vía de escape antes de que estalle la tormenta y caiga la noche.


Características Principales:

Temática: Horror gótico y supervivencia, con fuerte carga narrativa.
Componentes: Incluye losetas de pasillo y habitaciones, fichas de objetivo y miniaturas, con un peso aproximado de 1.6 kg a 2 kg.
Mecánicas: Juego rolero, con guía de juego interactiva y losetas aleatorias que garantizan rejugabilidad.
Jugadores: Cooperativo/Competitivo, con soporte para modo solitario.

Looking for Gabby's Dollhouse toys? In this a-meow-zing edition of MONOPOLY Junior, you can play by Gabby's Dollhouse figures! Jump around the game board visiting the Gabby Cats and exploring their rooms, from Cakey’s Kitchen to DJ Catnip's Music Room.

Key Features:

- PERFECT FOR Gabby's Dollhouse FANS: This new Junior edition of Hasbro's classic board game Monopoly is perfect for younger fans aged 5 and up!
- INCLUDES 4 PAPER TOKENS: choose from tokens including Gabby, Pandy Paws or Mercat! and tour recognisable spaces, like Bathroom, Music Room and The Fairy Tail Garden!
- UNIQUE GABBY'S DOLLHOUSE GAME BOARD featuring Gabby, Mercat, Pandy Paws, Dj Catnip, Catrat, Cakey, Pillowcat and Baby Box! Who do you like the most?
- BUILD YOUR GABBY'S DOLLHOUSE EMPIRE: Buy up pink sets and use your Gabby's themed Monopoly money and Pandy Paws, Mercat, Cakey or Gabby to expand your empire.
- WILL YOU OUTSMART YOUR OPPONENTS? Watch out for Kitty Cat Surprise cards, which could help or hinder your chances of success!

-description from publisher's website
family: Versions & Editions: Versions with IP after the original release

description:
Monopoly is a roll-and-move game where players move around the game board buying or trading properties, developing their properties with houses and hotels. When opponents land on players owned property, the owning player collects rent from the landing player. Owning sets of properties increases the rent paid to the player who owns them, by the player who lands on the square. The ultimate goal is to have the most money and drive opponents into bankruptcy.

The classic Monopoly, now in a Danish edition! Buy Aalborg, Billund, or Copenhagen Airport, or cities like Vejle and Esbjerg.

You’ve still got a long way to go. Use your new hopped-up hyperdrive to rearrange your ROVE modules faster than the speed of light!

Hyperdrive Hops introduces new movement cards that can be used to teleport modules anywhere on the board!

—description from the publisher

Lander Mode is a promo card, which allows you to finish a game of Astro ROVE and combine it with a game of ROVE. Lander will literally bridge the gap between the two games.

Once you've completed a normal game of Astro ROVE, place this card over the last mission to initiate Lander Mode, then continue your game using the original ROVE rules and cards.

—description from the publisher

A Gritty Game of Hue-and-Cry.

Stop, thief! — or are they?

Posse Comitatus is a small-box bluffing card game themed on the historical hue and cry of pre-police London.

The Mechanic

On your turn you slide a card face-down across the table to any opponent and declare a suspect type ("This is a Smuggler"). The receiver picks one of three responses:


     Call TRUTH — flip the card. If you're right, the sender pays a coin and adds the card to their rap sheet. If you're wrong, you do.
     Call LIE — same logic, opposite direction.
     Pass it on — pick the card up secretly, then slide it face-down to a different player (anyone who hasn't already touched it during this cascade) with your own fresh declaration.


The cascade continues until somebody calls TRUTH or LIE. Only the FINAL claim — the one made just before the reveal — is tested. If you bluffed and the next player passed it on, you got away with it.

Three of any one suspect type on your rap sheet and you're DISGRACED — out of the game. Last citizen standing wins.

The Two Specials

Magistrate (3 in deck) — When revealed, the receiver dodges entirely. No rap sheet penalty, no coin paid. The receiver also clears one card from their existing rap sheet.

Wanted Felon (6 in deck) — When revealed, all stakes triple. Three Wanted Felons on a rap sheet is a disgrace, just like any other type.

Optional Variants


    Hue and Cry! — Bystanders can side-bet on the reveal.
    The Bribe — Sender openly offers coins to influence the call.
    The Long Chase — Cards passed by 3+ players pay double when called.


 
Background
 The mechanic is a refined cousin of Cockroach Poker (Jacques Zeimet, 2004): pass the card, claim, counter-claim, the wrong person ends up holding the bag. Two design refinements:
 A 60-card deck with eight distinct suspect types at calibrated frequencies — Magistrate count was reduced from 6 to 3 and three suspect types bumped from 8 to 9 each, after a 300-game Monte Carlo simulation showed the original mix made empty-hand the dominant disgrace mode instead of the intended rap-sheet-3.
 Two special cards that change the consequence of a reveal — Magistrate as a relief valve, Wanted Felon as a stakes amplifier.

The History Is Real

Every card type, every flavor line, and every variant is rooted in 18th-century London street history. The hue and cry was the actual law from the Statute of Winchester (1285) until the Metropolitan Police Act of 1829 — refusal to join a chase was a criminal offense.

The Magistrate's effect echoes R v Pinney (1832), which established that a magistrate who acted in good faith escaped prosecution even when the riot still happened. The £40 Wanted Felon is the actual bounty under the Highwayman Act of 1697 — the trade Jonathan Wild built his criminal empire on before being hanged at Tyburn in 1725.

—description from the designer

A fantasy boardgame where dragons and poker collide. Ditch the old rules and fall in love with a wildly, fresh, mind-bending twist on poker. In WONOff, baby dragons scamper back to their castle when you play “WONOFF” hands—cards that are delightfully "one-off" from classic poker.

Sneaky KNOCKOUT cards can flip the board in a heartbeat, turning sure wins into chaos and making every round a thrilling gamble of wit and luck. Vivid fantasy art, fast turns, and unpredictable twists keep every game fresh, fierce, and full of laughs.

Why you’ll snatch this off the shelf:

Unforeseen Outcomes: Traditional losing hands become brilliant winners
Heart-stealing characters: Adorable baby dragons that players will love
Vivid playing surface: Bold, color-drenched fantasy design
Easy to learn, fun to master: Perfect for newbies and seasoned card players
Party-perfect pacing: 2–7 players — quick rounds & big, unexpected moments

—description from the designer

Warrior Heroes Adventures in Talomir: Our updated and final Fantasy version!

Compatible with Warring Heroes To Be King and Warring Fleets!

Warrior Heroes Adventures in Talomir.

What: Sword and Sorcery Fantasy rules.
Scale: Man to Man skirmish level.
Your Role: Lead a small group of Adventurers in a variety of Adventures
Playability: Designed for solo, same-side, and head to head play.
Playable with the counters we’ve included or any minis you may already have. 
In Warrior Heroes Adventures in Talomir you explore the Fantasy world of Talomir, home to a wide variety of unique Races. 
Build your Band with a variety of Characters. Track the progress of your Star, you, with light bookkeeping. Gain rewards when you succeed, suffer penalties when you fail.

Inside you’ll find:

     Over 40 unique Races and Nations to play.
     140 color counters.
     Play on the 2 color Battle Boards that are included or the tabletop.
     A variety of Attributes to make your Characters unique.
     Multiple Character Classes to choose from.
     An easy Campaign Tracking system to see if your Star, you, increases in skill while gaining Fame and Fortune.
     Over a dozen unique Adventures to have; some you choose, some are thrust upon you.


All this and much more.

Do you have what it takes to be a Hero?

—description from the publisher

They fly the Stygustanni flag my Lord.
A Stygustanni Fleet so far west? That’s a question best answered later as you see them bearing down on your ships.
Fantasy Naval Warfare on the High Seas of Adventure!
Send the signals – fire on my command, you say to the drummer. He jumps to it and drums out the cadence. 
Ready the guns, the Captain says as his Crew swings into action. You’re far from any closest port and losing is not an option.
The Crews are ready, the Stygustanni boats get closer…closer…closer.

Warrior Heroes – Warring Fleets is set in the mythical world of Talomir and is compatible with Warrior Heroes – Adventures in Talomir as well as our Big Battle rules – Warrior Heroes – To Be King!

Talomir is a land where dozens of Nations vie for dominance and like other games, we've provided you with Navy Lists that describe your ships and how to assemble them for Battle. Other games allow for fighting individual Battles, but we've taken it farther, much farther.

We’ve added a simple to use, fast play Campaign System that ties all your Battles together to insure that every Battle means something. With the Warrior Heroes – Warring Fleets Campaign System, you are the Commander of the Fleet, a Pirate or taking on well-paying Merchant jobs.

And best of all; you can play solo, cooperatively against the game, or competitively, head to head.

Fire! The signal cannon fires one shot. A moment passes; your ships fire in unison and the Battle is on!

—description from the publisher

Majority Rules: Good to Go is a laid-back bracket-style party game where players debate and vote on head-to-head matchups to decide what comes out on top. Each round presents two options—anything from foods to everyday choices—and the group argues, laughs, and ultimately picks a winner to advance through the bracket. There’s no scoring or real competition; the fun comes from the conversations, disagreements, and seeing what the group crowns as the final champion.

It’s basically more about the arguments than the outcome, making it a low-pressure, just-hang-out kind of game.

boardgame: スカイジャンケン (Sky Rock-Paper-Scissors)
description:
スカイジャンケン (Sky Rock–Paper–Scissors) is a home version of rock–paper–scissors that was exhibited at “Is This a Game? Exhibition 3.”

Players choose one of three hands—rock, scissors, or paper—and compete; if you lose, you take damage.
At its core, it’s rock–paper–scissors, but it includes design elements that allow it to function as a strategic game. As you continue playing, it begins to feel more like an abstract strategy game, where you try to read several moves ahead of your opponent.

—description from the publisher

The 70's Trivia Game consists of 71 cards with trivia questions in 5 different categories. The cards are placed face down on the table and the player on the active player's right asks the trivia question in the chosen category. A correct answer is awarded one point. The first player to get 2 points in each category wins the game.

Mormagic takes children to Mamopi Island, home of the number-loving Mormels. In this mathematical twist on the classic game of Snakes and Ladders, players race to be the first to reach square 100. Instead of rolling a die, players draw cards that show the Mormels arranged in different positions, such as 'above and below' or 'inside and outside'. By correctly describing what they see, they unlock a number and move their Mormel forward. If a player draws a red-edged card, however, their opponents get to choose their move for them, adding a fun layer of playful strategy. Through this clever mechanism, children naturally build skills in spatial reasoning, number comparison, and expressive language as they race to the treasure chest.

—description from the publisher

Mormath is a dynamic collection of five educational mini-games, designed to help children master numbers from 0 to 20 through play. Using the charming Mormels family and included number cards, the games cover a wide range of early math skills. Children can make up and listen to quirky number stories, compete in a back-and-forth dice race for 'gold nuggets', or test their strategy in 'Bull's eye', a Nim-like game of removal. Other activities include a game of calculated risk with card exchanges, and a number-based version of Blackjack. This collection seamlessly blends entertainment with education, covering cardinal, ordinal, and arithmetic number concepts that prepare children for school in an enjoyable way.

—description from the publisher

The Peppers monster family has lost track of time playing on the beach, and now they must rush home before silly flying fish take over all the rooms in their magical house! Flying Fish is a cooperative and imaginative game where players must work together to guide the Peppers home safely. On your turn, look at the Pepper card in your shell and describe it vividly—by its colors, patterns, and hobbies—or by miming its actions, without ever saying its multiplication-based name. The other players must work together to guess its full name, like "9|7|63." A correct guess sends the Pepper safely into the house, but a wrong one lets a flying fish slip inside. The group shares a victory if all the Peppers are home before the house fills with fish, making this an exciting and physical way to practice multiplication tables.

—description from the publisher

The Peppers monster family has gotten themselves completely mixed up, and players must restore order in their magical, 100-room house. Matogo offers two clever card games in one box. In "Full House," players must play cards from their hands strategically to fill the house floor by floor and corridor by corridor, scoring points for completing rows. In the "Guessing game for Peppers pros," players take turns showing the picture side of a Pepper card to others, challenging them to quickly shout its correct name—a three-part code that represents a multiplication fact, such as "3|4|12." Both games transform learning multiplication tables into an exciting visual experience, helping children build confidence with numbers and patterns in a fun, competitive setting.

—description from the publisher

Find and collect sets of matching petroglyphs in a four by four tableau of face down cards.

The four different colours make up a set in different ways. Red and yellow sets are two cards. A blue set is three cards. A green set is anywhere between one and six cards.

If you turn over cards of different colours, you may shuffle a row or column before the turn moves to the next player.

17 cards. Each game one card is put aside without players knowing which card it is.

—description from the designer

Mnemory transforms the challenge of learning multiplication tables into a clever visual memory game set in the Peppers' magical house. Inspired by ancient Greek mnemonic techniques, the game links every multiplication fact to a unique, colorful monster whose name is the calculation's code (for example, 3x4=12 is Pepper "3|4|12"). In the game, players take turns revealing Pepper cards to find and match them to their corresponding spots on the multiplication table. With two difficulty levels—from matching a pair to a single revealed card—the game grows with the child. As they play, children naturally build strong visual and spatial associations that help the times tables stick, turning abstract math into an enjoyable and memorable adventure.

At its core, Spell Clash is a fast-paced tournament game built on the timeless foundation of rock-paper-scissors, elevated through magic, strategy, and deck building.

Players step into the role of dueling spellcasters, competing in a structured tournament where players draft cards after dueling another opponent. After three duels and 4 drafts players compete in an elimination tournament to determine the winner of Spell Clash!

—description from the designer

Avast, ye scurvy dog! You're treading where you don't belong; the bowels of a dread pirate vessel. Your very life is at stake, unless you can gather your wits to blend in with the crew, find allies among these unsavory fellows, and find refuge on dry land. 'Tis a puzzling endeavor, ye can be sure!

The Mutiny Mystery is part of the 18Escape line of games, each of which pack a full escape room game experience into a tiny package! As a group, you will solve a series of puzzles to unravel a mystery.

Puzzles may test your perception, wordplay, and lateral thinking. English language fluency is required.

When you have solved a puzzle on the cards, you will enter the answer into the companion website (compatible with all smart phones and computers) and it will check whether you are correct. If you are, the website will tell you which card or cards to take next. The website also contains a graduated hint system, so if you get stuck you can get just a small hint about the current puzzle to get moving again without giving too much away.

This system is non-destructible: some groups may choose to write on cards, but this is not required. A group cannot re-play the game, but can pass it on to another group for their use.

This entry in the series takes about an hour to solve, and is rated 3 out of 3 in difficulty.

—description from the publisher

You’ve met someone cute — and now your brain is scrambled. Pride festivities are in full swing, the streets are packed, and the clock is ticking for you to collect what you need for your first Pride celebration. Solve clues, gather decorations, and piece together the perfect fit before time runs out. Can you escape the chaos and kick off Pride Month with a bang?

The Pride Pursuit is part of the 18Escape line of games, each of which pack a full escape room game experience into a tiny package! As a group, you will solve a series of puzzles to unravel a mystery.

Puzzles may test your perception, wordplay, and lateral thinking. English language fluency is required.

When you have solved a puzzle on the cards, you will enter the answer into the companion website (compatible with all smart phones and computers) and it will check whether you are correct. If you are, the website will tell you which card or cards to take next. The website also contains a graduated hint system, so if you get stuck you can get just a small hint about the current puzzle to get moving again without giving too much away.

This system is non-destructible: some groups may choose to write on cards, but this is not required. A group cannot re-play the game, but can pass it on to another group for their use.

This entry in the series takes about an hour to solve, and is rated 1 out of 3 in difficulty.

—description from the publisher

Dark Stars: Unseen Universe is a thrilling strategy board game where players lead unique civilizations in a galaxy filled with hidden dangers and scarce resources.

In a galaxy teeming with hidden dangers and scarce resources, you must lead your unique civilization to dominance. Balance your aggression with caution, form alliances, and manage your resources wisely to outlast your rivals.

With dynamic gameplay, unpredictable events, and the thrill of potential betrayal, every game promises a new possibility.

Will you conquer the unseen universe and ensure your civilization’s survival?

—description from the publisher

A logic puzzle who's aim is for you to defuse a bomb and complete your mission.  It's an "escape game in a box" style game.

This game is designed for both solo play and small teams. Experienced puzzlers typically solve it in 60–90 minutes, while those newer to puzzles may take 2–3 hours to unravel all the clues.

Perfect for groups, the puzzles are printed on individual cards so you can divide and conquer—work together on different clues and combine your findings to reveal the final solution.

—description from the publisher

A case file style mystery solving game that is set in the shadowy world in the aftermath of WW2, where a member of the monuments men team has been reported as Missing in Action.

When a body is discovered during home renovations, it's up to you as the detective to solve the cold case.  You are presented with numerous documents and memorabilia to help solve the case and decide between five possible suspects, while exploring the history of that period.

Game play takes between 2-4 hours, it's designed as totally "off line content" though there are online hints for those who want them.

—description from the publisher

War & Wagers is a competitive 2-6 player card game where players bet chips to represent their life in the game. Players use our customised deck of cards, with each card representing a particular unit. The unit's strength and a unique action are shown on each card, tailored to affect specific suits.

The game features strategic betting and tactical gameplay, as players must carefully manage their actions and units to outlast their opponents.

—description from the publisher

Vampire Tower is a free ruleset for 52 standard card deck.

In this adventure game with RPG progression elements players gotta manage very limited resources inside a tower infested with vampires.

Players can choose from 4 different characters:

     The Hunter
     The Seer
     The Priest
     The Alchemist


Each turn a player will draw a card from the top of the tower deck.

If the card drawn is a number card it is an item that can be collected. Otherwise if the card is a royalty card, all players must prepare to battle against the vampire!

The game ends when either all players die or all vampire lords are defeated.

boardgame: The Last Shelter: Whispers of the Mist
description:
The Last Shelter: Whispers of the Mist is an atmospheric, high-stakes survival game for 1-5 players, set on the fog-shrouded coast of Mazandaran. Following a mysterious climate shift, a deadly mist has engulfed the land, hiding monstrous threats within its depths. In this world, survival follows a brutal logic: Fog means monsters, and Fire means safety.

The game seamlessly blends a tense "I Cut, You Choose" exploration phase with deep Worker Placement and Tableau Building mechanics. Each round begins with a psychological battle during the Explore Phase, where players must divide discovered resources into groups, knowing their rivals will pick first.

Once resources are gathered, players must strategically deploy their survivors across a modular grid to perform vital actions: constructing, hunting, or gathering water. You can bravely face the monsters using dice and tactical Trap Tiles, or spend precious oil to light a Fire Torch—a unique mechanic that allows you to flip action tiles to clear the fog, performing actions safely without triggering a battle.

As you progress, you will build your ultimate refuge through Engine Building; placing Blue Upgrade cards on your player board grants permanent bonuses, from combat advantages to resource efficiency. Every player controls one of 5 highly asymmetric characters—like the Well-Digger or the Hunter—each with unique storage limits and specialized skills that dictate their survival strategy.

The race for survival ends when a player completes the second level of their shelter, but only the one with the most Victory Points will be crowned the ultimate survivor. Can you keep the fires burning, outsmart your rivals, and survive the whispers of the mist?

—description from the designer

Bee Friendly is a 2–6 player tile-laying game where players build their own bee-friendly garden by drafting pairs of flower tiles—one visible and one hidden.
Score immediately by matching colours and flower types with adjacent tiles.
Easy to learn in seconds, plays in around 15 minutes, and designed for families and casual gamers.

—description from the designer

Family Football Board Game is a quick play, drive-by-drive American Football game that's so simple to play, grandparents can play with their grandchildren.

Included with the game are 20 all time great teams from the past. The ratings, scoring abilities, and defensive strengths are based on actual historical data. Other card sets are available as a separate purchase, including modern and historic season sets, as well as great team compilation sets.

Each player rolls dice to determine the outcome of each drive, using the ratings of each offensive unit, compared to the strength of the opposing defensive unit. Kickoff and punt returns for touchdowns are also possible, as are fumbles, interceptions, and safeties. Other rare plays include fumble and interception returns for touchdowns.

As a comical option, there is a rule set to include REFBALL! as part of the game. REFBALL! allows you to choose a team that the league and referees "favor," to allow for random, conspiracy driven, fixed outcomes on a drive. Just when a team thinks their drive outcome is set, the referees change the outcome ... sometimes, in a teams favor ... sometimes ... costing a team a scoring opportunity.

—description from the designer

According to tradition, the Senate was established by Romulus, the founder of Rome. It was made up of 100 members chosen from among the patricians and was structured according to the tribal order typical of the Indo-European populations of that historical period. These early communities often included in their tribal councils the “elders,” men of some experience and wisdom.

The first Roman families were called gens (“clans”). Each of them was made up of an aggregation of families under a common patriarch, called pater (from the Latin “father”), who was the undisputed head of the gens.

When the original gentes aggregated into a community, the patres were selected from among the founders of the various families to form a federal council, which then took the name of the Senate. Thus, the patres understood that it was now necessary to have a single man to guide them. For these reasons they elected a king (rex), and invested him with sovereign powers.

In Pollex you are one of the would-be kings, and by playing your cards right, you can collect the indispensable consent of the plebeians and patricians, thus proving that you are, of all the candidates, the most astute strategist and the only one worthy of becoming the next king of Rome.

—description from the publisher

In Lurid Cove, 3 to 5 sophisticated adults take on the roles of married couples living in a suburban cul-de-sac in the 1960s, desperately striving to have the highest social standing by the end of the game. You’ll send your characters to a variety of locations to gossip, seduce, blackmail, throw parties, dig thru your neighbor’s trash,  and more – all while acquiring and revealing increasingly unsavory secrets about your rivals.
   
But be careful who you dish the dirt about, because the secrets you reveal will bring you closer to victory, but they’ll also give your rivals unique powers that bend and break the rules of the game!
    
—description from the publisher

Outraged, the king adjusted his crown and looked out from his balcony: over villages, hills, and the enchanted forest to Thunder Mountain. There, in the distance, lay the source of all evil! There lived the monster that sent out the Beastlings to enslave the land.

But now he'd had enough. The king stood up straight, looking determinedly ahead. Now he would take his royal sword, his royal armor, his royal ... At that moment, there was a loud roar and hiss from Thunder Mountain, and the king quickly dropped on the floor.

When it grew quiet again, he picked up his crown and cautiously peered over the edge of the balcony's railing. 
Heroes. He would send the heroes. They would take care of it.

Beastlings is a game in which you are sent by the king to defeat Beastlings and finally face the great monster at Thunder Mountain. You travel through hedge mazes, dark hills, and catacombs ... and in the end, the player who has accumulated the most gold on their journey wins.

The mechanism is simple: when it's your turn, you roll the hero dice three times and then face the Beastlings that are currently in front of you. With skillful dice distribution, you can defeat as many of them as possible, receive gold in return, and move steps closer to Thunder Mountain.

But take a close look to see if it might be better to lure other Beastlings. Maybe you need a bait or should ask the King's Guard for help (and thus be allowed to take the King's Guard die with you). And you have to take care to prepare for the big battle against the monster at Thunder Mountain.

Beastlings is a fun family game for up to 5 players, where luck with the dice and the right strategy for fighting the Beastlings are key.

—description from the publisher

In a short few years of unrelenting carnage following the descent into world war, the Russian and Austro-Hungarian empires collapsed into ruin and passed from the march of time. Plan 19 was the name of the Russian operational war plan and, from its inception in 1910, evolved into a strategy that demanded both offesnsive action against Germany and Austria-Hungary.

This 12 page booklet contains rules and Orders of Battle for Russian, Austro-Hungarian and Serbian armies in 1914, plus a deck of 9 cards to be used with the core card deck in the 1914 main rulebook.

The card deck feature two cards each for Russian, Serbian, Austro-Hungarian and German (Eastern Front) forces, plus a blank to create your own.

—description from the publisher

Step into the role of an Explorer of the Invisible:
Journey across seven iconic worlds—from realms where Spooky reigns as Princess or Witch (the universe of Carine-M) to the epic lands of Dragons (the universe of Élian). Along the way, you will encounter Ghosts, Spirits, Monsters, Chimeras, and the Bewitched Van...


     Your mission?


Summon the six fundamental Augurs to unlock each world before your opponents do. But beware the forces of the Oculus Malignus, which could, at any moment, alter the course of your destiny.

Gameplay

     Setup: Deal 6 cards (for 2 to 5 players) or 5 cards (for 6 or more players). The remaining cards form the Oracle's Reserve. 
     The Request: Ask an opponent for one of their "Auguries" (you must hold at least one card from that specific World to make the request). A World is considered complete once you have collected all 6 of its Key Cards. 
         Success: The opponent hands you the card, and you take another turn. 
         Failure: The opponent replies, "Knock on the table 3 times and draw a card from the Reserve." 
    
     The Vision: If you draw the specific card you requested, announce, "Vision Fulfilled!" and continue playing. Otherwise, your turn ends. 
     Interferences of the Oculus Malignus (Optional "Spell" Booster): Play these forces during your turn. Once the effect has resolved, immediately discard the card.


Contents:
