Designed to augment the base Zombicide experience rather than stand alone, Squid Game Expansion introduces fresh mechanics and narrative flavor while relying on the cooperative zombie-survival core that defines Zombicide.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Comic Book Boom!: Comic Drake – Your Guide to Comics
description:
A promotional card made in collaboration with the Comic Drake YouTube channel.

During the cleanup phase, take a research token for each amount of readership the book had.

Ноль семь ("Zero Seven") is a psychological quiz game where you need to answer correctly the questions that mostly begin with the words «How many...». Any answer must be a whole number from 0 to 7, and the players bid for the number they consider correct with a combination of their number cards (-1,0,1,2,3,4) so that their sum equals the estimated answer. The players can’t use bidding cards they’ve placed as bets before, so they have to plan carefully which cards they should keep and which ones they are ready to sacrifice to get the full hand back. There is also an “Elementary!” rule which allows to profit from all other players answering correctly. Most of the times the winner is not the one who reads a lot of books, but the one who is the most cunning!

The new edition of the game is designed for up to 7 players and also contains new modes, including cooperative, and some new questions.

Словечки ("Wordlets") is a funny, fast-paced game where players put together pictures and guess what they are. Each card features 3 short words. Using the game components (discs, sticks, and strings), the player creates a picture that represents one of the words on their card. The other players then guess which word the picture is meant to be.

Place the sticks, discs, and strings in the center so everyone can reach them, put the scoring track nearby, and give each player a card from the shuffled deck. The game has two stages: first, all players simultaneously build a picture of one word from their card using the components; then, each player shows their picture and card while the others guess which word was illustrated. Every correct guess earns the guesser 1 point, while the picture's creator earns 2 points. If no one guesses correctly, the creator gets 0 points.

The game ends as soon as any player reaches 14 points – that player is the winner. If two or more players reach 14 points in the same round, they share the victory.

—description from the publisher (translated)

boardgame: The Great Commission: Roman Oppression Card Game
description:
The Great Commission: Roman Oppression is a stand-alone sequel and expansion to The Great Commission Strategic Card Game, with two exciting developments: A new competitive mode and more cards, mechanics and game modes for the original co-op/solo game!

Set in the first century AD, the early church is established but still struggles with internal sin, conflict and spiritual issues. The Roman State, exerting severe political, economic, and religious oppression, is determined to honour Caesar.

The bold contemporary artwork is drawn from the New Testament books of the Word for Word Bible Comic.

—description from the designer

Welcome to the rooftops of Mars.

Here, your future is paid for in time credits and victories. 
In ROOFER, every match is more than just a contest; it’s a gamble against the void, a challenge to gravity… and to your friends. Roofer is a sports game set in the universe of our skirmish game Mars: Code Aurora. Rooftag is a sport growing in popularity amongst the colonists of the Arches.

Each team aims to win the match against the opposing team. 
There are two ways to be declared the winner of such a competition. 
If a team manages to score three points by loading the data into its Kube before transferring it to the opposing Download Terminal, it wins the match. Alternatively, if a team pushes all the opposing Roofers off the Spot—the area where Rooftag takes place—it is declared the winner.

A game of Rooftag pits two teams of Roofers against each other on a 4x8-square playing area called the Spot. 
Each team consists of 1 to 3 Roofers. Only one Roofer from each team is present on the Spot at any one time, with the Roofers facing off in duels.

At the beginning of each turn, players secretly program their athletes actions, and then alternatively resolve them.

Prepare your teams. Perfect your strategies. Run fast, strike hard or fall with style.

ROOFER: Only the void awaits you below.

Roofer offers a range of miniatures created by Rêvves Création and Trashfire Studio. Other creators will join the adventure to offer their own vision of the Roofers.
It is, of course, possible to use other miniatures from sci-fi ranges to play with your own miniatures.

—description from the publisher

A whisper on dying lips. A badly scratched map. Everyone received a tip to join the hunt for the abandoned gold convoy. But are they ready to face what caused the disappearance of so many souls? The desert shows no mercy to the foolish or the greedy…

Forgotten West is a miniature-agnostic wargame where you can use any miniatures you like to depict the contents of the book.  Players alternate by activating one model at a time, performing actions like shooting, looting, or jumping on a horse for a quick escape. You won’t only have to worry about your opponent, as enemy hostiles follow a scripted algorithm designed to disrupt your plans.

Up to two players can join forces to survive the unforgiving desert, or compete against each other to claim the gold said to lie deep beneath its dunes. Create a band of five outlaws and play through a ten-scenario campaign in search of a lost convoy. Watch your gang ride through the desert as it amasses riches, wounds, and experience by surviving violent shootouts, growing better at what they do, until the bitter end.

If a full campaign is too much, the game also offers the option to randomly generate self-contained scenarios that can be played in a quick, single session.

—description from the designer

Snowball! is a family friendly, fast paced deckbuilder for 2–5 players. It’s colourful, cartoony and built for quick, joyful rounds — about 3 minutes each — so it’s perfect for families, game nights and newcomers.

If you like fast paced deckbuilding games that are easy to play and hard to put down
You will love this!

❄Win by emptying your hand completely – the first player to play every card in their deck Snowballs to Victory!

Its fun because everyone plays at once, so there’s no long waiting around. A random market of cards ensures no two games are ever the same. 
There's even plenty of opportunities to mess with your opponents and shift the whole game dynamic. All leading to multiple viable strategies, meaningful choices every turn, and satisfying combos. 
It's a game everyone will want to play again and again!

—description from the designer

The Nexus is an unstable dimension, where the ever-shifting landscape forms delicate bridges between the portals leading to countless worlds. When a portal opens into the Nexus, a guardian is chosen to protect it. The Keeper then bears the heavy burden of defending their kingdom from otherworldly invasions.

Nexus Keepers is a modular arena game: Each player can join the fray with a Keeper of their choice. The Nexus Cubes—which previously housed the miniatures—are then unfolded to form a small battlefield.

The game features a collection of characters, each more outlandish than the last: imps, ice dwarves, chaos monkeys... choose your champion and discover their unique abilities!

The objective is simple: be the last one standing on the board! To achieve this, you’ll have to throw your weight around and push your opponents through a portal.

Age: Nexus Keepers is a highly accessible game. It is generally recommended for ages 7 and up.

Players: We recommend games of 2 to 6 players for an enjoyable gaming experience. However, Nexus Keepers can accommodate as many challengers as you wish. If your goal is to break the world record for the largest game, go right ahead!

Duration: Play round after round with lightning-fast matches! Allow 10 minutes for 2–3 players, and add 5 minutes for each additional player.

Scale: The Nexus Keepers range is designed with both collectors and painters in mind. The Keepers generally stand around 60mm tall.

May the most cunning player win!

Unique Packaging

The box housing the Keepers lies at the very heart of the entire project. 
Indeed! Even before writing a single rule, I knew I wanted to produce miniatures housed in a box that unfolded in this specific way. I felt it was such a shame to produce beautiful packaging—sometimes just as costly as the contents themselves!—only to have it inevitably end up in the trash; thus, the idea of ​​utilizing the packaging *as* the game board became the spark that ignited the flame of the Nexus Keepers project.

Each Keeper comes packaged inside its own Nexus Cube. This cube serves multiple purposes: transporting the miniature (which is magnetized to its starting square), displaying it in your home, and—of course—facing off against your opponents.

With Nexus Keepers, nothing goes to waste—everything transforms!

Arrrgh! Or not? is a competitive pirate card game set on the High Sea of Taradiddle. Players captain their ships through unpredictable voyages, gaining and losing Reputation as they encounter treasure, trouble, curses, monsters, ghosts, magic, and other hazards of the High Sea.

On each turn, players make choices that affect their voyage, their ship, and their standing among the other captains. Voyage cards create changing situations, while upgrades and other card effects alter how each ship handles the dangers ahead. Reputation rises and falls as players take risks, recover, improve their position, or suffer the consequences of bad luck and worse decisions.

Each game creates a different voyage through the combination of cards, ship development, and player choices. The player with the most Reputation at the end of the game wins.

—description from the designer

boardgame: ライデン1637 -チューリップマニアの終焉- (Leiden 1637: The End of Tulip Mania)
description:
Leiden 1637: The End of Tulip Mania (ライデン1637 -チューリップマニアの終焉-) is a speculative trading game in which players take on the role of tulip merchants.

Players exchange bulbs in the market and plant them in their fields, aiming to become the wealthiest merchant.

Each card consists of one color and one type of icon, representing a specific tulip variety.

On each turn, players perform the following actions:

Exchange cards from their hand with cards in the market
Place the exchanged cards into their field
Refill their hand

When placing a tulip card, it must be placed adjacent to an existing card.

In addition, players may create up to two rows at a time. When a row is completed, that player immediately takes an extra turn.

The game ends when a player completes a 4×3 field in a 4-player game, or a 4×4 field in a 2–3 player game.

At the end of the game, players build windmills and collectively determine the value of each color.

Final scoring is based on the following three categories:

The colors that make up each completed row
Majority bonuses for the five different icon types
The value of each color multiplied by the number of matching cards planted in your field

The player with the most victory points wins.

—description from the designer
publishers: シュピールディスオーダー (Spieldisorder)

description:
Leiden: The Dice Game (ライデン -チューリップ栽培の隆盛-) is a speculative game in which players take on the role of tulip merchants.

Players select bulb dice and choose whether to plant them in their fields or seek the help of artisans, all while aiming to become the wealthiest merchant. The dice determine both the color used in the field and the row where players may write.

Each turn, players are divided into an active player and the other players.

The active player rolls five dice and applies the results to the market.

Then, the active player chooses two of those dice to either color spaces in their field or mark progress with artisans.

The other players each choose one die from the remaining three dice to either color spaces in their fields or mark progress with artisans.

After that, the role of active player passes to the next player. Once every player has been the active player, the round ends.

At the end of each round, players record which color they want to affect the market at the end of the game.

The game ends after 4 rounds in a 2- or 4-player game, or 5 rounds in a 3-player game.

Finally, all recorded colors are applied to determine the final value of each color.

At the end of the game, players score points based on the following four categories:

Progress on the artisan track
The number of different colors surrounding the windmill
The value of each color multiplied by the number of spaces filled in that color
The largest group of orthogonally adjacent spaces of the same color

The player with the most victory points wins.

—description from the designer
publishers: シュピールディスオーダー (Spieldisorder)

description:
The War of the Gods has ended. The creators of wonders and destruction have departed beyond the stars, leaving behind only ruins, extinct peoples, and a fading aura of magic.
But even a fading world can be ignited again.
You play as one of the two Great Forces — Light or Darkness. Enter the struggle for control over the World, restore lost technologies and bring the miracles of the past back to life.

Aeternal Champions is a strategic dueling game based on mechanics of area control, tableau building, and chaotic drafting. Though chance is unpredictable, only your will can determine the fate of the World: to guide it toward the Light or plunge it into eternal Darkness.

—description from the designer

boardgame: ライデン1637 -チューリップマニアの終焉- ミニ拡張：チャート-(Leiden 1637: The End of Tulip Mania -mini expansion : chart-)
description:
Leiden 1637: The End of Tulip Mania – Mini Expansion: Chart can be played by using the components included in ライデン-チューリップ栽培の隆盛- (Leiden: The Dice Game) , or by printing the required materials and preparing five cubes.

In the base game, values were determined only at the very end of the game. This often made it difficult to plan ahead, leading to more reactive, spur-of-the-moment decisions.
In this mini expansion, however, value fluctuations are visualized during the game, allowing players to anticipate market trends to some extent.

—description from the designer
publishers: シュピールディスオーダー (Spieldisorder)

description:
Command ancient golems in rapid deck-building combat. Build up your arsenal of magical gems, power up your army, then rain down rocky mayhem upon your opponents to crush their heart gems. A game of careful planning, quick thinking, and hopefully no ruined friendships!

Last person standing escapes the ruins with their life… and the shiny loot!

—description from the publisher

Split Court is a two-player card game of constrained drafting and split-hand evaluation set using a standard playing card deck. Players compete to assemble the poker hands, using partial information and structured offers to influence both their own hand and their opponent’s options.

Each round begins with both players drafting cards through a sequence of four distinct actions, similar in structure to Hanamikoji. On each turn, a player draws a card, then selects one unused action: reserving a card face down, discarding two cards, offering three cards for the opponent to choose one, or dividing four cards into two sets for the opponent to select from. Each action may be used only once per round, with card trackers marking which options remain available. These decisions create a mix of hidden information, forced choices, and indirect control over the opponent’s hand.

After drafting, each player has eight cards and must divide them into a five-card hand and a three-card hand. The five-card hands are evaluated using standard poker rankings, while the three-card hands follow a condensed hierarchy adapted to the reduced deck. A Joker acts as a wild card, increasing flexibility when forming straights, flushes, or straight flushes.

Success depends on reading the opponent’s intentions, managing imperfect information, and allocating cards effectively between the two hands.

—description from the designer

Mindbug: Dinopunk Ice is a stand-alone game of Mindbug but can be played on its own, or mixed with any other Mindbug sets.

The acclaimed card battler co-designed by Richard Garfield returns with a revolutionary new game mode. All the depth and identity of deckbuilding reimagined into the fastest, most intense dueling experience ever created. Where every match is a new build.

In Mindbug, you summon hybrid creatures and send them to battle against your opponent. But be careful: your opponent may use one of their Mindbugs to take control of it. Outwit your opponent in a fascinating tactical duel in which having the best cards and playing them at the wrong time can be deadly for yourself.

Mindbug combines the accessibility and fairness of a simple card game with the strategic depth of a complex Strategy Card Game. The result is a skill-based dueling card game that feels utterly different from all the other card games you have played before.

—description from the publisher

Mindbug: Dinopunk Fire is a stand-alone game of Mindbug but can be played on its own, or mixed with any other Mindbug sets.

The acclaimed card battler co-designed by Richard Garfield returns with a revolutionary new game mode. All the depth and identity of deckbuilding reimagined into the fastest, most intense dueling experience ever created. Where every match is a new build.

In Mindbug, you summon hybrid creatures and send them to battle against your opponent. But be careful: your opponent may use one of their Mindbugs to take control of it. Outwit your opponent in a fascinating tactical duel in which having the best cards and playing them at the wrong time can be deadly for yourself.

Mindbug combines the accessibility and fairness of a simple card game with the strategic depth of a complex Strategy Card Game. The result is a skill-based dueling card game that feels utterly different from all the other card games you have played before.

—description from the publisher

Wisp & Whiskers is a 2–4 player abstract strategy game where glowing wisps lure curious cats through a moonlit forest.

Cats can’t resist chasing mysterious lights, especially the whimsical wisps drifting between the trees. Each turn, players place Wisp tokens throughout the forest, carefully positioning them to attract cats and set up scoring opportunities.

Every game features a unique scoring condition determined by a random card, ensuring no two playthroughs feel the same.

The game ends when a player can no longer place a Wisp or runs out of legal placements for a Wisp. At that point, players tally their points to see who has best guided the cats to their advantage.

It’s a strategic tug of war, as players compete to position cats in just the right places, maximizing their scoring based on how their Wisps are placed around cats when the game ends.

—description from the publisher

Zodianimo offers an evolving experience with 4 rulesets of increasing complexity (for ages 6, 7, 8, and 10+). At the heart of the game is the challenge of spotting animals based on their species and elements. New mechanisms are added throughout the games, allowing everyone to smoothly master rules that become increasingly intense, rich, and tactical.

OuiSi Nature ("we-see") is a set of 210 Visually Connecting Photo Cards that celebrate the beauty of our planet. Each box includes a guide full of creative games and activities that ignite curiosity about nature.

Each Photo Card "connects visually" with many others in the set, based on similar patterns, shapes and colors. Thousands of matches are awaiting discovery.

Rodízio is a tactical game about Brazilians' favorite way to eat pizza: watching them go by on the tables and knowing which one to eat! Inspired by the classic mancala game, you'll distribute the pizzas on the table to decide what goes on your plate and what will be available to your opponents.

In your turn, choose a column of cards to seed through the columns. The last card will determine what pizza to eat and add to your hand. Your goal is to cause losses by eating too much, but be careful! Whoever finishes the game with the highest score gets sick, and victory goes to the second-highest scorer.

Will you be able to balance your gluttony, or will your eyes grow bigger than your stomach?

—description from the designer

In the Brazilian Atlantic Forest, golden lion tamarins (micos-leões-dourados) are threatened with extinction — and you must protect them!

Mico-Leão is a dueling (or solo) strategy game inspired by the different species of this monkey from the Atlantic Forest. In each round, you will choose which card to bring to your sanctuary and what function it will serve: as food for the animals or as golden lion tamarins that, if properly fed, will bring victory points!

In your turn, you can choose between the next 3 cards on the rondel to put in your sanctuary. This also determines what options your opponent will have on their turn — thinking a few turns ahead is crucial to securing a card you need and complicating your opponent's situation.

—description from the designer

Estalinho is a pocket-sized dexterity game with artwork made of colorful paper, inspired by the Festa Junina (June Festivals) that take place throughout Brazil. Your goal is to launch your estalinho (popper) cards using your thumb and forefinger, trying to hit the bonfire card in the center of the table.

Hitting the bonfire gives you immediate points, but points are also awarded to whoever gets closest to it in each round. But watch out for your opponents: if someone manages to cover your card, it's worthless to you!

—description from the designer

This box contains the Deluxe versions of both Hotshots and the Inferno expansion. Featuring the same tense gameplay found in the retail version, this set includes upgraded, deluxe components. Fight the fire using plastic minis for all 8 Firefighters and 5 vehicles, all wooden tokens, wood firebreaks, a velvet bag, magnetic frames to hold the tiles, and flame pieces that light up.

Spore-adic! is a colorful, competitive token placement game for 2-6 players where each player attempts to score the most points by taking over as much of the Garden (game board) as possible.

Each turn you'll draw a card and can play up to two cards, targeting a player once per turn (including yourself). Some cards have a cost which is paid for from your token supply. There are three types of cards:

     Blue Cards: can be played on yourself
     Red Cards: can be played on others
     Black Cards: can be played on anyone


After playing cards, it's time to grow! You get 4 Growth Points to spend every turn, allowing you to grow (place on the board) mushroom and colony tokens. Some cards may add or remove your available growth points for that turn. During the growth phase, players can create rings of 1-3 empty spaces on the board called "Fairy Rings." This allows them to draw an additional card, and the empty spaces in a ring at the end of the game are will be worth additional points.

If you have more than 3 cards in your hand at the end of your turn you discard down to 3. If any of your mushroom chains get disconnected, there's a wilting mechanic that determines when tokens are removed from the board.

Players score 1 point per mushroom token on the board, 2 points per Fairy Ring space, and 3 points per colony.

The game ends if:

     Any player runs out of mushroom tokens
     Any player cannot place a token in two consecutive turns
     All players cannot place a token in consecutive turns


The player with the most points at the end of the game wins!

—description from the designer

Compete pushing your luck by being the first player completing the cockroach by throwing dice, the number 1 is the key to achieve the victory before your opponents.

Each player has 1 board to complete with each part of the cockroach, legs can be completed by throwing the number 1, the head can be completed with a pair of number 1 and the tail with 3 die of the number 1 (rolling at once)

Each turn the players will be throwing 5 dice, trying to accomplish the parts of the cockroach, they may throw again only if they obtain at least a number 1.

boardgame: Paolo and Francesca: Secrets in Gradara
description:
In the XIII Century Italy, 3–6 players lead noble families competing to control Gradara. Each round, players play 3 Intrigue cards (Craft, Faction, Area), face down, then reveal one, and those who control them, use the Protagonists - Paolo, Francesca or Gianciotto - to block Characters. Combining the Intrigue cards, players control or bribe from another family Characters based on their Craft, Faction and Area. Controlling Characters scores point. Events are revealed and they move Protagonists on their path and assign points. The game ends when the Characters goal is met by at least one player or the movements of the Protagonists lead Gianciotto to overtake the two lovers. Families' secret goals are revealed and additional points are scored. The highest score wins.

—description from the designer

Each player takes one of the large concession cards and places it, face up, in front of him. The 36 prizes are placed, face up, on the table, so all can see them. Each player chooses a pawn and places it at "Coney Island Start".
One player is selected Cashier and gives each player 15 dimes. 
The players to visit the various concessions by use of "Rides" and "Paths" in order to win prizes to fill their large concession card. Whenever a player lands on a "Ride" by throw of die, he must immediately pay to the Cashier the amount marked on that ride. A player does not need an exact count on the die to land on to the "Ride". On his next turn, player then decides which prizes he needs to fill his large concession card and notes where these prizes are on the Concession Stands. He then throws the die, moves the "Ride" with his pawn on it (clockwise) the number of spaces shown on the die, until he reaches the path leading to the prize he wants to win. If he throws a higher count than necessary, he moves his pawn the remaining amount along the path.

On his next turn, after reaching the concession stand of his choice, he may play number 1, 2 or 3 singly, a combination of two of them, or all three, but has to pay for each number 10c or 20c as indicated - by placing his coin or coins on that number or numbers. The Player then spins the prize spinner and wins or loses according to where the arrow rests. On the next turn, player may play again at the same concession stand or move along the paths to another "Ride" in order to play for prizes.

Whenever a player wins a prize, he places it on his large concession card over the corresponding picture. If a player has used up all his coins, before filling up his large concession card, he returns at once (without throwing the die) to "START". He then receives from the Cashier 15 more coins, but misses a turn. On his next turn he starts out again. The first player to fill his concession card with corresponding prize cards is the winner.

Tier List: O Jogo is a social deduction party game where players try to predict how others think.

Each round, one player takes on the role of the judge. They receive seven cards with random words or phrases, representing things they may like, dislike, or feel neutral about. In secret, the judge ranks these cards into a tier list, placing each one from S to F, and records their chosen order using a numbered answer sheet.

The other players only see the cards and must try to recreate the judge’s ranking as accurately as possible. The goal is not to find a correct answer, but to understand and predict the judge’s preferences.

At the end of the round, players score points based on how many positions they guessed correctly, according to the official scoring rules.

Each player becomes the judge twice during the game, without taking consecutive turns. The player with the highest total score at the end wins.

With a focus on social deduction, player insight, and group dynamics, Tier List: O Jogo delivers a light, highly interactive, and replayable experience, ideal for groups who enjoy discussion, humor, and reading people.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Fiebre de Papas por la Noche
description:
Fiebre de Papas por la Noche ("Fever of Potatoes at Night") is a new, fast-paced, party-style card game for 2 to 4 players, with games lasting 15 to 30 minutes, ideal for ages 8 and up.

Players must make pairs to create choreographies, and win the "Golden Potatoes."
Influenced by "Fever Night," players compete in a "Boogie Royale" to win the favor of judges like Michael Papson. Mechanics: It's a fast-paced and addictive game where players draw cards and create combinations, often called "Duchess Potatoes."

boardgame: Drużyna do Zadań Specjalnych: promo Głuszec
description:
Additional Character (Głuszec) with his tile and 10 Action cards.

—description from the designer

A dexterity game where all cards are divided between players. On your turn you reveal the top card of your deck and place it on top of the ball of wool. The card will tell you how you must do that:
With your strong hand
With your week hand
Grabbing it between your fingers
Throwing it.

If cards fell you pick them all.

The first player to get rid of all cards win.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

boardgame: Super Giant Monster Showdown 2026 Promo Pack
description:
An exclusive promo set of cards for Super Giant Monster Showdown: Kaiju Attack! This set was only available as a free pack for purchases of any SGMS product throughout 2026. 
The pack incudes 5 unique cards, Manatee, Acidic Blood, Power-Quake, Historical District and Surfing!

boardgame: Super Giant Monster Showdown: Killer Plants mini-expansion
description: A set of plant-themed cards for Super Giant Monster Showdown. The set comes with an exclusive kaiju figure, only available in this set, as well as a new 'Plant Kaiju Bonus' add-on rule.

The only way to really earn your viewers’ trust is by being consistently accurate no matter what Mother Nature throws at you!

Weekly Forecast 6-card expansions introduces achievements. With each play, check to see whether you've completed an achievement; complete all 6 within the week to become a master forecaster!

Deathgrip Dungeon is a solo card game of managing dwindling resources against impossible odds, played entirely in your hand. There's no table, no setup, no sprawl. You take on the role of a lone adventurer descending through a dungeon full of monsters and traps, carefully weighing which gear to use, and when.

Every encounter has a base difficulty you can see, but its true difficulty is shaped by what comes next. To overcome an encounter, you'll commit equipment from your pack. Commit too little, and you'll be crushed. Commit too much, and you've wasted resources that you'll need later. Smart play means anticipating what's ahead, taking calculated risks, and using the right tools at the right moment.

Clear an encounter and you'll be rewarded with valuable loot: enough to keep you going, but never enough to feel safe. The dungeon is full of treasures, but only for those who survive long enough to claim them. Every room is a risk-management puzzle: Spend just enough to win, save just enough to keep going. Clever adventurers will be wary of encounters they haven't yet seen, and empowered by the knowledge of what has already been overcome.

You win by reaching the end of the dungeon and escaping with your life. You lose if your gear runs out before the dungeon does.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: たこやきカードゲーム (Takoyaki Kaadogeemu)
description: Two players compete to be the first to finish their octopus fritter griddles.  Players draw cards from a shared supply and activate each card's power accordingly.  Fritter cards go onto the griddle and trigger a card action, cat cards allow a fritter to be stolen from the other player, the octopus card is wild and triggers a card action where placed, and blank cards end the player's turn.  The first player to complete eight fritters wins.

The ordinarily sleepy hamlet of Jorekburg bustles with activity. Tonight is the Embermoot, a storytelling contest and bonfire held every fourteenth winter solstice. You are one of many hopeful adventurers who have come to town with a knack for spinning tales. The stars are bright, the air is crisp, and the bonfire blazes. Step into the light and recount your daring deeds! Is your story the stuff of legends, or full of plot holes?

Questline is a fast playing, simultaneous play card game for 1-6 players. Compete to earn the most reputation stars (points) by chaining together a sequence of seven cards that tell your hero’s most compelling story, all in about 15 minutes.

Refine your hand swiftly in secret as you search for compatible numbered cards, each depicting events paired with icons and effects that play off of each other. Form these cards into an ascending numerical sequence, optimizing the reputation stars gained while avoiding numerical gaps.

Draw, discard, and swap cards with one another in real time. Discard cards you don’t want face up, where your neighbors can see them. Obtain new cards from the face down decks, from your discards, or from your neighbors discards! Keep an eye out for them to discard the perfect card for your hand.

Once you’re satisfied with your questline, remove your discards from play and claim an epilogue card. The first to finish claim the most valuable epilogues, so don’t take too long. But don’t be too hasty and find yourself with an unmemorable tale full of plot holes, either!

When everyone is finished, players then display their questline, recount their hero’s journey, and tally up. The player with the most stars is proclaimed a legendary storyteller and wins the game! Was your tale an epic victory, or a campfire tale best forgotten?

This game takes place in the World of Ulos setting, first introduced in Roll Player and now home to a series of fantasy games in a shared universe. No experience with other World of Ulos games is required to enjoy Questline. But you may see some familiar faces at the bonfire…

—description from the publisher

Accession is a political strategy game about kingship, betrayal and alliances. Inspired by off-kilter style games with political tension and depth, players are the kingdom’s House correspondents with different types of grand Schemes to pursue in order to ensure dominance. Will you ally with The Heretic's influence or will you ensure that the next King is a Royal Blood? Players will also build their tableau of cards with fiefs, cities and other cards that influence the political tides of the kingdom. Players can win by completing the open Schemes or abdicating their current alliance with a House to pursue a winning Scheme.

—description from the designer

Into the Frozen Void is a solo or cooperative tabletop miniatures game built for fast, tactical play. 
Using a streamlined D6 (six-sided dice) based system, the game emphasizes survival checks through simple success rolls. This keeps gameplay fluid so players can focus on world events or hazards. A major element of gameplay is vertical terrain — height levels play a critical role in movement, visibility and combat, turning every piece of the board into a strategic position.

The Frozen Void is a massive stretch of uncharted territory perched at the northern edge of two rival nations locked in a cold war. With the cold war fueling rapid technological advances and symbolic displays of power, both nations are seeking to demonstrate their unrivaled might to the world and be the first to claim the Frozen Void as a symbol of national pride.

Players take on the roles of the unfortunate individuals stationed at Station 4514. This collection of "expendables" are a mixture of seasoned arctic explorers, failed researchers and other unsavory individuals. Their main goal is to explore the area researching any strange anomalies they come across and laying claim to the uncharted area.

The Quick and Dirty:

     Pulp inspired arctic survival game
     1-4 players
     Average game length: 1 hour
     Easy to make terrain
     Miniature agnostic
     Easy to digest rules
     Print and play version included


—description from the publisher

In Possess Me, Satan, each ghost hunt is guided by your host and moderator, Satan. At the start of the game, one player takes on one of Satan’s powerful host roles, gaining special moderation abilities that can shape the flow of the night. Every other player is dealt a secret role card, becoming an Investigator, Evil Agent, Loner, or Chaotic character. Each with their own unique goals and abilities. Hidden among them, one unlucky soul has already been possessed, secretly becoming the Ghost.

Whenever Satan “turns out the lights,” all players close their eyes as the night phase begins. One by one, Satan calls on players to activate their abilities, if they have one. Investigators gather clues and hunt for the possessed player. Evil Agents work in the shadows to protect the Ghost and mislead the group. Loners pursue their own secret objectives, often helping or hurting either side depending on what benefits them most. Chaotic roles have no true allegiance, living only to stir paranoia, confusion, and madness. And somewhere in the darkness, the Ghost chooses who lives… and who dies.

When the lights come back on, Satan reveals who was killed during the night, if anyone. The surviving players, along with the dead, are free to share information, make accusations, and defend themselves. But in a game of lies, manipulation, and hidden agendas, can anyone truly be trusted? At any point during the day, the living players may call for an exorcism vote. If the majority agrees, the accused player is eliminated from the hunt.

The Investigators win by identifying and exorcising the Ghost before it’s too late. The Ghost and its Evil Agents claim victory if its just the Ghost and one other player, or if only evil is left in the hunt. Loners win by completing their personal objectives, no matter who survives. Chaotics may not have a victory condition at all, but that rarely stops them from turning the table upside down. Use logic, deception, deduction, and your special abilities to survive the night… or become part of the haunting.

—description from the publisher

You are the cat collecting hairs to make the biggest hairball. You can sabotage other cats (players), or try to, but when the Hairball! card comes out it's game over and everyone counts the hairs they have in their stack. The cat (player) with the most hairs in their hairball wins.

You start with a hand of 5 cards. On your turn, you play a card on yourself (place it in front of you to start your Hairball), then play one on an opponent... if you have more than two players it's the opponent of your choice... then you draw one card and play passes to the left.

If you don't have 2 playable cards in your hand at the start of your turn, then you discard what you do have (if applicable) and draw 5 new cards then take your turn as normal.

The game ends when the Hairball! card is drawn, then all hands are discarded and the players count the hairs in their piles. Highest number is the winner (there can be ties). To break a tie, play another round.

Minimum of 2 players, no max if you combine decks. Game play only takes about 5-15 minutes or less, and it's portable.

—description from the designer

Art Museum is an art-themed reinterpretation of the famous Game of Goose for up to 6 players. In the game, each player traverses through an art museum, attempting to be the first to collect their designated art piece along the way and display it in the main gallery.

Each player plays as one of the following historical painter pawns: Pablo Picasso, Salvador Dali, Andy Warhol, Vincent van Gogh, Frida Kahlo, and Banksy. The game is a simple roll and move game where you roll a numbered die and move that many spaces. Landing on a space with painting on it after a roll allows you to move to the next painting space and roll again. If you land on a space that depicts your artist's painting, you collect it and save it for the end of the game. There are various other spaces that have different effects (e.g., a bathroom space that makes players lose 2 turns if they land on it)

Once you reach the end, you need to land on space 60 by exact count, or land on the painting on space 58, which automatically transports you to the main gallery. If you're the first to reach the gallery and have already collected your painting, you automatically win. If you reach the gallery and you don't have your painting yet, you need to roll a yellow artist die until the die shows the face of your artist pawn in order to collect your painting and win.
family: Theme: Fine Art and Art Museums

description:
Draw a card from the draw pile or a card from the top of the discard pile.
     If you don’t want to use the card, discard it, and your turn ends.
     If you decide to use it, this card can be exchanged with ANY facedown board card and does not have to be used to start, continue, or complete a set. It can go anywhere.
     The board card you pick up and reveal MUST be used to start, continue, or complete a set.
     Continue exchanging cards as long as each move starts, continues, or completes a set.
     When you run out of options with your board cards and cannot start, continue, or complete a set, discard and your turn ends.
     Use Specialty cards and Wild cards to be more strategic.  


—description from the designer
CAH type game with Some cow themed cards and black and white spotted cards ala a cow hide.

boardgame: Age of Steam Expansion: Pittsburgh Metro / Denmark
description:
This map set includes Age of Steam: Pittsburgh Metro & Age of Steam: Denmark, two maps that approach railway development from very different angles.

In Pittsburgh Metro (3-5p), players face a dense, evolving urban landscape where new stations emerge over time, reshaping the board and creating fresh opportunities for clever planning. In Denmark (3-6p), players compete as divisions within the postwar Danish State Railways, struggling to modernize an aging fleet while scarce new trains impose limitations how quickly networks can improve. Denmark is nearly a one-for-one remake of JC's web-published version, with updated artwork and additional components to aid in gameplay.

Each map uses admin areas printed directly on the game board in place of the standard Display Boards, and each replaces the standard Goods Growth phase with its own map-specific demand system. Together, the pair offers two distinct challenges: one shaped by urban expansion, shifting development, and difficult terrain, the other by national modernization and limited train availability.

This set includes two maps, each in two distinct parts, printed double-sided, in full color on 11 × 22.55" (279 × 573 mm) 80# silk poster cover stock, as well as full-color rule sheet(s) in your choice of English, Japanese, and/or German. A punchboard with components for each game is included, along with 14 white cubes used specifically for Pittsburgh Metro.

Pittsburgh Metro is designed by Michael Webb.
Denmark is designed by JC Lawrence.

—description from the publisher

Blade Wrath – Fury of Vikings is a historical wargame that is scale agnostic, with rules to use 10 / 15 / 20 / 25 / 28mm miniatures.

Blade Wrath - Fury of Vikings is a mass skirmish game that brings to life the raids and conquests of Scandinavians during the Viking Age. 
To celebrate its release, an exciting range of 15mm Viking miniatures has been introduced to complement the game. 
The game has been developed from the author’s reenactment and historical experience, combining rules that replicate raiding sized battles from the Viking era. 
Players can choose from several unique Viking warbands and fight thrilling skirmishes against their opponents.

Like Fjord Serpents, Blade Wrath has oodles of customisation of leaders, heroes and warriors, and is designed to be played in matched play or campaign modes.

Apoteosis is a deck-building card game for 1 to 4 players, set in the universe of the role-playing game Tierras Quebradas.

Each player takes on the role of a hero who travels through the Tierras Quebradas seeking to attract the attention of the gods. To achieve this, they must accumulate Loyalty Points (LP): claim Quests, defeat Adversaries, forge alliances with Heroes and Allies, recover Artifacts, and win the favor of iconic Locations from the five Factions of the world.

The first player to reach 30 LP achieves their Apotheosis and wins the game. Their name will be remembered for all eternity.

Ring Poker is a 2–3 player card-drafting and hand-building game in which players construct and compare multiple poker hands.

Each round, six cards are turned face up in a ring. Players take turns selecting two adjacent cards, with gaps forming as cards are removed, limiting future options. The starting player rotates each round, and drafting continues until all cards have been taken over nine rounds.

At the end of the draft, each player uses their cards to build three five-card poker hands (Jokers are wild in straights and flushes). These hands are then revealed and compared position by position: best vs. best, second vs. second, third vs. third. A point is awarded for each winning comparison. The player with the highest total score wins, with ties broken by the strength of the lowest-ranked hand.

—description from the designer

Maya the Bee’s friends are collecting flowers in the poppy meadow. They want one of each color. Can you help them? With Maya’s help, you’ll find the right ones.

You win if you are the first to collect five different flowers.

▶ Includes a wooden Maya the Bee
▶ Suitable for free play and puzzling
▶ A first introduction to rule-based play

—description from publisher

Majas Freunde sammeln Blumen auf der Klatschmohnwiese. Sie wollen von jeder Farbe eine haben. Helft ihr ihnen dabei? Mit Majas Hilfe findet ihr die richtigen. Du gewinnst, wenn du zuerst fünf unterschiedliche Blumen findest.

▶ mit der Biene Maja aus Holz
▶ zum freien Puzzeln geeignet
▶ erstes Spielen nach Regeln

—description from publisher (German)

There’s a lot happening in the poppy meadow! Maya the Bee has discovered especially colorful flowers and wants to share them with her friends Willy, Lara,Barry, and Bella. Help Maya fish for the bright blossoms.
The first player to collect five different flowers wins.

▶ Fishing rod with magnetic Maya the Bee
▶ Includes 6 large wooden flowers
▶ Combines free play and a simple rules-based game

—description from publisher

Auf der Klatschmohnwiese ist viel los. Die Biene Maja hat ganz besonders bunte Blüten entdeckt. Diese möchte sie mit ihren Freunden Willi, Lara, Walter und Ulla teilen. Helft Maja und angelt die bunten Blüten.

Wer zuerst fünf verschiedene Blüten hat, gewinnt.

—description from publisher (German)

Maya the Bee is flying over the poppy meadow.
She is hard at work collecting nectar to bring back to the hive. There, Maya’s friends turn it into delicious, sweet honey. Help Maya fill all the honey pots.

Roll the die, fly Maya the Bee to a flower token of the
matching color. Take the token and drop it, flower side facing up, into the top opening of the honey tree.
Look what happens! The token spins around and tumbles out
of the bottom opening with the honey side facing up. Place the honey token on the sorting board. If you roll "Maya the Bee" one flower token goes out of play.

You've won together if you have placed six honey tokens on the sorting board. 
Did you have to remove too many flower tokens from the game? Then you can’t collect enough honey and unfortunately you lose.

▶ Includes a wooden Maya the Bee
▶ 3D beehive with an exciting visual effect
▶ Game materials encourage free play

—description from publisher

Maja fliegt über die Klatschmohnwiese. Wie es sich für eine fleißige Biene gehört, sammelt sie dabei natürlich kräftig Nektar ein, um ihn zum Bienenstock zu bringen. Dort machen Majas Freunde daraus leckeren süßen Honig. Helft Maja, alle Schalen mit Honig zu füllen.

▶ mit der Biene Maja aus Holz
▶ 3D-Bienenstock mit faszinierendem Effekt
▶ Spielmaterial animiert zum freien Spiel

—description from publisher (German)

Micro Cities: Roll & Write packs a thinky puzzle onto a single sheet of paper, proving big strategy comes in small packages. This game features detailed, vivid illustrations of a city across four distinct districts, allowing you to build a masterpiece with just a pencil and a few dice.

As a true roll and write game, it can be played literally anywhere, from a tiny airplane tray to a park bench. You must balance the Industrial engine for resources, the Commercial zone for trade, and the Residential neighborhoods for growth, all while keeping Central Park green for your citizens.

Micro Cities Roll & Write is all about:

Ultimate Portability: Designed to be played literally anywhere, from a cramped airplane tray to a coffee shop table.

Dice Dilemmas: Every single die is valuable, forcing a tough choice each round as you decide which district needs your attention most.

Strategic Depth: Manage your resource economy and invest in synergies to unlock powerful scoring bonuses.

Visual Appeal: The game sheet brings your urban landscape to life with beautiful, intricate artwork.

Whether you are a solo player or competing with friends, Micro Cities delivers a rewarding brain workout on a single page. You don't need a massive box to build a masterpiece. All it takes is one sheet, a few dice, and a plan.

—description from the publisher

In this cozy strategy game of beautiful bookshelves, you are a dragon who has hoarded too many books! Featuring 200 punny book cards, your goal is to organize your messy collection on the shelves of your bookcase board, scoring points for matching colors and genres, following trends, and alphabetizing.

However, tantalizing new books arrive at the bookshop every day, tempting you to enhance your shelves with new releases, knickknacks, and treats from the bookshop cafe. Will you build an efficient, glorious bookcase featuring books old and new, or succumb to the clutter?

—description from the publisher

DETROIT is a two-player racing game where players advance their pieces using triangular cones instead of dice.

As pieces move along the track, players can stack their own pieces to assemble cars. Once assembled, these cars become the key to winning the race.

Collisions add interaction: landing on an opponent’s piece sends it back to the start, creating constant tension between progress and disruption.

The goal is to assemble and deliver three cars to the finish before your opponent.

—description from the publisher
designers: ピーナツデザイン (PEANUTS DESIGN)
artists: ピーナツデザイン (PEANUTS DESIGN)
publishers: ピーナツデザイン (Peanuts Design)

description:
Grab a Pizza That! is a fast-paced, family-friendly card game themed around matching cards to "snatch" pizza slices. The game is designed for 2-6 players, aged 8 and up, with a playtime of approximately 15-30 minutes.
Collect four matching ingredient cards to grab a pizza slice token. The first player to collect six pizza slices wins. Players match cards and can "backstab" others, adding a competitive element to the game.

The Ultimate Friends Trivia Board Game. Put your Friends knowledge to the ultimate test. Play as one of the 6 main characters and race to the Central Perk sofa. Dont let the Curveball Trivia catch you!

To play, one player takes a Trivia card from the top of the pile and reads it aloud to the player to their left. If the answer is correct they move the number of spaces on the board as indicated at the top of the Trivia card. At the end of each turn, whether the question was answered correctly or not, move the Curveball Trivia Standee moves the same number of spaces as on the Trivia card (if it lands on a space occupied by another player they must answer a Curveball question correctly to stay where they are, if not they return to their sofa). Play then moves on to the next player. When moving around the outer circle of the game board, when landing on a tile it will have an image of a cup of coffee, sofa, turkey or lobster and this relates to the question that you will need to answer from the Trivia card.

When a player has completed one full lap of the outer ring on the board they move onto a white circle space between the outer and inner rings. To progress to the inner ring they must answer a question of their choice from cup of coffee, sofa, turkey or lobster – get it wrong and they can move one space on to the inner ring, get it correct and move the number of spaces as indicated on the card.

Complete one full lap of the inner ring and then move on to a white space in the center. Answer a question correctly to move from the white circle to the Central Perk image to win the game.

How Well do you and your partner work together? Is your combined brain power big enough to avoid the drop zone in this challenging game of trivia?

Working Together, you'll need to answer questions - or nominate another pair - to secure your position on the board. Get enough questions wring and you'll enter the drop zone and be out of the game. The Final surviving pair will be declared the winners

This promo pack contains 6 additional hero class cards with unique quest goals and exciting original artwork, plus one rules and setup card.

Add the Scout, Conjurer, Warlord, Fighter, Rogue, and Alchemist to your games and craft the tales of their heroic adventures.

Will these new adventurers be worthy of your storytelling skills?

SETUP
Before setting up the game, shuffle the Solstice mini-expansion cards into the deck of class cards.

NEW QUESTS
Several class cards in this mini-expansion have quests that score reputation stars for cards in the player's questline with masteries looking back for specific icons. These quests score reputation for each mastery even if the mastery condition(s) are not met.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: All Time Wrestling: Tag Team Edition
description:
All Time Wrestling: Tag Team Edition is an expansion that transforms the ATW system into a true tag team wrestling experience.

Built on the fast-paced, card-and-dice gameplay of the original game, Tag Team Edition introduces a complete overhaul of how matches are played. This is not just 2v2. It is a deeper, more strategic system that captures the chaos, teamwork and timing that define real tag team wrestling.

With a dedicated Tag Team Board, players now manage shared stamina, coordinate attacks and create powerful combo opportunities between partners.

New Tag Attack Cards allow both wrestlers to contribute to devastating double-team moves, while unique team abilities ensure every duo plays differently. The apron becomes a critical part of the action, enabling interference, breaking pins and triggering momentum-shifting effects.

The expansion introduces multiple ways to play:

1) 2v2 PvP for competitive tag matches
2) Co-op vs NPC where you team up against an automa heel team
3) Solo Tag Mode allowing one player to control both wrestlers

Featuring iconic tag teams from wrestling history including the Road Warriors, Rock ’n’ Roll Express, British Bulldogs and The Soviets, Tag Team Edition brings legendary partnerships into the ATW system. It also includes the Hall-of-Fame team Demolition as part of bundle content.

Designed for 1–4 players, the expansion is fully compatible with previous ATW releases, allowing players to mix wrestlers across sets and create dream tag teams.

—description from the publisher

Have fun with family and friends as you compete in this exciting game inspired by the thrilling and glamorous TV treasure Strictly Come Dancing.

Journey through training answering trivia questions, taking challenges and performing mini dance-offs, trying to won as many votes as possible.

Will you be the first to make it to the game's live show and dance to one of six tunes played on the revolving glitter ball speaker? Will you get a cha cha cha or a waltz, a jive or a Charleston?

Your fellow competitors vote and Craig Revel Horwood Scores. Will you get enough votes to life the ultimate prize... the glitter ball trophy?

The aim of the game is to have built the most Peppa Pigs by the end of the game. Throw the dice to obtain the body parts that you need to complete your Peppa Pig, but beware if your opponents throw a double six they can steal body parts

PAN! PAN! PAN! is a strategic board game where anticipation and positioning are key. Dive into the wacky world of a schoolyard cowboy duel! Each player leads a gang of formidable young gunslingers. Your objective is to strategically place your marksmen within a common grid. In this cork-pistol showdown, every card played face-down is a risky bet. The orientation of your character defines both your line of fire and the position of your protective barrels. The initiative order is ruthless: the fastest draw first, and a hit cowboy is eliminated before they can fire.

PAN! PAN! PAN! is a strategic board game of anticipation and positioning in a wacky schoolyard cowboy duel.

—description from the publisher

Словобраз ("WordForm") is a funny, fast-paced game where players put together pictures and guess what they are. Each card features 3 short words. Using the game components (discs, sticks, and strings), the player creates a picture that represents one of the words on their card. The other players then guess which word the picture is meant to be.

Place the sticks, discs, and strings in the center so everyone can reach them, put the scoring track nearby, and give each player a card from the shuffled deck. The game has two stages: first, all players simultaneously build a picture of one word from their card using the components; then, each player shows their picture and card while the others guess which word was illustrated. Every correct guess earns the guesser 1 point, while the picture's creator earns 2 points. If no one guesses correctly, the creator gets 0 points.

The game ends as soon as any player reaches 14 points – that player is the winner. If two or more players reach 14 points in the same round, they share the victory.

—description from the publisher (translated)

This game is a remake of the Словечки (Wordlets) game for fewer players with a new look.

Mind Incarnated is a 2-player strategy game set in a fractured reality where thoughts and myths manifest as physical entities. Players take the role of Mystikers who control these Manifestations in a direct duel.

Before the game begins, both players draft their cards from a shared, fully visible pool. There is no randomness during play. Each player controls one active unit at a time and can play new units, attach supporting cards, and activate abilities before declaring an attack. Combat is resolved through defined values such as speed, attack power, and life points.

When a unit is destroyed, its controller loses points for that unit and all attached cards. Because control over cards can change during the game, managing risk is essential.

To win the game, reduce your opponent’s total points to zero or force them into a state where they cannot play a unit.

All information is visible at all times unless explicitly stated otherwise, placing the focus on planning, counterplay, and long-term decision-making.

Hamsterium is a worker placement game about hamsters exploring space.

Send your hamsters to various facilities to gather resources, upgrade your laboratory, and launch drone hamsters into space.

Manage your energy carefully, overcome incoming asteroid threats, and take on missions to discover new celestial bodies and recover out-of-place artifact tiles. Aim to earn more victory points than anyone else!

Beneath its cute and charming visuals lies a game full of deep strategy and meaningful decisions.

Railway Porters is a board game for 2 to 4 players. You play as a porter, placing luggage dice onto station boards. There are 6 different station boards, each with its own unique scoring system, so you'll need to think carefully about where you place your dice. It's a fresh new dice-placement game where smart planning is mixed in with the exciting energy of a dice game!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Trismegistus: Book of Heavy Metal Promo Card
description:
Promo book card that was given out during Essen 2024.

Change in Setup
After you resolve all steps of the setup, give the Book card to the last player.

Change in Gameplay
When you are in possession of a Book card, and you are  drafting a die, you may exhaust it (flip it face down) to gain 2 Metal of that die type instead of 1 (you cannot resolve it while drafting ∞ die). At the end of a round, if the Book card is exhausted, flip it face up and pass it to the last player in turn order, or if you are the last player, pass it to the next player in reverse turn order.

Packing Heat is the ultimate set of skirmish rules for the modern era.  
With extremely deadly combat, tons of cinematic flair, and a Game Deck that keeps both players on their toes, no two games are the same!

Inspired by your favourite action movies and compatible with your existing miniatures, this book alone contains rules and stats for 19 Factions across 4 Settings, with loads more on the way!

     4 Settings, 19 Factions, 1 Book!  Not only that, but it's easy to make your own Settings and Factions.
     Fast and Brutal Combat!  In Packing Heat, everyone is vulnerable - like in real life, it only takes one lucky bullet...
     Huge Arsenal of Weapons and Tactics from Melee Weapons and Grenades to Attack Dogs and Cars - and let's not forget the guns!
     6 Thrilling Built-In Scenarios for every experience level, plus optional rules to enhance your own games!
family: History: Resistance during WW2 in Europe

description:
"Say Goodbye to the Tie"

In this version of Tic-Tac-Toe, boomerang-shaped red and white pieces are individually placed and can contribute to making either an X or an O.  The player taking the white pieces tries to make X's, while the red player makes O's.  This approach results in three twists.

The first twist is that the X or O formed can be either of one color or both colors; only the shape (X or O) matters.

The second twist is that there is a fourth way to win, a semi-diagonal. Since there are no distinct squares, an X or an O can straddle the border of what would be a traditional tic tac toe square, so the diagonal doesn't need to stretch from corner to corner.

The third twist is that even if no player achieved three-in-a-row during the placement of their pieces, players then take turns flipping their pieces until a three-in-a-row occurs.

Grim West: A Game Of Demonic Gold and Gunpowder

The Gold Rush brought thousands to the frontier, seeking fortune in the dirt. But they dug too deep.

Beneath the rock and ore, they uncovered ancient gates to Hell. Now, the frontier is a slaughterhouse where mining towns are overrun by hellish hordes. In Grim West, you lead a band of Wranglers—specialists, outlaws, and holy men—armed with iron, faith, and grit. Your mission is simple: hold back the darkness, protect the operations, and try to survive the night.

A Fast-Paced, Card-Driven Experience

Leave the dice and tape measures at home. Grim West uses a standard deck of playing cards for movement, combat, and soul-testing Bravado.

     Tactical Measurement: Distances are measured using the edges of your cards. Dictate your movement and range, making the game tactile and portable.
     The Power of the Deck: Every flip matters. High values win the day, but Aces are the wildcards of the West—representing critical hits for attackers or catastrophic failures for defenders.
     Brutal Combat: Attacks are fast and lethal. If your attack value doubles the defender's, you trigger an Overkill.


Build Your Posse

Recruit a team of 5 Wranglers from 7 distinct classes, each with unique abilities to turn the tide of battle:

     The Veteran: Hardened survivors who excel at defense and strike harder with black cards.
     The Surgeon: Essential for removing injuries in the heat of battle.
     The Harlot: Fueled by pain, becoming more dangerous as they take injuries.
     The Priest: A beacon of hope who provides Bravado bonuses and can ignore injuries through faith.
     And more... including the Tracker, Cowboy, and Bandit.


Equip your posse with period-accurate weaponry like Lever-Action Rifles, Six-Shooters, and Sabres, then bolster them with supernatural equipment like Holy Water or Infernal Tobacco.

Face the Legions of Hell
Battle a terrifying roster of demons, from the swarming Imps and ravenous Ghouls to the massive Abyssal Worms and the reality-warping Demon Lord. Be wary: a fallen Wrangler isn't always gone—they may become Possessed, turning their guns against their former allies.

Ways to Play

     Solo & Co-Op: Use the built-in AI directives to fight the demonic horde alone or with friends.
     Asymmetric Versus: One player leads the Wranglers while another commands the Forces of Hell.
     Campaign Play: Track your posse through the Hellfire Trail. Earn Bounty Coins to hire new recruits, buy gear, and upgrade your survivors—if they don't perish from their scars first.
     Modular Scenarios: Use the Scenario Crafter to generate hundreds of combinations of objectives, deployments, and special environmental rules like Cursed Fog or Hellfire Tremors.


What’s Included?
This digital package provides everything you need to start your journey into the dark frontier:

     Core Rules: Complete rules for movement, combat, and campaign play.
     Posse Builder: Tools to customize your Wranglers and print their reference cards.

WARS Napoleon’s Campaign brings players into the heart of early 19th-century European warfare, adapting the acclaimed WARS three-dimensional system to the Napoleonic era.

In this new expansion, players take on the role of Corps Generals, leading their forces across dynamic and tension-filled battlefields. 
Every decision matters: from choosing your army to managing order cards and executing maneuvers in the field. Only those who can combine strategic vision with tactical skill will be able to weaken the enemy and secure the victory points needed to win.

The game includes a rich set of components designed to fully immerse players in the Napoleonic period. Five complete armies — French, Austrian, British, Prussian, and Russian — are supported by 193 battalion bases, 42 token cards, 54 order cards, over 100 counters, and 6 objective markers. A comprehensive yet streamlined system that delivers variety without overwhelming gameplay.

WARS Napoleon’s Campaign introduces a new level of depth through its order card system, guiding players toward strategic objectives and new ways to earn victory points. This innovation integrates seamlessly with the established alternating command activation system, now enhanced by a new structure that separates strategic movement from tactical engagement movement, adding both tension and precision to battlefield control. The use of battalion-specific order tokens further expands decision-making, turning every unit into a critical asset to manage.

The result is a dynamic and engaging experience where no two battles play out the same, and every choice can shape the outcome. At the same time, the tactical freedom that made the WARS system popular among fans of three-dimensional wargaming remains intact, allowing players to adapt their command style and develop new strategies each time they play.

Despite its innovations, the game preserves one of its greatest strengths: accessibility. It is easy to learn, with a playtime of approximately 60 to 90 minutes. Its compact footprint — requiring only an 80×100 cm table — makes it an excellent choice for both casual sessions and organized events or tournaments.

WARS Napoleon’s Campaign is a natural evolution of the WARS system: accessible yet deep, fast-paced yet strategic, offering immersive and ever-changing battles. If you are looking for a truly innovative three-dimensional wargaming experience, this is your battlefield.

The WordWright’s Ledger
 Goal:

This is a game where Scrabble meets Math. Accumulate the most points by collecting words using math equations. Letter placement is controlled using simple math operations.

Components:
• 12 magnetic gameboard bases
• 12 magnetic connectors
• 50 magnetic letter tiles
• 4 dry erase score boards
• 1 dry erase marker
     
Setup:
• Assemble the game board by connecting the magnetic sections, in any configuration and orientation.
• Place and shuffle the letter tiles face down.
• Give each player five tiles as their “rack”.
• Give each player a score card.
• Have the dry erase pen handy.
• Agree upon a target score to win the game.
• Agree upon a minimum word length, default is three letters.

Simultaneous Game Play:
1. Roll both dice.
2. Call out the outcomes of two or more mathematically equations from the two numbers rolled (see chart below).
3. All players look for a space to drop a single letter from their rack of letters that matches one of the outcomes.
4. If a word is made (horizontally or vertically), that player records the point value of the letters on their score card. It is possible to form two words that intersect with a common letter.
5. That player can leave the word’s tiles in place or can remove them back into the draw pile.
6. Once all players have placed their letter, grab a replacement letter from the draw pile.
7. Repeat steps 1-6 until someone hits or exceeds the target score.

WordWright Spaces:
These are special spaces. You may only place a letter on a WordWright space when completing a word of four or more letters. Ignore the math outcome and must remove your word immediately after scoring.

Mathematically Equations of Dice Rolls:
 (chart)

Valid Words:
In this game, just about all words are valid including first names, places and countries. Acronyms are not valid.

FAQ:
• Do I need to place a letter adjacent to an adjacent letter? No. You may place your letter on any space matching a math outcome.

Tips:
• Remove your word when you feel you an opponent can expand your word for more points.
• Keep your word if you feel you can expand it on the next roll.

Optionally, you may use your own set of 50 Scrabble tiles.

—description from the designer

Rope Walkers is a joyful dice-rolling pocket game about balance, bravery, and keeping the world together. In this solo-only game, you play as a rope walker attending a festival to celebrate the day the world was split in two by a comet but held together by a single rope.

A play-through lasts 10-20 minutes, and your goal is to reach the other side of the rope tracker without falling. Each turn, you’ll roll two dice then decide how to use them to balance yourself. You can stop rolling whenever you like, or keep at it, pushing your luck to get as close to 14 and 15 as possible without going over.

Any other result will have varying degrees of adverse effects, but 14 and 15 will allow you to advance on the rope, which is needed to win.

Along the way, you’ll need to protect your Stamina, endure Environment effects, tend to Ailments, improve your Mastery, and manage a fleeting deck of Mastery cards that can be used to both replace die rolls and to access abilities.

Rope Walkers is about listening to the moment, choosing when to risk, and knowing when to hold steady. So take a breath, then take a step. The festival gatherers are waiting on the other side to celebrate with you.

—description from the designer

Snoop is a game of trickery in which the clues you give your team must first lead the snoopers astray. Choose clues to help your team guess the mystery word. It should be as simple as writing four clues...But wait, the other team gets to swoop in, snoop at two of the clues - and take their guesses first! Can your team outsmart the Snoopers and guess the word with the last two clues? Find out in Snoop!

boardgame: Pacific Heat: a Packing Heat Expansion
description:
Pacific Heat is the first Expansion for Packing Heat, adding the Pacific Theatre Setting to the ultimate squad combat wargame!

Expanding on the original rulebook's World War II setting, this 36-page setting module features Squads from the Sino-Japanese War and the clash of empires in Burma, all the way to the United States' "Island Hopping" campaigns – all whilst staying fast, brutal and fun!


     New Setting Rules for Jungle Fighting and Landing Vessels!
     5 Historical Factions:  Imperial Japan, British Empire, United States, Republic of China, plus local Militias & Guerrillas!
     2 Unique Scenarios:  "Amphibious Assault" and "The Bridge"!

Step into the world of cunning cats, where only the greediest and smartest will come out on top! In CAT’S, players take on the roles of rival cats competing over the best toys and treasures across 5 exciting rounds.

Each round begins with a bidding phase, where players secretly offer their Meow Coins to become the Big Boss Cat. The highest bidder earns the power to choose first—but being the boss comes at a cost!

The other players, acting as clever minions, split the available loot into sections. The Big Boss picks their favorite share, while the rest must strategically choose from what remains—or grab coins for future advantage.

With a mix of I Cut, You Choose, bidding, and tactical drafting, every decision matters. Will you divide the loot wisely, outbid your rivals, or collect the most valuable sets?

Score points through collecting toys, managing resources, and avoiding unwanted puppies. After 5 rounds, the cat with the highest score proves they are the ultimate top cat!

Easy to learn, full of clever decisions—perfect for players who enjoy interactive and strategic gameplay.

—description from the publisher

Enter a haunted world where timing, courage, and a bit of luck decide your fate! In GHOST, players compete to capture mysterious spirits by lining up in different haunted locations—but the deeper you go, the riskier it becomes.

During the game, players take turns placing their pieces into rows, forming queues to hunt ghosts. The further back you stand in line, the harder it is to succeed—but also the better odrer.

When all players have committed, each row is resolved using dice rolls. Success depends on the number of players in line and the risks you’ve taken. Fail, and you walk away empty-handed. Succeed, and you claim valuable ghost cards with unique abilities!

Use Little Ghosts to manipulate outcomes—reroll dice, block opponents, or turn the odds in your favor. But resources are limited, and every choice matters.

After 4 rounds, players score by multiplying the sets of symbols they’ve collected. The player with the highest score—and the most Ghost King rewards—wins!

A tense blend of push-your-luck, timing, and player interaction, perfect for those who enjoy risk and mind games.

—description from the publisher

Step into the role of a talent manager in a dazzling interstellar entertainment industry! In Galaxy Idol, players compete to build the most successful idol agency by scouting talent, forming powerful combinations, and outplaying their rivals.

Each round, players combine cards from their hand and the shared stage to create the strongest possible “performance”—inspired by classic poker-style combinations like straights and flushes. Reading the table and timing your moves is key to success.

But it’s not just about your own hand. Send your managers to invest in other players’ performances, betting on who will succeed based on limited information. Predict correctly, and you’ll earn big rewards. Misjudge, and you’ll fall behind.

With a blend of card strategy, betting, and player interaction, every round is filled with tension and clever mind games.

At the end of the show, only one manager will rise to the top as the ultimate galaxy idol producer!

A unique mix of poker mechanics and competitive management.

—description from the publisher

Antarc-Tic is a fast-paced tabletop game that combines the classic logic of tic-tac-toe with an exciting flicking mechanic. Players control a team of penguins, skillfully launching them across the icy board to claim positions and form winning lines.

Every move requires both precision and strategy—aim carefully, adjust your strength, and outmaneuver your opponent as the game board becomes increasingly dynamic. The familiar objective of lining up three is reimagined through physical interaction, turning each turn into a test of both planning and dexterity.

—description from the publisher

In Tydzień jaskiniowca, you must plan and survive seven days in the Stone Age. It’s up to you whether you try to invent the wheel, earn a mammoth hunter certificate, or learn the basics of fire safety. The bravest can look forward to games of tag with sharks and debates about whether the Ice Age is fake!

Choose the activities you’ll dedicate your time to, manage your stress levels, decide how to spend the money you earn, and remember: only a caveman who takes care of their well-being and work-life balance has a chance of making it through CEOlith with dignity!

Tydzień z życia jaskiniowca is a card-based planning game with no dice or board movement. It focuses on decision-making, time management, and humorously reflecting on modern work-life balance in a prehistoric setting.

Quick rules overview:


     Each round, 3 activity cards are revealed from the deck.
     All players simultaneously choose activities from 2 cards and write them into their weekly plan.
     After 8 rounds, the player who best balances work, learning, leisure, expenses, and stress wins.


—description from the publisher

boardgame: El Hilo Invisible: El juego de mesa
description:
The board game inspired by Miriam Tirado’s wonderful story, *The Invisible Thread*, is here.
In this cooperative game, help Nura discover what the thread that connects all her loved ones is like before it’s time to go to sleep.
Roll the dice, find the thread’s path among the tiles, and share your favorite memories with your loved ones.
Connect all the characters with their invisible thread before night falls, and you’ll win the game!

—description from the publisher

Llega el juego de mesa inspirado en el fantástico cuento de Miriam Tirado, El hilo invisible.
Ayudad a Nura en este juego cooperativo a conocer cómo es el hilo que conecta a todos sus seres queridos antes de que llegue la hora de irse a dormir.
Lanzad los dados, buscad el camino de hilo entre las losetas y compartid vuestros recuerdos preferidos con vuestros seres queridos.
Unid a todos los personajes con su hilo invisible antes de que se haga de noche y así ¡ganaréis la partida!

—description from the publisher (Spanish)

Gorges de l'ArDeck is a family-friendly flip & write board game that takes you to the heart of the Ardèche Gorges.
Each turn, you draw a card, mark your sheet, and trace your path to explore the iconic landscapes of the Ardèche river, optimize your route, and score as many points as possible.
